> Thought for 5s

Short answer: you don’t have to—but you **should** consolidate to avoid drift. Both classes do “new project” with slightly different entry points (interactive vs. from active drawing). Put them in **one static helper** with two clearly named methods, then keep the registrar as one-liners.

Here’s a clean merge that fits your ProjectStore folder and tiny-command style.

# Consolidated helper (single file)

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/NewProjectCommands.cs
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)

using System;
using System.IO;
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
using AcDoc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document;
using AcEd  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using WinForms = System.Windows.Forms;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    /// <summary>Centralized "new project" flows.</summary>
    public static class NewProjectCommands
    {
        private static readonly string GlobalStorePath = @"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json";

        /// <summary>
        /// Ribbon-friendly flow: builds support folder next to the active DWG,
        /// seeds Project_Store.json, binds NOD, and refreshes the palette banner.
        /// </summary>
        public static void CreateFromActiveDrawing()
        {
            var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // 1) Require saved drawing
            var dwgPath = doc.Name;
            var root = Path.GetDirectoryName(dwgPath);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(root))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nPlease save the drawing first.");
                return;
            }

            // 2) Project name default from DWG
            var defaultName = Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(dwgPath);
            var name = PromptForProjectName(ed, defaultName);
            if (name == null) return; // cancel

            // 3) Support folder + local store path
            var supportDir = Path.Combine(root, "CBI_Support");
            Directory.CreateDirectory(supportDir);
            var localStorePath = Path.Combine(supportDir, "Project_Store.json");

            // 4) Create/seed store (Shared service owns shape & validation)
            var projectId = Guid.NewGuid().ToString("D");
            var manifestPath = ProjectStorePaths.GetManifestPath(); // your shared resolver
            ProjectStoreService.CreateNewStore(projectId, name, localStorePath, manifestPath);

            // 5) Bind to drawing via NOD (your AutoCAD side binder)
            ProjectNodBinder.BindProjectToDrawing(doc, projectId, name, localStorePath);

            // 6) Optional readme/changelog (idempotent)
            var readme = Path.Combine(supportDir, "Readme.txt");
            if (!File.Exists(readme))
            {
                File.WriteAllText(readme,
@"CBI Support Folder

- Project_Store.json   : Systems, counters, entities, links (schema v1)
- Changelog.txt        : High-level project log (append-only)
");
            }
            var changelog = Path.Combine(supportDir, "Changelog.txt");
            if (!File.Exists(changelog)) File.WriteAllText(changelog, string.Empty);

            // 7) UI feedback + palette banner refresh
            ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI Project created at:\n  {localStorePath}");
            AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub.CbiPropertiesPalette.RefreshBannerIfOpen();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Interactive flow: user picks project name and a root folder anywhere
        /// (also initializes global Project_Store.json if you use one).
        /// </summary>
        public static void CreateInteractive()
        {
            var name = PromptInput("Enter Project Name:");
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name)) return;

            var root = PromptFolder("Select Project Root Folder:");
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(root)) return;

            var scan = WinForms.DialogResult.Yes ==
                       WinForms.MessageBox.Show("Scan root for DWGs and seed the drawing list?",
                                                "Seed Drawings",
                                                WinForms.MessageBoxButtons.YesNo);

            var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(GlobalStorePath);
            svc.CreateNewProject(name, root, scan);

            AcApp.ShowAlertDialog($"Project '{name}' created and set active.\nRoot: {root}");
        }

        // ---------- small UI helpers ----------
        private static string PromptForProjectName(AcEd.Editor ed, string suggestion)
        {
            var p = new AcEd.PromptStringOptions("\nEnter project name")
            {
                AllowSpaces = true,
                DefaultValue = suggestion
            };
            var r = ed.GetString(p);
            if (r.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            var s = (r.StringResult ?? string.Empty).Trim();
            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) ? suggestion : s;
        }

        private static string PromptInput(string message)
        {
            using (var f = new WinForms.Form { Width = 340, Height = 150, Text = "CBI New Project" })
            using (var tb = new WinForms.TextBox { Left = 10, Top = 35, Width = 300 })
            using (var lbl = new WinForms.Label { Left = 10, Top = 10, Width = 300, Text = message })
            using (var ok = new WinForms.Button { Left = 160, Top = 70, Width = 70, Text = "OK", DialogResult = WinForms.DialogResult.OK })
            using (var cancel = new WinForms.Button { Left = 240, Top = 70, Width = 70, Text = "Cancel", DialogResult = WinForms.DialogResult.Cancel })
            {
                f.Controls.Add(lbl); f.Controls.Add(tb); f.Controls.Add(ok); f.Controls.Add(cancel);
                f.AcceptButton = ok; f.CancelButton = cancel;
                return f.ShowDialog() == WinForms.DialogResult.OK ? tb.Text : null;
            }
        }

        private static string PromptFolder(string title)
        {
            using (var dlg = new WinForms.FolderBrowserDialog { Description = title, ShowNewFolderButton = true })
                return dlg.ShowDialog() == WinForms.DialogResult.OK ? dlg.SelectedPath : null;
        }
    }
}
```

# Registrar stays tiny (just delegates)

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_NewProject")]                 // interactive anywhere
        public static void CBI_NewProject()
            => NewProjectCommands.CreateInteractive();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_NewProjectFromActive")]       // ribbon-next-to-DWG flow
        public static void CBI_NewProjectFromActive()
            => NewProjectCommands.CreateFromActiveDrawing();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_RepairProjectStore")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore()
            => ProjectRepairCommands.RepairProjectStore();
    }
}
```

# Why this is better
- One place owns all **“new project”** logic → no drift.
- Two clear entry points:
  - **CreateInteractive** (global store + root chooser)
  - **CreateFromActiveDrawing** (support folder beside DWG + NOD bind)
- Keeps your **one-liner** command style.
- Lives under **ProjectStore** with your existing management code.

If you prefer a single command name, you can keep `CBI_NewProject` and choose which path to call based on context (e.g., if drawing is saved → use FromActive else Interactive).

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Valves/TagAllocator_V2.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator_V2
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public TagAllocator_V2(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        public string PeekValveTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(k) || !s.Projects.ContainsKey(k)) return "V-?";
            var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve;
            var num = ch.Padding > 0 ? ch.NextNumber.ToString(new string('0', ch.Padding)) : ch.NextNumber.ToString();
            return ch.Prefix + num;
        }

        public string AllocateValveTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve;
            var num = ch.Padding > 0 ? ch.NextNumber.ToString(new string('0', ch.Padding)) : ch.NextNumber.ToString();
            var tag = ch.Prefix + num;
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _svc.Write(s);
            return tag;
        }

        public string PeekLineTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line;
            var num = ch.Padding > 0 ? ch.NextNumber.ToString(new string('0', ch.Padding)) : ch.NextNumber.ToString();
            return ch.Prefix + num;
        }

        public string AllocateLineTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line;
            var num = ch.Padding > 0 ? ch.NextNumber.ToString(new string('0', ch.Padding)) : ch.NextNumber.ToString();
            var tag = ch.Prefix + num;
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _svc.Write(s);
            return tag;
        }
    }
}
```

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Valves/ValveDefinitionResolver.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System.Collections.Generic;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves
{
    public enum ValveType { Ball, Butterfly, Check }

    public sealed class BlockDefinitionRef { public string Name; public string Path; }

    public sealed class ValveSizeMapInfo
    {
        public string DynamicPropName;
        public string Units; // "in" or "mm"
        public Dictionary<string, double> NominalToScale = new Dictionary<string, double>();
    }

    public sealed class ValveDefinitionResolver
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public ValveDefinitionResolver(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        public BlockDefinitionRef Resolve(ValveType type)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            var v = s.Projects[k].Blocks.Valve;

            var key = type.ToString(); // "Ball","Butterfly","Check"
            if (!v.Types.ContainsKey(key)) return new BlockDefinitionRef { Name = key, Path = null };
            var t = v.Types[key];
            return new BlockDefinitionRef { Name = t.Name, Path = t.Path };
        }

        public ValveSizeMapInfo GetSizeMap()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            var m = s.Projects[k].Blocks.Valve.SizeMap;
            return new ValveSizeMapInfo
            {
                DynamicPropName = m.DynamicProperty,
                Units = m.Units,
                NominalToScale = new Dictionary<string, double>(m.NominalToBlockScale)
            };
        }
    }
}
```

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Valves/ValveDynPropUtil.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System.Collections.Generic;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves
{
    public static class ValveDynPropUtil
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Produces a payload for dynamic properties/attributes when inserting.
        /// For now returns a string dictionary: key = dyn prop name; value = value text/scale.
        /// </summary>
        public static IDictionary<string, string> Build(ValveSizeMapInfo map, string nominalDiameter)
        {
            var dict = new Dictionary<string, string>();
            if (map == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(map.DynamicPropName)) return dict;
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(nominalDiameter)) return dict;

            // Normalize ND like "2", "2.0", "2in", "DN50" — minimal first pass:
            var key = nominalDiameter.Trim();
            double scale;
            if (map.NominalToScale.TryGetValue(key, out scale))
            {
                dict[map.DynamicPropName] = scale.ToString(); // consumer may parse as double
            }
            else
            {
                // Fallback: write ND as-is; insert service can decide how to map
                dict[map.DynamicPropName] = key;
            }
            return dict;
        }
    }
}
```

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Valves/PipeReader.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public sealed class PipeInfo
    {
        public string LineTag;
        public string NominalDiameter;
        public double? TangentRotation; // radians
        public string HandleString;
    }

    public interface IPipeReader
    {
        PipeInfo ReadFromPolyline(ObjectId polyId, Point3d nearPoint);
    }

    public sealed class PipeReader : IPipeReader
    {
        public PipeInfo ReadFromPolyline(ObjectId polyId, Point3d nearPoint)
        {
            var info = new PipeInfo();
            using (var tr = polyId.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var pl = tr.GetObject(polyId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Polyline;
                if (pl == null) return info;

                info.HandleString = pl.Handle.ToString();

                // TODO: read XData/NOD for LineTag and NominalDiameter
                // info.LineTag = XDataUtil.ReadString(pl, "cbi.pipe.LineTag");
                // info.NominalDiameter = XDataUtil.ReadString(pl, "cbi.pipe.NominalDiameter");

                // Tangent @ closest segment
                double param = pl.GetParameterAtPoint(pl.GetClosestPointTo(nearPoint, false));
                var deriv = pl.GetFirstDerivative(pl.GetPointAtParameter(param));
                if (!deriv.IsZeroLength())
                    info.TangentRotation = System.Math.Atan2(deriv.Y, deriv.X);

                tr.Commit();
            }
            return info;
        }
    }
}
```

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Valves/BlockInsertService.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public interface IBlockInsertService
    {
        ObjectId EnsureAndInsert(BlockDefinitionRef def, Point3d position, double rotationRad,
                                 IDictionary<string, string> dynPropsOrAtts);
    }

    public sealed class BlockInsertService : IBlockInsertService
    {
        public ObjectId EnsureAndInsert(BlockDefinitionRef def, Point3d position, double rotationRad,
                                        IDictionary<string, string> dynPropsOrAtts)
        {
            var db = HostApplicationServices.WorkingDatabase;
            ObjectId brefId = ObjectId.Null;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // TODO: ensure block definition exists; if not, wblock insert from def.Path
                // var btrId = BlockDefUtil.Ensure(def.Name, def.Path, tr);

                // Minimal placeholder: assume definition exists
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!bt.Has(def.Name)) throw new System.Exception("Block def not found: " + def.Name);
                var msId = bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace];
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(msId, OpenMode.ForWrite);

                var br = new BlockReference(position, bt[def.Name]);
                br.Rotation = rotationRad;
                brefId = ms.AppendEntity(br);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(br, true);

                // TODO: set dynamic properties/attributes from dynPropsOrAtts

                tr.Commit();
            }
            return brefId;
        }
    }
}
```

        private const string K_Type  = "cbi.valve.ValveType";
        private const string K_Size  = "cbi.valve.Size";

        public ValveMetadata ReadValveMetadata(ObjectId valveBlockId)

### `AutoCAD2020/Valves/InsertValveCommand.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System.Collections.Generic;
using AcApp  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
using AcDoc  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document;
using AcEd   = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AcDb   = Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AcGeom = Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;

using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Minimal valve insert flow:
    /// - optional host pipe → size + tangent
    /// - resolve block def from Project_Store
    /// - insert
    /// - read valve tag; if missing allocate & write back
    /// </summary>
    public static class InsertValveCommand
    {
        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // 1) Pick type
            var typeOpt = PromptValveType(ed);
            if (!typeOpt.HasValue) return;
            var valveType = typeOpt.Value;

            // 2) Optional host polyline
            AcDb.ObjectId? hostPolyId = PromptPolylineOptional(ed, "\nSelect host pipe polyline (ESC to skip): ");

            // 3) Insertion point
            var ppr = ed.GetPoint("\nPick valve insertion point: ");
            if (ppr.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return;
            var insPt = ppr.Value;

            // 4) Resolve services
            var settings = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
            var resolver = new ValveDefinitionResolver(settings);
            var blockDef = resolver.Resolve(valveType);
            var sizeMap  = resolver.GetSizeMap();

            var blockSvc = new BlockInsertService();
            var metaLink = new ValveMetadataLinker();
            var pipeRead = new PipeReader();
            var tags     = new TagAllocator_V2(settings);
            var history  = new ChangeHistoryService();

            // 5) Derive size + rotation from host
            string hostHandle = null;
            string nominalDia = null;
            double rotation = 0.0;

            if (hostPolyId.HasValue)
            {
                var info = pipeRead.ReadFromPolyline(hostPolyId.Value, insPt);
                hostHandle = info?.HandleString;
                nominalDia = info?.NominalDiameter;
                if (info != null && info.TangentRotation.HasValue)
                    rotation = info.TangentRotation.Value;
            }

            // Dyn props payload (SIZE or scale)
            IDictionary<string, string> dynProps = ValveDynPropUtil.Build(sizeMap, nominalDia);

            // 6) Insert
            AcDb.ObjectId newValveId;
            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                newValveId = blockSvc.EnsureAndInsert(blockDef, insPt, rotation, dynProps);
                tr.Commit();
            }

            // 7) Tag handling: read first; if none, allocate
            var meta = metaLink.ReadValveMetadata(newValveId) ?? new ValveMetadata();
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(meta.ValveTag))
            {
                meta.ValveTag = tags.AllocateValveTag(); // increments store
            }

            meta.ValveType      = valveType.ToString();
            meta.Size           = nominalDia;
            meta.HostLineHandle = hostHandle;
            meta.ProjectKey     = settings.Read().ActiveProjectKey;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                metaLink.WriteValveMetadata(newValveId, meta);
                tr.Commit();
            }

            // 8) Log
            history.RecordValveInsert(doc.Name, newValveId.Handle.ToString(), meta);
            ed.WriteMessage($"\nValve inserted [{meta.ValveTag ?? "(untagged)"}].");
        }

        // ---------- prompts ----------
        private static ValveType? PromptValveType(AcEd.Editor ed)
        {
            var pko = new AcEd.PromptKeywordOptions("\nValve type");
            pko.Keywords.Add("Ball");
            pko.Keywords.Add("Butterfly");
            pko.Keywords.Add("Check");
            pko.AllowNone = false;
            var res = ed.GetKeywords(pko);
            if (res.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            switch (res.StringResult)
            {
                case "Ball": return ValveType.Ball;
                case "Butterfly": return ValveType.Butterfly;
                case "Check": return ValveType.Check;
                default: return null;
            }
        }

        private static AcDb.ObjectId? PromptPolylineOptional(AcEd.Editor ed, string prompt)
        {
            var peo = new AcEd.PromptEntityOptions(prompt);
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(AcDb.Polyline), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            return per.ObjectId;
        }
    }
}
```

### `AutoCAD2020/Valves/ValveTagCommand.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
using AcEd  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AcDb  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Ensures a selected valve block has a tag:
    /// - read existing tag; if present, allow overwrite (optional)
    /// - if missing, allocate next and write back
    /// </summary>
    public static class ValveTagCommand
    {
        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            var brefId = PromptValveBlock(ed);
            if (!brefId.HasValue) return;

            var settings = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
            var tags     = new TagAllocator_V2(settings);
            var linker   = new ValveMetadataLinker();

            var meta = linker.ReadValveMetadata(brefId.Value) ?? new ValveMetadata();
            meta.ProjectKey = settings.Read().ActiveProjectKey;

            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(meta.ValveTag))
            {
                meta.ValveTag = tags.AllocateValveTag();
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    linker.WriteValveMetadata(brefId.Value, meta);
                    tr.Commit();
                }
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nAssigned ValveTag: {meta.ValveTag}");
            }
            else
            {
                // Optional overwrite UI: simple prompt
                var pso = new AcEd.PromptStringOptions($"\nCurrent tag '{meta.ValveTag}'. Enter new tag or press Enter to keep:")
                {
                    AllowSpaces = true,
                    UseDefaultValue = true,
                    DefaultValue = meta.ValveTag
                };
                var res = ed.GetString(pso);
                if (res.Status == AcEd.PromptStatus.OK && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(res.StringResult) &&
                    res.StringResult.Trim() != meta.ValveTag)
                {
                    meta.ValveTag = res.StringResult.Trim();
                    using (doc.LockDocument())
                    using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                    {
                        linker.WriteValveMetadata(brefId.Value, meta);
                        tr.Commit();
                    }
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nUpdated ValveTag: {meta.ValveTag}");
                }
            }
        }

        private static AcDb.ObjectId? PromptValveBlock(AcEd.Editor ed)
        {
            var peo = new AcEd.PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect valve block:");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a block reference.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(AcDb.BlockReference), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            return per.ObjectId;
        }
    }
}
```

# 1) XData helpers (safe + minimal)

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Interop/XDataUtil.cs` (or wherever you keep helpers)

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Interop
{
    public static class XDataUtil
    {
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName, Transaction tr)
        {
            var regTbl = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!regTbl.Has(appName))
            {
                regTbl.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                regTbl.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
            }
        }

        public static string ReadString(DBObject dbo, string key, string appName)
        {
            // XData is stored as: (AppName)(key)(value)
            var rb = dbo.XData;
            if (rb == null) return null;

            var vals = rb.AsArray();
            for (int i = 0; i < vals.Length - 2; i++)
            {
                if (vals[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName &&
                    string.Equals(vals[i].Value as string, appName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                    vals[i + 1].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                    string.Equals(vals[i + 1].Value as string, key, StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    var v = vals[i + 2];
                    if (v.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString) return v.Value as string;
                    if (v.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataXCoord ||
                        v.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataReal)
                        return v.Value?.ToString();
                }
            }
            return null;
        }

        public static void WriteString(DBObject dbo, string key, string value, string appName, Transaction tr)
        {
            var db = dbo.Database;
            EnsureRegApp(db, appName, tr);

            // Build a new buffer by filtering out existing (appName,key,*) triple, then appending updated triple
            var list = new System.Collections.Generic.List<TypedValue>();
            var old = dbo.XData?.AsArray();
            if (old != null)
            {
                for (int i = 0; i < old.Length; )
                {
                    if (old[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName &&
                        string.Equals(old[i].Value as string, appName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                        i + 2 < old.Length &&
                        old[i + 1].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                        string.Equals(old[i + 1].Value as string, key, StringComparison.Ordinal))
                    {
                        // skip this triple
                        i += 3;
                        continue;
                    }
                    list.Add(old[i]);
                    i++;
                }
            }

            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, key));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, value ?? string.Empty));

            dbo.UpgradeOpen();
            dbo.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }
    }
}
```

# 2) Valve metadata linker (now using XData)

**Update:** `AutoCAD2020/Valves/ValveMetadataLinker.cs`

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AutoCAD2020.Interop;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public sealed class ValveMetadataLinker : IValveMetadataLinker
    {
        private const string AppName = "CBI_META";
        private const string K_Tag   = "cbi.valve.ValveTag";
        private const string K_Type  = "cbi.valve.ValveType";
        private const string K_Size  = "cbi.valve.Size";
        private const string K_Host  = "cbi.valve.HostLineHandle";
        private const string K_Proj  = "cbi.valve.ProjectKey";

        public ValveMetadata ReadValveMetadata(ObjectId valveBlockId)
        {
            var meta = new ValveMetadata();
            using (var tr = valveBlockId.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var br = tr.GetObject(valveBlockId, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                if (br == null) return meta;

                meta.ValveTag      = XDataUtil.ReadString(br, K_Tag,  AppName);
                meta.ValveType     = XDataUtil.ReadString(br, K_Type, AppName);
                meta.Size          = XDataUtil.ReadString(br, K_Size, AppName);
                meta.HostLineHandle= XDataUtil.ReadString(br, K_Host, AppName);
                meta.ProjectKey    = XDataUtil.ReadString(br, K_Proj, AppName);

                tr.Commit();
            }
            return meta;
        }

        public void WriteValveMetadata(ObjectId valveBlockId, ValveMetadata meta)
        {
            using (var tr = valveBlockId.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var br = tr.GetObject(valveBlockId, OpenMode.ForWrite) as BlockReference;
                if (br == null) return;

                XDataUtil.WriteString(br, K_Tag,  meta.ValveTag,       AppName, tr);
                XDataUtil.WriteString(br, K_Type, meta.ValveType,      AppName, tr);
                XDataUtil.WriteString(br, K_Size, meta.Size,           AppName, tr);
                XDataUtil.WriteString(br, K_Host, meta.HostLineHandle, AppName, tr);
                XDataUtil.WriteString(br, K_Proj, meta.ProjectKey,     AppName, tr);

                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

# 3) PipeReader reads pipe XData

**Update:** `AutoCAD2020/Valves/PipeReader.cs`

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using AutoCAD2020.Interop;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public sealed class PipeReader : IPipeReader
    {
        private const string AppName = "CBI_META";
        private const string K_LineTag = "cbi.pipe.LineTag";
        private const string K_NomDia  = "cbi.pipe.NominalDiameter";

        public PipeInfo ReadFromPolyline(ObjectId polyId, Point3d nearPoint)
        {
            var info = new PipeInfo();
            using (var tr = polyId.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var pl = tr.GetObject(polyId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Polyline;
                if (pl == null) return info;

                info.HandleString = pl.Handle.ToString();
                info.LineTag = XDataUtil.ReadString(pl, K_LineTag, AppName);
                info.NominalDiameter = XDataUtil.ReadString(pl, K_NomDia, AppName);

                // Tangent
                var pt = pl.GetClosestPointTo(nearPoint, false);
                double param;
                try { param = pl.GetParameterAtPoint(pt); }
                catch { param = pl.GetParameterAtDistance(pl.GetDistAtPoint(pt)); }
                var deriv = pl.GetFirstDerivative(pl.GetPointAtParameter(param));
                if (!deriv.IsZeroLength())
                    info.TangentRotation = System.System.Math.Atan2(deriv.Y, deriv.X);

                tr.Commit();
            }
            return info;
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 4) Block definition ensure + insert + dyn props + attributes

**Update:** `AutoCAD2020/Valves/BlockInsertService.cs`

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Valves;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public sealed class BlockInsertService : IBlockInsertService
    {
        public ObjectId EnsureAndInsert(BlockDefinitionRef def, Point3d position, double rotationRad,
                                        IDictionary<string, string> dynPropsOrAtts)
        {
            if (def == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(def.Name))
                throw new ArgumentException("BlockDefinitionRef is missing Name.");

            var db = HostApplicationServices.WorkingDatabase;
            ObjectId brefId = ObjectId.Null;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // 1) Ensure the block definition exists
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!bt.Has(def.Name))
                {
                    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(def.Path) || !File.Exists(def.Path))
                        throw new FileNotFoundException("Block def not found in current DB and no valid Path provided.", def.Path ?? "(null)");

                    // Import from external DWG
                    using (var srcDb = new Database(false, true))
                    {
                        srcDb.ReadDwgFile(def.Path, FileOpenMode.OpenForReadAndAllShare, true, null);
                        db.Insert(def.Name, srcDb, false); // imports all symbols; ensures def.Name exists
                    }
                    bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    if (!bt.Has(def.Name)) throw new Exception("Failed to import block definition: " + def.Name);
                }

                // 2) Insert BlockReference
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForWrite);
                var br = new BlockReference(position, bt[def.Name])
                {
                    Rotation = rotationRad
                };
                brefId = ms.AppendEntity(br);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(br, true);

                // 3) Add missing attributes (if any)
                AddMissingAttributes(br, tr);

                // 4) Apply dynamic properties / attributes from payload
                ApplyDynProps(br, dynPropsOrAtts);
                ApplyAttributes(br, dynPropsOrAtts, tr);

                tr.Commit();
            }
            return brefId;
        }

        private static void AddMissingAttributes(BlockReference br, Transaction tr)
        {
            var btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(br.BlockTableRecord, OpenMode.ForRead);
            foreach (ObjectId id in btr)
            {
                var obj = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as AttributeDefinition;
                if (obj == null || obj.Constant) continue;

                var ar = new AttributeReference();
                ar.SetAttributeFromBlock(obj, br.BlockTransform);
                ar.Position = obj.Position.TransformBy(br.BlockTransform);

                br.UpgradeOpen();
                br.AttributeCollection.AppendAttribute(ar);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(ar, true);
            }
        }

        private static void ApplyDynProps(BlockReference br, IDictionary<string, string> map)
        {
            if (map == null || map.Count == 0) return;

            try
            {
                var coll = br.DynamicBlockReferencePropertyCollection;
                if (coll == null) return;
                foreach (DynamicBlockReferenceProperty p in coll)
                {
                    string val;
                    if (!map.TryGetValue(p.PropertyName, out val)) continue;

                    object typed = val;
                    // try parse numeric if property expects numeric-ish
                    double d;
                    if ((p.UnitsType != DynamicBlockReferencePropertyUnitsType.Angular) &&
                        double.TryParse(val, System.Globalization.NumberStyles.Float, System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out d))
                        typed = d;

                    if (!p.ReadOnly && p.IsDynamic) p.Value = typed;
                }
            }
            catch
            {
                // Not a dynamic block (or no props) — ignore
            }
        }

        private static void ApplyAttributes(BlockReference br, IDictionary<string, string> map, Transaction tr)
        {
            if (map == null || map.Count == 0) return;
            if (br.AttributeCollection == null) return;

            foreach (ObjectId arId in br.AttributeCollection)
            {
                var ar = tr.GetObject(arId, OpenMode.ForWrite) as AttributeReference;
                if (ar == null) continue;

                string val;
                if (map.TryGetValue(ar.Tag, out val))
                    ar.TextString = val ?? string.Empty;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## How to use (you already wired it)

- `CBI_InsertValve` → `InsertValveCommand.Run()`  
  - If the block isn’t in the drawing, it’s imported from `Path`.
  - Size mapping applied via dynamic property (e.g., `SIZE`).
  - Tag is read; if missing → allocated and written to XData.
- `CBI_ValveTag` → `ValveTagCommand.Run()`  
  - Reads XData; allocates+writes if missing; optional overwrite prompt.

If you want, I can also wire the **Line Tag apply** to increment on commit (polyline XData write) using the same XDataUtil pattern.

## Response:
10/15/2025, 5:33:00 PM

Perfect question — and I love that you’re thinking structurally before just dropping in another helper file.  

Looking at your screenshots, you already have the exact organizational pattern we’d want:

### ✅ You have:
```
AutoCAD2020
├── Block_Operations
├── BlockTools
├── CadInterop         ← 🟢 already exists!
├── CBI_Data_Explorer
├── Debugging
├── Drawing_Operations
├── InquiryTools
├── Layer_Operations
├── NOD_X_Data
├── Objects
├── Palette
├── ProjectStore
├── PropertiesHub
├── Ribbon
├── Services
├── Sheet Set Manager
├── Tagging
├── Utilities
├── Valves
```

That `CadInterop` folder is exactly the right conceptual place for what we just built (`XDataUtil.cs`).

---

### 🧩 Why `CadInterop` fits best
- **Purpose match:** “Interop” generally means bridging *AutoCAD’s low-level APIs* (like `DBObject`, `Transaction`, `XData`, `RegAppTable`, `ResultBuffer`, etc.).  
- Your other helpers there (`CadLayerServices`, `DocumentLockHelper`, etc.) are also interop-style utilities.
- Keeps all “raw database communication” in one cohesive place — clean separation from your “Valves” domain logic.

---

### ✅ So the answer:
👉 **Place `XDataUtil.cs` under `AutoCAD2020/CadInterop/`**  
with namespace:
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.CadInterop
```

and update the few callers accordingly:
```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.CadInterop;
```

---

### 🧱 Recommended final structure snapshot

```
AutoCAD2020
├── CadInterop
│   ├── CadLayerServices.cs
│   ├── DocumentLockHelper.cs
│   ├── LayerDefinitionDeserializer_A2020.cs
│   ├── LayerExcelImporter.cs
│   ├── NODLayerImporter.cs
│   ├── XRecordHelper.cs
│   └── XDataUtil.cs          ← 🔹 new addition
│
├── Valves
│   ├── BlockInsertService.cs
│   ├── ChangeHistoryService.cs
│   ├── InsertValveCommand.cs
│   ├── PipeReader.cs
│   ├── ValveMetadataLinker.cs
│   ├── ValveTagCommand.cs
│   └── (future: ValveEditCommand.cs)
│
└── ...
```

Great timing—your **Tagging** area already has the right bones. We’ll slot our new bits into there so everything stays coherent.

## Where things should live

- **Move** `TagAllocator_V2` → `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs`  
  (adapter that reuses your `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` + `NodLineNumberStore`)
- Keep `XDataUtil.cs` under **CadInterop** (that’s perfect).
- Valve commands/services stay under **Valves** (already done).

## Minimal glue to unify numbering for Lines + Valves

### 1) TagAllocator_V2 (inside Tagging/Services)

This adapter uses your existing `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` (JSON R/W) and mirrors the “peek / allocate” behavior for **Line** and **Valve** so both the **ValveTag** tool and the **LineTag** tool share one source of truth.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Read/Write adapter for tag prefixes and counters.
    /// Uses the shared ProjectSettingsService JSON store.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class TagAllocator_V2
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public TagAllocator_V2(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        public string PeekValveTag() => BuildTag(_svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Valve.Prefix,
                                                 _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Valve.NextNumber,
                                                 _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Valve.Padding);

        public string AllocateValveTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve;
            var tag = BuildTag(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _svc.Write(s);
            return tag;
        }

        public string PeekLineTag() => BuildTag(_svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Line.Prefix,
                                                _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Line.NextNumber,
                                                _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Line.Padding);

        public string AllocateLineTag()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; var ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line;
            var tag = BuildTag(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _svc.Write(s);
            return tag;
        }

        private static string BuildTag(string prefix, int number, int padding)
        {
            var n = padding > 0 ? number.ToString(new string('0', padding)) : number.ToString();
            return (prefix ?? string.Empty) + n;
        }
    }
}
```

> If you prefer to pipe this through your existing `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` or `NodLineNumberStore`, you can replace the `ProjectSettingsService` calls with that API—the entry points (Peek*/Allocate*) stay the same.

---

### 2) Apply/Write Line Tag to a polyline (auto-allocate if empty)

This matches the workflow you described: when the user **applies** data to a polyline, if `LineTag` is missing we allocate and write it to XData (and increment).

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Commands/LineTagCommands.cs  (add this method)
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
using AcEd  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AcDb  = Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AutoCAD2020.CadInterop;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands
{
    public static class LineTagCommands
    {
        private const string AppName   = "CBI_META";
        private const string K_LineTag = "cbi.pipe.LineTag";

        /// <summary>
        /// Selects a polyline and ensures it has a LineTag in XData.
        /// If missing -> allocate next and write; if present -> keep (or prompt to overwrite if you want).
        /// </summary>
        public static void ApplyLineTagToPolyline()
        {
            var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // Pick a polyline
            var peo = new AcEd.PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect pipeline polyline: ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(AcDb.Polyline), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) return;

            var settings = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
            var alloc    = new TagAllocator_V2(settings);

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var pl = tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, AcDb.OpenMode.ForRead) as AcDb.Polyline;
                if (pl == null) return;

                var current = XDataUtil.ReadString(pl, K_LineTag, AppName);
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(current))
                {
                    var newTag = alloc.AllocateLineTag(); // increments JSON store
                    pl.UpgradeOpen();
                    XDataUtil.WriteString(pl, K_LineTag, newTag, AppName, tr);
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nAssigned LineTag: {newTag}");
                }
                else
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nExisting LineTag: {current}");
                    // Optional: prompt to overwrite and NOT increment the store if user typed a manual tag
                }

                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

And wire it as a tiny registrar stub:

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_ApplyLineTag")]
        public static void CBI_ApplyLineTag()
            => LineTagCommands.ApplyLineTagToPolyline();
    }
}
```

## Response:
10/15/2025, 5:42:47 PM

> **Clarifying architecture and module ownership**
>
> We need to reassure the architecture decisions here. For better management of Shared vs AutoCAD2020, I suggest keeping UI/host-agnostic classes like TagAllocator_V2, ValveDefinitionResolver, and ValveDynPropUtil in Shared. However, TagAllocator can stay in AutoCAD2020 with a hosting adapter. For consistency, mention versioning without the "_V2" suffix in Shared stable APIs. A clean ownership table would help track module responsibilities.
>
> Thought for 10s

You’re not overstepping at all—that’s exactly the right question.  
Short version: **keep anything AutoCAD-agnostic in Shared** so AutoCAD2020/2025/etc. can all reuse it. Only put code under `AutoCAD2020` if it references AutoCAD types (DBObjects, Transactions, Editor, etc.) or UI.

## What should live where

### Keep in **Shared** (reusable across versions)
- `ProjectSettingsService` (+ store models/validator) ✅
- `ITagAllocator` + **TagAllocator** (JSON-based, no AutoCAD refs) ← **move back to Shared** and drop the `_V2` suffix
- `IValveDefinitionResolver` + **ValveDefinitionResolver** ✅
- **ValveDynPropUtil** (pure mapping logic) ✅
- Any enums/POCOs (e.g., `ValveType`, `BlockDefinitionRef`, `ValveSizeMapInfo`) ✅

> Rule: if it compiles without `Autodesk.AutoCAD.*` using directives, it belongs in Shared.

### Keep in **AutoCAD2020** (host-specific)
- `PipeReader` (reads polyline XData, tangent)  
- `BlockInsertService` (ensures defs, inserts, dyn props/atts)  
- `ValveMetadataLinker` (XData/NOD IO)  
- `InsertValveCommand` / `ValveTagCommand` (command flows)  
- `XDataUtil` (under `CadInterop`)  
- `ChangeHistoryService` (if it touches DWG path/handles/log files tied to the host)

## About the “_V2” suffix
- Use `_V2` only for **transitional adapters** in the host project.  
- In **Shared**, keep API names **stable** (`TagAllocator`, `ValveDefinitionResolver`). Version via comments/semver, not class names.

## Concrete moves (recommended now)
1. **Move back to Shared** (and rename):
   - `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs` →  
     `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Tagging/TagAllocator.cs`
2. **Update usings** in:
   - `ValveTagCommand.cs` and `InsertValveCommand.cs` to use `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging.TagAllocator`.
3. Leave `ValveDefinitionResolver` and `ValveDynPropUtil` in Shared (as we had).
4. Keep `PipeReader`, `BlockInsertService`, `ValveMetadataLinker`, `XDataUtil` in AutoCAD2020.

## Tiny adapter (optional)
If your AutoCAD2020 codebase expects a “Tagging.Services” location, you can add a 1-liner adapter:

```csharp
// AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator.cs (optional shim)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using SharedAlloc = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging.TagAllocator;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator
    {
        private readonly SharedAlloc _inner;
        public TagAllocator(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _inner = new SharedAlloc(svc); }
        public string PeekValveTag() => _inner.PeekValveTag();
        public string AllocateValveTag() => _inner.AllocateValveTag();
        public string PeekLineTag()  => _inner.PeekLineTag();
        public string AllocateLineTag() => _inner.AllocateLineTag();
    }
}
```

(But ideally just reference the Shared one directly.)

## Sanity checklist
- [ ] Shared builds with **no** `Autodesk.AutoCAD.*` usings.  
- [ ] AutoCAD2020 references Shared types only via clean interfaces/POCOs.  
- [ ] `_V2` appears only in host adapters or temporary flows, not in Shared APIs.

This keeps your “Shared once, reuse for AutoCAD2020/2025/…” strategy intact and avoids churn when you spin a new host project.

## Response:
10/15/2025, 5:45:23 PM

Gotcha—you're asking specifically about **`TagAllocator_V2.cs`**.

### TL;DR
Put the **allocator** in **Shared** (rename to `TagAllocator`) because it only talks to the JSON store and has **no AutoCAD API refs**. Keep **host-only shims** in AutoCAD2020 if you want a stable call-site path.

---

## Decision rule
- **Lives in Shared** if: reads/writes `Project_Store.json`, prefixes, counters; no `Autodesk.AutoCAD.*`.
- **Lives in AutoCAD2020** if: touches Editor, Transactions, DBObject, XData, ObjectIds, etc.

`TagAllocator_V2` only hits `ProjectSettingsService` ⇒ **Shared**.

---

## Recommended move (cleanest reuse for 2020/2025+)

1) **Move & rename**
- From: `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs`
- To: `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Tagging/TagAllocator.cs`
- Namespace: `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging`

2) **Code (Shared)**
```csharp
// AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Tagging/TagAllocator.cs  (C# 7.3)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public TagAllocator(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        public string PeekValveTag()    => Build(_svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Valve);
        public string AllocateValveTag() => Allocate(ref _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Valve);

        public string PeekLineTag()     => Build(_svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Line);
        public string AllocateLineTag()  => Allocate(ref _svc.Read().Projects[_svc.Read().ActiveProjectKey].Tagging.Line);

        private static string Build(TagChannel ch)
        {
            var n = ch.Padding > 0 ? ch.NextNumber.ToString(new string('0', ch.Padding)) : ch.NextNumber.ToString();
            return (ch.Prefix ?? string.Empty) + n;
        }

        private string Allocate(ref TagChannel ch)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; ch = s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve; // overwritten next line by caller’s ref
            // NOTE: caller passes ref to either Line or Valve; rebuild ch:
            ch = (ReferenceEquals(ch, s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line) ? s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line : s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve);
            var tag = Build(ch);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _svc.Write(s);
            return tag;
        }
    }
}
```
*(If you prefer, keep the two Allocate methods separate without the `ref` trick—functionally equivalent.)*

3) **Update callers**
- In `InsertValveCommand.cs` and `ValveTagCommand.cs`:
  ```csharp
  using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging;

  var tags = new TagAllocator(settings);
  ```
- If you added a LineTag command: same change.

4) **(Optional) Host shim to preserve folder structure**
```csharp
// AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator.cs (shim)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using Shared = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging.TagAllocator;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator
    {
        private readonly Shared _inner;
        public TagAllocator(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _inner = new Shared(svc); }
        public string PeekValveTag() => _inner.PeekValveTag();
        public string AllocateValveTag() => _inner.AllocateValveTag();
        public string PeekLineTag() => _inner.PeekLineTag();
        public string AllocateLineTag() => _inner.AllocateLineTag();
    }
}
```

---

## Why this is worth it
- When you spin **AutoCAD2025**, the allocator is already reusable.
- Only host bits (XData, insert, pipe read) stay in AutoCAD202x projects.
- `_V2` suffix goes away in Shared; version via package/semver, not class names.

If you want, I can rewrite the allocator without the `ref` helper (two plain methods) and hand you the exact diff for your current files.

all good — here’s the exact placement plan you asked to repeat, plus a tight `TagAllocator_V2` that fits your existing **Tagging** area.

# Where things should live (repeat)

- **TagAllocator_V2.cs** → `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs`  
  (adapter that uses your existing `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` and/or `NodLineNumberStore`)

- **XDataUtil.cs** → `AutoCAD2020/CadInterop/`  ✅

- **Valve commands/services** (Insert, Tag, PipeReader, BlockInsertService, ValveMetadataLinker, ChangeHistory) → `AutoCAD2020/Valves/` ✅

# Drop-in: TagAllocator_V2 (uses your adapters)

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
// Purpose: One place to Peek/Allocate tags for Lines & Valves using project store.
// Dependencies: ProjectTagStoreAdapter (JSON bridge), NodLineNumberStore (optional NOD bridge)

using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator_V2
    {
        private readonly ProjectTagStoreAdapter _store;   // your existing adapter
        private readonly NodLineNumberStore _nod;         // optional (kept for back-compat)

        public TagAllocator_V2(ProjectSettingsService settingsService)
        {
            // Use your existing adapter types (already in Tagging/Services)
            _store = new ProjectTagStoreAdapter(settingsService);
            _nod   = new NodLineNumberStore(settingsService); // if you still mirror counters in NOD
        }

        // ----------- Valve -----------

        public string PeekValveTag()
        {
            var ch = _store.ReadValveChannel(); // returns {Prefix, NextNumber, Padding}
            return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
        }

        public string AllocateValveTag()
        {
            var ch = _store.ReadValveChannel();
            var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _store.WriteValveChannel(ch);
            // (optional) mirror to NOD for on-drawing continuity
            _nod?.SetValveNextNumber(ch.NextNumber);
            return tag;
        }

        // ----------- Line -----------

        public string PeekLineTag()
        {
            var ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
            return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
        }

        public string AllocateLineTag()
        {
            var ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
            var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _store.WriteLineChannel(ch);
            _nod?.SetLineNextNumber(ch.NextNumber);
            return tag;
        }

        // ----------- helpers -----------

        private static string Build(string prefix, int number, int padding)
        {
            var n = padding > 0 ? number.ToString(new string('0', padding)) : number.ToString();
            return (prefix ?? string.Empty) + n;
        }
    }

    // Expected minimal interface of your adapters (already in your tree):
    public sealed class ProjectTagStoreAdapter
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public ProjectTagStoreAdapter(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        // These methods should read/write the Tagging channels in Project_Store.json
        public TagChannel ReadValveChannel()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; return s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve;
        }
        public void WriteValveChannel(TagChannel ch)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve = ch; _svc.Write(s);
        }
        public TagChannel ReadLineChannel()
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; return s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line;
        }
        public void WriteLineChannel(TagChannel ch)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read(); var k = s.ActiveProjectKey; s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line = ch; _svc.Write(s);
        }
    }

    public sealed class NodLineNumberStore
    {
        // keep signatures minimal; implement if you still mirror counters to NOD
        public NodLineNumberStore(ProjectSettingsService _){}

        public void SetValveNextNumber(int next) { /* optional NOD mirror */ }
        public void SetLineNextNumber(int next)  { /* optional NOD mirror */ }
    }

    // mirror of your shared model for compile
    public sealed class TagChannel
    {
        public string Prefix { get; set; }
        public int NextNumber { get; set; }
        public int Padding { get; set; }
    }
}
```

Use it anywhere you need tags:

```csharp
// InsertValveCommand / ValveTagCommand / LineTagCommands
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;
// ...
var allocator = new TagAllocator_V2(new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json"));
var tag = allocator.AllocateValveTag(); // or Peek*
```

That keeps everything aligned with your **Tagging** folder, doesn’t touch Shared unless you want to later, and preserves your upgrade path for 2025 by keeping host-agnostic models/services in Shared and host-specific wiring in AutoCAD2020.

## Prompt:
10/16/2025, 9:59:15 AM

I am confused. Do I have 2 TagAllocator_V2.cs one in Autocad_Shared_Project and the other in autocad2020?

## Response:
10/16/2025, 9:59:16 AM

Excellent question — and this is where a lot of people trip up when structuring multi-version AutoCAD solutions like yours. Let’s clear it up cleanly.  

---

## 🧭 The short, clear answer

✅ **You only need one `TagAllocator_V2.cs` — in `AutoCAD2020`, not in Shared.**

Here’s why:

- The allocator touches **AutoCAD-specific data context** through the `ProjectSettingsService` that lives inside your AutoCAD2020 project (and deals with the JSON store location, active drawing context, etc.).  
- It is not pure data logic — it’s *project-state logic tied to the running AutoCAD session.*

So:
```
AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs   ✅ KEEP HERE
AutoCAD_Shared_Project/Tagging/TagAllocator_V2.cs ❌ DO NOT DUPLICATE
```

---

## 💡 Why not Shared?

Your **Shared project** should only contain logic that:
- Has no `Autodesk.AutoCAD.*` references, and
- Does not depend on the active document, editor, or AutoCAD runtime.

The `TagAllocator_V2`:
- Reads/writes JSON via `ProjectSettingsService` (which lives in AutoCAD2020)
- Can later be extended to mirror NOD values or XData tags (AutoCAD-specific)
→ therefore, it **belongs to the host layer** (`AutoCAD2020`).

---

## 🗂️ Clean final layout

```
AutoCAD_Shared_Project/
 └── ProjectStore/
      ├── ProjectSettingsService.cs
      ├── ProjectStoreValidator.cs
      └── ProjectModels.cs  (no AutoCAD refs)

AutoCAD2020/
 ├── Tagging/
 │    ├── Commands/
 │    │     └── LineTagCommands.cs
 │    ├── Services/
 │    │     ├── TagAllocator_V2.cs   ✅  (the one we keep)
 │    │     └── ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs
 │    └── Parsers/
 ├── Valves/
 │    ├── InsertValveCommand.cs
 │    ├── ValveTagCommand.cs
 │    └── ValveMetadataLinker.cs
 └── CadInterop/
      ├── XDataUtil.cs
      ├── DocumentLockHelper.cs
      └── CadLayerServices.cs
```

---

## 🧩 Optional: If you later migrate to AutoCAD 2025
You’ll simply:
- Copy the **AutoCAD2020** project folder → **AutoCAD2025**
- Reference the same **Shared project**
- All Shared logic stays stable (no need to touch it)
- The new 2025 project gets its own copy of `TagAllocator_V2.cs` and other host-specific services.

---

### ✅ Summary

| File | Location | Reason |
|------|-----------|--------|
| `TagAllocator_V2.cs` | `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/` | Needs project + host context |
| `XDataUtil.cs` | `AutoCAD2020/CadInterop/` | Works directly with DBObject/XData |
| `ValveTagCommand.cs` | `AutoCAD2020/Valves/` | Command layer |
| Shared JSON models | `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/` | Version-neutral |

---

## ✅ Drop-in version of `XDataUtil.cs`
Here’s the working file to add:

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/CadInterop/XDataUtil.cs
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
// Purpose: Safe read/write helpers for XData values (string-based) on DBObjects.

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using System.Linq;

namespace AutoCAD2020.CadInterop
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Helper for reading/writing string-based XData on AutoCAD entities.
    /// </summary>
    public static class XDataUtil
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Reads the first string value matching a given appName/key pair.
        /// Returns null if not found.
        /// </summary>
        public static string ReadString(DBObject obj, string key, string appName)
        {
            if (obj == null) return null;

            var rb = obj.XData;
            if (rb == null) return null;

            var data = rb.AsArray();
            if (data == null || data.Length == 0) return null;

            // Find app name marker and subsequent key
            for (int i = 0; i < data.Length - 2; i++)
            {
                if (data[i].TypeCode == 1001 && (string)data[i].Value == appName)
                {
                    for (int j = i + 1; j < data.Length - 1; j++)
                    {
                        if (data[j].TypeCode == 1000 && (string)data[j].Value == key)
                            return data[j + 1].Value as string;
                    }
                }
            }
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Writes a string key/value pair to XData (creates reg app if missing).
        /// </summary>
        public static void WriteString(DBObject obj, string key, string value, string appName, Transaction tr)
        {
            if (obj == null || tr == null) return;

            var db = obj.Database;
            EnsureRegAppTableRecord(db, appName, tr);

            var existing = obj.XData?.AsArray()?.ToList() ?? new System.Collections.Generic.List<TypedValue>();
            // Remove old entries for this app/key
            for (int i = existing.Count - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            {
                if (existing[i].TypeCode == 1001 && (string)existing[i].Value == appName)
                {
                    // Remove until next 1001 or end
                    int j = i + 1;
                    while (j < existing.Count && existing[j].TypeCode != 1001)
                    {
                        if (existing[j].TypeCode == 1000 && (string)existing[j].Value == key)
                        {
                            existing.RemoveAt(j + 1);
                            existing.RemoveAt(j);
                            break;
                        }
                        j++;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Append new values
            existing.Add(new TypedValue(1001, appName));
            existing.Add(new TypedValue(1000, key));
            existing.Add(new TypedValue(1000, value ?? string.Empty));

            obj.XData = new ResultBuffer(existing.ToArray());
        }

        private static void EnsureRegAppTableRecord(Database db, string appName, Transaction tr)
        {
            var regTable = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!regTable.Has(appName))
            {
                regTable.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                regTable.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

   ```csharp
   // File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs
   // v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)

   using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

   namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
   {
       /// <summary>
       /// Central Peek/Allocate for Line & Valve tags backed by Project_Store.json.
       /// Uses existing ProjectTagStoreAdapter + (optional) NodLineNumberStore.
       /// </summary>
       public sealed class TagAllocator_V2
       {
           private readonly ProjectTagStoreAdapter _store;   // your existing adapter
           private readonly NodLineNumberStore _nod;         // your existing NOD mirror (optional)

           public TagAllocator_V2(ProjectSettingsService settingsService)
           {
               _store = new ProjectTagStoreAdapter(settingsService);
               _nod   = new NodLineNumberStore(settingsService);
           }

           // ===== Valve =====
           public string PeekValveTag()
           {
               TagChannel ch = _store.ReadValveChannel(); // TagChannel from Shared project
               return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
           }

           public string AllocateValveTag()
           {
               TagChannel ch = _store.ReadValveChannel();
               var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
               ch.NextNumber++;
               _store.WriteValveChannel(ch);
               _nod?.SetValveNextNumber(ch.NextNumber); // optional mirror
               return tag;
           }

           // ===== Line =====
           public string PeekLineTag()
           {
               TagChannel ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
               return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
           }

           public string AllocateLineTag()
           {
               TagChannel ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
               var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
               ch.NextNumber++;
               _store.WriteLineChannel(ch);
               _nod?.SetLineNextNumber(ch.NextNumber); // optional mirror
               return tag;
           }

           private static string Build(string prefix, int number, int padding)
           {
               var n = padding > 0 ? number.ToString(new string('0', padding)) : number.ToString();
               return (prefix ?? string.Empty) + n;
           }
       }
   }
   ```

   > Note: `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` and `NodLineNumberStore` should be the **real** ones already in your repo (see your screenshot under `Tagging/Services`). If their method names differ, adjust the 4 calls accordingly:
   > - `ReadValveChannel()` / `WriteValveChannel(TagChannel)`
   > - `ReadLineChannel()` / `WriteLineChannel(TagChannel)`
   > - `SetValveNextNumber(int)` / `SetLineNextNumber(int)` (optional)

## 1) Implement `ProjectTagStoreAdapter` (reads/writes JSON Tagging channels)

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/ProjectTagStoreAdapter.cs`
```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Thin adapter over ProjectSettingsService for tag prefixes/counters.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class ProjectTagStoreAdapter
    {
        private readonly ProjectSettingsService _svc;
        public ProjectTagStoreAdapter(ProjectSettingsService svc) { _svc = svc; }

        private ProjectEntry ActiveProject(out ProjectStoreSnapshot snap)
        {
            snap = _svc.Read();
            var key = snap.ActiveProjectKey;
            return (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) && snap.Projects.ContainsKey(key))
                ? snap.Projects[key]
                : null;
        }

        // ---- Valve channel ----
        public TagChannel ReadValveChannel()
        {
            ProjectStoreSnapshot s; var p = ActiveProject(out s);
            return p != null ? p.Tagging.Valve : new TagChannel { Prefix = "V-", NextNumber = 1, Padding = 0 };
        }

        public void WriteValveChannel(TagChannel channel)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read();
            var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(k) && s.Projects.ContainsKey(k))
            {
                s.Projects[k].Tagging.Valve = channel ?? new TagChannel { Prefix = "V-", NextNumber = 1, Padding = 0 };
                _svc.Write(s);
            }
        }

        // ---- Line channel ----
        public TagChannel ReadLineChannel()
        {
            ProjectStoreSnapshot s; var p = ActiveProject(out s);
            return p != null ? p.Tagging.Line : new TagChannel { Prefix = "L-", NextNumber = 1000, Padding = 0 };
        }

        public void WriteLineChannel(TagChannel channel)
        {
            var s = _svc.Read();
            var k = s.ActiveProjectKey;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(k) && s.Projects.ContainsKey(k))
            {
                s.Projects[k].Tagging.Line = channel ?? new TagChannel { Prefix = "L-", NextNumber = 1000, Padding = 0 };
                _svc.Write(s);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagAllocator_V2.cs
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public sealed class TagAllocator_V2
    {
        private readonly ProjectTagStoreAdapter _store;
        private readonly NodLineNumberStore _nod;

        public TagAllocator_V2(ProjectSettingsService settingsService)
        {
            _store = new ProjectTagStoreAdapter(settingsService);
            _nod   = new NodLineNumberStore(settingsService);
        }

        public string PeekValveTag()
        {
            TagChannel ch = _store.ReadValveChannel();
            return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
        }

        public string AllocateValveTag()
        {
            TagChannel ch = _store.ReadValveChannel();
            var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _store.WriteValveChannel(ch);
            _nod.SetValveNextNumber(ch.NextNumber);
            return tag;
        }

        public string PeekLineTag()
        {
            TagChannel ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
            return Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
        }

        public string AllocateLineTag()
        {
            TagChannel ch = _store.ReadLineChannel();
            var tag = Build(ch.Prefix, ch.NextNumber, ch.Padding);
            ch.NextNumber++;
            _store.WriteLineChannel(ch);
            _nod.SetLineNextNumber(ch.NextNumber);
            return tag;
        }

        private static string Build(string prefix, int number, int padding)
        {
            var n = padding > 0 ? number.ToString(new string('0', padding)) : number.ToString();
            return (prefix ?? string.Empty) + n;
        }
    }
}
```

Alright—let’s knock out **Step 6: fix the New Project JSON builder** so `RootHint` and `Drawings[]` are always correct.

# 1) Shared: NewProjectBuilder tweaks (robust scan + normalization)

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/NewProjectBuilder.cs`  
Replace the file contents with this (safe superset of what you had):

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static class NewProjectBuilder
    {
        public static ProjectEntry Create(string projectKey, string rootHint, IEnumerable<string> seedDrawingPaths = null)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectKey)) throw new ArgumentException("projectKey required");
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(rootHint)) throw new ArgumentException("rootHint required");

            var normalizedRoot = NormalizePath(rootHint);

            var entry = new ProjectEntry
            {
                ProjectKey = projectKey.Trim(),
                RootHint = normalizedRoot,
                Drawings = new List<DrawingEntry>()
            };

            if (seedDrawingPaths != null)
            {
                foreach (var p in seedDrawingPaths)
                {
                    var n = NormalizePath(p);
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(n))
                        entry.Drawings.Add(new DrawingEntry { Path = n });
                }
            }

            // Ensure list is never null (already set) and contains no duplicates
            Dedup(entry.Drawings);
            return entry;
        }

        public static IEnumerable<string> DiscoverDwgs(string rootHint, int max = 1000)
        {
            var results = new List<string>();
            var root = NormalizePath(rootHint);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(root) || !Directory.Exists(root)) return results;

            try
            {
                foreach (var f in Directory.EnumerateFiles(root, "*.dwg", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                {
                    results.Add(NormalizePath(f));
                    if (results.Count >= max) break;
                }
            }
            catch { /* ignore filesystem races */ }

            return results;
        }

        public static string NormalizePath(string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) return null;
            try { return Path.GetFullPath(path); }
            catch { return path.Trim(); }
        }

        private static void Dedup(List<DrawingEntry> list)
        {
            if (list == null) return;
            var seen = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            for (int i = list.Count - 1; i >= 0; i--)
            {
                var p = list[i]?.Path ?? string.Empty;
                if (seen.Contains(p)) list.RemoveAt(i);
                else seen.Add(p);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 2) Shared: ProjectSettingsService.CreateNewProject fix

**File:** `AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectSettingsService.cs`  
Replace the **CreateNewProject** method with this improved version (other methods unchanged):

```csharp
// v2.0.0 (C# 7.3) — drop-in for the existing method
public void CreateNewProject(string projectKey, string rootHint, bool scanForDrawings)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(projectKey))
        throw new ArgumentException("projectKey required", nameof(projectKey));
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(rootHint))
        throw new ArgumentException("rootHint required", nameof(rootHint));

    var snap = Read();
    if (snap.Projects == null) snap.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>();

    if (snap.Projects.ContainsKey(projectKey))
        throw new InvalidOperationException("Project already exists: " + projectKey);

    // Normalize and seed drawings (optionally)
    var normalizedRoot = NewProjectBuilder.NormalizePath(rootHint);
    var drawings = scanForDrawings
        ? NewProjectBuilder.DiscoverDwgs(normalizedRoot)
        : (IEnumerable<string>)new List<string>();

    var entry = NewProjectBuilder.Create(projectKey, normalizedRoot, drawings);

    // Ensure Tagging + Blocks defaults exist
    if (entry.Tagging == null) entry.Tagging = new TaggingEntry();
    if (entry.Blocks == null)  entry.Blocks  = new BlocksEntry();

    snap.Projects[projectKey] = entry;
    snap.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;

    // Validate & persist
    var issues = ProjectStoreValidator.Validate(snap);
    // We tolerate empty Drawings[] but NEVER null RootHint or null lists.
    var critical = issues.FindAll(i =>
        i.Path.EndsWith(".RootHint", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
        i.Message.IndexOf("null", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0);

    if (critical.Count > 0)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid new project: " + string.Join("; ", critical.ConvertAll(c => c.Message)));

    Write(snap);
}
```

### What this guarantees
- `RootHint` is always a **normalized, non-empty** path.
- `Drawings` is always an **array** (possibly empty), never null.
- No duplicate drawing paths.
- Defaults for `Tagging` and `Blocks` are seeded if missing.

---

# 3) AutoCAD2020: New Project commands (no change unless you want DWG-added seeding)

You’ve got two flows:
- `CBI_NewProject` (interactive picker; already passes `scan` true/false)
- `CBI_NewProjectFromActive` (separate).  

If you want the **current DWG** added when you don’t scan the entire root, you can pass a one-off `seedDrawingPaths` list. To support that, add an **overload** in `ProjectSettingsService`:

```csharp
// Add this overload (optional)
public void CreateNewProject(string projectKey, string rootHint, IEnumerable<string> seedDrawingPaths)
{
    var snap = Read();
    if (snap.Projects == null) snap.Projects = new Dictionary<string, ProjectEntry>();
    if (snap.Projects.ContainsKey(projectKey))
        throw new InvalidOperationException("Project already exists: " + projectKey);

    var normalizedRoot = NewProjectBuilder.NormalizePath(rootHint);
    var entry = NewProjectBuilder.Create(projectKey, normalizedRoot, seedDrawingPaths);

    if (entry.Tagging == null) entry.Tagging = new TaggingEntry();
    if (entry.Blocks == null)  entry.Blocks  = new BlocksEntry();

    snap.Projects[projectKey] = entry;
    snap.ActiveProjectKey = projectKey;

    var issues = ProjectStoreValidator.Validate(snap);
    var critical = issues.FindAll(i =>
        i.Path.EndsWith(".RootHint", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
        i.Message.IndexOf("null", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0);
    if (critical.Count > 0)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid new project: " + string.Join("; ", critical.ConvertAll(c => c.Message)));

    Write(snap);
}
```

Then, in your AutoCAD2020 **CreateFromActive** command, you could do:

```csharp
// after computing supportDir/local store path, etc.
var svc = new ProjectSettingsService(storePath /* or global path if you centralize */);
svc.CreateNewProject(name, root, new [] { doc.Name }); // seeds current DWG only
```

(If you prefer to keep the global store path fixed, stick with the existing `CreateNewProject(name, root, scan)`—the Shared code is now robust either way.)
