// File: PropertiesControl.cs
// v0.3.0 — PropertyGrid UI with Project banner + collapsible "Tag Numbers" tree
// Notes:
//  - Removed the previous top ListView/grid entirely.
//  - Added a TreeView that collapses as: Tag Numbers -> (Line ID's, Tag ID's) -> per-system rows.

using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using System.Drawing;                      // Point/Size/Color/Cursors
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;        // NodLineNumberStore
using System.Collections.Generic;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;   //ProjectSettingsService

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    // IMPORTANT: partial + inherits UserControl
    public partial class PropertiesControl : UserControl
    {

        private readonly PropertyGrid _grid = new PropertyGrid();
        private readonly ToolStrip _bar = new ToolStrip();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lbl = new ToolStripLabel("Source:");
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblSource = new ToolStripLabel("—");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnApply = new ToolStripButton("Apply");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRevert = new ToolStripButton("Revert");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnSave = new ToolStripButton("Save");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnLoad = new ToolStripButton("Load");
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep = new ToolStripSeparator();
        private readonly ToolStripControlHost _chkAutoHost;
        private readonly CheckBox _chkAuto = new CheckBox { Text = "Auto-apply", Checked = true };

        // preview labels (right side)
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblLineNext = new ToolStripLabel();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblTagNext = new ToolStripLabel();

        public bool IsEditing { get; private set; }
        public event EventHandler EditingStateChanged;

private void SetEditing(bool editing)
        {
            if (IsEditing == editing) return;
            IsEditing = editing;
            EditingStateChanged?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
        }

        // Call this once in ctor AFTER InitializeComponent();
        private void WireEditingState()
        {
            // Add any control types you use for input
            foreach (Control c in GetAllInputs(this))
            {
                c.Enter += (s, e) => SetEditing(true);
                c.Leave += (s, e) => SetEditing(false);
            }
        }

        // Helper: enumerate inputs recursively
        private IEnumerable<Control> GetAllInputs(Control root)
        {
            foreach (Control c in root.Controls)
            {
                if (c is TextBoxBase || c is ComboBox || c is CheckBox || c is NumericUpDown)
                    yield return c;
                foreach (var child in GetAllInputs(c)) yield return child;
            }
        }

        // Project banner (below toolbar)
        private readonly Label lblProjectBanner = new Label
        {
            Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            Height = 24,
            TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft,
            Font = new Font("Segoe UI", 9F, FontStyle.Bold),
            ForeColor = Color.DodgerBlue,
            Padding = new Padding(6, 0, 0, 0),
            Text = "Project: (none)"
        };

        // Collapsible "Tag Numbers" tree: Master -> (Line ID's, Tag ID's) -> per-system rows
        private readonly TreeView _tvNext = new TreeView
        {
            Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            Height = 140,                      // adjust as preferred
            HideSelection = false,
            ShowLines = true,
            ShowRootLines = true
        };

        private object _currentModel;
        private object _snapshot;

        // resize grip state
        private readonly Panel _grip = new Panel();
        private bool _dragging;
        private Point _dragStartScreen;
        private Size _startSize;

        public event EventHandler ApplyRequested;      // <— host subscribes
        public object SelectedModel => _currentModel;  // <— host reads

        public PropertiesControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();        // <— must exist in Designer partial

            // NEW: track when the user is actively typing in editors
            WireEditingState();

            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;

            // Use mono font so "name | number" rows align nicely in the tree
            try { _tvNext.Font = new Font("Consolas", 8.5f, FontStyle.Regular); } catch { /* ignore */ }

            _grid.Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                // Keep previews in sync if Prefix changed on tool model
                var name = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                if (string.Equals(name, nameof(LineTagSettings.Prefix), StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt)
                        RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
                }

                if (_chkAuto.Checked)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            };

            _chkAutoHost = new ToolStripControlHost(_chkAuto) { Margin = new Padding(8, 2, 4, 2) };
            _btnApply.Click += (s, e) => ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            _btnRevert.Click += (s, e) => Rebind(_snapshot);
            _btnSave.Click += (s, e) => SavePreset();
            _btnLoad.Click += (s, e) => LoadPreset();

            // Toolbar composition (left → right)
            _bar.Items.Add(_lbl);
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblSource);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnApply);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRevert);
            _bar.Items.Add(_sep);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnSave);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnLoad);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_chkAutoHost);

            // previews on the right
            _lblLineNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblTagNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblLineNext);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("  ") { Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right });
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblTagNext);

            _bar.Dock = DockStyle.Top;

            // Dock stacking order: toolbar (top), banner (top), tree (top), grid (fill)
            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(_tvNext);
            Controls.Add(lblProjectBanner);
            Controls.Add(_bar);

            // resize grip (bottom-right)
            _grip.Size = new Size(14, 14);
            _grip.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Bottom | AnchorStyles.Right;
            _grip.Cursor = Cursors.SizeNWSE;
            _grip.BackColor = Color.Transparent;
            _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);

            _grip.MouseDown += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Button != MouseButtons.Left) return;
                CbiPropertiesPalette.EnsureFloating();
                _dragging = true;
                _dragStartScreen = Cursor.Position;
                _startSize = CbiPropertiesPalette.GetSize();
                _grip.Capture = true;
            };
            _grip.MouseMove += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (!_dragging) return;
                var cur = Cursor.Position;
                int dx = cur.X - _dragStartScreen.X;
                int dy = cur.Y - _dragStartScreen.Y;
                var newSize = new Size(_startSize.Width + dx, _startSize.Height + dy);
                CbiPropertiesPalette.SetSize(newSize);
            };
            _grip.MouseUp += (s, e) =>
            {
                _dragging = false;
                _grip.Capture = false;
            };
            this.Resize += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            };

            // your force-apply props
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grid.Refresh();

                var propName = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                bool forceApply =
                    string.Equals(propName, nameof(PipeRunProperties.LayerName), StringComparison.Ordinal) ||
                    string.Equals(propName, nameof(PipeRunProperties.PipingRunType), StringComparison.Ordinal);

                if (_chkAuto.Checked || forceApply)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            };

            // show initial project name + numbers on first load
            this.Load += (s, e) =>
            {
                RefreshProjectBanner();
                RefreshNextNumbersTree();
            };

            Controls.Add(_grip);
        }

        // --------- Public API used by host ---------

        public void Clear()
        {
            _currentModel = null;
            _snapshot = null;
            _grid.SelectedObject = null;
            _lblSource.Text = "—";
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (none)";
            _tvNext.Nodes.Clear();
        }

        public void ShowHome(string prefix)
        {
            Bind(new Placeholder(), "Tool");
            RefreshHomePreview(prefix);
        }

        //public void RefreshHomePreview(string prefix)
        //{
        //    try
        //    {
        //        var p = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "PR-" : prefix;
        //        var line = NodLineNumberStore.Peek("LINE", p, 4);
        //        var tag = NodLineNumberStore.Peek("TAG", p, 4);
        //        _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {line}";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = $"Tag next: {tag}";
        //    }
        //    catch
        //    {
        //        _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
        //    }
        //}

        public void RefreshHomePreview(string prefix)
        {
            try
            {
                // Use the central allocator so the preview matches what tools will use
                var settings = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
                var allocator = new TagAllocator_V2(settings);

                // Show the next (peek) values. If you prefer numbers only, we can add helpers later.
                var nextLine = allocator.PeekLineTag();
                var nextValve = allocator.PeekValveTag();

                _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {nextLine}";
                _lblTagNext.Text = $"Tag next: {nextValve}";
            }
            catch
            {
                _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
                _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            }
        }

        public void Bind(object model, string sourceLabel)
        {
            if (model == null) model = new Placeholder();
            _currentModel = model;

            // if the home model, pull live Tag Numbers from the store before cloning/display
            var lt = _currentModel as LineTagSettings;
            if (lt != null)
            {
                try { lt.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
            }

            _snapshot = DeepClone(_currentModel);
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            _lblSource.Text = string.IsNullOrEmpty(sourceLabel) ? InferLabel(model) : sourceLabel;

            if (lt != null) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        public void CommitPendingEdits() 

        {
            try
            { this.ValidateChildren(); }
            catch { }
        }

// --------- helpers ---------

        private void Rebind(object snap)
        {
            _currentModel = DeepClone(snap ?? new Placeholder());
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        private static object DeepClone(object instance)
        {
            if (instance == null) return null;
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(instance);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, instance.GetType());
        }

        private string InferLabel(object model) => model == null ? "—" : model.GetType().Name;

        private void SavePreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var sfd = new SaveFileDialog
            {
                Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json",
                FileName = _currentModel.GetType().Name + ".json"
            })
            {
                if (sfd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(_currentModel, Formatting.Indented);
                File.WriteAllText(sfd.FileName, json);
            }
        }

        public void RefreshProjectBanner()
        {
            try
            {
                // Get current project name via your bridge
                var name = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectContextBridge.GetCurrentProjectName();
                lblProjectBanner.Text = $"Project: {name}";
            }
            catch
            {
                lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (unavailable)";
            }

            // keep the collapsible tree in sync with the active project
            RefreshNextNumbersTree();
        }

        private void LoadPreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var ofd = new OpenFileDialog { Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json" })
            {
                if (ofd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = File.ReadAllText(ofd.FileName);
                var loaded = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, _currentModel.GetType());
                if (loaded != null) Bind(loaded, _lblSource.Text);
            }
        }

        private void RefreshNextNumbersTree()
        {
            _tvNext.BeginUpdate();
            try
            {
                _tvNext.Nodes.Clear();

                var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null)
                {
                    var root = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");
                    root.Nodes.Add("(no active document)");
                    root.Expand();
                    return;
                }

                if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding))
                {
                    var rootEmpty = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");
                    rootEmpty.Nodes.Add("(no project bound)");
                    rootEmpty.Expand();
                    return;
                }

                var storePath = binding.StorePath;
                var pairs = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextPairs(storePath);

                // Master
                var rootNode = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");

                // Sub-headers
                var lineNode = rootNode.Nodes.Add("Line ID's");
                var tagNode = rootNode.Nodes.Add("Tag ID's");

                // Row formatting
                int namePad = 22;
                foreach (var p in pairs)
                {
                    var name = (p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? "").PadRight(namePad);
                    if (p.LineNext.HasValue)
                        lineNode.Nodes.Add($"{name} | {p.LineNext.Value}");
                    if (p.TagNext.HasValue)
                        tagNode.Nodes.Add($"{name} | {p.TagNext.Value}");
                }

                rootNode.Expand();
                lineNode.Expand();
                tagNode.Expand();
            }
            catch
            {
                _tvNext.Nodes.Clear();
                var n = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");
                n.Nodes.Add("(error reading store)");
                n.Expand();
            }
            finally
            {
                _tvNext.EndUpdate();
            }
        }

        private sealed class Placeholder
        {
            [Category("Info")]
            [DisplayName("Tip")]
            public string Message => "Select an entity or run a tool to see its properties here.";
        }
    }
}

// v0.1.1 (C# 7.3) — Provider registry + resolution (no records)

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class SelectionContext
    {
        public ObjectId Id { get; private set; }
        public Document Doc { get; private set; }
        public SelectionContext(ObjectId id, Document doc) { Id = id; Doc = doc; }
    }

    public interface IPropertySourceProvider
    {
        bool CanProvideFor(SelectionContext ctx);
        object GetSelectedObject(SelectionContext ctx);
        void Apply(object editedModel, SelectionContext ctx);
    }

    public static class PropertyRouter
    {
        // KEEP ONLY THIS ONE
        private static readonly List<IPropertySourceProvider> _providers = new List<IPropertySourceProvider>
        {
            new PipeRunPropertyProvider(),
            new InstrumentPropertyProvider(),
            new ComponentPropertyProvider(),
            new LineTagSettingsProvider()
        };

        public static object Resolve(SelectionContext ctx)
        {
            foreach (var p in _providers)
            {
                try { if (p.CanProvideFor(ctx)) return p.GetSelectedObject(ctx); }
                catch { }
            }
            return ResolveForTool();
        }

        public static object ResolveForTool() { return MasterProperties.LineTag; }

        private static bool _isApplying;

        public static void ApplyIfPossible(object model, SelectionContext ctx)
        {
            if (model == null || ctx == null) return;

            if (_isApplying) return;
            _isApplying = true;
            try
            {
                foreach (var p in _providers)
                {
                    try
                    {
                        if (p.CanProvideFor(ctx))
                        {
                            var current = p.GetSelectedObject(ctx);
                            if (current != null && current.GetType() == model.GetType())
                            {
                                p.Apply(model, ctx);
                                // 🚦 defer palette refresh to avoid overwriting with stale model
                                //System.Windows.Forms.Application.DoEvents();
                                //_ui?.BeginInvoke((Action)(() => _ui?.RefreshFromSelection()));

                                // NEW: re-read the selection and rebind on the next idle tick
                                CbiPropertiesPalette.RequestRefreshSoon();

                                return;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    catch { /* swallow to keep UI stable */ }
                }

                if (object.ReferenceEquals(model, MasterProperties.LineTag))
                    MasterProperties.Save();
            }
            finally
            {
                _isApplying = false;
            }
        }

    }
}

// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs
// Purpose: Dynamic, expandable groups for PropertyGrid
// Target:  C# 7.3

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using SysPair = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextPair;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Wraps a name→number map so each key appears as a read-only property in a PropertyGrid.
    /// </summary>
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display; // used for ToString()

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>();
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        #region ICustomTypeDescriptor
        AttributeCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetAttributes() => AttributeCollection.Empty;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetClassName() => GetType().Name;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetComponentName() => null;
        TypeConverter ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter() => new TypeConverter();
        EventDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultEvent() => null;
        PropertyDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultProperty() => null;
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEditor(Type editorBaseType) => null;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents(Attribute[] attributes) => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents() => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties(Attribute[] attributes)
            => ((ICustomTypeDescriptor)this).GetProperties();

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties()
        {
            // one read-only PropertyDescriptor per system
            var props = _values.Keys
                .OrderBy(k => k, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Select(k => new NumberPropDescriptor(k, _values[k]))
                .ToArray();

            return new PropertyDescriptorCollection(props, readOnly: true);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Required by ICustomTypeDescriptor — return the object that owns the properties.
        /// </summary>
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetPropertyOwner(PropertyDescriptor pd) => this;
        #endregion

        private sealed class NumberPropDescriptor : PropertyDescriptor
        {
            private readonly long _value;

            public NumberPropDescriptor(string name, long value)
                : base(name, null) => _value = value;

            public override Type ComponentType => typeof(DynamicNumberGroup);
            public override bool IsReadOnly => true;
            public override Type PropertyType => typeof(long);

            public override bool CanResetValue(object component) => false;
            public override object GetValue(object component) => _value;
            public override void ResetValue(object component) { }
            public override void SetValue(object component, object value) { }
            public override bool ShouldSerializeValue(object component) => false;
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Container shown under the "Tag Numbers" category in LineTagSettings.
    /// Holds two expandable read-only groups: Line ID’s and Tag ID’s.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        public static TagNumbersView FromPairs(IEnumerable<SysPair> pairs)
        {
            var view = new TagNumbersView();

            var line = (pairs ?? Enumerable.Empty<SysPair>())
                .Where(p => p.LineNext.HasValue)
                .ToDictionary(
                    p => p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    p => p.LineNext.Value);

            var tag = (pairs ?? Enumerable.Empty<SysPair>())
                .Where(p => p.TagNext.HasValue)
                .ToDictionary(
                    p => p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    p => p.TagNext.Value);

            view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(line, line.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{line.Count} system(s)");
            view.TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag, tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)");

            return view;
        }
    }
}

// XDataHelpers.cs  (C# 7.3)
// Shared helpers for reading/writing simple key/value XData under a RegApp.
// Layout written: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]…
// Safe to use from property providers, match-commands, palettes, etc.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;   // Document, Application, LockDocument()
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;      // ObjectId, Entity, ResultBuffer, RegAppTable, etc.

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>Lightweight map with a convenience getter.</summary>
    public sealed class XDataMap : Dictionary<string, string>
    {
        public string Get(string key)
        {
            string v; return TryGetValue(key, out v) ? v : null;
        }
    }

    public static class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>True if the entity has any XData for the given app name.</summary>
        public static bool HasAppName(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return false;
            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return false;
                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                return rb != null && rb.AsArray().Length > 0;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Reads app-specific XData into a string→string map. Missing/invalid returns empty map.</summary>
        public static XDataMap Read(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            var map = new XDataMap();
            if (id.IsNull) return map;

            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return map;

                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                if (rb == null) return map;

                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                // Expect: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]...
                for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    if (arr[i].TypeCode == 1000 && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == 1000)
                    {
                        var k = arr[i].Value as string;
                        var v = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                        if (k != null) map[k] = v ?? string.Empty;
                    }
                }
            }
            return map;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Writes the map as XData (1001 app + 1000 key/value pairs). Overwrites any previous XData for that app.
        /// Robust: resolves owning Document (no OwnerDocument dependency), doc-locks if possible,
        /// ensures RegApp in the same TX, and force-opens entity on locked layers.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Write(ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return;

            var db = id.Database;
            if (db == null) return;

            var doc = FindDocumentFor(db);

            // Lock when we can (palette/modeless safe); otherwise best-effort write.
            if (doc != null)
            {
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                {
                    WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
                }
            }
            else
            {
                WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
            }
        }

        // ---------- internals ----------

        private static void WriteCore(Database db, ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // Ensure the RegApp exists in THIS transaction
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(app))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = app };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }

                // Open the entity for write even if its layer is locked
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                var list = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                if (map != null)
                {
                    foreach (var kv in map)
                    {
                        list.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Key ?? string.Empty));
                        list.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Value ?? string.Empty));
                    }
                }

                ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Resolves the owning Document for a Database without using Database.OwnerDocument.
        /// Works across MDI; returns null if not found.
        /// </summary>
        private static Document FindDocumentFor(Database db)
        {
            if (db == null) return null;
            var dm = Application.DocumentManager;
            foreach (Document d in dm)
            {
                try { if (d != null && d.Database == db) return d; } catch { }
            }
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures a RegApp exists (utility if you need it separately).
        /// </summary>
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName)
        {
            if (db == null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(appName)) return;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(appName))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}

For PropertiesControl, we should standardize on the v0.4 store, using `SafeStoreAllocator.Peek` for previewing and replacing `RefreshHomePreview` to align with the new store process.

> **Reviewing v0.4 schema**

Also, the `TagAllocator_V2` in `PropertiesControl v0.3.0` looks like leftover code.

Awesome—thanks for the full drop. Here’s the final pass with concrete, “paste-this” fixes and a tidy list of remaining duplicates.

---

# Biggest takeaways

1) **Stick to one project store + one allocator.**  
   You’ve still got 3 different number sources in this last batch:
   - `NodLineNumberStore` (old)
   - `ProjectSettingsService/TagAllocator_V2` (snapshot-era)
   - v0.4 JSON (`ProjectTagStore` + `SafeStoreAllocator`)

   ✅ **Pick v0.4 only** (what we aligned the rest of the code to earlier). Remove the other two from the palette code.

2) **One “match pipe run” implementation.**  
   You have both `PipeRunMatchCommands` *and* `PipeRunMatchService` doing the same thing. Keep the **Service**, make the command call it.

3) **One “system next” reader.**  
   You’ve got `GetSystemNextPairs` **and** `GetSystemNextList`. Keep **one**; below I show how to use `GetSystemNextList` everywhere (simpler: just `SystemKey/Name/Prefix/Next`).

4) **Keep a single `XDataHelpers`.**  
   You pasted an older commented version plus the new one. Keep only the **final** version you ended with (the one that finds the owning `Document`, doc-locks, and force-opens on locked layers).

---

## 1) Palette: remove legacy allocators and peek from the v0.4 store

### Replace `PropertiesControl.RefreshHomePreview(...)` with:

```csharp
public void RefreshHomePreview(string _)
{
    try
    {
        // Use the bound store (v0.4)
        var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
        if (doc == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }

        if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
            string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
        {
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            return;
        }

        var store = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.LoadOrCreate(binding.StorePath);

        // choose the first two systems (or GENERAL twice if only one)
        string firstKey = null, secondKey = null;
        foreach (var key in store.Systems.Keys)
        {
            if (firstKey == null) { firstKey = key; continue; }
            if (secondKey == null) { secondKey = key; break; }
        }
        if (firstKey == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }
        if (secondKey == null) secondKey = firstKey;

        var a = store.Systems[firstKey];
        var b = store.Systems[secondKey];

        string fmtA = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.FormatId(a.Prefix, a.Next, a.Pad);
        string fmtB = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.FormatId(b.Prefix, b.Next, b.Pad);

        // label them as “Line” and “Tag” previews (purely UI; both are systems)
        _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {fmtA}";
        _lblTagNext.Text  = $"Tag next: {fmtB}";
    }
    catch
    {
        _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
        _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
    }
}
```

> This drops `TagAllocator_V2` and `ProjectSettingsService` from the palette and peeks directly from the v0.4 store’s `Systems[*].Next`.

### Replace the tree reader to use **one** reader (`GetSystemNextList`) everywhere

In `RefreshNextNumbersTree()` swap:

```csharp
var pairs = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextPairs(storePath);
...
foreach (var p in pairs)
{
    var name = (p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? "").PadRight(namePad);
    if (p.LineNext.HasValue) lineNode.Nodes.Add($"{name} | {p.LineNext.Value}");
    if (p.TagNext.HasValue)  tagNode.Nodes.Add($"{name} | {p.TagNext.Value}");
}
```

with:

```csharp
var list = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(storePath);

var rootNode = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");
var lineNode = rootNode.Nodes.Add("Line ID's");
var tagNode  = rootNode.Nodes.Add("Tag ID's");

int namePad = 22;
foreach (var s in list)
{
    var name = (s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? "").PadRight(namePad);
    var text = $"{name} | {s.Next}";
    // Show same numbers under both headings (UI-only grouping) or, if you later
    // split into LINE/TAG systems, drop each into the proper branch by key.
    lineNode.Nodes.Add(text);
    tagNode.Nodes.Add(text);
}
rootNode.Expand(); lineNode.Expand(); tagNode.Expand();
```

Then **remove** `GetSystemNextPairs` usages in `LineTagSettings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()` too—point it at `GetSystemNextList` or delete that property group if you prefer the tree only.

---

## 2) Kill the duplicate “match pipe run” command

Keep `PipeRunMatchService` as the implementation. Replace `PipeRunMatchCommands.MatchPipeRunXData()` body with:

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_MATCH_PIPERUN")]
public static void MatchPipeRunXData() => PipeRunMatchService.RunInteractive();
```

You can delete the rest of the command class (all helpers in there are duplicated in the Service).

---

## 3) Keep only one `XDataHelpers`

Delete the earlier commented block. Keep the **final** `XDataHelpers.cs` you pasted last (the one with `FindDocumentFor`, `WriteCore`, `EnsureRegApp`). All providers/commands already reference that shape.

---

## 4) Providers look good (minor nits only)

- `ComponentPropertyProvider` / `InstrumentPropertyProvider`: 👍 solid heuristics.  
  Optional: add `try { XDataHelpers.EnsureRegApp(ctx.Id.Database, AppName); } catch { }` *before* writes (you already do via Write())—so safe.

- `LayerNameConverter`: 👍  
  Optional: cache layer names per-document if you notice perf churn on very large drawings.

- `LayerRules`: 👍 simple and centralized.

---

## 5) LineTagSettings + MasterProperties

If you keep the PropertyGrid “Tag Numbers” **ExpandableObject** view, retarget it to `GetSystemNextList` or drop it (since the palette tree now shows the numbers). Minimal change:

```csharp
public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
{
    TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView(); // or delete this whole feature
    // or re-implement TagNumbersView.FromList(...) around GetSystemNextList if you want
}
```

Or keep it, but rewrite `TagNumbersView` to consume `SystemNextInfo` instead of `SystemNextPair`.

---

## 6) Small cleanups you can do quickly

- In `PropertyRouter._providers`, the order is fine. Keep the tool settings provider last (it can’t “claim” anything anyway).
- In both `PipeRunMatchService` and provider `Apply` methods, you already set the entity layer after XData write. Good.
- `ComponentProperties.Spec` is hidden `[Browsable(false)]` — nice future-proofing.

---

## What we removed / unified (summary)

- ❌ `NodLineNumberStore` and `TagAllocator_V2` from the palette.  
- ❌ Duplicate *match* logic: commands now delegate to `PipeRunMatchService`.  
- ❌ Duplicate “system next” shape: prefer `GetSystemNextList` everywhere.  
- ❌ Extra `XDataHelpers` copy: keep only the final robust one.

With those changes, all roads lead to the **v0.4** store (`ProjectTagStore`) and your UI always reflects the same single source of truth.

If you want, I can also give you a tiny `PeekFormatted(systemKey)` helper on top of `ProjectTagStore` (it just formats `Systems[system].Next`), but the snippet in `RefreshHomePreview` already shows the pattern.

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<SystemNextInfo> list)
{
    var view = new TagNumbersView();
    var dict = list?.ToDictionary(l => l.Name ?? l.SystemKey ?? "", l => l.Next);
    view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(dict, $"{dict?.Count ?? 0} system(s)");
    view.TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(dict, $"{dict?.Count ?? 0} system(s)");
    return view;
}
```

Then `RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()` calls `FromList()` instead.

---

### 🔵 **Option 2 — Remove it completely (recommended)**
Since your **palette tree** now shows “Tag Numbers,” you don’t need this property grid section anymore.

So you can safely **delete** the whole method body and property:

```csharp

[Browsable(false)]   // optional if you keep it
public TagNumbersView TagNumbers { get; private set; }
```

and just remove `RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()` entirely.  
That’s the cleaner path if you’ve moved visual display to the collapsible tree.

## Response:
10/20/2025, 10:56:23 AM

Excellent question 👏 — that note in **Point 4** referred to a small **optional safety addition** inside each `Apply()` method of your **property providers**, e.g.  
`ComponentPropertyProvider.Apply()` and `InstrumentPropertyProvider.Apply()`.

---

### 💡 Why add it
`XDataHelpers.Write()` already ensures a RegApp table record exists **inside** its own transaction.  
But if something goes wrong (rare AutoCAD DB mismatch, modeless state, etc.), calling  
`EnsureRegApp()` first gives one extra layer of protection before writing.

---

### ✅ Here’s exactly where it goes

#### In **ComponentPropertyProvider.Apply**
Put this single line **right before** the final call to `XDataHelpers.Write(...)`:

```csharp
public void Apply(object editedModel, SelectionContext ctx)
{
    var m = (ComponentProperties)editedModel;
    var map = new XDataMap
    {
        ["SchemaVersion"] = "1.0",
        ["ComponentTag"] = m.ComponentTag,
        ["ComponentType"] = m.ComponentType,
        ["Size"] = m.Size,
        ["Rating"] = m.Rating,
        ["EndConnection"] = m.EndConnection,
        ["ValveType"] = m.ValveType,
        ["Operator"] = m.Operator,
        ["FailAction"] = m.FailAction,
        ["ServiceCritical"] = m.ServiceCritical ? "true" : "false"
    };

    // 🟢 OPTIONAL safety addition:
    try { XDataHelpers.EnsureRegApp(ctx.Id.Database, AppName); } catch { }

    XDataHelpers.Write(ctx.Id, AppName, map);
}
```

---

#### And likewise in **InstrumentPropertyProvider.Apply**

```csharp
public void Apply(object editedModel, SelectionContext ctx)
{
    var m = (InstrumentProperties)editedModel;
    var map = new XDataMap
    {
        ["SchemaVersion"] = "1.0",
        ["InstrTag"] = m.InstrTag,
        ["LoopTag"] = m.LoopTag,
        ["DeviceType"] = m.DeviceType,
        ["Location"] = m.Location,
        ["SignalType"] = m.SignalType,
        ["RangeLow"] = m.RangeLow?.ToString(),
        ["RangeHigh"] = m.RangeHigh?.ToString(),
        ["Setpoint"] = m.Setpoint?.ToString(),
        ["Owner"] = m.Owner,
        ["CalibrationDue"] = m.CalibrationDue
    };

    // 🟢 OPTIONAL safety addition:
    try { XDataHelpers.EnsureRegApp(ctx.Id.Database, AppName); } catch { }

    XDataHelpers.Write(ctx.Id, AppName, map);
}
```

---

### 🔧 Summary

- It goes **immediately before** any `XDataHelpers.Write()` call.  
- Purpose: guarantees that the RegApp name (e.g. `CBI_COMPONENT` or `CBI_INSTRUMENT`)  
  exists before writing, avoiding edge-case `eKeyNotFound` exceptions.  
- Harmless if redundant—`Write()` also ensures it inside its transaction.  

So you can safely add this line to every provider’s `Apply()` method for extra stability in long modeless sessions.

```csharp
public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
{
```

## Response:
10/20/2025, 11:49:14 AM

Excellent question — and you caught a subtle one 👀  

The **Item 5** comment you’re referring to —  

> “Then remove `GetSystemNextPairs` usages in `LineTagSettings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()` too — point it at `GetSystemNextList` or delete that property group if you prefer the tree only.”  

was **about the TreeView**, not the PropertyGrid fields.  

Let me unpack that clearly 👇  

---

### 💬 What Item 5 originally meant

That note came from the version where there were **two parallel ways** to show tag counters:

| UI Mode | Data Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------------|----------|
| 🪴 **TreeView** (`_tvNext`) | `GetSystemNextList()` | Show hierarchical systems and IDs in a collapsible tree |
| 🧱 **PropertyGrid group (“Tag Numbers”)** | `GetSystemNextPairs()` | Show two simple expandable fields: Line ID’s + Tag ID’s |

---

### 💡 The intent of the comment
It was basically saying:
> “If you decide to keep only the **TreeView**, then stop using `GetSystemNextPairs` and switch everything to `GetSystemNextList` instead.”  

In other words, the comment only applied if you were **dropping the PropertyGrid fields** and keeping the tree.

---

### ✅ Your case (the opposite)
You said:
> “I prefer to drop the tree view and use the fields as seen in image.”

Perfect — that means you’re keeping the **PropertyGrid fields** (the “Tag Numbers → Line ID’s / Tag ID’s” section).  
So you should **keep using `GetSystemNextPairs()`** and safely **ignore** that old Item 5 comment.

---

### ✅ TL;DR Summary

| Option | Keep | Remove | Method to Use |
|---------|-------|--------|----------------|
| **TreeView UI** | `_tvNext` | PropertyGrid group | `GetSystemNextList()` |
| **PropertyGrid fields (your choice)** | TagNumbersView | TreeView | `GetSystemNextPairs()` ✅ |

So yes — Item 5 was **about the TreeView**, not the fields you’re keeping.

> **Diagnosing compile errors**
>
> The errors seem to indicate brace mismatches or misplaced code in `PipeRunMatchCommands.cs`. Errors like CS0116 and CS0103 suggest a missing closing brace for the class/namespace or extra code outside it. I'd suggest ensuring class methods are properly wrapped and the `AppName` constant stays within scope. A snippet showing the corrected structure would help.
>
> **Crafting instructions for code fix**
>
> For the `PipeRunMatchCommands.cs`, we’ll move helper methods inside the class or into a separate static class. Also, to resolve the undefined `AppName` error caused by it going out of scope, we recommend passing it as a parameter or ensuring it's defined in scope with proper braces. The file should include necessary `using` statements, account for the `CommandMethod` attribute, and properly address the helper methods. Let’s consolidate that into one clean, corrected file.
>
> Thought for 27s

Those 5 errors are classic “brace/scope got messed up” symptoms in **PipeRunMatchCommands.cs**:

- **CS0116** at 124/155/171 = code ended up **outside** any class/struct.
- **CS0103 ‘AppName’…** at 136 = your constant fell **out of scope** because of that.
- **CS1022** at 188 = compiler reached the end of file while still expecting a `}`.

Fix by putting everything back **inside one class** and keeping the `AppName` constant in scope. Here’s a clean, paste-in replacement for the whole file that compiles (helpers are private static methods inside the class):

```csharp
// File: PipeRunMatchCommands.cs
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class PipeRunMatchCommands
    {
        private const string AppName = "CBI_PIPE_RUN";

        [CommandMethod("CBI_MATCH_PIPERUN")]
        public static void MatchPipeRunXData()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // 1) Pick source
            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect SOURCE pipe run (polyline) with CBI data: ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            var srcMap = XDataHelpers.Read(per.ObjectId, AppName);
            if (srcMap == null || srcMap.Count == 0)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nSource has no CBI_PIPE_RUN XData. Aborting.");
                return;
            }

            // 2) Targets
            var pso = new PromptSelectionOptions { MessageForAdding = "\nSelect TARGET pipe runs (polylines): " };
            var filter = new SelectionFilter(new[] { new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Start, "LWPOLYLINE,POLYLINE") });
            var psr = ed.GetSelection(pso, filter);
            if (psr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            // Next TagSeqNo (global max + 1)
            int nextSeq = GetNextTagSeqNo(doc.Database);

            var keysToSkip = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase) { "TagSeqNo", "ItemTag" };

            // Layer policy from source
            string sourceLayerName = srcMap.Get("LayerName");
            string sourceRunTypeRaw = srcMap.Get("PipingRunType"); // "Primary"/"Secondary" or legacy
            string ruleLayerFromSrc = LayerRules.LayerForPipeRun(sourceRunTypeRaw);

            XDataHelpers.EnsureRegApp(doc.Database, AppName);

            int applied = 0;
            foreach (SelectedObject sel in psr.Value)
            {
                if (sel == null) continue;
                ObjectId id = sel.ObjectId;

                var dstMap = XDataHelpers.Read(id, AppName);

                var newMap = new XDataMap();
                foreach (var kv in srcMap)
                    if (!keysToSkip.Contains(kv.Key))
                        newMap[kv.Key] = kv.Value;

                // TagSeqNo policy
                string existingSeq = dstMap.Get("TagSeqNo");
                if (!int.TryParse(existingSeq, out _))
                {
                    existingSeq = nextSeq.ToString();
                    nextSeq++;
                }
                newMap["TagSeqNo"] = existingSeq;

                // Recompute ItemTag
                var nominal = newMap.Get("NominalDiameter");
                var fluid = newMap.Get("FluidCode");
                var pmc = newMap.Get("PipingMaterialClass");
                var insul = newMap.Get("InsulationPurpose");
                newMap["ItemTag"] = PipeRunProperties.BuildItemTag(nominal, fluid, existingSeq, pmc, insul);

                // Layer decision
                var dstLayerName = dstMap.Get("LayerName");
                var layerToApply = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sourceLayerName) ? sourceLayerName
                                   : !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dstLayerName) ? dstLayerName
                                   : ruleLayerFromSrc;

                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(layerToApply))
                    newMap["LayerName"] = layerToApply;

                // Write XData
                XDataHelpers.Write(id, AppName, newMap);

                // Apply real entity layer
                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(layerToApply))
                {
                    EnsureLayerExists(doc.Database, layerToApply);
                    using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                    {
                        var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                        if (ent != null && !ent.IsErased) ent.Layer = layerToApply;
                        tr.Commit();
                    }
                }

                applied++;
            }

            ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI Match Pipe Run: applied properties to {applied} target(s).");
        }

        // ---------- Helpers (keep inside the class) ----------
        private static int GetNextTagSeqNo(Database db)
        {
            int max = 0;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);

                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                {
                    var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                    if (ent == null) continue;
                    var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(AppName);
                    if (rb == null) continue;

                    var arr = rb.AsArray();
                    for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
                    {
                        if (arr[i].TypeCode == 1000 && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == 1000)
                        {
                            var k = arr[i].Value as string;
                            if (!string.Equals(k, "TagSeqNo", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
                            var v = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                            if (int.TryParse(v, out var n) && n > max) max = n;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
            return max + 1;
        }

        private static void EnsureLayerExists(Database db, string layerName)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(layerName)) return;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var lt = (LayerTable)tr.GetObject(db.LayerTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!lt.Has(layerName))
                {
                    lt.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new LayerTableRecord { Name = layerName };
                    lt.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### What to check in your file
- Make sure there are **exactly three closing braces** at the end: `}` (end helper), `}` (end class), `}` (end namespace).
- Ensure you don’t have a **second** partial copy of `PipeRunMatchCommands` in another file; if you do, keep only one definition or rename.

After replacing the file with the snippet above, rebuild — those **CS0116/CS0103/CS1022** errors should disappear.

## Response:

> **Clarifying command workflow**
>
> There seem to be a few issues with the existing command structure. Some commands, like `CreateFromActiveDrawing`, are missing the necessary `[CommandMethod]` tag to be recognized. Also, `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.Run` should likely have this attribute, as it was used earlier. I propose a list of updated commands like `CBI_PROP_PALETTE` to open the properties palette and others to create support folders or bind data correctly.
>

>

I'll suggest adding this attribute to both the `CBI_RepairProjectStore` and other relevant methods. Also, we need to correct the `TryGetBoundProject` method ensuring it properly checks both old and new dictionary structures for project binding.
>
> Thought for 35s

Great questions—this is mostly wiring + a tiny binder fix. Here’s the clean, recommended setup and exactly which commands your ribbon/menu buttons should call.

# ✅ Button → Command map

Use these command names in your ribbon:

1) **CBI Properties**  
`CBI_PROP_PALETTE` (already works)

2) **New Project (from current DWG)**  
`CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG`

3) **New / Browse Project (interactive)**  
`CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE`

4) **Bind Current Drawing to Project** (create CBI_Support if missing, add DWG to store, then bind)  
`CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG`

5) **Open Support Folder** (opens folder that contains Project_Store.json for the active DWG)  
`CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER`

6) **Repair Project Store** (optional toolbox item)  
`CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE`

---

# 🧩 Add the `[CommandMethod]` attributes

A few of your helper methods were **not** exposed as AutoCAD commands. Add these attributes (or replace the methods with the versions below):

```csharp
// NewProjectCommands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class NewProjectCommands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG")]
        public static void CreateFromActiveDrawing() { /* existing body unchanged */ }

        [CommandMethod("CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE")]
        public static void CreateInteractive() { /* existing body unchanged */ }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// BindCurrentDrawingCommand.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class BindCurrentDrawingCommand
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG")]
        public static void Run() { /* existing body unchanged */ }
    }
}
```

```csharp

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class OpenSupportFolderCommand
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER")]
        public static void OpenFromActiveDrawing() { /* existing body unchanged */ }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// Commands_ProjectRepair.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectRepairCommands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
        public static void CBI_RepairProjectStore() { /* existing body unchanged */ }
    }
}
```

---

# 🪛 Fix the binder (why the banner said “no project bound”)

Your current `ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(...)` stub was looking for `NOD["CBI"]["ProjectStore"]` and never returned a valid binding. Bring back the working reader for the legacy record we write (`NOD["CBI"]["Project"] = Xrecord(Id, Name, StorePath)`), with a safe fallback:

```csharp
// ProjectBindingService.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public struct ProjectBinding
    {
        public string ProjectId;
        public string Name;
        public string StorePath;
        public bool IsValid => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(StorePath);
    }

    public static class ProjectBindingService
    {
        public static bool TryGetBoundProject(Document doc, out ProjectBinding binding)
        {
            binding = default(ProjectBinding);
            if (doc == null) return false;

            var db = doc.Database;
            try
            {
                using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
                {
                    var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    if (nod != null && nod.Contains("CBI"))
                    {
                        var cbi = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt("CBI"), OpenMode.ForRead);
                        if (cbi != null && cbi.Contains("Project"))
                        {
                            var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(cbi.GetAt("Project"), OpenMode.ForRead);
                            var data = xr.Data?.AsArray();
                            if (data != null && data.Length >= 3)
                            {
                                binding.ProjectId = data[0].Value as string ?? string.Empty;
                                binding.Name      = data[1].Value as string ?? string.Empty;
                                binding.StorePath = data[2].Value as string ?? string.Empty;
                                return binding.IsValid;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }

                // Fallback to the legacy helper (if present)
                var path = ProjectTagPath.GetPath(db, db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction());
                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path))
                {
                    binding = new ProjectBinding { ProjectId = "", Name = "", StorePath = path };
                    return true;
                }
            }
            catch { /* swallow */ }

            return false;
        }

        public static bool TryGetBoundProject(out ProjectBinding binding)
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            return TryGetBoundProject(doc, out binding);
        }
    }
}
```

> Why this matters: both **New Project** and **Bind Current Drawing** call `ProjectNodBinder.BindProjectToDrawing(...)` which writes `NOD["CBI"]["Project"]`. The palette banner and your “Tag Numbers” fields read via `ProjectBindingService`. With the fixed reader, the banner will correctly update when you flip tabs.

---

# 🧭 Workflow (what each button is for)

**A) New project from the current DWG**
1. Save the drawing.
2. Run **`CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG`**.  
   - Creates `CBI_Support\Project_Store.json` next to the DWG,
   - seeds a v0.4 store,
   - **binds** the DWG to that store (NOD),
   - refreshes the Properties palette banner.

**B) New project anywhere (browse for root)**
- Run **`CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE`**.  
  Lets you pick a name + root and (optionally) scan DWGs. This is your “wizard” path; use when the project isn’t “next to the DWG”.

**C) Existing DWG not yet associated to any project**
- Open that DWG, then run **`CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG`**.  
  - If `CBI_Support\Project_Store.json` doesn’t exist **in that DWG’s folder**, it creates it,
  - adds this DWG to the drawing list,
  - writes the **NOD binding** for this DWG,
  - refreshes the palette banner.

**D) Open the project’s support folder**  
- Run **`CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER`** to open the folder that contains the bound `Project_Store.json`.

**E) Properties palette auto-refresh**  
- The palette host (`CbiPropertiesPalette`) already hooks **DocumentActivated** and **selection** events and calls `RefreshBannerIfOpen()`. Once the binder above is fixed, the banner and Tag Numbers will switch correctly as you click between drawing tabs (bound vs not bound).

---

# 🔁 What to change in the ribbon/menu now

- Point **New Project** button to `CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG`.
- Add a button **Bind Drawing to Project** → `CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG`.
- (Optional) Add **New Project (Interactive)** → `CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE`.
- (Optional) Add **Open Support Folder** → `CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER`.
- Leave **CBI Properties** pointing to `CBI_PROP_PALETTE`.

That’s it. Once those command names are wired, the banner should never be stale, and you’ve got a clear path for both **new** and **existing** drawings.

## Response:
10/20/2025, 12:15:43 PM

> **Designing the refresh tool**
>
> It seems the user wants a button that updates properties like pipe size from a polyline to related valve blocks along the pipeline. It would scan the polyline for changes in XData (like NominalDiameter), then propagate those updates to valves. Possibly, there’s a need to add a symbol block to the pipeline too.
>
> **Button and algorithm proposal**
>
> The user wants a "Refresh associated properties" button for updating block data. My idea is to create a command like "CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED." It would identify source polylines and use either proximity or XData (like "OwnerRunHandle") to connect it to relevant block references. We could also have an option for “Refresh All in Drawing,” though that might be error-prone.
>
> **Button design proposal**
>
> The user wants a button with a new command, `CBI_REFRESH_FROM_RUN`. This would update properties like size or material for blocks based on a polyline's XData. We’ll store relevant XData for both polylines and valve blocks, offering options for refreshing everything or specific blocks. I'll detail the options and search distance.
>
> **Updating components from polyline**
>
> To update blocks, I'll look for BlockReference entities with XData matching a run handle or within a proximity threshold to the polyline. Then, I'll update their properties like size and material. There's also the option to modify dynamic properties or attributes. I'll create functions and code stubs to handle mapping and updating the components.
>
> Thought for 1m 38s

Love this idea—one “refresh” tool that pushes pipe-run XData (size, fluid, class, etc.) into the valve/inline blocks that are associated with that run. Here’s a clean way to do it that fits your current architecture (XDataHelpers, providers, same RegApps).

---

# What you’ll get

- **Command:** `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED`  
  Select a pipe run (polyline). It finds associated component blocks and updates:
  - their **CBI_COMPONENT** XData (e.g., Size, FluidCode, PipingMaterialClass), and
  - their **block attribute** `SIZE` or **dynamic property** `SIZE` when present.

- **Command (optional helper):** `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`  
  Pick a run, then pick blocks to *associate* them (stores the run’s handle on each block). This makes refresh precise. If you don’t use this, the refresh can still fall back to “nearest run” within a tolerance.

- **Ribbon button:** “Refresh From Pipe Run” → calls `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED`.

---

# Association model (simple & robust)

- **Pipe run** (polyline) XData: app = `CBI_PIPE_RUN` (you already use this).
- **Component block** (valves, fittings) XData: app = `CBI_COMPONENT` (you already use this).
- Add one key on the component:
  - `OwnerRunHandle` = the **Handle** string of the pipe run entity.  
    (Set by `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`; refresh also *repairs* this when it confidently finds a nearest run.)

This avoids brittle layer/name heuristics and makes refresh deterministic.

---

# Drop-in code (new file)

```csharp
// File: PipeRunAssociationCommands.cs
// C# 7.3 – AutoCAD 2020

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class PipeRunAssociationCommands
    {
        private const string RunApp  = "CBI_PIPE_RUN";
        private const string CompApp = "CBI_COMPONENT";
        private const double NearTol = 0.75; // drawing units – tweak for your scale

        // ---------------- Commands ----------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Refresh component blocks associated with the selected pipe run:
        /// - copies NominalDiameter/FluidCode/PipingMaterialClass from run to component XData,
        /// - updates block attribute or dynamic property named "SIZE" if present.
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED")]
        public static void RefreshAssociated()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                // Pick a polyline (pipe run)
                var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect PIPE RUN (polyline): ");
                peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline),  true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);

                var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
                if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                int updated = RefreshFromRun(doc, per.ObjectId);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nRefreshed {updated} component(s) from the selected run.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Refresh failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Attach selected blocks to a selected run (stores OwnerRunHandle on components).
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN")]
        public static void AttachToRun()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                // 1) Pick run
                var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect PIPE RUN (polyline): ");
                peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline),  true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);

                var perRun = ed.GetEntity(peo);
                if (perRun.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                string runHandle;
                using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
                {
                    runHandle = perRun.ObjectId.Handle.ToString();
                }

                // 2) Pick blocks to attach
                var pso = new PromptSelectionOptions { MessageForAdding = "\nSelect COMPONENT blocks:" };
                var filter = new SelectionFilter(new[]
                {
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Start, "INSERT")
                });
                var psr = ed.GetSelection(pso, filter);
                if (psr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                int attached = 0;
                foreach (SelectedObject so in psr.Value)
                {
                    if (so?.ObjectId.IsNull != false) continue;

                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(so.ObjectId, CompApp);
                    map["OwnerRunHandle"] = runHandle;
                    XDataHelpers.Write(so.ObjectId, CompApp, map);
                    attached++;
                }

                ed.WriteMessage($"\nAttached {attached} block(s) to the run.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Attach failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // ---------------- Core logic ----------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Reads run XData and pushes mapped fields to all associated component blocks.
        /// </summary>
        public static int RefreshFromRun(Document doc, ObjectId runId)
        {
            var db = doc.Database;

            var runMap = XDataHelpers.Read(runId, RunApp);
            if (runMap == null || runMap.Count == 0) return 0;

            // Fields we propagate
            var size = runMap.Get("NominalDiameter");
            var fluid = runMap.Get("FluidCode");
            var pmc = runMap.Get("PipingMaterialClass");

            // Gather targets: first by explicit association; if none, fallback to proximity
            var targets = FindAssociatedBlocks(db, runId);
            if (targets.Count == 0)
                targets = FindBlocksNearRun(db, runId, NearTol);

            int updated = 0;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                foreach (var id in targets)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null || br.IsErased) continue;

                    // Update component XData
                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(id, CompApp);
                    map["OwnerRunHandle"] = runId.Handle.ToString();  // ensure association
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(size))  map["Size"] = size;
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fluid)) map["FluidCode"] = fluid;
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pmc))   map["PipingMaterialClass"] = pmc;
                    XDataHelpers.Write(id, CompApp, map);

                    // Best-effort: push to attribute or dynamic prop named "SIZE"
                    br.UpgradeOpen();
                    bool touched = TrySetAttribute(br, "SIZE", size) | TrySetDynProp(br, "SIZE", size);
                    if (touched) br.RecordGraphicsModified(true);

                    updated++;
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }

            return updated;
        }

        // ---- finders ----

        private static List<ObjectId> FindAssociatedBlocks(Database db, ObjectId runId)
        {
            var list = new List<ObjectId>();
            string handle = runId.Handle.ToString();

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);

                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null) continue;

                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(id, CompApp);
                    if (string.Equals(map.Get("OwnerRunHandle"), handle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                        list.Add(id);
                }
            }
            return list;
        }

        private static List<ObjectId> FindBlocksNearRun(Database db, ObjectId runId, double tol)
        {
            var list = new List<ObjectId>();

            // Get curve geometry of the run
            Curve runCurve = null;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                runCurve = tr.GetObject(runId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Curve;
                if (runCurve == null) return list;

                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);

                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null) continue;

                    // quick reject: only consider blocks that look like components
                    var hasCompX = XDataHelpers.HasAppName(id, CompApp);
                    if (!hasCompX) continue;

                    var pt = br.Position;
                    try
                    {
                        var param = runCurve.GetParameterAtPoint(runCurve.GetClosestPointTo(pt, extend: false));
                        var onCurve = runCurve.GetPointAtParameter(param);
                        if (pt.DistanceTo(onCurve) <= tol)
                            list.Add(id);
                    }
                    catch { /* ignore geometry edge-cases */ }
                }
            }
            return list;
        }

        // ---- writers to block content ----

        private static bool TrySetAttribute(BlockReference br, string tag, string value)
        {
            if (br.AttributeCollection.IsNull || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tag)) return false;
            bool changed = false;
            foreach (ObjectId arId in br.AttributeCollection)
            {
                var ar = (AttributeReference)arId.GetObject(OpenMode.ForWrite, false);
                if (ar == null) continue;
                if (string.Equals(ar.Tag, tag, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    ar.TextString = value ?? string.Empty;
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            return changed;
        }

        private static bool TrySetDynProp(BlockReference br, string propName, string value)
        {
            try
            {
                var props = br.DynamicBlockReferencePropertyCollection;
                if (props == null) return false;

                foreach (DynamicBlockReferenceProperty p in props)
                {
                    if (string.Equals(p.PropertyName, propName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                        !p.ReadOnly)
                    {
                        // attempt numeric first, else set string
                        double d; object toSet = (double.TryParse(value, out d)) ? (object)d : (object)(value ?? "");
                        p.Value = toSet;
                        return true;
                    }
                }
            }
            catch { }
            return false;
        }
    }
}
```

---

# How to use it

1) **Associate blocks to a run once** (best practice)  
   Run `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`, pick the run, then the valve/inline blocks on that run.

2) **Edit pipe-run XData** (size, class, fluid, etc.)

3) **Refresh**  
   Run `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED`, pick the run. Blocks tied to that run (or near it if not attached) will:
   - get their **CBI_COMPONENT** XData updated (`Size`, `FluidCode`, `PipingMaterialClass`),
   - and have their visible **SIZE** attribute/dyn-prop updated when found.

---

# Ribbon/menu

Add a new button **“Refresh From Pipe Run”** → `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED`.  
(Optional) Add **“Attach Components to Run”** → `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`.

---

# Notes & tweaks

- **What fields to push?** Right now: `NominalDiameter → Size`, plus `FluidCode`, `PipingMaterialClass`. Add/remove lines in `RefreshFromRun()` to suit your standard.
- **Nearest-run fallback** is handy for legacy drawings; once you run refresh it also writes `OwnerRunHandle` so the next refresh is exact.
- **Tolerance** (`NearTol`) is 0.75 units—adjust for your scale.
- If you’d like this to run automatically **after Apply** in the palette when the user changes a run’s size, call `PipeRunAssociationCommands.RefreshFromRun(...)` from your `PipeRunPropertyProvider.Apply(...)` (after `XDataHelpers.Write(...)`), guarded by a small try/catch.

This gives your users a single, reliable button to synchronize valve symbols with the pipe they live on—no more mismatched sizes.

Yep—those need a little wiring cleanup. Here’s the **correct mapping** to the implementations you already have:

- **New Project (next to DWG):** `NewProjectCommands.CreateFromActiveDrawing()`
- **New Project (interactive anywhere):** `NewProjectCommands.CreateInteractive()`
- **Bind current DWG to project store in same folder:** `BindCurrentDrawingCommand.Run()`
- **Open the DWG’s support folder:** `OpenSupportFolderCommand.OpenFromActiveDrawing()`
- **Repair global/local project store JSON:** `ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore()`

The `ProjectBinding()` call you wrote **doesn’t exist**—use `NewProjectCommands.CreateInteractive()`.

---

# Drop-in command wrapper (put in any `public static class`, e.g. `CbiProjectCommands.cs`)

```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    // One tiny wrapper class that exposes stable command names for the ribbon.
    public static class CbiProjectCommands
    {
        // Create CBI_Support\Project_Store.json next to the active DWG,
        // bind the DWG to it, and refresh the palette banner.
        [CommandMethod("CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG")]
        public static void Cbi_NewProject_FromDwg()
            => NewProjectCommands.CreateFromActiveDrawing();

        // Interactive “anywhere” new project flow (prompt for name + root).
        [CommandMethod("CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE")]
        public static void Cbi_NewProject_Interactive()
            => NewProjectCommands.CreateInteractive();

        // Bind *this* DWG to the store in its folder (create if missing, add DWG to list).
        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG")]
        public static void Cbi_Bind_Current_Dwg()
            => BindCurrentDrawingCommand.Run();

        // Open the folder that contains this drawing’s Project_Store.json.
        [CommandMethod("CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER")]
        public static void Cbi_Open_Support_Folder()
            => OpenSupportFolderCommand.OpenFromActiveDrawing();

        // Validate/repair the store JSON (fills missing defaults, etc.).
        [CommandMethod("CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE")]
        public static void Cbi_Repair_Project_Store()
            => ProjectRepairCommands.CBI_RepairProjectStore();
    }
}
```

### Why this helps
- Gives you **stable command names** for the ribbon/QAT.
- Keeps the actual logic in your existing classes.
- Avoids the bad reference to non-existent `ProjectBinding()`.

---

# Button → Command mapping (for your ribbon)

- **New Project (From DWG):** `CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG`
- **New Project (Interactive):** `CBI_NEWPROJECT_INTERACTIVE`
- **Bind Current Drawing:** `CBI_BIND_CURRENT_DWG`
- **Open Support Folder:** `CBI_OPEN_SUPPORT_FOLDER`
- **Repair Project Store:** `CBI_REPAIR_PROJECT_STORE`

(Optional from our earlier idea)
- **Attach Components to Run:** `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`
- **Refresh From Pipe Run:** `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOCIATED`

Hook those up and you’re set.
