Absolutely—here are the two “drop-in” pieces you asked for.

---

# 1) ProjectStoreService helpers (read/write)

Add these to **`AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService`** (same partial class as your other helpers). They’re safe if the file/node is missing and keep everything under the existing `Config` and `Tagging` nodes.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// (inside namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore)
using System.IO;
using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;

public static partial class ProjectStoreService
{
    // -------- Global Valve Prefix --------
    public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string storePath, string prefix)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;

        JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
            ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
            : new JObject();

        var tagging = (JObject)(root["Tagging"] ?? (root["Tagging"] = new JObject()));
        tagging["GlobalValvePrefix"] = prefix ?? "V";

        File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString());
    }

    // Already in your file, included here for completeness:
    // public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string storePath) { ... }

    // -------- GraceDays --------
    public static int ReadGraceDays(string storePath)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return 7;
        try
        {
            var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
            var cfg = root["Config"] as JObject;
            return (int?)cfg?["GraceDays"] ?? 7;
        }
        catch { return 7; }
    }

    public static void WriteGraceDays(string storePath, int days)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;

        JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
            ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
            : new JObject();

        var cfg = (JObject)(root["Config"] ?? (root["Config"] = new JObject()));
        cfg["GraceDays"] = days < 0 ? 0 : days;

        File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString());
    }

    // -------- PreferReleasedLowestFirst --------
    public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string storePath)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return true;
        try
        {
            var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
            var cfg = root["Config"] as JObject;
            return (bool?)cfg?["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] ?? true;
        }
        catch { return true; }
    }

    public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string storePath, bool value)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;

        JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
            ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
            : new JObject();

        var cfg = (JObject)(root["Config"] ?? (root["Config"] = new JObject()));
        cfg["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] = value;

        File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString());
    }
}
```

> These match your `CreateNewStore` seed (`Config.GraceDays`, `Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst`, and `Tagging.GlobalValvePrefix`) and won’t blow away anything else in the JSON.

---

# 2) Drop-in `LineTagSettings` (palette model)

Replace your current class with this version. It adds the two project config fields, plus the sync methods. I’ve kept `TagNumbers` but **hidden** from the grid so you don’t have to refactor anything else right now.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/LineTagSettings.cs
// v0.2 — adds project-config (GraceDays, PreferReleasedLowestFirst) and JSON sync

using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class LineTagSettings
    {
        // ---------- Appearance ----------
        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Height of annotation text in drawing units.")]
        public double TextHeight { get; set; } = 0.125;

        [Category("Appearance")]
        [Description("Layer to place tag entities on.")]
        public string Layer { get; set; } = "C-ANNO-TAG";

        // ---------- General ----------
        [Category("General")]
        [Description("String prepended to computed valve/line tags (default comes from project JSON).")]
        public string Prefix { get; set; } = "V";

        [Category("Behavior")]
        [Description("If true, increments a counter on each placement.")]
        public bool AutoIncrement { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- Project Config (from Project_Store.json) ----------
        [Category("Project Config")]
        [DisplayName("Grace Days")]
        [Description("Days before a released tag becomes eligible for reuse.")]
        public int GraceDays { get; set; } = 7;

        [Category("Project Config")]
        [DisplayName("Reuse Lowest Released First")]
        [Description("If true, reuse the lowest available released number before allocating a new one.")]
        public bool PreferReleasedLowestFirst { get; set; } = true;

        // ---------- (Legacy) Tag Numbers view (hidden now) ----------
        [Browsable(false)]
        public TagNumbersView TagNumbers { get; private set; } = new TagNumbersView();

        /// <summary>Loads Prefix + project config from the bound Project_Store.json (no UI calls here).</summary>
        public void LoadProjectConfig()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return;

            // Read from JSON store
            Prefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath);
            GraceDays = ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(binding.StorePath);
            PreferReleasedLowestFirst = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath);
        }

        /// <summary>Writes Prefix + project config back to Project_Store.json.</summary>
        public void SaveProjectConfig()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                return;

            ProjectStoreService.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath, Prefix);
            ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(binding.StorePath, GraceDays);
            ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath, PreferReleasedLowestFirst);
        }

        /// <summary>(Optional) Legacy: if you still need numbers in the grid, repoint to GetSystemNextList.</summary>
        public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) { TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView(); return; }

            if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
                return;
            }

            // If you keep the numbers property: use the newer list API
            var list = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(binding.StorePath);
            // Convert to TagNumbersView (if you still show it anywhere)
            TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromPairs_V2(list); // Helper provided below
        }
    }

    public static class MasterProperties
    {
        private const string FileName = "LineTagSettings.json";
        private static LineTagSettings _lineTag;
        public static LineTagSettings LineTag
            => _lineTag ?? (_lineTag = Load() ?? new LineTagSettings());

        public static void Save()
        {
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(LineTag, Formatting.Indented);
            File.WriteAllText(FileName, json);
        }

        private static LineTagSettings Load()
        {
            if (!File.Exists(FileName)) return null;
            var json = File.ReadAllText(FileName);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<LineTagSettings>(json);
        }
    }
}
```

And if you still reference `TagNumbersView.FromPairs(...)` elsewhere, add this tiny adapter so you don’t have to keep the old `SystemNextPair` shape around:

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs (add this to the same file)
using System.Linq;
using System.Collections.Generic;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class TagNumbersViewAdapters
    {
        // Adapter for ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo -> TagNumbersView
        public static TagNumbersView FromPairs_V2(IEnumerable<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo> list)
        {
            // In the new shape, we only have one Next per system (not LineNext/TagNext split).
            // We'll place it under "Tag ID's" and leave Line ID's empty by default.
            var tagMap = (list ?? Enumerable.Empty<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo>())
                .ToDictionary(
                    s => s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    s => s.Next);

            var view = new TagNumbersView();
            view.TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(tagMap, tagMap.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tagMap.Count} system(s)");
            view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)");
            return view;
        }
    }
}
```

> If you truly don’t want to show numbers in the palette anymore, you can delete `TagNumbersView` entirely and remove `RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()`. Leaving it hidden as above keeps compatibility for now.

---

If you’re good with this branch, ping me and I’ll take you back to where we left off (menu/ribbon wiring + reassociation workflow tweaks).

I have a tagnumbersdynamic.cs can we compare notes.

// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/TagNumbersDynamic.cs
// Purpose: Dynamic, expandable groups for PropertyGrid
// Target:  C# 7.3

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using SysPair = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextPair;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Wraps a name→number map so each key appears as a read-only property in a PropertyGrid.
    /// </summary>
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display; // used for ToString()

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>();
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        #region ICustomTypeDescriptor
        AttributeCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetAttributes() => AttributeCollection.Empty;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetClassName() => GetType().Name;
        string ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetComponentName() => null;
        TypeConverter ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter() => new TypeConverter();
        EventDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultEvent() => null;
        PropertyDescriptor ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetDefaultProperty() => null;
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEditor(Type editorBaseType) => null;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents(Attribute[] attributes) => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;
        EventDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetEvents() => EventDescriptorCollection.Empty;

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties(Attribute[] attributes)
            => ((ICustomTypeDescriptor)this).GetProperties();

        PropertyDescriptorCollection ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetProperties()
        {
            // one read-only PropertyDescriptor per system
            var props = _values.Keys
                .OrderBy(k => k, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Select(k => new NumberPropDescriptor(k, _values[k]))
                .ToArray();

            return new PropertyDescriptorCollection(props, readOnly: true);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Required by ICustomTypeDescriptor — return the object that owns the properties.
        /// </summary>
        object ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetPropertyOwner(PropertyDescriptor pd) => this;
        #endregion

        private sealed class NumberPropDescriptor : PropertyDescriptor
        {
            private readonly long _value;

            public NumberPropDescriptor(string name, long value)
                : base(name, null) => _value = value;

            public override Type ComponentType => typeof(DynamicNumberGroup);
            public override bool IsReadOnly => true;
            public override Type PropertyType => typeof(long);

            public override bool CanResetValue(object component) => false;
            public override object GetValue(object component) => _value;
            public override void ResetValue(object component) { }
            public override void SetValue(object component, object value) { }
            public override bool ShouldSerializeValue(object component) => false;
        }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Container shown under the "Tag Numbers" category in LineTagSettings.
    /// Holds two expandable read-only groups: Line ID’s and Tag ID’s.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        public static TagNumbersView FromPairs(IEnumerable<SysPair> pairs)
        {
            var view = new TagNumbersView();

            var line = (pairs ?? Enumerable.Empty<SysPair>())
                .Where(p => p.LineNext.HasValue)
                .ToDictionary(
                    p => p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    p => p.LineNext.Value);

            var tag = (pairs ?? Enumerable.Empty<SysPair>())
                .Where(p => p.TagNext.HasValue)
                .ToDictionary(
                    p => p.Name ?? p.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    p => p.TagNext.Value);

            view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(line, line.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{line.Count} system(s)");
            view.TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag, tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)");

            return view;
        }
    }
}

Finally, I’ll provide code diffs and a minimal update for binary compatibility.

> Thought for 11s

Great—your `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` looks solid. The only mismatch is that it’s wired to the **old** shape `SystemNextPair` (with `LineNext/TagNext`). Your store now exposes **`SystemNextInfo`** via `GetSystemNextList(...)` (single `Next` per system). Two easy options:

## Option A (recommended): Keep your file and add a second factory for the new shape
No breaking changes; you can keep `FromPairs(...)` for any legacy callers and add `FromList(...)` for the new API.

### Minimal patch to `TagNumbersDynamic.cs`
Add this new adapter method at the bottom of the file (inside `TagNumbersView`):

```csharp
// Adapter for the newer ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo (single Next per system)
public static TagNumbersView FromList(
    IEnumerable<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    // With the new shape we only have one "Next" per system.
    // We'll put it under Tag ID's and leave Line ID's empty.
    var tag = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo>())
        .ToDictionary(
            s => s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
            s => s.Next
        );

    var view = new TagNumbersView();
    view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)");
    view.TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag, tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)");
    return view;
}
```

> Keep `FromPairs(IEnumerable<SysPair>)` exactly as-is so nothing else breaks.

### Then, update your palette loader to call the new factory
In `LineTagSettings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()`:

```csharp
// OLD (commented out in your snippet)
// var pairs = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextPairs(binding.StorePath);
// TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromPairs(pairs);

// NEW
var list = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(binding.StorePath);
TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);
```

## Option B: Fully migrate and delete the old alias
If you’ve removed all call sites that use `SystemNextPair`, you can:
- Delete the `using SysPair = ...SystemNextPair;`
- Remove `FromPairs(...)`
- Use only `FromList(...)`

---

### TL;DR
- Your existing `DynamicNumberGroup` and `TagNumbersView` are fine.
- Add `TagNumbersView.FromList(IEnumerable<SystemNextInfo>)` (code above).
- Switch the palette code to call `GetSystemNextList(...)` + `FromList(...)`.

This keeps everything compiling now and lets you phase out the old `SystemNextPair` whenever you’re ready.

> **Addressing code errors**
>
> To fix the errors, we need to ensure that the missing method definitions, like ReadGlobalValvePrefix and ReadGraceDays, are correctly placed inside the static partial class `ProjectStoreService` and within the right namespace. Those CS0116 errors point to misplaced brackets or namespaces, suggesting code is outside class boundaries. Also, for `TagNumbersDynamic`, check the `FromList` method placement, and ensure the `SystemNextInfo` class is fully qualified or properly referenced.
>
> **Addressing code structure and fixes**
>
> For the `TagNumbersView`, `LineIds`, and `TagIds` setters: the `FromList` method is allowed to set them, but external code cannot. The dictionary type mismatch (CS1503) issue at line 135 may be due to a null default causing type inference. I’ll suggest fixes: 1) Ensure `ProjectStoreService.cs` has correct braces and method placement for static read/write methods. 2) Add the right `using` directives in `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` and ensure `FromList` is static. 3) Update `LineTagSettings.cs` to use relevant `ProjectStoreService` methods efficiently.
>
> Thought for 18s

You’ve got two categories of issues:

1) missing APIs (read/write config + next-list) and  
2) placement/type/scope errors (methods outside class/namespace, wrong type names, private setters, etc.).

Below are **drop-in fixes** for each file.

---

# 1) ProjectStoreService.cs — add the missing APIs *inside* the static partial class

Your errors (CS0116) also mean some methods/braces are outside the class or namespace. First, make sure you have:

```csharp
namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // <<< everything below goes INSIDE this class >>>
```

Then add (or keep) `SystemNextInfo`, `GetSystemNextList`, and the four read/write helpers **inside** the class. This compiles against the JSON you showed (with Tagging.{GlobalValvePrefix,Pad}, Config.{GraceDays,PreferReleasedLowestFirst} and Systems object).

```csharp
// Keep this type in ProjectStoreService scope so callers can reference PSI.SystemNextInfo
public sealed class SystemNextInfo
{
    public string SystemKey { get; set; } = "";
    public string Name { get; set; } = "";
    public string Prefix { get; set; } = ""; // optional per-system
    public long Next { get; set; } = 0;
}

public static List<SystemNextInfo> GetSystemNextList(string storePath)
{
    var list = new List<SystemNextInfo>();
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath))
        return list;

    var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));

    // Prefer v0.4 "Systems" object
    var systemsV04 = root["Systems"] as JObject;
    if (systemsV04 != null)
    {
        foreach (var p in systemsV04.Properties())
        {
            var o = p.Value as JObject; if (o == null) continue;
            list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
            {
                SystemKey = p.Name,
                Name  = (string)(o["System"] ?? p.Name) ?? p.Name,
                Prefix = (string)(o["Prefix"] ?? ""),
                Next = (long?)(o["Next"] ?? 0)
            });
        }
        return list.OrderBy(x => x.SystemKey).ToList();
    }

    // Back-compat to earlier schema variants if needed:
    var sysObj = root["systems"] as JObject;
    if (sysObj != null)
    {
        foreach (var prop in sysObj.Properties())
        {
            var o = prop.Value as JObject;
            if (o == null) continue;
            list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
            {
                SystemKey = prop.Name,
                Name = (string)(o["name"] ?? prop.Name),
                Prefix = (string)(o["prefix"] ?? o["Prefix"] ?? ""),
                Next = (long?)(o["next"] ?? o["Next"]) ?? 0
            });
        }
    }
    var sysArr = root["systems"] as JArray;
    if (sysArr != null)
    {
        foreach (var tok in sysArr.OfType<JObject>())
        {
            list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
            {
                SystemKey = (string)(tok["key"] ?? tok["systemKey"] ?? tok["name"] ?? ""),
                Name = (string)(tok["name"] ?? tok["key"] ?? ""),
                Prefix = (string)(tok["prefix"] ?? tok["Prefix"] ?? ""),
                Next = (long?)(tok["next"] ?? tok["Next"]) ?? 0
            });
        }
    }

    return list
        .GroupBy(x => x.SystemKey ?? x.Name ?? "")
        .Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(x => x.Next).First())
        .OrderBy(x => x.SystemKey)
        .ToList();
}

// ---------- Config read helpers ----------
public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(string storePath)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return "V";
    try
    {
        var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
        var tag = root["Tagging"] as JObject;
        return (string)(tag?["GlobalValvePrefix"]) ?? "V";
    }
    catch { return "V"; }
}

public static int ReadGraceDays(string storePath)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return 7;
    try
    {
        var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
        var cfg = root["Config"] as JObject;
        return (int?)(cfg?["GraceDays"]) ?? 7;
    }
    catch { return 7; }
}

public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(string storePath)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return true;
    try
    {
        var root = JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
        var cfg = root["Config"] as JObject;
        return (bool?)(cfg?["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"]) ?? true;
    }
    catch { return true; }
}

// ---------- Config write helpers ----------
public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(string storePath, string value)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;
    var prefix = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? "V" : value;

    JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
        ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
        : new JObject();

    if (root["Tagging"] == null) root["Tagging"] = new JObject();
    ((JObject)root["Tagging"])["GlobalValvePrefix"] = prefix;

    File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString(Formatting.Indented));
}

public static void WriteGraceDays(string storePath, int graceDays)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;

    JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
        ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
        : new JObject();

    if (root["Config"] == null) root["Config"] = new JObject();
    ((JObject)root["Config"])["GraceDays"] = graceDays;

    File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString(Formatting.Indented));
}

public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(string storePath, bool prefer)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) return;

    JObject root = File.Exists(storePath)
        ? JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath))
        : new JObject();

    if (root["Config"] == null) root["Config"] = new JObject();
    ((JObject)root["Config"])["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] = prefer;

    File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString(Formatting.Indented));
}
```

Finally, close the class and namespace:

```csharp
    } // end class ProjectStoreService
} // end namespace
```

> The CS0116s at lines 314/332/344/359/371 are almost always “brace drift.” After pasting the block above, make sure there’s no stray method sitting **outside** `ProjectStoreService` or the namespace.

---

# 2) TagNumbersDynamic.cs — ensure `FromList` is **inside** TagNumbersView and uses the right type

Add a namespace alias at the top (optional but tidy):

```csharp
using PSI = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;
```

Now add `FromList` **inside** the `TagNumbersView` class (not at file scope – CS0116 happens when you put methods at namespace level). Also, you can set `LineIds`/`TagIds` because you’re inside the class (private setter is fine here).

```csharp
public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSI.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var tag = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSI.SystemNextInfo>())
        .ToDictionary(
            s => s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
            s => s.Next
        );

    var view = new TagNumbersView();
    view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)");
    view.TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag, tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)");
    return view;
}
```

This addresses:

- CS0426: refers to `PSI.SystemNextInfo` (fully qualified).
- CS0272/CS1503: you’re setting `LineIds`/`TagIds` **inside** the class with the correct `Dictionary<string,long>`.

---

# 3) LineTagSettings.cs — call the new functions

Update `RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()`:

```csharp
public void RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()
{
    var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
    if (doc == null) { TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView(); return; }

    if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
        string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
    {
        TagNumbers = new TagNumbersView();
        return;
    }

    // Read palette-visible config (optional, if you display it)
    var globalPrefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath);
    var graceDays    = ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(binding.StorePath);
    var preferReuse  = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath);

    // If you want to keep Prefix in sync with the store’s global prefix, do:
    // this.Prefix = globalPrefix;

    var list = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(binding.StorePath);
    TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(list);
}
```

(And wherever you save edits from the palette, call the corresponding `Write*` helpers with the active store path.)

---

# 4) Fix for the “namespace cannot directly contain…” errors

- Ensure every new method you added in any file sits inside a **class/struct** and inside a **namespace** block.
- Common culprit: you pasted `FromList(...)` at the bottom of `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` outside the `TagNumbersView` class. Move it **inside** the class.
- In `ProjectStoreService.cs`, verify the order of closing braces `}` matches: method → class → namespace. The IntelliSense “Structure” view in VS helps here.

---

Once you make these patches:

- `CS0117` for missing methods/types will vanish (APIs now exist).
- `CS0426` resolves by referencing `PSI.SystemNextInfo`.
- `CS0272` and `CS1503` go away because `FromList` is inside the class and uses `Dictionary<string,long>`.
- `CS0116` disappears after you move methods back inside classes/namespaces.

If anything still complains, tell me the *new* line numbers and I’ll zero in further.

Excellent — you spotted exactly the issue 👍  

Here’s what’s going on:  
Right now, you have **two (or even three)** copies of `FromList(...)`, and one of them is **outside** the `TagNumbersView` class (after the closing brace `}` for that class).  
That’s why you’re still getting the “namespace cannot directly contain members” errors.

Let’s fix it cleanly.

---

### ✅ **Final, Correct Layout**

Your file `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` should look like this (structure and braces are crucial):

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using PSI = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService;   // 👈 add this alias

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public sealed class DynamicNumberGroup : ICustomTypeDescriptor
    {
        private readonly Dictionary<string, long> _values;
        private readonly string _display;

        public DynamicNumberGroup(Dictionary<string, long> values, string display)
        {
            _values = values ?? new Dictionary<string, long>();
            _display = display ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public override string ToString() => _display;

        // (ICustomTypeDescriptor implementation omitted for brevity)
        // keep your existing code here
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Container shown under "Tag Numbers" category in LineTagSettings.
    /// Holds two expandable read-only groups: Line IDs and Tag IDs.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class TagNumbersView
    {
        [DisplayName("Line ID's")]
        [Description("Next line numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup LineIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        [DisplayName("Tag ID's")]
        [Description("Next tag numbers per system (read-only).")]
        [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
        public DynamicNumberGroup TagIds { get; private set; }
            = new DynamicNumberGroup(null, "(none)");

        // === OLD FACTORY (optional, can stay commented) ===
        // public static TagNumbersView FromPairs(IEnumerable<SysPair> pairs) { ... }

        // === NEW FACTORY (the ONLY FromList inside the class) ===
        public static TagNumbersView FromList(IEnumerable<PSI.SystemNextInfo> systems)
        {
            var tag = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<PSI.SystemNextInfo>())
                .ToDictionary(
                    s => s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
                    s => s.Next
                );

            var view = new TagNumbersView();
            view.LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)");
            view.TagIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag, tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)");
            return view;
        }
    }
} // 👈 make sure this is the final brace
```

---

## Response:
10/21/2025, 1:43:39 PM

Perfect — that’s an easy one 👍  

That error (`CS0266`) means the code is trying to assign a **nullable long (`long?`)** to a **non-nullable long (`long`)** in your `GetSystemNextList()` method.

---

### ✅ Fix

Find this block in your `ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList()` (the v0.4 “Systems” section):

```csharp
Next = (long?)(o["Next"] ?? 0)
```

and replace it with:

```csharp
Next = (long?)(o["Next"]) ?? 0
```

---

### 💡 Why
The old code `(o["Next"] ?? 0)` forces `??` on a `JToken` before the cast,  
so it produces a nullable long (`long?`) assignment that doesn’t auto-unbox.  
By swapping the order, you explicitly cast the token to `long?` first,  
and then use `?? 0` to safely fall back to zero if it’s null.

---

### ✅ Full corrected line

Here’s the final version of that section for clarity:

```csharp
list.Add(new SystemNextInfo
{
    SystemKey = p.Name,
    Name  = (string)(o["System"] ?? p.Name) ?? p.Name,
    Prefix = (string)(o["Prefix"] ?? ""),
    Next = (long?)(o["Next"]) ?? 0   // ✅ fixed cast
});
```

---

After this edit, the branch should compile cleanly with **no errors**.

---

### 🧩 1. Files changed in this “branch” work

| File | Purpose | Impact on existing code |
|------|----------|--------------------------|
| **`ProjectStoreService.cs`** (Shared Project) | Added config read/write helpers (`ReadGraceDays`, `ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst`, etc.) and unified `GetSystemNextList`. | ✅ *Safe additive changes*. These are **new helper methods**, not replacements. Any existing code that calls the old methods (`GetSystemNextPairs`, etc.) will still compile (we didn’t delete them). |
| **`LineTagSettings.cs`** (AutoCAD2020) | Now calls `ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList()` instead of older `GetSystemNextPairs()`. Also reads new JSON config values (prefix, grace days, preference). | ⚠️ *Minor behavioral change.* The palette now expects the new JSON shape (v0.4). If you open an old project store file (v0.3 or earlier), the new reader will still work — it’s backward-compatible, but older fields like `tagCounters` will only fill partial data. |
| **`TagNumbersDynamic.cs`** (AutoCAD2020) | Cleaned up so there’s only one `FromList()` factory that uses the new `SystemNextInfo` format. | ✅ *Safe*. Old `FromPairs()` is commented out, so existing code that expected that method may need updating to `FromList()`. We already did that in `LineTagSettings`. |
| **`ProjectStore.json` schema** | Introduced `"Config"` and `"Tagging"` nodes with fields: `GraceDays`, `PreferReleasedLowestFirst`, `GlobalValvePrefix`. | ⚠️ *Schema upgrade.* If older JSONs are missing these sections, defaults will apply. No crash risk — the read helpers handle nulls gracefully. |

---

### ⚙️ 2. What this means for your **previous branch (ProjectStore / Palette / Commands)**

| Area | Compatibility | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Palette display** (`CBI Properties` tab) | ✅ Compatible | It will show the new fields (prefix, grace days, preference) automatically. |
| **Valve / Line Tag tools** | ✅ Compatible | They’ll still use the same store path and system keys. Prefix usage will shift toward reading from `GlobalValvePrefix`, which is now standardized. |
| **Commands like `CBI_NEWPROJECT_FROM_DWG`** | ✅ Compatible | They still create `Project_Store.json`. The structure was enhanced (added Config + Tagging), but older creation logic was replaced only if you used the new `CreateNewStore()` method. |
| **Undo / History logic** | ✅ No change | History array untouched. |
| **ApplyChanges / ChangeHistory** | ✅ No impact | Not tied to project store structure. |
| **Future “Reassociate Valves”** | ✅ No change | Still reads XData / component mappings independently of project store. |

---

### 💡 3. The main *upstream effect*
From now on, any part of the code that depends on project configuration (like global valve prefix, grace days, or release order) should pull from `ProjectStoreService.Read*()` rather than maintaining local variables.  
This actually simplifies future work — everything references one source of truth.

## How this affects Step 2 and onward

### Step 2 — `TagNumbersDynamic` / `TagNumbersView`
**What changed:** We standardized on a *single* shape: `SystemNextInfo` and a *single* adapter:
- Keep **`TagNumbersView.FromList(IEnumerable<ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo>)`** only.

### Step 3 — `PropertiesControl` integration
- When binding the “Tool” model (`LineTagSettings`), call `settings.LoadFromStore(doc)` and `settings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()`.
- On banner refresh (doc switch), call **both** `RefreshProjectBanner()` and `RefreshTagNumbersFromStore()` so the Tag Numbers view always matches the *active* project.
- You decided to **drop the TreeView**—that’s fine. The expandable `TagNumbers` property in the grid is the source of truth now.

**Impact:** If you removed the TreeView UI, nothing else is required as long as the grid shows `TagNumbers`.

### Step 4 — `ProjectStoreService` helpers
- We added:  
  - `GetSystemNextList(storePath)`  
  - `ReadGlobalValvePrefix`, `WriteGlobalValvePrefix`  
  - `ReadGraceDays`, `WriteGraceDays`  
  - `ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst`, `WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst`
- `CreateNewStore()` seeds `Config` and `Tagging`.

**Impact:** Tools that need those values should **read from the store (or via `LineTagSettings` which mirrors the store)**. No breaking changes—these are additive.

### Step 5 — Tools / Allocator (later steps)
- When placing tags/valves, read the **effective prefix** from the palette (`LineTagSettings.Prefix`). That already respects the store value but allows a one-off override per session—exactly your intent.
- Anything that previously read `GetSystemNextPairs` now uses `GetSystemNextList`.

- [ ] `TagNumbersDynamic.cs` → only one `FromList(...)` method exists (and it uses `SystemNextInfo`). No duplicate at file bottom.

## 🌿 MAIN BRANCH CONTINUATION (Step 2 → End)

---

### **Step 2 — `TagNumbersDynamic.cs`**
✅ This file is already current; just confirm you have this *single* method near the bottom.

```csharp
// Adapter for ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo (single Next per system)
public static TagNumbersView FromList(
    IEnumerable<AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo> systems)
{
    var tag = (systems ?? Enumerable.Empty<
        AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.SystemNextInfo>())
        .ToDictionary(
            s => s.Name ?? s.SystemKey ?? string.Empty,
            s => s.Next);

    var view = new TagNumbersView
    {
        LineIds = new DynamicNumberGroup(new Dictionary<string, long>(), "(none)"),
        TagIds  = new DynamicNumberGroup(tag,
                  tag.Count == 0 ? "(none)" : $"{tag.Count} system(s)")
    };
    return view;
}
```

---

### **Step 3 — Extend the Palette (`PropertiesControl`)**
Add a `LoadFromStore()` helper and hook it into palette refresh.  
This keeps the “Home → Tool → Selection” transitions current when switching drawings.

```csharp
// inside PropertiesControl.cs (or your derived class)
public void LoadFromStore(Document doc)
{
    var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;

    // Sync store-based values
    settings.Prefix                   = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull());
    settings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore();
    RefreshPropertyGrid(settings);
}

private void RefreshPropertyGrid(object model)
{
    propertyGrid.SelectedObject = model;
    propertyGrid.Refresh();
}
```

Then, in your palette host (`CbiPropertiesPalette`):

```csharp
private static void BindToolHome()
{
    var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;
    settings.LoadFromStore(Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument);
    _ui.Bind(settings, "Tool");
}
```

Now, every time you activate a new document or hit *Home*, the palette re-syncs the prefix, tag numbers, and config values from that drawing’s bound `Project_Store.json`.

---

### **Step 4 — Add Config Display to Palette (Grace Days + Preference)**

In `PropertiesControl.Designer.cs` (or wherever you layout UI):

```csharp
// Example quick section above Tag Numbers
Label lblConfig = new Label { Text = "Project Configuration", Dock = DockStyle.Top };
NumericUpDown numGrace = new NumericUpDown
{
    Minimum = 0, Maximum = 30, Dock = DockStyle.Top,
    Value = (decimal)ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull())
};
CheckBox chkPreferLowest = new CheckBox
{
    Text = "Prefer Released Tags (Lowest First)",
    Checked = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull()),
    Dock = DockStyle.Top
};
panelConfig.Controls.Add(chkPreferLowest);
panelConfig.Controls.Add(numGrace);
panelConfig.Controls.Add(lblConfig);
```

Optional save-back logic (e.g., on *Apply* click):

```csharp
ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(storePath, (int)numGrace.Value);
ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath, chkPreferLowest.Checked);
```

---

### **Step 5 — Summary of Runtime Flow**

| Action | Effect |
|--------|---------|
| **User opens drawing** | Palette auto-loads project store → populates prefix, grace days, tag list |
| **User edits prefix** | `LineTagSettings.Prefix` overrides global temporarily |
| **Tag/Valve tools** | Use `MasterProperties.LineTag.Prefix` when generating tags |
| **User switches drawings** | `DocumentActivated` triggers palette rebind → refresh from that drawing’s project store |
| **New Project** | `CreateNewStore()` seeds JSON with `Config` + `Tagging` → palette displays immediately |
| **Reassociate Valves / Line Tags** | Pulls prefix + numbering rules from `LineTagSettings` (no change needed) |

---

### ✅ Next Logical Scope (after Step 5)
Return to the **Valve / PipeRunAssociation** workstream:
- Validate your ribbon “Reassociate Valves” button.
- Confirm XData propagation (size → valve block) works using the current prefix.
- Then test tag numbering consistency across multiple drawings.

## Response:
10/21/2025, 2:05:09 PM

Perfect question — and you caught that nuance exactly right.  
Because your **designer stub is intentionally empty** (you’re building the UI manually in `PropertiesControl.cs`), the snippet from **Step 4** does **not** belong in the `.Designer.cs` file.  

Here’s how to integrate it properly:

---

### ✅ Correct Placement

Put the Step 4 code block inside **`PropertiesControl.cs`**, typically in your constructor or a `BuildUI()` method — wherever you’re already creating and arranging the dynamic controls (like the property grid and banner).  

For example:

```csharp
// File: PropertiesControl.cs
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public partial class PropertiesControl : UserControl
    {
        private Panel panelConfig;
        private NumericUpDown numGrace;
        private CheckBox chkPreferLowest;

        public PropertiesControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
            BuildUI();
        }

        private void BuildUI()
        {
            // Main panel setup
            panelConfig = new Panel
            {
                Dock = DockStyle.Top,
                Height = 80,
                Padding = new Padding(6)
            };

            var lblConfig = new Label
            {
                Text = "Project Configuration",
                Dock = DockStyle.Top,
                Font = new System.Drawing.Font("Segoe UI", 9F, System.Drawing.FontStyle.Bold)
            };

            numGrace = new NumericUpDown
            {
                Minimum = 0,
                Maximum = 30,
                Dock = DockStyle.Top,
                Value = (decimal)ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull())
            };

            chkPreferLowest = new CheckBox
            {
                Text = "Prefer Released Tags (Lowest First)",
                Dock = DockStyle.Top,
                Checked = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull())
            };

            panelConfig.Controls.Add(chkPreferLowest);
            panelConfig.Controls.Add(numGrace);
            panelConfig.Controls.Add(lblConfig);

            // Add to the main control (before your property grid, for example)
            Controls.Add(panelConfig);
        }

        // Called when Apply is clicked or project is refreshed
        public void SaveConfigToStore()
        {
            var storePath = ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull();
            ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(storePath, (int)numGrace.Value);
            ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath, chkPreferLowest.Checked);
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 🧭 Why This Approach
- Keeps your designer stub clean and minimal (as you’ve intended).
- Keeps all dynamic UI building in **`PropertiesControl.cs`**, which is easier to version and extend.
- `SaveConfigToStore()` can later be hooked into your palette’s **Apply** or **Commit Edits** flow.

---

Would you like me to show how to hook `SaveConfigToStore()` into your existing Apply button / `ApplyRequested` event in the palette, so those user edits write straight back into the JSON?

using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using System.Drawing;                      // Point/Size/Color/Cursors
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;        // NodLineNumberStore
using System.Collections.Generic;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;   //ProjectSettingsService

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    // IMPORTANT: partial + inherits UserControl
    public partial class PropertiesControl : UserControl
    {

        private readonly PropertyGrid _grid = new PropertyGrid();
        private readonly ToolStrip _bar = new ToolStrip();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lbl = new ToolStripLabel("Source:");
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblSource = new ToolStripLabel("—");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnApply = new ToolStripButton("Apply");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRevert = new ToolStripButton("Revert");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnSave = new ToolStripButton("Save");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnLoad = new ToolStripButton("Load");
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep = new ToolStripSeparator();
        private readonly ToolStripControlHost _chkAutoHost;
        private readonly CheckBox _chkAuto = new CheckBox { Text = "Auto-apply", Checked = true };

        // preview labels (right side)
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblLineNext = new ToolStripLabel();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblTagNext = new ToolStripLabel();

        public bool IsEditing { get; private set; }
        public event EventHandler EditingStateChanged;

private void SetEditing(bool editing)
        {
            if (IsEditing == editing) return;
            IsEditing = editing;
            EditingStateChanged?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
        }

        // Call this once in ctor AFTER InitializeComponent();
        private void WireEditingState()
        {
            // Add any control types you use for input
            foreach (Control c in GetAllInputs(this))
            {
                c.Enter += (s, e) => SetEditing(true);
                c.Leave += (s, e) => SetEditing(false);
            }
        }

        // Helper: enumerate inputs recursively
        private IEnumerable<Control> GetAllInputs(Control root)
        {
            foreach (Control c in root.Controls)
            {
                if (c is TextBoxBase || c is ComboBox || c is CheckBox || c is NumericUpDown)
                    yield return c;
                foreach (var child in GetAllInputs(c)) yield return child;
            }
        }

        // Project banner (below toolbar)
        private readonly Label lblProjectBanner = new Label
        {
            Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            Height = 24,
            TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft,
            Font = new Font("Segoe UI", 9F, FontStyle.Bold),
            ForeColor = Color.DodgerBlue,
            Padding = new Padding(6, 0, 0, 0),
            Text = "Project: (none)"
        };

        //---------------- Removed the tree view control from top of palette -----------------------

        //// Collapsible "Tag Numbers" tree: Master -> (Line ID's, Tag ID's) -> per-system rows
        //private readonly TreeView _tvNext = new TreeView
        //{
        //    Dock = DockStyle.Top,
        //    Height = 140,                      // adjust as preferred
        //    HideSelection = false,
        //    ShowLines = true,
        //    ShowRootLines = true
        //};

        private object _currentModel;
        private object _snapshot;

        // resize grip state
        private readonly Panel _grip = new Panel();
        private bool _dragging;
        private Point _dragStartScreen;
        private Size _startSize;

        public event EventHandler ApplyRequested;      // <— host subscribes
        public object SelectedModel => _currentModel;  // <— host reads

        public PropertiesControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();        // <— must exist in Designer partial

            // NEW: track when the user is actively typing in editors
            WireEditingState();

            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;

            //// Use mono font so "name | number" rows align nicely in the tree
            //try { _tvNext.Font = new Font("Consolas", 8.5f, FontStyle.Regular); } catch { /* ignore */ }

            _grid.Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                // Keep previews in sync if Prefix changed on tool model
                var name = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                if (string.Equals(name, nameof(LineTagSettings.Prefix), StringComparison.Ordinal))
                {
                    if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt)
                        RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
                }

                if (_chkAuto.Checked)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            };

            _chkAutoHost = new ToolStripControlHost(_chkAuto) { Margin = new Padding(8, 2, 4, 2) };
            _btnApply.Click += (s, e) => ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            _btnRevert.Click += (s, e) => Rebind(_snapshot);
            _btnSave.Click += (s, e) => SavePreset();
            _btnLoad.Click += (s, e) => LoadPreset();

            // Toolbar composition (left → right)
            _bar.Items.Add(_lbl);
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblSource);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnApply);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRevert);
            _bar.Items.Add(_sep);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnSave);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnLoad);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_chkAutoHost);

            // previews on the right
            _lblLineNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblTagNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblLineNext);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("  ") { Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right });
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblTagNext);

            _bar.Dock = DockStyle.Top;

            // Dock stacking order: toolbar (top), banner (top), tree (top), grid (fill)
            Controls.Add(_grid);
            //Controls.Add(_tvNext);
            Controls.Add(lblProjectBanner);
            Controls.Add(_bar);

            // resize grip (bottom-right)
            _grip.Size = new Size(14, 14);
            _grip.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Bottom | AnchorStyles.Right;
            _grip.Cursor = Cursors.SizeNWSE;
            _grip.BackColor = Color.Transparent;
            _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);

            _grip.MouseDown += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Button != MouseButtons.Left) return;
                CbiPropertiesPalette.EnsureFloating();
                _dragging = true;
                _dragStartScreen = Cursor.Position;
                _startSize = CbiPropertiesPalette.GetSize();
                _grip.Capture = true;
            };
            _grip.MouseMove += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (!_dragging) return;
                var cur = Cursor.Position;
                int dx = cur.X - _dragStartScreen.X;
                int dy = cur.Y - _dragStartScreen.Y;
                var newSize = new Size(_startSize.Width + dx, _startSize.Height + dy);
                CbiPropertiesPalette.SetSize(newSize);
            };
            _grip.MouseUp += (s, e) =>
            {
                _dragging = false;
                _grip.Capture = false;
            };
            this.Resize += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            };

            // your force-apply props
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grid.Refresh();

                var propName = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                bool forceApply =
                    string.Equals(propName, nameof(PipeRunProperties.LayerName), StringComparison.Ordinal) ||
                    string.Equals(propName, nameof(PipeRunProperties.PipingRunType), StringComparison.Ordinal);

                if (_chkAuto.Checked || forceApply)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            };

            // show initial project name + numbers on first load
            this.Load += (s, e) =>
            {
                RefreshProjectBanner();
                //RefreshNextNumbersTree();
            };

            Controls.Add(_grip);
        }

        // --------- Public API used by host ---------

        public void Clear()
        {
            _currentModel = null;
            _snapshot = null;
            _grid.SelectedObject = null;
            _lblSource.Text = "—";
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (none)";
            //_tvNext.Nodes.Clear();
        }

        public void ShowHome(string prefix)
        {
            Bind(new Placeholder(), "Tool");
            RefreshHomePreview(prefix);
        }

        //public void RefreshHomePreview(string prefix)
        //{
        //    try
        //    {
        //        var p = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prefix) ? "PR-" : prefix;
        //        var line = NodLineNumberStore.Peek("LINE", p, 4);
        //        var tag = NodLineNumberStore.Peek("TAG", p, 4);
        //        _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {line}";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = $"Tag next: {tag}";
        //    }
        //    catch
        //    {
        //        _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
        //    }
        //}

        //public void RefreshHomePreview(string prefix)
        //{
        //    try
        //    {
        //        // Use the central allocator so the preview matches what tools will use
        //        var settings = new ProjectSettingsService(@"C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json");
        //        var allocator = new TagAllocator_V2(settings);

        //        // Show the next (peek) values. If you prefer numbers only, we can add helpers later.
        //        var nextLine = allocator.PeekLineTag();
        //        var nextValve = allocator.PeekValveTag();

        //        _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {nextLine}";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = $"Tag next: {nextValve}";
        //    }
        //    catch
        //    {
        //        _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
        //        _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
        //    }
        //}

        public void RefreshHomePreview(string _)
        {
            try
            {
                // Use the bound store (v0.4)
                var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }

                if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                    string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                {
                    _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
                    _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
                    return;
                }

                var store = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.LoadOrCreate(binding.StorePath);

                // choose the first two systems (or GENERAL twice if only one)
                string firstKey = null, secondKey = null;
                foreach (var key in store.Systems.Keys)
                {
                    if (firstKey == null) { firstKey = key; continue; }
                    if (secondKey == null) { secondKey = key; break; }
                }
                if (firstKey == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }
                if (secondKey == null) secondKey = firstKey;

                var a = store.Systems[firstKey];
                var b = store.Systems[secondKey];

                string fmtA = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.FormatId(a.Prefix, a.Next, a.Pad);
                string fmtB = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.FormatId(b.Prefix, b.Next, b.Pad);

                // label them as “Line” and “Tag” previews (purely UI; both are systems)
                _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {fmtA}";
                _lblTagNext.Text  = $"Tag next: {fmtB}";
            }
            catch
            {
                _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
                _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —";
            }
        }

        public void Bind(object model, string sourceLabel)
        {
            if (model == null) model = new Placeholder();
            _currentModel = model;

            // if the home model, pull live Tag Numbers from the store before cloning/display
            var lt = _currentModel as LineTagSettings;
            if (lt != null)
            {
                try { lt.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
            }

            _snapshot = DeepClone(_currentModel);
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            _lblSource.Text = string.IsNullOrEmpty(sourceLabel) ? InferLabel(model) : sourceLabel;

            if (lt != null) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        public void CommitPendingEdits() 

        {
            try
            { this.ValidateChildren(); }
            catch { }
        }

// --------- helpers ---------

        private void Rebind(object snap)
        {
            _currentModel = DeepClone(snap ?? new Placeholder());
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        private static object DeepClone(object instance)
        {
            if (instance == null) return null;
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(instance);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, instance.GetType());
        }

        private string InferLabel(object model) => model == null ? "—" : model.GetType().Name;

        private void SavePreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var sfd = new SaveFileDialog
            {
                Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json",
                FileName = _currentModel.GetType().Name + ".json"
            })
            {
                if (sfd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(_currentModel, Formatting.Indented);
                File.WriteAllText(sfd.FileName, json);
            }
        }

        public void RefreshProjectBanner()
        {
            try
            {
                // Get current project name via your bridge
                var name = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectContextBridge.GetCurrentProjectName();
                lblProjectBanner.Text = $"Project: {name}";
            }
            catch
            {
                lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (unavailable)";
            }

            // keep the collapsible tree in sync with the active project
            //RefreshNextNumbersTree();
        }

        private void LoadPreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var ofd = new OpenFileDialog { Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json" })
            {
                if (ofd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = File.ReadAllText(ofd.FileName);
                var loaded = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, _currentModel.GetType());
                if (loaded != null) Bind(loaded, _lblSource.Text);
            }
        }

        public void LoadFromStore(Document doc)
        {
            var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;

            // Sync store-based values
            settings.Prefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull());
            settings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore();
            RefreshPropertyGrid(settings);
        }

        private void RefreshPropertyGrid(object model)
        {
            propertyGrid.SelectedObject = model;
            propertyGrid.Refresh();
        }

//---------------- Removed the tree view control from top of palette -----------------------

        //private void RefreshNextNumbersTree()
        //{
        //    _tvNext.BeginUpdate();
        //    try
        //    {
        //        _tvNext.Nodes.Clear();

        //        var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
        //        if (doc == null)
        //        {
        //            var root = _tvNext.Nodes.Add("Tag Numbers");
        //            root.Nodes.Add("(no active document)");
        //            root.Expand();

        //        // Row formatting

        //            tagNode.Nodes.Add(text);

        //        n.Expand();

        //    finally
        //    {
        //        _tvNext.EndUpdate();
        //    }
        //}

        private sealed class Placeholder
        {
            [Category("Info")]
            [DisplayName("Tip")]
            public string Message => "Select an entity or run a tool to see its properties here.";
        }

}

Absolutely—here’s a cleaned, drop-in **PropertiesControl.cs** that matches your current intent (tree view removed, banner kept, single PropertyGrid, correct usings, no duplicate event wires, fixed `propertyGrid` → `_grid`, and a small helper to pull store values into the home model).

### What I fixed/changed
- Removed duplicate `_grid.PropertyValueChanged` subscription (merged logic into one handler).
- Replaced stray `propertyGrid` references with `_grid`.
- Added missing `using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;` for `Document`.
- Kept your “no designer UI” pattern—everything is still built in code.
- `RefreshHomePreview` uses the bound store (v0.4) and shows two formatted previews.
- `LoadFromStore` safely pulls global prefix + tag numbers and refreshes the grid.
- Minor guardrails and tidy-ups (null/exception safe, comments, consistent stacking order).

---

```csharp
// File: PropertiesControl.cs
using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using System.Drawing;                      // Point/Size/Color/Cursors
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;             // Document, Application
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;              // ProjectStoreService
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;                         // ProjectBindingService, ProjectContextBridge, ProjectTagStore

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    // IMPORTANT: partial + inherits UserControl
    public partial class PropertiesControl : UserControl
    {
        // UI
        private readonly PropertyGrid _grid = new PropertyGrid();
        private readonly ToolStrip _bar = new ToolStrip();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lbl = new ToolStripLabel("Source:");
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblSource = new ToolStripLabel("—");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnApply = new ToolStripButton("Apply");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRevert = new ToolStripButton("Revert");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnSave = new ToolStripButton("Save");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnLoad = new ToolStripButton("Load");
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep = new ToolStripSeparator();
        private readonly ToolStripControlHost _chkAutoHost;
        private readonly CheckBox _chkAuto = new CheckBox { Text = "Auto-apply", Checked = true };

        // preview labels (right side)
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblLineNext = new ToolStripLabel();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblTagNext = new ToolStripLabel();

        // Project banner (below toolbar)
        private readonly Label lblProjectBanner = new Label
        {
            Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            Height = 24,
            TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft,
            Font = new Font("Segoe UI", 9F, FontStyle.Bold),
            ForeColor = Color.DodgerBlue,
            Padding = new Padding(6, 0, 0, 0),
            Text = "Project: (none)"
        };

        // State
        private object _currentModel;
        private object _snapshot;

        // resize grip
        private readonly Panel _grip = new Panel();
        private bool _dragging;
        private Point _dragStartScreen;
        private Size _startSize;

        // Editing focus hint (so the palette host can debounce refresh while user is typing)
        public bool IsEditing { get; private set; }
        public event EventHandler EditingStateChanged;

        public event EventHandler ApplyRequested;      // host subscribes
        public object SelectedModel => _currentModel;  // host reads

        public PropertiesControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();  // minimal designer stub

            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;

            // ----- PropertyGrid -----
            _grid.Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                // 1) Keep the preview labels in sync if LineTagSettings.Prefix changed
                var name = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                if (string.Equals(name, nameof(LineTagSettings.Prefix), StringComparison.Ordinal))
                    if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);

                // 2) Force-apply on fields that must immediately push to DWG (layer/kind)
                bool forceApply =
                    string.Equals(name, nameof(PipeRunProperties.LayerName), StringComparison.Ordinal) ||
                    string.Equals(name, nameof(PipeRunProperties.PipingRunType), StringComparison.Ordinal);

                if (_chkAuto.Checked || forceApply)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);

                // Refresh grid to reflect any formatting/read-only changes
                _grid.Refresh();
            };

            // ----- Toolbar -----
            _chkAutoHost = new ToolStripControlHost(_chkAuto) { Margin = new Padding(8, 2, 4, 2) };
            _btnApply.Click  += (s, e) => ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            _btnRevert.Click += (s, e) => Rebind(_snapshot);
            _btnSave.Click   += (s, e) => SavePreset();
            _btnLoad.Click   += (s, e) => LoadPreset();

            _bar.Items.Add(_lbl);
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblSource);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnApply);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRevert);
            _bar.Items.Add(_sep);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnSave);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnLoad);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_chkAutoHost);

            // Right-aligned preview labels
            _lblLineNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblTagNext.Alignment  = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text  = "Tag next: —";
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblLineNext);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("  ") { Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right });
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblTagNext);
            _bar.Dock = DockStyle.Top;

            // ----- Stack: toolbar (top), banner (top), grid (fill) -----
            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(lblProjectBanner);
            Controls.Add(_bar);

            // ----- Resize grip (bottom-right) -----
            _grip.Size = new Size(14, 14);
            _grip.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Bottom | AnchorStyles.Right;
            _grip.Cursor = Cursors.SizeNWSE;
            _grip.BackColor = Color.Transparent;
            _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            _grip.MouseDown += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Button != MouseButtons.Left) return;
                CbiPropertiesPalette.EnsureFloating();
                _dragging = true;
                _dragStartScreen = Cursor.Position;
                _startSize = CbiPropertiesPalette.GetSize();
                _grip.Capture = true;
            };
            _grip.MouseMove += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (!_dragging) return;
                var cur = Cursor.Position;
                int dx = cur.X - _dragStartScreen.X;
                int dy = cur.Y - _dragStartScreen.Y;
                var newSize = new Size(_startSize.Width + dx, _startSize.Height + dy);
                CbiPropertiesPalette.SetSize(newSize);
            };
            _grip.MouseUp += (s, e) =>
            {
                _dragging = false;
                _grip.Capture = false;
            };
            this.Resize += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            };
            Controls.Add(_grip);

            // Editing state tracking (optional signal to the host)
            WireEditingState();

            // Initial banner + preview
            this.Load += (s, e) =>
            {
                RefreshProjectBanner();
                RefreshHomePreview("(init)");
            };
        }

        // ---------- Editing state helpers ----------
        private void SetEditing(bool editing)
        {
            if (IsEditing == editing) return;
            IsEditing = editing;
            EditingStateChanged?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
        }

        private void WireEditingState()
        {
            foreach (Control c in GetAllInputs(this))
            {
                c.Enter += (s, e) => SetEditing(true);
                c.Leave += (s, e) => SetEditing(false);
            }
        }

        private IEnumerable<Control> GetAllInputs(Control root)
        {
            foreach (Control c in root.Controls)
            {
                if (c is TextBoxBase || c is ComboBox || c is CheckBox || c is NumericUpDown)
                    yield return c;
                foreach (var child in GetAllInputs(c)) yield return child;
            }
        }

        // ---------- Public API used by host ----------
        public void Clear()
        {
            _currentModel = null;
            _snapshot = null;
            _grid.SelectedObject = null;
            _lblSource.Text = "—";
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
            _lblTagNext.Text  = "Tag next: —";
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (none)";
        }

        public void ShowHome(string prefix)
        {
            Bind(new Placeholder(), "Tool");
            RefreshHomePreview(prefix);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Refreshes the two preview labels at the top using the bound store (v0.4).
        /// </summary>
        public void RefreshHomePreview(string _)
        {
            try
            {
                var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }

                if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                    string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                {
                    _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
                    _lblTagNext.Text  = "Tag next: —";
                    return;
                }

                var store = ProjectTagStore.LoadOrCreate(binding.StorePath);

                // Choose first two systems for previews (or same if only one)
                string firstKey = null, secondKey = null;
                foreach (var key in store.Systems.Keys)
                {
                    if (firstKey == null) { firstKey = key; continue; }
                    if (secondKey == null) { secondKey = key; break; }
                }
                if (firstKey == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —"; _lblTagNext.Text = "Tag next: —"; return; }
                if (secondKey == null) secondKey = firstKey;

                var a = store.Systems[firstKey];
                var b = store.Systems[secondKey];

                string fmtA = ProjectTagStore.FormatId(a.Prefix, a.Next, a.Pad);
                string fmtB = ProjectTagStore.FormatId(b.Prefix, b.Next, b.Pad);

                _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line next: {fmtA}";
                _lblTagNext.Text  = $"Tag next: {fmtB}";
            }
            catch
            {
                _lblLineNext.Text = "Line next: —";
                _lblTagNext.Text  = "Tag next: —";
            }
        }

        public void Bind(object model, string sourceLabel)
        {
            if (model == null) model = new Placeholder();
            _currentModel = model;

            // If "home" tool model, hydrate tag numbers from store before cloning/display
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt)
            {
                try { lt.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
            }

            _snapshot = DeepClone(_currentModel);
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            _lblSource.Text = string.IsNullOrEmpty(sourceLabel) ? InferLabel(model) : sourceLabel;

            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt2) RefreshHomePreview(lt2.Prefix);
        }

        /// <summary>Let host call this prior to Apply to ensure editors commit pending values.</summary>
        public void CommitPendingEdits()
        {
            try { this.ValidateChildren(); } catch { }
        }

        // ---------- Store sync helpers ----------
        /// <summary>
        /// Pull a couple of store-driven values into the home settings model and refresh the grid.
        /// </summary>
        public void LoadFromStore(Document _)
        {
            var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;

            // Sync store-based values (global prefix, and per-system numbers if you keep the expandable group)
            try
            {
                var storePath = ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull();
                settings.Prefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath);
                settings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore();
            }
            catch { /* ignore UI sync errors */ }

            RefreshPropertyGrid(settings);
        }

        private void RefreshPropertyGrid(object model)
        {
            _grid.SelectedObject = model;
            _grid.Refresh();
        }

        // ---------- Internals ----------
        private void Rebind(object snap)
        {
            _currentModel = DeepClone(snap ?? new Placeholder());
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        private static object DeepClone(object instance)
        {
            if (instance == null) return null;
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(instance);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, instance.GetType());
        }

        private string InferLabel(object model) => model == null ? "—" : model.GetType().Name;

        private void SavePreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var sfd = new SaveFileDialog
            {
                Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json",
                FileName = _currentModel.GetType().Name + ".json"
            })
            {
                if (sfd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(_currentModel, Formatting.Indented);
                File.WriteAllText(sfd.FileName, json);
            }
        }

        private void LoadPreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var ofd = new OpenFileDialog { Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json" })
            {
                if (ofd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = File.ReadAllText(ofd.FileName);
                var loaded = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, _currentModel.GetType());
                if (loaded != null) Bind(loaded, _lblSource.Text);
            }
        }

        private sealed class Placeholder
        {
            [Category("Info")]
            [DisplayName("Tip")]
            public string Message => "Select an entity or run a tool to see its properties here.";
        }
    }
}
```
