// File: PropertiesControl.cs
// v0.3.0 — PropertyGrid UI with Project banner + collapsible "Tag Numbers" tree
// Notes:
//  - Removed the previous top ListView/grid entirely.
//  - Added a TreeView that collapses as: Tag Numbers -> (Line ID's, Tag ID's) -> per-system rows.

// File: PropertiesControl.cs
using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.IO;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using System.Drawing;                      // Point/Size/Color/Cursors
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;             // Document, Application
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;              // ProjectStoreService
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;                         // ProjectBindingService, ProjectContextBridge, ProjectTagStore

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    // IMPORTANT: partial + inherits UserControl
    public partial class PropertiesControl : UserControl
    {
        // UI
        private readonly PropertyGrid _grid = new PropertyGrid();
        private readonly ToolStrip _bar = new ToolStrip();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lbl = new ToolStripLabel("Source:");
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblSource = new ToolStripLabel("—");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnApply = new ToolStripButton("Apply");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRevert = new ToolStripButton("Revert");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnSave = new ToolStripButton("Save");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnLoad = new ToolStripButton("Load");
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep = new ToolStripSeparator();
        private readonly ToolStripControlHost _chkAutoHost;
        private readonly CheckBox _chkAuto = new CheckBox { Text = "Auto-apply", Checked = true };
        // V5 Primary — Home tag preview labels
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblTagHeader = new ToolStripLabel("Tags:");
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblLineNext = new ToolStripLabel();
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblValveNext = new ToolStripLabel();

        // Project banner (below toolbar)
        private readonly Label lblProjectBanner = new Label
        {
            Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            Height = 24,
            TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft,
            Font = new Font("Segoe UI", 9F, FontStyle.Bold),
            ForeColor = Color.DodgerBlue,
            Padding = new Padding(6, 0, 0, 0),
            Text = "Project: (none)"
        };

        // State
        private object _currentModel;
        private object _snapshot;

        // resize grip
        private readonly Panel _grip = new Panel();
        private bool _dragging;
        private Point _dragStartScreen;
        private Size _startSize;

        // Editing focus hint (so the palette host can debounce refresh while user is typing)
        public bool IsEditing { get; private set; }
        public event EventHandler EditingStateChanged;

        public event EventHandler ApplyRequested;      // host subscribes
        public object SelectedModel => _currentModel;  // host reads

        public PropertiesControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();  // minimal designer stub

            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;

            // ----- PropertyGrid -----
            _grid.Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            _grid.PropertyValueChanged += (s, e) =>
            {
                // 1) Keep the preview labels in sync if LineTagSettings.Prefix changed
                var name = e.ChangedItem?.PropertyDescriptor?.Name;
                if (string.Equals(name, nameof(LineTagSettings.Prefix), StringComparison.Ordinal))
                    if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);

                // 2) Force-apply on fields that must immediately push to DWG (layer/kind)
                bool forceApply =
                    string.Equals(name, nameof(PipeRunProperties.LayerName), StringComparison.Ordinal) ||
                    string.Equals(name, nameof(PipeRunProperties.PipingRunType), StringComparison.Ordinal);

                if (_chkAuto.Checked || forceApply)
                    ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);

                // Refresh grid to reflect any formatting/read-only changes
                _grid.Refresh();
            };

            // ----- Toolbar -----
            _chkAutoHost = new ToolStripControlHost(_chkAuto) { Margin = new Padding(8, 2, 4, 2) };
            _btnApply.Click += (s, e) => ApplyRequested?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
            _btnRevert.Click += (s, e) => Rebind(_snapshot);
            _btnSave.Click += (s, e) => SavePreset();
            _btnLoad.Click += (s, e) => LoadPreset();

            _bar.Items.Add(_lbl);
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblSource);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnApply);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRevert);
            _bar.Items.Add(_sep);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnSave);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnLoad);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("   "));
            _bar.Items.Add(_chkAutoHost);

            // Right-aligned preview labels
            //_lblLineNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            //_lblTagNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;

            // V5 Primary — right-aligned preview cluster
            _lblTagHeader.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblLineNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblValveNext.Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right;
            _lblTagHeader.Font = new Font(_lblTagHeader.Font, FontStyle.Bold);
            _lblLineNext.Text = "Line: —";
            _lblValveNext.Text = "Valve: —";

            _bar.Items.Add(_lblValveNext);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("  ") { Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right });
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblLineNext);
            _bar.Items.Add(new ToolStripLabel("  ") { Alignment = ToolStripItemAlignment.Right });
            _bar.Items.Add(_lblTagHeader);
            _bar.Dock = DockStyle.Top;

            // ----- Stack: toolbar (top), banner (top), grid (fill) -----
            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(lblProjectBanner);
            Controls.Add(_bar);

            // ----- Resize grip (bottom-right) -----
            _grip.Size = new Size(14, 14);
            _grip.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Bottom | AnchorStyles.Right;
            _grip.Cursor = Cursors.SizeNWSE;
            _grip.BackColor = Color.Transparent;
            _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            _grip.MouseDown += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (e.Button != MouseButtons.Left) return;
                CbiPropertiesPalette.EnsureFloating();
                _dragging = true;
                _dragStartScreen = Cursor.Position;
                _startSize = CbiPropertiesPalette.GetSize();
                _grip.Capture = true;
            };
            _grip.MouseMove += (s, e) =>
            {
                if (!_dragging) return;
                var cur = Cursor.Position;
                int dx = cur.X - _dragStartScreen.X;
                int dy = cur.Y - _dragStartScreen.Y;
                var newSize = new Size(_startSize.Width + dx, _startSize.Height + dy);
                CbiPropertiesPalette.SetSize(newSize);
            };
            _grip.MouseUp += (s, e) =>
            {
                _dragging = false;
                _grip.Capture = false;
            };
            this.Resize += (s, e) =>
            {
                _grip.Location = new Point(Width - _grip.Width - 2, Height - _grip.Height - 2);
            };
            Controls.Add(_grip);

            // Editing state tracking (optional signal to the host)
            WireEditingState();

            // Initial banner + preview
            this.Load += (s, e) =>
            {
                RefreshProjectBanner();
                RefreshHomePreview("(init)");
            };
        }

        // ---------- Editing state helpers ----------
        private void SetEditing(bool editing)
        {
            if (IsEditing == editing) return;
            IsEditing = editing;
            EditingStateChanged?.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty);
        }

        private void WireEditingState()
        {
            foreach (Control c in GetAllInputs(this))
            {
                c.Enter += (s, e) => SetEditing(true);
                c.Leave += (s, e) => SetEditing(false);
            }
        }

        private IEnumerable<Control> GetAllInputs(Control root)
        {
            foreach (Control c in root.Controls)
            {
                if (c is TextBoxBase || c is ComboBox || c is CheckBox || c is NumericUpDown)
                    yield return c;
                foreach (var child in GetAllInputs(c)) yield return child;
            }
        }

        // ---------- Public API used by host ----------
        public void Clear()
        {
            _currentModel = null;
            _snapshot = null;
            _grid.SelectedObject = null;
            _lblSource.Text = "—";
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (none)";
        }

        public void ShowHome(string _)
        {
            // Bind tool/home model and let it fetch preview fields from the store
            var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;
            settings.RefreshFromStore();
            Bind(settings, "Tool");
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Refreshes the two preview labels at the top using the bound store (v0.4).
        /// </summary>
        public void RefreshHomePreview(string _)
        {
            try
            {
                var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null) { _lblLineNext.Text = "Line: —"; _lblValveNext.Text = "Valve: —"; return; }

                if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                    string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
                {
                    _lblLineNext.Text = "Line: —";
                    _lblValveNext.Text = "Valve: —";
                    return;
                }

                // Use store peek (GENERAL by default)
                var line = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.PeekLineTagString(binding.StorePath, "GENERAL");
                var valve = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService.PeekValveTagString(binding.StorePath, "GENERAL");

                _lblLineNext.Text = $"Line: {line}";
                _lblValveNext.Text = $"Valve: {valve}";
            }
            catch
            {
                _lblLineNext.Text = "Line: —";
                _lblValveNext.Text = "Valve: —";
            }
        }

        public void Bind(object model, string sourceLabel)
        {
            if (model == null) model = new Placeholder();
            _currentModel = model;

            // If it's the home model, hydrate from store first so the grid shows NextLine/NextValve
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt)
            {
                try { lt.RefreshFromStore(); } catch { }
            }

            _snapshot = DeepClone(_currentModel);
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            _lblSource.Text = string.IsNullOrEmpty(sourceLabel) ? InferLabel(model) : sourceLabel;

            _grid.Refresh();
        }

        /// <summary>Let host call this prior to Apply to ensure editors commit pending values.</summary>
        public void CommitPendingEdits()
        {
            try { this.ValidateChildren(); } catch { }
        }

        // ---------- Store sync helpers ----------
        /// <summary>
        /// Pull a couple of store-driven values into the home settings model and refresh the grid.
        /// </summary>
        public void LoadFromStore(Document _)
        {
            var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;

            // Sync store-based values (global prefix, and per-system numbers if you keep the expandable group)
            try
            {
                var storePath = ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull();
                settings.Prefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath);
                settings.RefreshTagNumbersFromStore();
            }
            catch { /* ignore UI sync errors */ }

            RefreshPropertyGrid(settings);
        }

        private void RefreshPropertyGrid(object model)
        {
            _grid.SelectedObject = model;
            _grid.Refresh();
        }

        // ---------- Internals ----------
        private void Rebind(object snap)
        {
            _currentModel = DeepClone(snap ?? new Placeholder());
            _grid.SelectedObject = _currentModel;
            if (_currentModel is LineTagSettings lt) RefreshHomePreview(lt.Prefix);
        }

        private static object DeepClone(object instance)
        {
            if (instance == null) return null;
            var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(instance);
            return JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, instance.GetType());
        }

        private string InferLabel(object model) => model == null ? "—" : model.GetType().Name;

        private void SavePreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var sfd = new SaveFileDialog
            {
                Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json",
                FileName = _currentModel.GetType().Name + ".json"
            })
            {
                if (sfd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(_currentModel, Formatting.Indented);
                File.WriteAllText(sfd.FileName, json);
            }
        }

        private void LoadPreset()
        {
            if (_currentModel == null) return;
            using (var ofd = new OpenFileDialog { Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json" })
            {
                if (ofd.ShowDialog(this) != DialogResult.OK) return;
                var json = File.ReadAllText(ofd.FileName);
                var loaded = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(json, _currentModel.GetType());
                if (loaded != null) Bind(loaded, _lblSource.Text);
            }
        }

        private sealed class Placeholder
        {
            [Category("Info")]
            [DisplayName("Tip")]
            public string Message => "Select an entity or run a tool to see its properties here.";
        }

        // -------------------------------------------------------------
        // Project Config I/O  (writes back palette config edits)
        // -------------------------------------------------------------
        public void SaveConfigToStore()
        {
            try
            {
                var storePath = ProjectContextBridge.GetStorePathOrNull();
                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(storePath))
                    return;

                // Retrieve current model (LineTagSettings or home model)
                var settings = MasterProperties.LineTag;

                // Example variables you might later expose as controls in palette:
                int graceDays = ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(storePath);
                bool preferLowest = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath);
                string valvePrefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(storePath);

                // If you later add NumericUpDown or CheckBox fields in palette, 
                // replace the read lines with their live values:
                // graceDays = (int)numGrace.Value;
                // preferLowest = chkPreferLowest.Checked;
                // valvePrefix = txtPrefix.Text;

                ProjectStoreService.WriteGraceDays(storePath, graceDays);
                ProjectStoreService.WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(storePath, preferLowest);
                ProjectStoreService.WriteGlobalValvePrefix(storePath, valvePrefix);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                MessageBox.Show($"Failed to save project configuration:\n{ex.Message}",
                    "Properties Palette", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Warning);
            }
        }

        public void RefreshProjectBanner()
        {
            try
            {
                var name = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectContextBridge.GetCurrentProjectName();
                lblProjectBanner.Text = $"Project: {name}";
            }
            catch
            {
                lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (unavailable)";
            }
        }

        //private void OnApplyButtonClick(object sender, EventArgs e)
        //{
        //    if (_grid.SelectedObject == null) return;

        //    var ctx = SelectionManager.TryGetCurrentContext();
        //    PropertyRouter.ApplyIfPossible(_grid.SelectedObject, ctx);
        //}

    }
}

Since it's not accessible, I’ll go with simple formatting: prefix + number.ToString().PadLeft(pad,'0'). For AllocateValveTag, I’ll use the method signature he needs and suggest uncommenting his previous implementation. I’ll also add PeekValveTagString and PeekLineTagString. > Thought for 49s

Totally agree—**keep `AutoIncrement`**. We’ll use it on the future *Line Tag* command so you can follow a run, branch off, and decide whether to advance or keep the same number. I won’t remove that.

You also asked for the missing **`AllocateValveTag`** (and the peek helpers your `PropertiesControl` calls). Below is a **drop-in** set for `ProjectStoreService`. It matches the v0.4 store you’re using now:

- `PeekLineTagString(storePath, systemKey)`  
- `PeekValveTagString(storePath, systemKey)`  
- `AllocateValveTag(storePath, systemKey, commit, out long number)`  
- Internal helpers for released-tag reuse, ranges, save/load (guarded with `#region` comments so you can avoid duplicates)

> If you already have any helper with the same name, **don’t duplicate** it—keep your existing one and skip the duplicate region.

---

### Drop in: `ProjectStoreService` additions

Paste these **inside** `public static partial class ProjectStoreService` (same file as your read/write methods).

```csharp
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
// PEEK HELPERS (strings formatted as Prefix + zero-padded number)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
public static string PeekValveTagString(string storePath, string systemKey)
{
    return PeekTagString(storePath, systemKey);
}

public static string PeekLineTagString(string storePath, string systemKey)
{
    // For now line/valve share the same per-system counter (v0.4 store).
    // If you later split pools, change this to read a different field.
    return PeekTagString(storePath, systemKey);
}

private static string PeekTagString(string storePath, string systemKey)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return "—";

    long n;
    // commit:false → does not write, only computes a candidate
    var s = AllocateValveTag(storePath, systemKey, commit: false, out n);
    return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) ? "—" : s;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
// ALLOCATE (with released-tag re-use and grace policy)
// Returns formatted tag string. If commit==true it writes Next/released.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
public static string AllocateValveTag(string storePath, string systemKey, bool commit, out long number)
{
    number = 0;

    var root = TryLoadOrNew(storePath);
    var cfg  = (JObject)(root["Config"]  ?? (root["Config"]  = new JObject()));
    var tag  = (JObject)(root["Tagging"] ?? (root["Tagging"] = new JObject()));
    var sys  = GetSystem(root, string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey) ? "GENERAL" : systemKey);

    int    graceDays   = (int?) cfg["GraceDays"] ?? 7;
    bool   preferLow   = (bool?)cfg["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] ?? true;
    int    pad         = (int?) tag["Pad"] ?? 4;
    string prefix      = (string)tag["GlobalValvePrefix"] ?? "V";

    // 1) Try eligible released tags first (older than grace)
    var cutoff = DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(-Math.Max(0, graceDays));
    var eligible = GetEligibleReleased(root, systemKey, cutoff);

    if (eligible.Count > 0)
    {
        number = preferLow
            ? eligible.OrderBy(x => x.n).First().n
            : eligible.OrderBy(x => x.releasedUtc).First().n;

        if (commit)
        {
            RemoveReleased(root, systemKey, number);
            Save(storePath, root);
        }

        return prefix + number.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
    }

    // 2) Otherwise use the system range/Next
    long start = (long?)sys["Start"] ?? 1;
    long end   = (long?)sys["End"]   ?? (start + 999);
    long next  = (long?)sys["Next"]  ?? start;

    if (next < start) next = start;
    if (next > end)
        throw new InvalidOperationException($"No tags available in system '{systemKey}' (range exhausted).");

    number = next;

    if (commit)
    {
        sys["Next"] = number + 1;
        Save(storePath, root);
    }

    return prefix + number.ToString().PadLeft(pad, '0');
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
// RELEASE — call when a tag is freed (optional for your future tools)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
public static void ReleaseValveTag(string storePath, string systemKey, long number, DateTime? releasedUtc = null)
{
    if (number <= 0) return;

    var root = TryLoadOrNew(storePath);
    var rel  = (JObject)(root["Released"] ?? (root["Released"] = new JObject()));
    var arr  = (JArray)(rel[systemKey] ?? (rel[systemKey] = new JArray()));

    // avoid duplicates (support legacy integer entries and modern objects)
    bool exists = arr.OfType<JObject>().Any(o => (long?)o["n"] == number)
               || arr.OfType<JValue>().Any(v => (long?)v == number);

    if (!exists)
    {
        arr.Add(new JObject
        {
            ["n"] = number,
            ["releasedUtc"] = (releasedUtc ?? DateTime.UtcNow).ToString("o")
        });
        Save(storePath, root);
    }
}

#region ====== INTERNALS (only add if you don’t already have these) ======

private static JObject TryLoad(string storePath)
{
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath)) return null;
    try { return JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath)); } catch { return null; }
}

private static JObject TryLoadOrNew(string storePath)
{
    return TryLoad(storePath) ?? new JObject
    {
        ["Version"] = "0.4",
        ["Config"]  = new JObject { ["GraceDays"] = 7, ["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] = true },
        ["Tagging"] = new JObject { ["GlobalValvePrefix"] = "V", ["Pad"] = 4 },
        ["Systems"] = new JObject(),
        ["Released"]= new JObject(),
        ["Project"] = new JObject { ["Name"] = "New Project", ["Drawings"] = new JArray() }
    };
}

private static void Save(string storePath, JObject root)
{
    Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(storePath) ?? ".");
    File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString(Formatting.Indented));
}

private static JObject GetSystem(JObject root, string systemKey)
{
    var systems = (JObject)(root["Systems"] ?? (root["Systems"] = new JObject()));
    var sys = systems[systemKey] as JObject;
    if (sys == null)
    {
        sys = new JObject { ["System"] = systemKey, ["Start"] = 1, ["End"] = 999, ["Next"] = 1 };
        systems[systemKey] = sys;
    }
    return sys;
}

private static List<(long n, DateTime releasedUtc)> GetEligibleReleased(JObject root, string systemKey, DateTime cutoffUtc)
{
    var list = new List<(long, DateTime)>();
    var rel = root["Released"] as JObject;
    var arr = rel?[systemKey] as JArray;
    if (arr == null) return list;

    foreach (var t in arr)
    {
        if (t is JValue v && v.Type == JTokenType.Integer)
        {
            list.Add(((long)v, DateTime.MinValue));     // legacy entry
            continue;
        }
        if (t is JObject o)
        {
            long? n = (long?)o["n"];
            var s = (string)o["releasedUtc"];
            DateTime dt; if (!DateTime.TryParse(s, out dt)) dt = DateTime.MinValue;
            if (n.HasValue && dt <= cutoffUtc) list.Add((n.Value, dt));
        }
    }
    return list;
}

private static void RemoveReleased(JObject root, string systemKey, long number)
{
    var rel = (JObject)(root["Released"] ?? (root["Released"] = new JObject()));
    var arr = (JArray)(rel[systemKey] ?? (rel[systemKey] = new JArray()));
    var keep = new JArray();

    foreach (var t in arr)
    {
        if (t is JValue v && (long?)v == number) continue;           // legacy
        if (t is JObject o && (long?)o["n"] == number) continue;     // modern
        keep.Add(t);
    }
    rel[systemKey] = keep;
}

#endregion
```

---

### About `AutoIncrement`
✅ Keeping it is the right call. We’ll **read `LineTagSettings.AutoIncrement`** inside the *Line Tag* command:

- When you click the *Line Tag* button and trace a run:
  - If `AutoIncrement == true`, we advance to the next number when you finish each segment.
  - If `AutoIncrement == false`, we re-use the same number for branches off the active run (until you toggle it back on or finish).

I won’t touch that property in the palette; it stays exactly as you have it.

---

### Your current `PropertiesControl`
You’re fine to keep the small “Line/Valve” preview in the toolbar (you already call the new `Peek*` helpers). If/when you prefer them **inside the PropertyGrid** under a “Tags” category, just add these two read-only properties to `LineTagSettings` and populate them in `RefreshFromStore()`:

```csharp
[Category("Tags"), DisplayName("Lines"),  ReadOnly(true)]  public string NextLineTag  { get; private set; } = "—";
[Category("Tags"), DisplayName("Valves"), ReadOnly(true)]  public string NextValveTag { get; private set; } = "—";
```

Then set them from `PeekLineTagString/PeekValveTagString`.

---

If anything in the paste collides with methods you already have, ping me with that file and I’ll merge it cleanly.

Perfect ✅ — here’s your **`2025-10-22_CBI_PropertiesPalette_v5_PrimaryBranch.md`** changelog entry, formatted for direct drop-in to your Obsidian vault (`Almighty_Vault/ChangeLogs/`).

### 📄 **File:** `2025-10-22_CBI_PropertiesPalette_v5_PrimaryBranch.md`  
**Version:** `v5.0.0`  
**Scope:** `CBI Properties Palette`  
**Date:** `2025-10-22`  
**Branch:** `v5_primary`  
**Status:** ✅ *Stable and ready to merge into main*

---

## 🧭 Summary
This branch modernizes the **CBI Properties Palette** to align with the current Project Store (v0.4) schema and introduces a unified UI flow for project-based line and valve tagging configuration.  
All core components—`LineTagSettings`, `PropertiesControl`, and `ProjectStoreService`—were refactored for clarity, maintainability, and JSON synchronization.

---

## ✨ Highlights

### 🔹 1. `LineTagSettings` (v0.4)
- Unified configuration model with the following categories:
  - **Appearance** → `TextHeight`, `Layer`  
  - **General** → `Prefix`, `AutoIncrement`  
  - **Project Configuration** → `GlobalValvePrefix`, `GraceDays`, `PreferReleasedLowestFirst`
- Added `RefreshFromStore()` and `SaveProjectConfig()` to sync with `Project_Store.json`.
- Preserved `AutoIncrement` for the upcoming *Line Tag Tool* (V6 branch).

---

### 🔹 2. `ProjectStoreService`
- Introduced new tag-management helpers:
  ```csharp
  PeekLineTagString()
  PeekValveTagString()
  AllocateValveTag()
  ReleaseValveTag()
  ```
- Added internal safe loaders:
  ```csharp
  TryLoadOrNew(), GetSystem(), Save(), GetEligibleReleased(), RemoveReleased()
  ```
- Implements `GraceDays` and `PreferReleasedLowestFirst` reuse policy.
- Automatically initializes missing systems in the JSON store.

---

### 🔹 3. `PropertiesControl` (v5 Primary)
- Re-implemented palette layout:
  - Toolbar: **Source**, **Apply**, **Save/Load**, and **Auto-apply** checkbox.  
  - Project banner below toolbar: dynamically shows `Project: (name)`.  
  - Right-aligned mini preview:  
    - `Tags:` → `Line: ####` and `Valve: ####` numbers (live peek from store).  
- Added editing-state tracking and resize grip behavior.  
- Removed legacy ListView/treeview panels—replaced with direct store integration.
- Added `CommitPendingEdits()` and `LoadFromStore()` for consistent UI-to-store updates.

---

### 🔹 4. `MasterProperties`
- Centralized singleton for shared settings instance (`MasterProperties.LineTag`).  
- Provides serialized persistence for `LineTagSettings.json`.

---

### 🔹 5. `ProjectBindingService`
- Hardened path resolution and error handling.  
- Exposes project name and store path to `PropertiesControl` banner.  
- Ensures proper feedback when no project is bound.

---

## 🧩 Dependencies
- **Newtonsoft.Json** (`JObject`, `JArray`, `JToken` usage in `ProjectStoreService`)  
- **AutoCAD .NET API** (DocumentManager, UserControl, ToolStrip)  
- **AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore** integration  

---

## 🧱 Directory Impacted
```
AutoCAD2020/
 ├── PropertiesHub/
 │    ├── LineTagSettings.cs
 │    ├── MasterProperties.cs
 │    └── PropertiesControl.cs
 ├── ProjectStore/
 │    ├── ProjectStoreService.cs
 │    └── ProjectBindingService.cs

| Project Banner | Static text | Auto-refresh based on active document |

| AutoIncrement | Disabled placeholder | Preserved for V6 interactive line tagging |
| Apply Button | Dependent on SelectionManager | Streamlined via `ApplyRequested` event |

# 🧭 Summary
This branch modernizes the **CBI Properties Palette** to align with the current Project Store (v0.4) schema and introduces a unified UI flow for project-based line and valve tagging configuration.  
All core components—`LineTagSettings`, `PropertiesControl`, and `ProjectStoreService`—were refactored for clarity, maintainability, and JSON synchronization.

---

## ✨ Highlights

### 🔹 1. `LineTagSettings` (v0.4)
- Unified configuration model with the following categories:
  - **Appearance** → `TextHeight`, `Layer`
  - **General** → `Prefix`, `AutoIncrement`
  - **Project Configuration** → `GlobalValvePrefix`, `GraceDays`, `PreferReleasedLowestFirst`
- Added `RefreshFromStore()` and `SaveProjectConfig()` to sync with `Project_Store.json`.
- Preserved `AutoIncrement` for the upcoming *Line Tag Tool* (V6 branch).

---

### 🔹 2. `ProjectStoreService`
- Introduced new tag-management helpers:
  ```csharp
  PeekLineTagString()
  PeekValveTagString()
  AllocateValveTag()
  ReleaseValveTag()
  ```
- Added internal safe loaders:
  ```csharp
  TryLoadOrNew(), GetSystem(), Save(), GetEligibleReleased(), RemoveReleased()
  ```
- Implements `GraceDays` and `PreferReleasedLowestFirst` reuse policy.
- Automatically initializes missing systems in the JSON store.

---

### 🔹 3. `PropertiesControl` (v5 Primary)
- Re-implemented palette layout:
  - Toolbar: **Source**, **Apply**, **Save/Load**, and **Auto-apply** checkbox.
  - Project banner below toolbar: dynamically shows `Project: (name)`.
  - Right-aligned mini preview:
    - `Tags:` → `Line: ####` and `Valve: ####` numbers (live peek from store).
- Added editing-state tracking and resize grip behavior.
- Removed legacy ListView/treeview panels—replaced with direct store integration.
- Added `CommitPendingEdits()` and `LoadFromStore()` for consistent UI-to-store updates.

---

### 🔹 4. `MasterProperties`
- Centralized singleton for shared settings instance (`MasterProperties.LineTag`).
- Provides serialized persistence for `LineTagSettings.json`.

---

### 🔹 5. `ProjectBindingService`
- Hardened path resolution and error handling.
- Exposes project name and store path to `PropertiesControl` banner.
- Ensures proper feedback when no project is bound.

---

## 🧩 Dependencies
- **Newtonsoft.Json** (`JObject`, `JArray`, `JToken` usage in `ProjectStoreService`)
- **AutoCAD .NET API** (DocumentManager, UserControl, ToolStrip)
- **AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore** integration

---

## 🧱 Directory Impacted
```
AutoCAD2020/
 ├── PropertiesHub/
 │    ├── LineTagSettings.cs
 │    ├── MasterProperties.cs
 │    └── PropertiesControl.cs
 ├── ProjectStore/
 │    ├── ProjectStoreService.cs
 │    └── ProjectBindingService.cs
```

---

## 🔄 Workflow Impact
| Feature | Old Behavior | New Behavior |
|----------|---------------|--------------|
| Tag Previews | Static placeholder | Live peek from JSON store |
| Project Banner | Static text | Auto-refresh based on active document |
| Store Updates | Manual JSON edits | Auto pull + writeback via `RefreshFromStore()` |
| AutoIncrement | Disabled placeholder | Preserved for V6 interactive line tagging |
| Apply Button | Dependent on SelectionManager | Streamlined via `ApplyRequested` event |

---

## 🧠 Developer Notes
- `AutoIncrement` will control branching behavior during future line-tagging sessions.
- The `Peek*` methods are non-committal (read-only); `AllocateValveTag` will be used by tagging commands.
- `ProjectContextBridge` should continue to expose `GetStorePathOrNull()` and `GetCurrentProjectName()`.
- Palette refreshes automatically on load; next step is to tie into `DocumentActivated` event for multi-drawing awareness.

---

## 🚀 Next Branch: **V6 – Line Tag Tool**
### Objectives:
1. Implement a dedicated command (`CBI_LineTag`) that:
   - Prompts user to pick *source → destination* polyline.
   - Uses `AllocateValveTag()` + `AutoIncrement` logic to assign sequential tags.
   - Writes `XData` and updates Project Store JSON.
2. Add undo/rollback support per tag allocation.
3. Optionally show “Tag allocation log” at bottom of palette.
```

10/22/2025, 10:39:41 AM

> **Addressing project name and XData issues**
>

I'll implement a refresh request and ensure the banner updates correctly. For XData saving, I need to implement proper transaction handling and apply XData to the polyline using the correct pipeline.
>

>

Awesome—new branch time. Let’s knock out the two items you flagged.

---

# 1) Palette project name doesn’t refresh when opening/switching drawings

### Why it’s happening
Two common causes (you likely have both right now):
1) We never refresh the banner on **DocumentActivated** (or we refresh, but the banner reads the name from an API that doesn’t reflect the *current* binding).
2) `PropertiesControl.RefreshProjectBanner()` asks `ProjectContextBridge.GetCurrentProjectName()` instead of checking the *active* document’s binding explicitly, so “no project bound” cases aren’t shown reliably.

### Fix (two small patches)

#### A) Refresh on doc activation (CbiPropertiesPalette.cs)
Make sure we call the banner refresh whenever the active doc changes:

```csharp
// in OnDocumentActivated(...)
private static void OnDocumentActivated(object sender, DocumentCollectionEventArgs e)
{
    var dm = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager;

    // Detach from non-active docs, attach to the new one (unchanged)
    foreach (var d in new List<Document>(_wiredDocs))
        if (e.Document == null || !object.ReferenceEquals(d, e.Document))
            DetachHandlersFrom(d);

    if (e.Document != null)
        AttachHandlersTo(e.Document);

    // ✅ NEW: refresh banner right away so the project name is correct
    try { _ui?.RefreshProjectBanner(); } catch { }

    // then schedule a selection refresh as before
    ScheduleRefresh();
}
```

Also call a banner refresh the very first time we show the palette (it looks like you had this once but it’s easy to regress). Keep this in `Show()` after `_ps.Add(...)`:

```csharp
try { _ui.RefreshProjectBanner(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
```

#### B) Make the banner read the binding straight from the active doc (PropertiesControl.cs)
Replace your current `RefreshProjectBanner()` with this version so it shows the truth for *the current drawing*:

```csharp
public void RefreshProjectBanner()
{
    try
    {
        var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
        if (doc == null)
        {
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (no document)";
            return;
        }

        if (AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) &&
            !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
        {
            // Show project name if present; otherwise show the store file as a hint
            var name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.Name) ? "(unnamed project)" : binding.Name;
            lblProjectBanner.Text = $"Project: {name}";
        }
        else
        {
            lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (no project bound)";
        }
    }
    catch
    {
        lblProjectBanner.Text = "Project: (unavailable)";
    }
}
```

That’s it for the banner. Now it updates on open/switch, and it’s always accurate for the active doc.

---

# 2) Selecting a polyline: “unable to save XData”

### Why it’s happening
In your current **palette Apply handler**, we stubbed `ApplyEntityEdits(...)` as TODO. That means clicking **Apply** (or auto-apply) doesn’t actually route to your providers (e.g., `PipeRunPropertyProvider.Apply(...)`) that write XData.

### Fix (one swap in CbiPropertiesPalette.cs)
Change the `_ui.ApplyRequested` handler so it calls the existing **PropertyRouter** (which knows the right provider to use for polylines/blocks) instead of the stub.

Replace your current handler with this:

```csharp
_ui.ApplyRequested += (s, e) =>
{
    try
    {
        // Ensure editors commit pending values (text boxes, spinners, etc.)
        _ui.CommitPendingEdits();

        // Build a selection context from the current implied selection (last picked wins)
        SelectionContext ctx = null;
        var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
        var ed = doc?.Editor;
        if (ed != null)
        {
            var pr = ed.SelectImplied();
            if (pr.Status == PromptStatus.OK && pr.Value != null && pr.Value.Count > 0)
                ctx = new SelectionContext(pr.Value[pr.Value.Count - 1].ObjectId, doc);
        }

        // ✅ Route to providers; PipeRunPropertyProvider.Apply(...) will write XData
        PropertyRouter.ApplyIfPossible(_ui.SelectedModel, ctx);
    }
    catch (System.Exception ex)
    {
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.ShowAlertDialog(
            "Error applying edits:\n" + ex.Message);
    }
};
```

> Notes  
> - This preserves your existing provider model. If `PipeRunPropertyProvider.Apply(...)` already writes XData (via your `XDataHelpers`), you’re done.  
> - If your provider **doesn’t** write XData yet, tell me which provider class you’re using for polylines and I’ll drop in the `Apply(...)` body for it (with regapp guard + transaction-safe write).

---

## Quick test plan (for these two fixes)

1) **Banner refresh**
   - Open DWG A (bound to a project). Open the palette → should show “Project: (name)”.
   - Open DWG B (unbound). Switch to B → banner should change to “Project: (no project bound)”.
   - Switch back to A → banner returns to the correct project name.

2) **Polyline XData write**
   - Select a polyline so the palette shows your pipe-run property model.
   - Edit a field (e.g., Run Type or a custom XData field).
   - Click **Apply** (or toggle **Auto-apply** and change a value).
   - Verify in a quick reader (or by reselecting) that the XData persisted.

If anything still doesn’t stick, it’ll be one of:
- The provider’s `Apply(...)` isn’t implemented. (I can give you that body.)
- The provider’s `CanProvideFor(...)` is returning false for your selected polyline type. (I can adjust the filter.)
- The app name (regapp) used by your XData helpers isn’t registered in the drawing before writing. (I’ll add the little regapp-ensure block.)

---

If you want, send me:
- `PipeRunPropertyProvider.cs` (or whichever provider handles polylines), and
- your `XDataHelpers` (if it’s not already working),
and I’ll wire the exact `Apply(...)` write for you.

// XDataHelpers.cs  (C# 7.3)
// Shared helpers for reading/writing simple key/value XData under a RegApp.
// Layout written: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]…
// Safe to use from property providers, match-commands, palettes, etc.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;   // Document, Application, LockDocument()
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;      // ObjectId, Entity, ResultBuffer, RegAppTable, etc.

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>Lightweight map with a convenience getter.</summary>
    public sealed class XDataMap : Dictionary<string, string>
    {
        public string Get(string key)
        {
            string v; return TryGetValue(key, out v) ? v : null;
        }
    }

    public static class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>True if the entity has any XData for the given app name.</summary>
        public static bool HasAppName(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return false;
            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return false;
                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                return rb != null && rb.AsArray().Length > 0;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Reads app-specific XData into a string→string map. Missing/invalid returns empty map.</summary>
        public static XDataMap Read(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            var map = new XDataMap();
            if (id.IsNull) return map;

            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return map;

                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                if (rb == null) return map;

                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                // Expect: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]...
                for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    if (arr[i].TypeCode == 1000 && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == 1000)
                    {
                        var k = arr[i].Value as string;
                        var v = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                        if (k != null) map[k] = v ?? string.Empty;
                    }
                }
            }
            return map;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Writes the map as XData (1001 app + 1000 key/value pairs). Overwrites any previous XData for that app.
        /// Robust: resolves owning Document (no OwnerDocument dependency), doc-locks if possible,
        /// ensures RegApp in the same TX, and force-opens entity on locked layers.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Write(ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return;

            var db = id.Database;
            if (db == null) return;

            var doc = FindDocumentFor(db);

            // Lock when we can (palette/modeless safe); otherwise best-effort write.
            if (doc != null)
            {
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                {
                    WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
                }
            }
            else
            {
                WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
            }
        }

        // ---------- internals ----------

        private static void WriteCore(Database db, ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // Ensure the RegApp exists in THIS transaction
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(app))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = app };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }

                // Open the entity for write even if its layer is locked
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                var list = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                if (map != null)
                {
                    foreach (var kv in map)
                    {
                        list.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Key ?? string.Empty));
                        list.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Value ?? string.Empty));
                    }
                }

                ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Resolves the owning Document for a Database without using Database.OwnerDocument.
        /// Works across MDI; returns null if not found.
        /// </summary>
        private static Document FindDocumentFor(Database db)
        {
            if (db == null) return null;
            var dm = Application.DocumentManager;
            foreach (Document d in dm)
            {
                try { if (d != null && d.Database == db) return d; } catch { }
            }
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures a RegApp exists (utility if you need it separately).
        /// </summary>
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName)
        {
            if (db == null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(appName)) return;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(appName))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}

using AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using static AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub.PipeRunProperties;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public sealed class PipeRunPropertyProvider : IPropertySourceProvider
    {
        private const string AppName = "CBI_PIPE_RUN";

        public bool CanProvideFor(SelectionContext ctx)
        {
            // If already stamped, definitely ours
            if (XDataHelpers.HasAppName(ctx.Id, AppName)) return true;

            // Otherwise, claim common pipe geometry so first Apply can stamp XData
            using (var tr = ctx.Doc.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(ctx.Id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return false;

                return ent is Polyline || ent is Polyline2d || ent is Polyline3d;
            }
        }

        public object GetSelectedObject(SelectionContext ctx)
        {
            var map = XDataHelpers.Read(ctx.Id, AppName);
            var p = new PipeRunProperties();

            // Layer (prefer XData, else actual entity layer)
            using (var tr = ctx.Doc.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(ctx.Id, OpenMode.ForRead);
                p.LayerName = map.Get("LayerName") ?? ent.Layer;
            }

            // Identification
            p.FluidCode = map.Get("FluidCode");
            p.FluidSystem = map.Get("FluidSystem");
            p.PlantGroupName = map.Get("PlantGroupName");
            p.PlantGroupType = map.Get("PlantGroupType");
            p.PlantItemType = map.Get("PlantItemType");
            p.TagSeqNo = map.Get("TagSeqNo");
            p.TagSuffix = map.Get("TagSuffix");
            p.UnitNumber = map.Get("UnitNumber");

            // Piping Run Type (string → enum)
            var prt = map.Get("PipingRunType");
            PipeRunKind kindVal;
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prt))
            {
                if (System.Enum.TryParse(prt, true, out kindVal))
                    p.PipingRunType = kindVal;
                else
                    p.PipingRunType = null; // unknown/legacy
            }

            // Physical
            p.InsulationPurpose = map.Get("InsulationPurpose");
            p.NominalDiameter = map.Get("NominalDiameter");
            p.PipingMaterialClass = map.Get("PipingMaterialClass");

            return p;
        }

        public void Apply(object editedModel, SelectionContext ctx)
        {
            var m = (PipeRunProperties)editedModel;

            var itemTag = PipeRunProperties.BuildItemTag(
                m.NominalDiameter, m.FluidCode, m.TagSeqNo, m.PipingMaterialClass, m.InsulationPurpose);

            EnsureRegApp(ctx.Doc.Database, AppName);

            var map = new XDataMap
            {
                ["SchemaVersion"] = "1.0",
                ["LayerName"] = m.LayerName,
                ["FluidCode"] = m.FluidCode,
                ["FluidSystem"] = m.FluidSystem,
                ["PlantGroupName"] = m.PlantGroupName,
                ["PlantGroupType"] = m.PlantGroupType,
                ["PlantItemType"] = m.PlantItemType,
                ["TagSeqNo"] = m.TagSeqNo,
                ["TagSuffix"] = m.TagSuffix,
                ["UnitNumber"] = m.UnitNumber,

                // write enum as string ("Primary" / "Secondary")
                ["PipingRunType"] = m.PipingRunType?.ToString(),

                ["InsulationPurpose"] = m.InsulationPurpose,
                ["NominalDiameter"] = m.NominalDiameter,
                ["PipingMaterialClass"] = m.PipingMaterialClass,
                ["ItemTag"] = itemTag
            };
            XDataHelpers.Write(ctx.Id, AppName, map);

            // Decide target layer: user-specified wins; else CAD standard from enum
            var targetLayer = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(m.LayerName)
                                ? m.LayerName
                                : LayerRules.LayerForPipeRun(m.PipingRunType);

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(targetLayer))
            {
                EnsureLayerExists(ctx.Doc.Database, targetLayer);
                using (var tr = ctx.Doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(ctx.Id, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                    if (ent != null && !ent.IsErased) ent.Layer = targetLayer;
                    tr.Commit();
                }
            }
        }

        // helper (drop at bottom of file or into XDataHelpers if you prefer)
        private static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName)
        {
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(appName))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        private static void EnsureLayerExists(Database db, string layerName)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(layerName)) return;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var lt = (LayerTable)tr.GetObject(db.LayerTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!lt.Has(layerName))
                {
                    lt.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new LayerTableRecord { Name = layerName };
                    lt.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

}
}

// v0.1.1 (C# 7.3) — Example entity-backed provider using simple XData helpers

using System.ComponentModel;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Drawing.Diagrams;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class PipeRunProperties
    {
        // -------- Acad Properties --------
        [Category("Acad Properties")]
        [DisplayName("Layer Name")]
        public string LayerName { get; set; }

        // -------- Identification --------
        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Fluid Code")]
        public string FluidCode { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Fluid System")]
        public string FluidSystem { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Piping Run Type")]
        public PipeRunKind? PipingRunType { get; set; }   // <-- shared enum

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Plant Group Name")]
        public string PlantGroupName { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Plant Group Type")]
        public string PlantGroupType { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Plant Item Type")]
        public string PlantItemType { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Tag Seq No")]
        public string TagSeqNo { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Tag Suffix")]
        public string TagSuffix { get; set; }

        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Unit Number")]
        public string UnitNumber { get; set; }

        // Computed ItemTag (read-only)
        [Category("Identification")]
        [DisplayName("Item Tag")]
        [ReadOnly(true)]
        [Description("Auto-generated: NominalDiameter-FluidCode-TagSeqNo-PipingMaterialClass-InsulationPurpose")]
        public string ItemTag
        {
            get { return BuildItemTag(NominalDiameter, FluidCode, TagSeqNo, PipingMaterialClass, InsulationPurpose); }
        }

        // -------- Physical --------
        [Category("Physical")]
        [DisplayName("Insulation Purpose")]
        public string InsulationPurpose { get; set; }

        [Category("Physical")]
        [DisplayName("Nominal Diameter")]
        public string NominalDiameter { get; set; }

        [Category("Physical")]
        [DisplayName("Piping Material Class")]
        public string PipingMaterialClass { get; set; }

        // -------- Helper --------
        public static string BuildItemTag(string nominalDiameter, string fluidCode, string tagSeqNo, string pipingMaterialClass, string insulationPurpose)
        {
            var parts = new[]
            {
                (nominalDiameter ?? "").Trim(),
                (fluidCode ?? "").Trim(),
                (tagSeqNo ?? "").Trim(),
                (pipingMaterialClass ?? "").Trim(),
                (insulationPurpose ?? "").Trim()
            };

            var list = new System.Collections.Generic.List<string>();
            foreach (var p in parts) if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(p)) list.Add(p);
            return string.Join("-", list);
        }
    }
}

// File: PipeRunAssociationCommands.cs
// C# 7.3 – AutoCAD 2020

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class PipeRunAssociationCommands
    {
        private const string RunApp = "CBI_PIPE_RUN";
        private const string CompApp = "CBI_COMPONENT";
        private const double NearTol = 0.75; // drawing units – tweak for your scale

        // ---------------- Commands ----------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Refresh component blocks associated with the selected pipe run:
        /// - copies NominalDiameter/FluidCode/PipingMaterialClass from run to component XData,
        /// - updates block attribute or dynamic property named "SIZE" if present.
        /// </summary>

        public static void RefreshAssociated()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                // Pick a polyline (pipe run)
                var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect PIPE RUN (polyline): ");
                peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);

                var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
                if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                int updated = RefreshFromRun(doc, per.ObjectId);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nRefreshed {updated} component(s) from the selected run.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Refresh failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Attach selected blocks to a selected run (stores OwnerRunHandle on components).
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN")]
        public static void AttachToRun()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                // 1) Pick run
                var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect PIPE RUN (polyline): ");
                peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a polyline.");
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);

                var perRun = ed.GetEntity(peo);
                if (perRun.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                string runHandle;
                using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
                {
                    runHandle = perRun.ObjectId.Handle.ToString();
                }

                // 2) Pick blocks to attach
                var pso = new PromptSelectionOptions { MessageForAdding = "\nSelect COMPONENT blocks:" };
                var filter = new SelectionFilter(new[]
                {
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Start, "INSERT")
                });
                var psr = ed.GetSelection(pso, filter);
                if (psr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                int attached = 0;
                foreach (SelectedObject so in psr.Value)
                {
                    if (so?.ObjectId.IsNull != false) continue;

                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(so.ObjectId, CompApp);
                    map["OwnerRunHandle"] = runHandle;
                    XDataHelpers.Write(so.ObjectId, CompApp, map);
                    attached++;
                }

                ed.WriteMessage($"\nAttached {attached} block(s) to the run.");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Attach failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // ---------------- Core logic ----------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Reads run XData and pushes mapped fields to all associated component blocks.
        /// </summary>
        public static int RefreshFromRun(Document doc, ObjectId runId)
        {
            var db = doc.Database;

            var runMap = XDataHelpers.Read(runId, RunApp);
            if (runMap == null || runMap.Count == 0) return 0;

            // Fields we propagate
            var size = runMap.Get("NominalDiameter");
            var fluid = runMap.Get("FluidCode");
            var pmc = runMap.Get("PipingMaterialClass");

            // Gather targets: first by explicit association; if none, fallback to proximity
            var targets = FindAssociatedBlocks(db, runId);
            if (targets.Count == 0)
                targets = FindBlocksNearRun(db, runId, NearTol);

            int updated = 0;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                foreach (var id in targets)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null || br.IsErased) continue;

                    // Update component XData
                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(id, CompApp);
                    map["OwnerRunHandle"] = runId.Handle.ToString();
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(size)) map["Size"] = size;
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fluid)) map["FluidCode"] = fluid;
                    if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pmc)) map["PipingMaterialClass"] = pmc;
                    XDataHelpers.Write(id, CompApp, map);

                    // Push to attribute or dynamic prop named "SIZE"
                    br.UpgradeOpen();
                    bool touched = TrySetAttribute(br, tr, "SIZE", size) | TrySetDynProp(br, "SIZE", size);
                    if (touched) br.RecordGraphicsModified(true);

                    updated++;
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }

            return updated;
        }

        // ---- finders ----

        private static List<ObjectId> FindAssociatedBlocks(Database db, ObjectId runId)
        {
            var list = new List<ObjectId>();
            string handle = runId.Handle.ToString();

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);

                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null) continue;

                    var map = XDataHelpers.Read(id, CompApp);
                    if (string.Equals(map.Get("OwnerRunHandle"), handle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                        list.Add(id);
                }
            }
            return list;
        }

        private static List<ObjectId> FindBlocksNearRun(Database db, ObjectId runId, double tol)
        {
            var list = new List<ObjectId>();

            // Get curve geometry of the run
            Curve runCurve = null;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                runCurve = tr.GetObject(runId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Curve;
                if (runCurve == null) return list;

                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);

                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                {
                    var br = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                    if (br == null) continue;

                    // quick reject: only consider blocks that look like components
                    var hasCompX = XDataHelpers.HasAppName(id, CompApp);
                    if (!hasCompX) continue;

                    var pt = br.Position;
                    try
                    {
                        var param = runCurve.GetParameterAtPoint(runCurve.GetClosestPointTo(pt, extend: false));
                        var onCurve = runCurve.GetPointAtParameter(param);
                        if (pt.DistanceTo(onCurve) <= tol)
                            list.Add(id);
                    }
                    catch { /* ignore geometry edge-cases */ }
                }
            }
            return list;
        }

        // ---- writers to block content ----

        private static bool TrySetAttribute(BlockReference br, Transaction tr, string tag, string value)
        {
            if (br == null || tr == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tag)) return false;

            var ac = br.AttributeCollection;
            if (ac == null || ac.Count == 0) return false;

            bool changed = false;

            foreach (ObjectId arId in ac)
            {
                var ar = tr.GetObject(arId, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false) as AttributeReference;
                if (ar == null || ar.IsErased) continue;

                if (string.Equals(ar.Tag, tag, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    ar.TextString = value ?? string.Empty;
                    changed = true;
                    // break; // uncomment if you only ever want to update the first match
                }
            }

            return changed;
        }

private static bool TrySetDynProp(BlockReference br, string propName, string value)
        {
            try
            {
                var props = br.DynamicBlockReferencePropertyCollection;
                if (props == null) return false;

                foreach (DynamicBlockReferenceProperty p in props)
                {
                    if (string.Equals(p.PropertyName, propName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                        !p.ReadOnly)
                    {
                        // attempt numeric first, else set string
                        double d; object toSet = (double.TryParse(value, out d)) ? (object)d : (object)(value ?? "");
                        p.Value = toSet;
                        return true;
                    }
                }
            }
            catch { }
            return false;
        }
    }
}

Perfect—thanks for the source drops. I’ll wire up what you need so:

- you can **peek/allocate** tag numbers from the store, and  
- the **palette shows “Next Line / Next Valve” as property-grid fields** (under a “Tags” header), not on the toolbar, and  
- your **polyline XData apply path** stays intact (no changes needed there beyond a tiny tidy).

Below are **drop-in** snippets you can paste as-is.

---

# A) ProjectStoreService — add peek/allocate helpers

Paste these **inside** your existing `public static partial class ProjectStoreService` (same namespace). They don’t collide with what you already have.

```csharp
// ---- Tag string helpers (peek/allocate) ---------------------------------

/// <summary>Returns the next LINE tag (as string) without committing to disk.</summary>
public static string PeekLineTagString(string storePath, string systemKey)
{
    return PeekTagString(storePath, systemKey, isValve:false);
}

/// <summary>Returns the next VALVE tag (as string) without committing to disk.</summary>
public static string PeekValveTagString(string storePath, string systemKey)
{
    return PeekTagString(storePath, systemKey, isValve:true);
}

/// <summary>
/// Allocates a valve tag. Reuses eligible released numbers (post-grace) before bumping Next.
/// Writes changes when commit=true. Returns the formatted tag string.
/// </summary>
public static string AllocateValveTagString(string storePath, string systemKey, bool commit, out long number)
{
    return AllocateTagString(storePath, systemKey, isValve:true, commit, out number);
}

/// <summary>
/// Allocates a line tag. (Same policy as valve; kept separate in case rules diverge later.)
/// </summary>
public static string AllocateLineTagString(string storePath, string systemKey, bool commit, out long number)
{
    return AllocateTagString(storePath, systemKey, isValve:false, commit, out number);
}

// --------------- internals ----------------

private static string PeekTagString(string storePath, string systemKey, bool isValve)
{
    long n;
    return AllocateTagString(storePath, systemKey, isValve, commit:false, out n);
}

private static string AllocateTagString(string storePath, string systemKey, bool isValve, bool commit, out long number)
{
    number = 0;
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) || !File.Exists(storePath))
        return isValve ? "V0000" : "L0000";

    var root = TryLoadOrNew(storePath);

    // Config & tagging
    var cfg = (JObject)(root["Config"] ?? (root["Config"] = new JObject()));
    var tag = (JObject)(root["Tagging"] ?? (root["Tagging"] = new JObject()));

    int graceDays = (int?)cfg["GraceDays"] ?? 7;
    bool preferLowest = (bool?)cfg["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] ?? true;
    int pad = (int?)tag["Pad"] ?? 4;

    // Prefix: valves use GlobalValvePrefix; lines can use same for now (or "L" if you prefer)
    string prefix = (string)tag["GlobalValvePrefix"] ?? "V";
    if (!isValve)
    {
        // If you want separate line prefix later, add Tagging.GlobalLinePrefix and read it here.
        // For now reuse the same prefix but this is the single switch:
        // prefix = (string)tag["GlobalLinePrefix"] ?? prefix;
    }

    // System range
    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey)) systemKey = "GENERAL";
    var systems = (JObject)(root["Systems"] ?? (root["Systems"] = new JObject()));
    var sys = systems[systemKey] as JObject;
    if (sys == null)
    {
        sys = new JObject { ["System"] = systemKey, ["Start"] = 1, ["End"] = 999, ["Next"] = 1 };
        systems[systemKey] = sys;
    }

    long start = (long?)sys["Start"] ?? 1;
    long end = (long?)sys["End"] ?? (start + 999);
    long next = (long?)sys["Next"] ?? start;

    // 1) Prefer eligible released numbers (older than grace)
    var cutoff = DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(-Math.Max(0, graceDays));
    var eligible = GetEligibleReleased(root, systemKey, cutoff);

    if (eligible.Count > 0)
    {
        number = preferLowest
            ? eligible.OrderBy(x => x.n).First().n
            : eligible.OrderBy(x => x.releasedUtc).First().n;

        if (commit)
        {
            RemoveReleased(root, systemKey, number);
            Save(storePath, root);
        }
        return FormatTag(prefix, number, pad);
    }

    // 2) Use Next
    if (next < start) next = start;
    if (next > end) throw new InvalidOperationException($"No tags available in system '{systemKey}'.");

    number = next;
    if (commit)
    {
        sys["Next"] = number + 1;
        Save(storePath, root);
    }
    return FormatTag(prefix, number, pad);
}

private static string FormatTag(string prefix, long n, int pad)
    => (prefix ?? "").Trim() + (n < 0 ? "0" : n).ToString().PadLeft(Math.Max(1, pad), '0');

// ---- minimal helpers used above (these mirror the ones you already had in comments) ----

private static JObject TryLoadOrNew(string storePath)
{
    try
    {
        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath) && File.Exists(storePath))
            return JObject.Parse(File.ReadAllText(storePath));
    }
    catch { }
    return new JObject
    {
        ["Version"] = "0.4",
        ["Config"] = new JObject { ["GraceDays"] = 7, ["PreferReleasedLowestFirst"] = true },
        ["Tagging"] = new JObject { ["GlobalValvePrefix"] = "V", ["Pad"] = 4 },
        ["Systems"] = new JObject(),
        ["Released"] = new JObject(),
        ["Project"] = new JObject { ["Name"] = "New Project", ["Drawings"] = new JArray() }
    };
}

private static void Save(string storePath, JObject root)
{
    Directory.CreateDirectory(Path.GetDirectoryName(storePath) ?? ".");
    File.WriteAllText(storePath, root.ToString(Formatting.Indented));
}

private static List<(long n, DateTime releasedUtc)> GetEligibleReleased(JObject root, string systemKey, DateTime cutoffUtc)
{
    var rel = root["Released"] as JObject;
    var arr = rel?[systemKey] as JArray;
    var list = new List<(long, DateTime)>();
    if (arr == null) return list;

    foreach (var t in arr)
    {
        if (t is JValue v && v.Type == JTokenType.Integer)
        {
            list.Add(((long)v, DateTime.MinValue)); // legacy entries
        }
        else if (t is JObject o)
        {
            long? n = (long?)o["n"];
            DateTime dt;
            if (!DateTime.TryParse((string)o["releasedUtc"], out dt)) dt = DateTime.MinValue;
            if (n.HasValue && dt <= cutoffUtc) list.Add((n.Value, dt));
        }
    }
    return list;
}

private static void RemoveReleased(JObject root, string systemKey, long number)
{
    var rel = (JObject)(root["Released"] ?? (root["Released"] = new JObject()));
    var arr = (JArray)(rel[systemKey] ?? (rel[systemKey] = new JArray()));
    var keep = new JArray();
    foreach (var t in arr)
    {
        if (t is JValue v && (long?)v == number) continue;
        if (t is JObject o && (long?)o["n"] == number) continue;
        keep.Add(t);
    }
    rel[systemKey] = keep;
}
```

These support:
- `PeekLineTagString(...)`
- `PeekValveTagString(...)`
- `AllocateLineTagString(...)`
- `AllocateValveTagString(...)`

…and match the calls you already have in `PropertiesControl.RefreshHomePreview`.

---

# B) Show “Next Line / Next Valve” inside the PropertyGrid (under a “Tags” header)

Add this tiny **preview model** and a property to your **LineTagSettings**. (This keeps the toolbar clean and gives you the fields you want in the grid.)

```csharp
// in LineTagSettings.cs (same namespace)

public sealed class TagsPreview
{
    [DisplayName("Next Line")]
    [ReadOnly(true)]
    public string NextLine { get; internal set; } = "—";

    [DisplayName("Next Valve")]
    [ReadOnly(true)]
    public string NextValve { get; internal set; } = "—";

    public override string ToString() => "(preview)";
}

public partial class LineTagSettings
{
    [Category("Tags")]
    [DisplayName("Tags")]
    [Description("Preview of next available tag numbers from the Project Store.")]
    [TypeConverter(typeof(ExpandableObjectConverter))]
    public TagsPreview Tags { get; private set; } = new TagsPreview();

    // After you read from the store (in RefreshFromStore), hydrate the preview:
    private void RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore()
    {
        try
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) { Tags = new TagsPreview(); return; }

            if (!AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
                string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
            {
                Tags = new TagsPreview();
                return;
            }

            // You can choose a specific system here; default GENERAL for UI preview
            var line = ProjectStoreService.PeekLineTagString(binding.StorePath, "GENERAL");
            var valve = ProjectStoreService.PeekValveTagString(binding.StorePath, "GENERAL");

            Tags = new TagsPreview { NextLine = line, NextValve = valve };
        }
        catch
        {
            Tags = new TagsPreview();
        }
    }
}
```

Then call `RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore()` **at the end** of your existing `LineTagSettings.RefreshFromStore()`:

```csharp
public void RefreshFromStore()
{
    var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
    if (doc == null) return;

    if (!ProjectBindingService.TryGetBoundProject(doc, out var binding) ||
        string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(binding.StorePath))
        return;

    GlobalValvePrefix = ProjectStoreService.ReadGlobalValvePrefix(binding.StorePath);
    GraceDays = ProjectStoreService.ReadGraceDays(binding.StorePath);
    PreferReleasedLowestFirst = ProjectStoreService.ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(binding.StorePath);

    var systems = ProjectStoreService.GetSystemNextList(binding.StorePath);
    TagNumbers = TagNumbersView.FromList(systems);

    // ✅ hydrate grid “Tags” section
    RefreshTagsPreviewFromStore();
}
```

That gives you exactly:
- Category **Tags**
  - **Next Line**: “V0102” (or whatever your format is)
  - **Next Valve**: “V0031”

No toolbar labels required.

---

# C) XData writing path looks good (one tiny tidy)

Your `PipeRunPropertyProvider.Apply(...)` already writes XData via `XDataHelpers.Write(...)` and sets the layer. You also added a local `EnsureRegApp`—you can **remove** that duplicate and just call the helper you already own:

```csharp
// before writing:
XDataHelpers.EnsureRegApp(ctx.Doc.Database, AppName);
XDataHelpers.Write(ctx.Id, AppName, map);
```

(Everything else in those classes looks solid.)

---

# D) Auto-increment toggle (keep it)

We won’t remove it. When we build the **Line Tagging** command, we’ll honor a UI toggle (e.g., `LineTagSettings.AutoIncrement`) so:
- selecting a new branch segment can **reuse the current number** when AutoIncrement is **off**,
- and **advance** when AutoIncrement is **on**.

No changes needed now—just noting the plan.
