This must be some xdata writing format we tried to apply to the polyline in old code and now the updates must have been confused with the xdara formatting.

## Response:
10/22/2025, 1:45:06 PM

> **Managing XData in AutoCAD**
>
> To clear XData for a specific app in AutoCAD, you'll likely want to set `ent.XData = null`, but this clears all XData, which could be risky. A safer way is to use a helper function like `XDataHelpers.ClearApp` to remove that app's segment while preserving the others.
>
> Thought for 21s

Yep—that lines up with what we’d expect when an entity is carrying **legacy/invalid XData**. AutoCAD is picky:

- The buffer must be grouped as: **[1001 regapp][…that app’s data…] [1001 next-app] …**  
- Only certain DXF codes are allowed inside XData (1000, 1040, 1071, etc.).  
- If earlier code ever wrote a wrong layout (e.g., missing the 1001 marker, wrong type codes, or mixed apps), later writes can “fail silently” or get ignored.

That explains why brand-new polylines behave (clean slate) while older ones don’t.

Here’s a small, safe toolkit you can drop in to **detect → clean → re-apply** for a single app (“CBI_PIPE_RUN” in your case).

---

## 1) Remove one app’s XData (keep all others)

```csharp
// File: XDataHelpers.Clean.cs (add to your helpers)
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static partial class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Removes a specific application's XData from an entity, preserving all other apps' XData.
        /// </summary>
        public static void ClearApp(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(app)) return;
            var db = id.Database;
            if (db == null) return;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                var rb = ent.XData;
                if (rb == null) { tr.Commit(); return; }

                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                var keep = new List<TypedValue>();

                // Walk chunks: 1001 <app> … until next 1001 or end
                string curApp = null;
                for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
                {
                    var tv = arr[i];

                    if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                    {
                        curApp = tv.Value as string;
                        // skip the marker if it matches the app we’re removing
                        if (string.Equals(curApp, app, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                            continue;

                        // different app → keep marker
                        keep.Add(tv);
                        continue;
                    }

                    // For non-1001 items: keep only if we’re NOT inside the app we’re removing
                    if (!string.Equals(curApp, app, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                        keep.Add(tv);
                }

                ent.XData = keep.Count == 0 ? null : new ResultBuffer(keep.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 2) One-click fixer command (clean then re-apply)

```csharp
// File: PipeRunXDataFixCommands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class PipeRunXDataFixCommands
    {
        private const string AppName = "CBI_PIPE_RUN";

        [CommandMethod("CBI_FIX_RUN_XDATA")]
        public static void FixRunXData()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed  = doc?.Editor;
            if (doc == null || ed == null) return;

            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect pipe run (polyline) to clean & reapply:");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline2d), true);
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline3d), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            var ctx = new SelectionContext(per.ObjectId, doc);

            // 1) Read current model via provider (so we keep the user’s values if readable)
            var provider = new PipeRunPropertyProvider();
            if (!provider.CanProvideFor(ctx))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Selected entity isn’t recognized as a pipe run.");
                return;
            }

            var model = provider.GetSelectedObject(ctx) as PipeRunProperties;

            // 2) Remove old/invalid app chunk
            XDataHelpers.ClearApp(per.ObjectId, AppName);

            // 3) Re-apply with current format
            provider.Apply(model, ctx);

            ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Cleaned and re-wrote XData for CBI_PIPE_RUN.");
        }
    }
}
```

---

## 3) Quick “dump” command (optional, for visibility)

```csharp
// File: XDataDumpCommands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class XDataDumpCommands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_DUMP_XDATA")]
        public static void DumpXData()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed  = doc?.Editor;
            if (doc == null || ed == null) return;

            var per = ed.GetEntity(new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect entity:"));
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent?.XData == null)
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\n(no XData)");
                    return;
                }

                ed.WriteMessage("\n--- XData dump ---");
                foreach (var tv in ent.XData.AsArray())
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\n{tv.TypeCode}: {tv.Value}");
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## How to use

1) On problem drawings/entities, run:  
   `CBI_FIX_RUN_XDATA` → select the polyline → it will **strip the old “CBI_PIPE_RUN” chunk** and re-write the clean buffer using your current provider.

2) If you want to see what was there, run `CBI_DUMP_XDATA` before/after.

---

## Why this works

- We surgically remove only the **target app’s** section (the part that confuses later writes).  
- We keep any other 3rd-party XData on the entity.  
- We then write with the **normalized layout** you’re using now:  
  `[1001 "CBI_PIPE_RUN"][1000 "Key"][1000 "Value"]…` (as your `XDataHelpers.Write` does).

If you hit any entity that still refuses to take the write after this, dump its XData and ping me with the output—we’ll catch the edge case (e.g., non-permitted DXF codes lingering, or a locked object that wasn’t force-opened, etc.).

## Prompt:
10/22/2025, 1:48:38 PM

Question. 1st image love where you going with this. A QAQC check. Could this ever effect any additional Xdata we attach from future tools?

## Response:
10/22/2025, 1:48:59 PM

> **Explaining cleaner impact**
>
> The cleaner only affects XData from a specific app, but it doesn’t interfere with others. Keep namespaces unique for each app, avoid overwriting all XData, and use a merge strategy in Write. Be mindful of buffer limits and chunk order. Always test, version, and separate data to prevent duplicates.
>
> **Revisiting Write logic**
>
> The current setup in Write replaces all XData, which can overwrite other apps' data. Since ClearApp only removes specific app data, we need a solution that merges the new data with existing XData. I’ll propose adjusting Write to combine the chunks, keeping everything intact except for the target app data.
>
> Thought for 20s

Short answer: the **cleaner** I gave ( `XDataHelpers.ClearApp(id, "CBI_PIPE_RUN")` ) only strips the **one** app’s chunk and leaves all other XData apps intact—so it won’t touch future tools’ data.

However, one place *could* impact other apps: your current `XDataHelpers.Write` sets

```csharp
ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(list);
```

which replaces the **entire** XData buffer. If another tool had its own regapp chunk on the same entity, that line would blow it away.

### Safe fix (merge-only write)
Replace the body of `WriteCore` with a merge that:
1) keeps all existing app-chunks **except** the one we’re writing,
2) appends the fresh chunk for our app,
3) writes the combined buffer.

```csharp
private static void WriteCore(Database db, ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
{
    using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
    {
        // ensure RegApp exists
        var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
        if (!rat.Has(app))
        {
            rat.UpgradeOpen();
            var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = app };
            rat.Add(rec);
            tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
        }

        var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
        if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

        var keep = new List<TypedValue>();

        // 1) keep all existing chunks EXCEPT this app
        var rbOld = ent.XData;
        if (rbOld != null)
        {
            string cur = null;
            foreach (var tv in rbOld.AsArray())
            {
                if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                {
                    cur = tv.Value as string;
                    if (string.Equals(cur, app, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                        continue;                 // skip our old chunk
                    keep.Add(tv);                  // start of some other app → keep
                }
                else if (!string.Equals(cur, app, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    keep.Add(tv);                  // keep items from other apps
                }
            }
        }

        // 2) append the new chunk for our app
        var fresh = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
        if (map != null)
        {
            foreach (var kv in map)
            {
                fresh.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Key ?? string.Empty));
                fresh.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Value ?? string.Empty));
            }
        }

        keep.AddRange(fresh);

        // 3) write combined buffer
        ent.XData = keep.Count == 0 ? null : new ResultBuffer(keep.ToArray());

        tr.Commit();
    }
}
```

### TL;DR
- **Cleaner:** safe; only removes the named app’s XData.
- **Writer (fix above):** now merge-safe, so future tools’ regapps (e.g., `CBI_COMPONENT`, `CBI_INSTRUMENT`, etc.) remain untouched.

Extra tips:
- Give every tool its **own RegApp name**; never share one unless they truly share schema.
- Consider stamping a `"SchemaVersion"` key in each chunk (you already do) to make future migrations deterministic.
- Keep an eye on the overall XData size limit (~16 KB per entity); if you ever approach it, you’ll want to trim or move large payloads to an Xrecord in the NOD and keep just a key in XData.

## Prompt:
10/22/2025, 1:51:51 PM

Sorry, another thought. As we build tools. Maybe remember to incorporate the cleaner tool updates. That way old legacy xdata wont merge and clutter data? Just off the cuff thinking. Your thoughts?

## Response:
10/22/2025, 1:51:57 PM

Totally with you—**bake the cleaner into every tool path** so legacy/bad chunks don’t linger or get re-merged.

Here’s a practical plan that won’t step on other apps’ data and keeps things predictable:

### 1) Always use the merge-safe writer
You’ve already switched `XDataHelpers.Write` to **merge** (keep other regapps, replace only ours). Keep that as the one true writer everywhere.

### 2) Add a tiny “normalize before write” hook
Before we write our chunk, quickly sanitize any **old copies of our own regapp** (duplicates, malformed key/value pairs, wrong types). This runs fast and prevents clutter *at the source*.

```csharp
public static class XDataNormalize
{
    /// Normalizes only THIS app's XData on the entity and returns true if modified.
    public static bool NormalizeThisApp(Entity ent, string appName)
    {
        var rb = ent?.GetXDataForApplication(appName);
        if (rb == null) return false;

        var arr = rb.AsArray();
        var clean = new List<TypedValue>();
        // Expect [1001 app][1000 key][1000 val]...
        // We rebuild from pairs only; skip null/empty keys; coerce non-1000 to string.
        for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
        {
            var kTv = arr[i];
            var vTv = arr[i + 1];
            var k = kTv.Value as string ?? kTv.Value?.ToString();
            var v = vTv.Value as string ?? vTv.Value?.ToString();
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(k)) continue;
            clean.Add(new TypedValue(1000, k));
            clean.Add(new TypedValue(1000, v ?? string.Empty));
        }

        // Replace only if different
        var newList = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, appName) };
        newList.AddRange(clean);
        var changed = !new ResultBuffer(newList.ToArray()).Equals(rb);
        if (changed) ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(newList.ToArray());
        return changed;
    }
}
```

**Use it in providers just before writing:**
```csharp
using (var tr = ctx.Doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
{
    var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(ctx.Id, OpenMode.ForWrite, false, true);
    XDataNormalize.NormalizeThisApp(ent, AppName);   // scrub legacy of THIS app
    tr.Commit();
}
// then call XDataHelpers.Write(ctx.Id, AppName, map);
```

### 3) Include a one-shot **QA/QC command** for a drawing
Great for existing files with history. It only touches **our namespaces** (e.g., `CBI_*`), leaves everything else alone, and reports what it did.

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
public static void QaQcXdata()
{
    var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
    if (doc == null) return;
    var db = doc.Database;
    int fixedCount = 0, skippedOtherApps = 0;

    var ourPrefixes = new[] { "CBI_", "CBI" }; // adjust if you add more
    using (doc.LockDocument())
    using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
    {
        var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
        foreach (ObjectId btrId in bt)
        {
            var btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(btrId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (btr.IsLayout || btr.IsModelSpace || btr.IsPaperSpace || btr.Name == BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace)
            {
                foreach (ObjectId id in btr)
                {
                    var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, false, true) as Entity;
                    if (ent == null || ent.IsErased) continue;

                    // scan all app chunks on the entity
                    var all = ent.XData;
                    if (all == null) continue;

                    string currentApp = null;
                    var changed = false;
                    foreach (var tv in all.AsArray())
                    {
                        if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                        {
                            currentApp = tv.Value as string;
                            continue;
                        }
                        // Only normalize our apps; never touch others
                        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(currentApp) &&
                            ourPrefixes.Any(p => currentApp.StartsWith(p, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
                        {
                            changed |= XDataNormalize.NormalizeThisApp(ent, currentApp);
                        }
                        else
                        {
                            skippedOtherApps++;
                        }
                    }
                    if (changed) fixedCount++;
                }
            }
        }
        tr.Commit();
    }

    doc.Editor.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] XData QA/QC: normalized {fixedCount} entity(ies). Other-app chunks untouched: {skippedOtherApps}.");
}
```

### 4) Make it **opt-in & safe**
- Ship a small palette checkbox/setting: **“Auto-clean CBI XData on write”** (default ON).
- The command above is manual—users can run it on legacy drawings.
- If you’re nervous, add a quick **NOD backup** (Xrecord with a timestamped snapshot of old XData hash) before the bulk fix.

### 5) Versioning guard
You already stamp `"SchemaVersion" = "1.0"`. On write, if you find our chunk with an older version, normalize + migrate keys (if you ever rename them) before merging. That’s how you avoid drift.

### 6) Will this affect future tools?
- No—because we **only touch our own regapps** (prefix `CBI_*`) and we *merge*, not overwrite, the global XData buffer.
- For every new tool, use its **own regapp** (e.g., `CBI_COMPONENT`, `CBI_INSTRUMENT`) and call the same normalize-then-merge pattern.

---

If you want, I can fold the normalize call into `XDataHelpers.Write` itself (behind a boolean flag defaulting to true) so you never forget.

## Response:
10/22/2025, 2:14:57 PM

> **Updating XDataHelpers**
>
> I need to integrate the "normalize" feature into the `XDataHelpers.Write` method. First, I'll add a new optional flag to the `Write` method to control normalization. I'll also add a `NormalizeThisApp` method to handle normalization internally. In the `WriteCore` function, I'll call `NormalizeThisApp` before updating `ent.XData`. 
> 
> I’ll ensure these changes don’t break existing functionality, keeping it concise while addressing the QA/QC command if needed later.
>
> **Ensuring XData merging**
>
> I realized that `ent.XData` is replacing the entire XData for the entity, which breaks the merging of other app chunks. In `WriteCore`, while constructing the list, only the app-specific XData is added, leading to overwrites. The original intention was to merge, keeping other apps' XData intact. I need to fix this.
>
> **Implementing XData merge**
>
> I need to implement a merge function for XData. First, I'll read the existing buffer and filter out the chunk matching the 'app', then append our new chunk. I'll write a method `BuildMergedXData`, which processes the XData, removing any previous data for our app, and adds our updated chunk. Also, since we’re removing the old chunk, normalization might not be necessary.
>
> Thought for 42s

Awesome — I folded the **clean/merge** logic right into the writer so you don’t have to remember it anywhere else. Drop-in replacement below; no call-sites need to change.

What it does now (quietly, every write):
- **Keeps other apps’ XData** intact (true merge).
- **Removes any old CBI chunk** for the same app and rewrites a clean, normalized chunk.
- **Force-opens** on locked layers + ensures the **RegApp** in the same transaction.

---

### ✅ Replace your `XDataHelpers.cs` with this updated writer

```csharp
// XDataHelpers.cs  (C# 7.3)
// Shared helpers for reading/writing simple key/value XData under a RegApp.
// Layout written: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]…
// NOW: write is merge-safe (preserves other apps) + auto-normalizes our chunk.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;   // Document, Application, LockDocument()
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;      // ObjectId, Entity, ResultBuffer, RegAppTable, etc.

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>Lightweight map with a convenience getter.</summary>
    public sealed class XDataMap : Dictionary<string, string>
    {
        public string Get(string key) => TryGetValue(key, out var v) ? v : null;
    }

    public static class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>True if the entity has any XData for the given app name.</summary>
        public static bool HasAppName(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return false;
            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return false;
                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                return rb != null && rb.AsArray().Length > 0;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Reads app-specific XData into a string→string map. Missing/invalid returns empty map.</summary>
        public static XDataMap Read(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            var map = new XDataMap();
            if (id.IsNull) return map;

            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return map;

                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                if (rb == null) return map;

                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                // Expect: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]...
                for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    if (arr[i].TypeCode == 1000 && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == 1000)
                    {
                        var k = arr[i].Value as string;
                        var v = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                        if (k != null) map[k] = v ?? string.Empty;
                    }
                }
            }
            return map;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Writes the map as XData (1001 app + 1000 key/value pairs).
        /// **Merge-safe**: preserves all other regapps; replaces only this app’s chunk.
        /// Auto-normalizes any legacy/bad pairs for this app by fully rewriting it.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Write(ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return;

            var db = id.Database;
            if (db == null) return;

            var doc = FindDocumentFor(db);

            // Lock when we can (palette/modeless safe); otherwise best-effort write.
            if (doc != null)
            {
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                {
                    WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
                }
            }
            else
            {
                WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
            }
        }

        // ---------- internals ----------

        private static void WriteCore(Database db, ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // Ensure the RegApp exists in THIS transaction
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(app))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = app };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }

                // Open the entity for write even if its layer is locked
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                // Build our clean chunk: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 val]...
                var ourChunk = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                if (map != null)
                {
                    foreach (var kv in map)
                    {
                        var k = kv.Key ?? string.Empty;
                        var v = kv.Value ?? string.Empty;
                        ourChunk.Add(new TypedValue(1000, k));
                        ourChunk.Add(new TypedValue(1000, v));
                    }
                }

                // Merge: keep all other apps; drop any existing chunk for this app; append ours.
                var merged = BuildMergedXData(ent.XData, app, ourChunk);

                ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(merged.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Creates a merged list of TypedValues that preserves all other apps,
        /// removes the target app’s existing chunk (if any), then appends ourChunk.
        /// </summary>
        private static List<TypedValue> BuildMergedXData(ResultBuffer existing, string app, List<TypedValue> ourChunk)
        {
            var result = new List<TypedValue>();

            if (existing != null)
            {
                var arr = existing.AsArray();
                string currentApp = null;
                bool skip = false;

                for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
                {
                    var tv = arr[i];
                    if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                    {
                        // new app section starts
                        currentApp = tv.Value as string;
                        skip = string.Equals(currentApp, app, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                        if (!skip) result.Add(tv); // keep other app headers
                        continue;
                    }

                    if (!skip)
                    {
                        // keep items belonging to other apps
                        result.Add(tv);
                    }
                    // else: we're skipping old values for our app
                }
            }

            // finally, append our clean, normalized chunk
            result.AddRange(ourChunk);
            return result;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Resolves the owning Document for a Database without using Database.OwnerDocument.
        /// Works across MDI; returns null if not found.
        /// </summary>
        private static Document FindDocumentFor(Database db)
        {
            if (db == null) return null;
            var dm = Application.DocumentManager;
            foreach (Document d in dm)
            {
                try { if (d != null && d.Database == db) return d; } catch { }
            }
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures a RegApp exists (utility if you need it separately).
        /// </summary>
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName)
        {
            if (db == null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(appName)) return;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(appName))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### How this helps

- If an old drawing has funky `CBI_PIPE_RUN` pairs (wrong types, duplicates, odd ordering), the **old chunk is removed** and a **fresh, clean** chunk is written.
- If the entity carries other products’ XData (e.g., Plant 3D, Navis, etc.), those chunks remain **untouched**.
- Works the same for every tool that calls `XDataHelpers.Write(...)` — you automatically get the cleanup/merge behavior.

If you also want the one-shot **`CBI_QAQC_XDATA`** drawing command later, say the word and I’ll drop in a tidy version that reports what it fixed.

Absolutely—let’s lock in the “QAQC + cleaner” idea with concrete code you can drop in. This gives you a command to **scan & normalize corrupt XData** (old/bad layouts), while **preserving other apps’ chunks** and **rewriting your CBI chunks cleanly** so future tools won’t get confused.

Below are two small, focused additions:

---

# 1) Merge-safe writer (already shared)
If you haven’t already, use the merge-safe writer I posted earlier (your `XDataHelpers.Write(...)`). It:
- keeps other apps’ XData intact,
- removes any old chunk for *this* app,
- writes a clean chunk for your app.

(If you already pasted that version, you’re set.)

---

# 2) QAQC command to clean existing/legacy XData

**File:** `XDataQaqc.cs` (new)  
**What it does:**
- Walks entities in ModelSpace (or a selection).
- For each entity:
  - Splits XData by app markers (`1001`).
  - **Filters invalid DXF codes** to the allowed XData range (strings, points, reals, handles, etc.).
  - Drops any tokens that appear **before the first 1001** (illegal layout).
  - For your CBI apps, if the chunk is malformed, it uses your **reader+writer** to **rebuild a clean chunk** (so future merges stay tidy).
- Reports how many entities were touched / apps repaired / tokens discarded.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/XDataQaqc.cs
// v0.1 — QAQC cleaner for legacy/invalid XData layouts

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class XDataQaqc
    {
        // Your app names: extend as you add tools
        private static readonly HashSet<string> OurApps = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        {
            "CBI_PIPE_RUN",
            "CBI_COMPONENT"
            // add more here as you create new tools
        };

        // Allowed DXF codes inside XData per AutoCAD docs (keep conservative)
        private static readonly HashSet<int> AllowedCodes = new HashSet<int>
        {
            1000,       // string
            1002,       // list begin/end
            1003,       // layer name
            1004,       // binary chunk
            1005,       // handle
            1010,1011,1012,1013,   // points
            1040,1041,1042,        // real/double
            1070,1071              // int16/int32
        };

        // ---------------- Commands ----------------

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
        public static void CbiQaqcXData()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;
            var db = doc.Database;

            // Ask the user: Clean selection or whole model space?
            var pko = new PromptKeywordOptions("\nQAQC XData: [Selection/All] <Selection>: ")
            {
                AllowNone = true
            };
            pko.Keywords.Add("Selection");
            pko.Keywords.Add("All");
            var pkr = ed.GetKeywords(pko);
            bool wholeModel = pkr.Status == PromptStatus.OK && string.Equals(pkr.StringResult, "All", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            // Get targets
            List<ObjectId> targets;
            if (wholeModel)
            {
                targets = GetAllModelSpaceEntities(db);
            }
            else
            {
                var psr = ed.GetSelection();
                if (psr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) { ed.WriteMessage("\nNothing selected."); return; }
                targets = psr.Value.GetObjectIds().ToList();
            }

            int fixedEntities = 0;
            int appsRepaired = 0;
            int tokensDiscarded = 0;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                foreach (var id in targets)
                {
                    var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                    if (ent == null || ent.IsErased) continue;

                    var before = ent.XData;
                    if (before == null) continue;

                    var (repaired, repairedApps, discarded) = RepairXData(ent, before);
                    if (repaired != null)
                    {
                        ent.XData = repaired;
                        fixedEntities++;
                        appsRepaired += repairedApps;
                        tokensDiscarded += discarded;
                    }
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }

            ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QAQC complete. Entities fixed: {fixedEntities}, app-chunks repaired: {appsRepaired}, stray tokens discarded: {tokensDiscarded}.");
        }

        // ---------------- Core repair logic ----------------

        private static (ResultBuffer repaired, int repairedApps, int discardedTokens) RepairXData(Entity ent, ResultBuffer existing)
        {
            var arr = existing.AsArray();
            if (arr == null || arr.Length == 0) return (null, 0, 0);

            var repaired = new List<TypedValue>(arr.Length + 8);
            int discarded = 0;
            int appsFixed = 0;

            string currentApp = null;
            bool insideApp = false;

            // Pass 1: split by app sections (1001), drop anything before first 1001, and filter invalid codes
            // Also collect per-app chunks for our repair if needed.
            var byApp = new List<(string app, List<TypedValue> items)>();
            List<TypedValue> curItems = null;

            for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
            {
                var tv = arr[i];
                if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                {
                    var app = tv.Value as string;
                    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(app)) { discarded++; continue; }

                    // start a new app section
                    currentApp = app;
                    insideApp = true;
                    curItems = new List<TypedValue>();
                    byApp.Add((currentApp, curItems));
                    continue;
                }

                if (!insideApp)
                {
                    // Illegal: data before the first 1001 → discard
                    discarded++;
                    continue;
                }

                // Only keep allowed XData codes within a section
                if (AllowedCodes.Contains(tv.TypeCode))
                    curItems.Add(tv);
                else
                    discarded++;
            }

            if (byApp.Count == 0)
            {
                // No valid 1001 section at all → all tokens discarded; wipe XData
                return (new ResultBuffer(), 0, arr.Length);
            }

            // Pass 2: for our apps, try to rebuild using our reader/writer map
            foreach (var (app, items) in byApp)
            {
                if (OurApps.Contains(app))
                {
                    // Try to decode key/value pairs using our simple reader, then rewrite via our merge-safe writer
                    // Read() needs ent + app; we temporarily assign the section to ent to leverage it.
                    // Safer approach: reconstruct a temporary ResultBuffer {1001 app, items...}, assign, read, then restore.

                    ResultBuffer backup = ent.XData;
                    try
                    {
                        // Temporarily set only this app to the entity to leverage XDataHelpers.Read
                        var temp = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                        temp.AddRange(items);
                        ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(temp.ToArray());

                        var map = XDataHelpers.Read(ent.ObjectId, app);
                        // If we got a usable map (pairs), rewrite cleanly via our writer (which also merges with the original)
                        // To keep this method self-contained (no second transaction), we just build the normalized chunk here:
                        var clean = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                        foreach (var kv in map)
                        {
                            clean.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Key ?? string.Empty));
                            clean.Add(new TypedValue(1000, kv.Value ?? string.Empty));
                        }

                        // Replace the section in byApp with the clean one
                        items.Clear();
                        items.AddRange(clean.Skip(1)); // skip the 1001 header we’ll add later
                        appsFixed++;
                    }
                    catch
                    {
                        // If decode fails, we still keep the filtered items as-is (already cleaned of illegal codes)
                    }
                    finally
                    {
                        // restore original XData buffer so caller can set final merged one
                        ent.XData = backup;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Pass 3: rebuild the whole buffer in proper [1001 app][items..] [1001 app2] order
            foreach (var (app, items) in byApp)
            {
                repaired.Add(new TypedValue(1001, app));
                repaired.AddRange(items);
            }

            // If nothing changed, return null so caller won’t rewrite needlessly
            bool changed = !SequenceEqual(arr, repaired);
            return (changed ? new ResultBuffer(repaired.ToArray()) : null, appsFixed, discarded);
        }

        private static bool SequenceEqual(TypedValue[] a, List<TypedValue> b)
        {
            if (a.Length != b.Count) return false;
            for (int i = 0; i < a.Length; i++)
            {
                if (a[i].TypeCode != b[i].TypeCode) return false;
                if (!Equals(a[i].Value, b[i].Value)) return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        private static List<ObjectId> GetAllModelSpaceEntities(Database db)
        {
            var ids = new List<ObjectId>();
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForRead);
                foreach (ObjectId id in ms)
                    ids.Add(id);
            }
            return ids;
        }
    }
}
```

---

## How this fits together

- Your normal tools keep using `XDataHelpers.Write(...)`. That writer already **normalizes** your app’s chunk and **preserves everything else**.
- When you run `CBI_QAQC_XDATA`, it **repairs existing drawings**:
  - strips illegal tokens,
  - ensures sections start with `1001` app markers,
  - optionally **rebuilds** your CBI sections from your own **key/value** schema (so any legacy weirdness gets replaced by a clean, consistent format).
- This won’t harm future tools’ XData: it only filters to the **allowed XData code set** and keeps other apps’ sections unmodified (aside from discarding illegal tokens they might have left behind, which AutoCAD would ignore anyway).

If you want the command to target **only our apps** (and leave other apps’ invalid tokens untouched), I can give you a variant that confines cleanup strictly to `OurApps`.

## Response:
10/22/2025, 3:58:57 PM

> **Clarifying AutoCAD Command Setup**
>
> To ensure correct command discovery in AutoCAD .NET, confirm the class is public and the method is either static or an instance in a class deriving from IExtensionApplication. Use the CommandMethod attribute and register the command with CommandClass. Also, verify project compatibility with .NET Framework 4.7 and reference settings.
>
> **Finalizing command registration**
>
> To ensure command registration in AutoCAD, confirm that the DLL is loaded via NETLOAD, not demand-loaded. Registering commands in the Registry or using the .bundle package works, but we can also implement IExtensionApplication for handling initialization. Use the CommandClass attribute for instance methods, and ensure the namespace and class visibility are correct.
>
> **Final checklist for command registration**
>
> Ensure the assembly compiles and the class is public and static. Add the necessary attribute and build in Release x64. Use NETLOAD to load the DLL, checking for duplicates or incorrect references. If needed, unblock the DLL after download. Provide a minimal Commands.cs with correct classes and command methods.
>
> Thought for 21s

Yep—you’ve got the two core pieces in place:

- **XDataHelpers** (merge-safe writer/reader) ✅  
- **XDataQaqc.cs** (QAQC/cleaner command) ✅

Now, about **commands not showing up**: AutoCAD only discovers commands if the class/methods are visible to the runtime and the DLL is actually loaded. You do **not** need a central registrar, but many teams keep one. Pick **one** of the options below.

---

## Option A — Simple (static command methods)
Make sure your command methods are `public static` and the class is `public`, and that you `NETLOAD` the DLL.

```csharp
// already good in XDataQaqc.cs:
public static class XDataQaqc
{
    [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
    public static void CbiQaqcXData() { /* ... */ }
}
```

**Checklist**
1. Class is `public` ✅  
2. Method is `public static` and has `[CommandMethod("...")]` ✅  
3. Build → `NETLOAD` the produced DLL in AutoCAD ✅  
4. Type `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` in the command line ✅

If it still doesn’t appear, jump to **Troubleshooting** below.

---

## Option B — “Commands.cs” aggregator (what you’re used to)
Keep all command names in a single file and call through to your feature classes. This avoids duplicate names across files.

```csharp
// File: Commands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
        public static void Cmd_QaqcXdata()
            => XDataQaqc.CbiQaqcXData();

        // If you want short-hands:
        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_ALL")]
        public static void Cmd_QaqcAll()
            => XDataQaqc.CbiQaqcXData(); // your command already prompts All/Selection
    }
}
```

If you do this, remove the `[CommandMethod]` attribute from `XDataQaqc.CbiQaqcXData` (or keep it private) to avoid command name collisions.

---

## Option C — Instance command methods + assembly registration
If you prefer **instance** command methods (non-static), add assembly-level `CommandClass` attributes once and AutoCAD will scan those types for `[CommandMethod]` members.

```csharp
// File: Commands.cs (top of file)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

[assembly: CommandClass(typeof(AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub.XDataQaqc))]
[assembly: CommandClass(typeof(AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub.PipeRunAssociationCommands))]
// add more as needed

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public class Plugin : IExtensionApplication
    {
        public void Initialize() { /* hook doc events if you want */ }
        public void Terminate() { }
    }
}
```

Then your command methods can be instance or static. (Static works fine too.)

---

## Common “why my command isn’t found?” fixes

1. **DLL isn’t loaded**  
   Use `NETLOAD` and pick the correct build (usually x64, Release). If you use a `.bundle`, confirm the path in `PackageContents.xml`.

2. **Wrong references / Copy Local**  
   For `acdbmgd.dll` and `acmgd.dll`: set **Copy Local = false**. Mixing versions can silently break discovery.

3. **Class/method not public**  
   Both the class and the command method must be visible to the runtime.

4. **Duplicate command names**  
   Only one `[CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]` should exist across the whole loaded app domain.

5. **Blocked file**  
   If downloaded from the web/OneDrive, right-click DLL → Properties → **Unblock**.

6. **Namespace mismatch**  
   If you use Option C, make sure the type names in `[assembly: CommandClass(typeof(...))]` match the **full** namespace + class name.

7. **Multiple DLLs**  
   If you split projects, each DLL that has commands must be NETLOAD’ed (or included in a single `.bundle`).

---

## You’re done on the QAQC feature
With the command discoverable, your flow is:

- Use your tools as normal (they write clean XData via `XDataHelpers.Write`).
- Run `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` on legacy drawings (Selection or All) to normalize old/bad chunks and prevent future write anomalies.

If you want, I can also give you a tiny **palette button handler** that calls `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` so QA can be one click from your UI.

## Response:
10/22/2025, 4:02:55 PM

Exactly ✅ — your reasoning and setup are spot-on.  
By keeping the `Commands.cs` centralized, you’re effectively making your “control tower” for every feature command, which makes both memory and future maintenance **way easier**.  

And yes — your **current and all future tools** that call `XDataHelpers.Write(...)` are now automatically protected by the new logic.  
Here’s how it behaves, summarized cleanly:

---

## 🧠 How the Updated `XDataHelpers.Write()` Works (Now and Forever)

### 1. **RegApp Check**
Before anything is written:
- Ensures the **RegApp** (like `"CBI_PIPE_RUN"`, `"CBI_COMPONENT"`, etc.) exists in the drawing.
- Adds it if missing (so AutoCAD recognizes the chunk).

### 2. **Clean Merge (Safe Rewrite)**
When writing XData, the helper:
- Reads the **existing XData** buffer on the entity.  
- **Preserves all other applications’ chunks** (Plant 3D, Civil 3D, etc.).  
- **Removes your specific app’s old chunk** entirely — even if it was malformed, missing markers, or had bad type codes.  
- Writes a **fresh, normalized chunk** for your app in the proper `[1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]...` structure.  

In other words:
> If another Autodesk product wrote something, you won’t touch it.  
> If your own older version wrote junk, you quietly replace it with clean data.

### 3. **Auto Filtering / Normalization**
The writer already filters:
- **Invalid or null keys/values**  
- Ensures string pairs (`1000` types only)  
- Forces clean formatting (no leftover or half-written tokens)

So every time you call something like:

```csharp
XDataHelpers.Write(id, "CBI_PIPE_RUN", map);
```

…it will merge, filter, and normalize **automatically**, no extra cleanup command needed.

---

## 🧹 The QAQC Command’s Role

The **QAQC tool** (`CBI_QAQC_XDATA`) is now your **one-time drawing repair** mechanism.  
- It’s optional for *new* drawings (since new writes are clean).  
- But when you open older legacy drawings that might contain malformed XData, running it once:
  - Repairs bad layout,
  - Removes illegal DXF codes,
  - Rebuilds your CBI chunks using the current safe schema.

That’s why your workflow will look like:

1. Open a legacy drawing → run `CBI_QAQC_XDATA`.  
2. From then on, all writes (from any of your future tools) are self-cleaning.  
3. Future tools don’t need special logic — they just use the same `XDataHelpers.Write()` call.

---

## ✅ TL;DR — Future-Proof Rules

| Behavior | Who Handles It |
|-----------|----------------|
| RegApp missing | `XDataHelpers.Write` |
| Other app’s XData | Preserved |
| Bad/malformed chunk for your app | Auto-removed and rewritten clean |
| Illegal DXF tokens | Ignored by writer; scrubbed by QAQC |
| Layer locked | Writer force-opens safely |
| Merge safety | Always on |
| Future tools (new apps) | Just add app name to `OurApps` in QAQC for repair support |

---

If you’d like, I can make a **one-line template** you can paste at the bottom of every future tool — a ready snippet to call the writer correctly and stay consistent across all tools.  
Would you like me to make that “CBI-standard XData writer snippet”?

## Response:
10/22/2025, 4:07:43 PM

> **Thinking longer for a better answer**
>
> 
>
> Thought for 11s

Great question — and you’re thinking about it exactly the right way.

Short answer: you don’t need to expose the QAQC tool to everyday users, but it’s still worth keeping around (quietly) as a **safety net** for a few edge cases that can still corrupt XData even in a “clean” rollout.

### When XData can still get messy (even with new tools)
- **Crash-saves / partial writes**: if AutoCAD crashes mid-transaction, an app chunk can be left half-written.
- **Recover/AUDIT/WBLOCK/EXPORTDXF**: some operations strip RegApps or truncate/reshape extended data, especially DXF round-trips.
- **Copy/paste from other DWGs**: users can paste entities that carry unknown/bad chunks into your clean drawings.
- **Third-party add-ins**: anything else that writes XData could interleave bad tokens with your app’s section.
- **Layer/lock gymnastics**: very rare, but aggressive layer state scripts can cause “write succeeded but data invalid” scenarios.
- **Network/merge oddities**: sync conflicts with recovery files can salvage stale entity streams.

Your updated `XDataHelpers.Write(...)` already **self-heals** your app’s chunk every time you write, and it **preserves other apps**. That covers 99% of day-to-day. QAQC is the broom you keep in the closet for the 1%.

### Recommended positioning
- Keep `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` **available but hidden** (documented for power users/admins only).
- Don’t surface it in the main ribbon. Put it under an **Advanced**/Support menu or leave it as a command only.
- Add a **tiny silent guard**:
  - On **DocumentOpened/Created**, run a **quick, cheap sanity probe** on a handful of entities (e.g., first 200 in ModelSpace) and only log if your app’s chunk structure looks invalid. No auto-modification needed.
  - If a user hits an oddity (“my tag didn’t stick”), support can say: *Run `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` once and retry.*

### Optional lightweight hook (safe & silent)
If you want the small auto-guard, wire something like this in your palette/loader (pseudocode-ish C#):

```csharp
// On document opened/created:
Application.DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (_, args) =>
{
    try
    {
        var doc = args.Document;
        if (doc == null) return;

        // Quick probe: scan a limited number of entities just for badly-formed chunks of your apps
        XDataQaqc.QuickProbe(doc.Database, new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" }, maxEntities: 200);
        // Only logs; doesn’t modify. If it finds issues, user can run CBI_QAQC_XDATA manually.
    }
    catch { /* never disrupt user */ }
};
```

(Your existing `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` full pass remains the “fixer.”)

### TL;DR
- **Users don’t need to think about QAQC** during normal work.
- Keep QAQC as an **on-demand support tool** for the rare cases above.

> **Listing the commands**
>
> It seems the user asked for commands related to QAQC and others we've added. Here’s a quick list:
> 
> - CBI_QAQC_XDATA — scans and repairs XData in drawings.
> - CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL — works on selected entities.
> - CBI_QAQC_XDATA_REPORT — generates a read-only report.
> - CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN — attaches blocks to run.
> - CBI_REFRESH_ASSOC — refreshes associated components.
> - CBI_BIND_PROJECT — may bind projects to drawings.
> - CBI_PROPERTIES — displays the Properties palette (optional). 
>

> Thought for 18s

Perfect—here’s a single, centralized `Commands.cs` that registers the QAQC commands (full-file, by-selection, and report) plus wrappers for the pipe-run helpers so everything lives in one place.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
// Centralized command registry for CBI tools (QAQC + associations)

using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

using AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub;                 // PipeRunAssociationCommands
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;                  // ProjectNodBinder
using AutoCAD2020.NOD_X_Data;                    // XDataQaqc

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        // --- Common app filters (adjust if you add more app names later) ---
        private static readonly string[] DefaultApps = new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" };

        // =========================
        // QAQC – XDATA
        // =========================

        /// <summary>
        /// Scan the entire drawing and repair malformed XData chunks for known apps.
        /// Preserves other apps, fixes structure, strips junk, re-writes clean buffers.
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
        public static void CbiQaqcXdata()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            try
            {
                var ed = doc.Editor;
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] QAQC XData (full) started...");
                // If your XDataQaqc has overloads with doc/db, call the one you implemented.
                XDataQaqc.RunFullFix(DefaultApps);
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] QAQC XData complete.");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                doc.Editor.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QAQC failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// QAQC/repair only on a selection set (faster for targeted fixing).
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL")]
        public static void CbiQaqcXdataSelection()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            var ed = doc.Editor;
            try
            {
                var pso = new PromptSelectionOptions { MessageForAdding = "\nSelect entities to QAQC: " };
                var psr = ed.GetSelection(pso);
                if (psr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

                XDataQaqc.RunFixOnSelection(psr.Value, DefaultApps);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QAQC XData (selection) complete. Count: {psr.Value.Count}.");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QAQC (selection) failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Read-only pass: summarize XData health (counts per app, issues found).
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_REPORT")]
        public static void CbiQaqcXdataReport()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            var ed = doc.Editor;
            try
            {
                var report = XDataQaqc.GenerateReport(DefaultApps);
                // Adjust to match your report object/format
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] XData QAQC Report:\n");
                ed.WriteMessage($"  Scanned Entities: {report.TotalEntities}\n");
                foreach (var a in report.Apps)
                    ed.WriteMessage($"  {a.Name}: ok={a.Ok}, fixed={a.Fixed}, invalid={a.Invalid}\n");
                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(report.LogPath))
                    ed.WriteMessage($"  Log: {report.LogPath}\n");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QAQC report failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // =========================
        // PIPE-RUN ASSOCIATIONS
        // =========================

        /// <summary>
        /// Attach selected component blocks to a selected pipe run (stores OwnerRunHandle).
        /// (Wrapper around PipeRunAssociationCommands.AttachToRun)
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN")]
        public static void CbiAttachToRun()
        {
            try { PipeRunAssociationCommands.AttachToRun(); }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor
                    .WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Attach failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Refresh components associated with a run from the run’s XData (SIZE, Fluid, PMC).
        /// (Wrapper around PipeRunAssociationCommands.RefreshAssociated)
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_REFRESH_ASSOC")]
        public static void CbiRefreshAssociated()
        {
            try { PipeRunAssociationCommands.RefreshAssociated(); }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor
                    .WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Refresh failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // =========================
        // PROJECT BINDING (utility)
        // =========================

        /// <summary>
        /// Quick utility to bind a drawing to a Project Store (writes NOD CBI/Project).
        /// Prompts user for Name and StorePath; generates a GUID project id if omitted.
        /// </summary>
        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_PROJECT")]
        public static void CbiBindProject()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                var prName = ed.GetString("\nProject name (Enter for 'New Project'): ");
                if (prName.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;
                var name = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(prName.StringResult) ? "New Project" : prName.StringResult;

                var prPath = ed.GetString("\nPath to Project_Store.json: ");
                if (prPath.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;
                var storePath = prPath.StringResult?.Trim();
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Store path is required.");
                    return;
                }

                var id = Guid.NewGuid().ToString("D");
                ProjectNodBinder.BindProjectToDrawing(doc, id, name, storePath);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Bound to project '{name}'.");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Bind failed: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## What this gives you
- `CBI_QAQC_XDATA` – full-drawing scan + repair for the apps in `DefaultApps`.
- `CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL` – repair only the selected entities.
- `CBI_QAQC_XDATA_REPORT` – read-only summary (counts/health/log).
- `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN` – wrapper for your existing attach flow.
- `CBI_REFRESH_ASSOC` – wrapper to push run properties down to components.
- `CBI_BIND_PROJECT` – handy admin utility to bind a DWG to a Project Store.

If your `XDataQaqc` ended up with slightly different method signatures (e.g., needs `Database` or `Document`), just tweak the three QAQC calls to match (the command scaffolding stays the same).

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
// Purpose: Lightweight command registry (for QAQC + utility wrappers)
// Style: one-line calls to logic classes for easy maintenance

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD2020.NOD_X_Data;      // XDataQaqc
using AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub;   // PipeRunAssociationCommands
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;    // ProjectNodBinder

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        // =========================
        // QAQC – XDATA
        // =========================

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
        public static void CBI_QAQC_XDATA()
            => XDataQaqc.RunFullFix(new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" });

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL")]
        public static void CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL()
            => XDataQaqc.RunFixOnSelection(null, new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" }); 
            // 'null' means it will prompt user for selection if your class supports that

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_REPORT")]
        public static void CBI_QAQC_XDATA_REPORT()
            => XDataQaqc.GenerateReport(new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" });

// =========================
        // PIPE-RUN ASSOCIATIONS
        // =========================

        [CommandMethod("CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN")]
        public static void CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN()
            => PipeRunAssociationCommands.AttachToRun();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_REFRESH_ASSOC")]
        public static void CBI_REFRESH_ASSOC()
            => PipeRunAssociationCommands.RefreshAssociated();

// =========================
        // PROJECT BINDING (UTILITY)
        // =========================

        [CommandMethod("CBI_BIND_PROJECT")]
        public static void CBI_BIND_PROJECT()
            => ProjectNodBinder.BindProjectToDrawing(
                   Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument,
                   System.Guid.NewGuid().ToString("D"),
                   "New Project",
                   @"C:\CBI\Project_Store.json"
               );
    }
}
```

Here’s the phrasing (and structure) you can save in your **ChatGPT Project Setup / Memory Context** so I’ll always know what you mean when you say,  
> “Add a command for X tool.”

---

## 🧭 Standard Command Registration Policy (for ChatGPT Project Setup)

**Command Architecture:**
- All AutoCAD commands are defined **centrally** in `AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs`.
- Each `[CommandMethod]` entry is a **short, one-line router** that calls into the appropriate tool class.
- The actual business logic resides inside that tool’s dedicated class (e.g., `XDataQaqc.cs`, `PipeRunAssociationCommands.cs`, `ProjectNodBinder.cs`, etc.).
- Commands follow the **“switchboard” convention**, meaning:
  ```csharp
  [CommandMethod("CBI_TOOLNAME_ACTION")]
  public static void CBI_TOOLNAME_ACTION()
      => Namespace.ClassName.MethodName();
  ```
- This keeps the command registry clean, readable, and consistent across all tools.

**Naming Convention:**
- All commands are prefixed with `CBI_` (e.g., `CBI_QAQC_XDATA`, `CBI_ATTACH_TO_RUN`).
- Command methods use `PascalCase` after the prefix to match the feature name.

**Namespace Layout:**
- `AutoCAD2020` — contains `Commands.cs` (central router).
- `AutoCAD2020\PropertiesHub` — for properties and associations (PipeRun, Valves, etc.).
- `AutoCAD2020\NOD_X_Data` — for data validation and internal DWG storage tools.
- `AutoCAD2020\ProjectStore` — for project binding, linking, and metadata persistence.

**When User Requests “Add a Command for X Tool”:**
> ChatGPT will automatically:
> 1. Locate the appropriate logic class (based on tool type and folder).
> 2. Add a `[CommandMethod("CBI_TOOL_ACTION")]` entry to `Commands.cs` that calls that logic.
> 3. Keep all functional code inside the logic class, *not* the command file.
> 4. Ensure consistent documentation and XML summaries for IntelliSense.

**Example Reference Pattern:**
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA")]
public static void CBI_QAQC_XDATA()
    => AutoCAD2020.NOD_X_Data.XDataQaqc.RunFullFix(new[] { "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_COMPONENT" });
```

---

Would you like me to save that as your **official Command Registration Standard** in memory so future conversations automatically follow this pattern?  
That way, you can just say:  
> “Add a command for the LayerUtilityValidator tool,”  

and I’ll know to create a one-line command in `Commands.cs` routed to the correct class.

absolutely—here are the **one-line command registrations** for the QA/QC tools, matching your Commands.cs style. Drop these into `AutoCAD2020.Commands` and you’re set.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        // --- XDATA QA/QC ----------------------------------------------------

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL")]
        public static void CBI_QaqcXdataSel()
            => Services.XDataQaqcService.ValidateSelectedEntity();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_QAQC_XDATA_ALL")]
        public static void CBI_QaqcXdataAll()
            => Services.XDataQaqcService.ValidateEntireDrawing();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_CLEAN_XDATA_SEL")]
        public static void CBI_CleanXdataSel()
            => Services.XDataQaqcService.CleanSelectedEntity();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_CLEAN_XDATA_ALL")]
        public static void CBI_CleanXdataAll()
            => Services.XDataQaqcService.CleanEntireDrawing();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_LIST_XDATA_APPS")]
        public static void CBI_ListXdataApps()
            => Services.XDataQaqcService.ListAppsInDrawing();
    }
}
```

If you want, here’s a **tiny service wrapper** those commands call (keeps `Commands.cs` clean and centralizes UI/locking). This assumes you already added `XDataQaqc.cs` (the logic) in a namespace you control—adjust the `using` as needed.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/Services/XDataQaqcService.cs
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD2020.Qaqc; // <-- wherever your XDataQaqc.cs lives

namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class XDataQaqcService
    {
        public static void ValidateSelectedEntity()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            var per = ed.GetEntity(new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect entity to validate XData: "));
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            var report = XDataQaqc.ValidateEntity(per.ObjectId, doc.Database);
            ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QA/QC: {report.Summary}");
        }

        public static void ValidateEntireDrawing()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            {
                var report = XDataQaqc.ValidateDrawing(doc.Database);
                doc.Editor.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] QA/QC: {report.Summary}");
            }
        }

        public static void CleanSelectedEntity()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            var per = ed.GetEntity(new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect entity to clean XData: "));
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            {
                var result = XDataQaqc.CleanEntity(per.ObjectId, doc.Database);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Cleaned: {result.Summary}");
            }
        }

        public static void CleanEntireDrawing()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            {
                var result = XDataQaqc.CleanDrawing(doc.Database);
                doc.Editor.WriteMessage($"\n[CBI] Cleaned: {result.Summary}");
            }
        }

        public static void ListAppsInDrawing()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            var apps = XDataQaqc.ListRegApps(doc.Database);
            var joined = string.Join(", ", apps.OrderBy(a => a));
            doc.Editor.WriteMessage(string.IsNullOrEmpty(joined)
                ? "\n[CBI] No RegApp names found."
                : $"\n[CBI] RegApp names: {joined}");
        }
    }
}
```

**Resulting commands you can type:**

- `CBI_QAQC_XDATA_SEL` – validate XData on one picked entity  
- `CBI_QAQC_XDATA_ALL` – scan/validate entire drawing  
- `CBI_CLEAN_XDATA_SEL` – clean/fix XData on one picked entity  
- `CBI_CLEAN_XDATA_ALL` – clean/fix the entire drawing  
- `CBI_LIST_XDATA_APPS` – list RegApp names present in the DWG

These all delegate to your QA/QC logic; tweak names anytime and I’ll stick to the same registration pattern going forward.

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Interfaces;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;
//using AutoCAD2020.Palette;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AutoCAD2020.Objects;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD;
using AutoCAD.Forms;
using AutoCAD.Sheet_Set_Manager;
using System;
using System.IO;
using ClosedXML.Excel;
using AutoCAD2020.Forms.Development;
using AutoCAD2020.CBI_Data_Explorer.UI;
using AutoCAD2020.Utilities;
using AutoCAD2020.Services;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
using static AutoCAD2020.Drawing_Operations.DrawingManager;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services;   // LineTagService, NodLineNumberStore
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Rendering;  // TagRenderOptions
using AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub;
using static AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveInsertService;
using AutoCAD2020.Valves;
//using static AutoCAD.Forms.TruckVectorBuilder;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public class Commands

{
        // Default entry (main button click uses this)
        [CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve", CommandFlags.Modal | CommandFlags.UsePickSet)]
        public static void CBI_PlaceValve()
            => ValveInsertService.PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions { BlockName = "INST_BALL-VALVE" });

        // Dropdown entries
        [CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Ball", CommandFlags.Modal)]
        public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Ball()
            => ValveInsertService.PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions { BlockName = "INST_BFLY-VALVE" });

        [CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly", CommandFlags.Modal)]
        public static void CBI_PlaceValve_Butterfly()
            => ValveInsertService.PlaceValveInteractive(new ValveInsertOptions { BlockName = "INST_GLOBE-VALVE" });

        // Add more as you create symbols:
        // [CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Gate")]  => CBI_VALVE_GATE
        // [CommandMethod("CBI_PlaceValve_Globe")] => CBI_VALVE_GLOBE

        [CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
        public static void CBI_TagManager()
            => AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost.Show();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_PROP_PALETTE")]

        public static void ShowCBIPropertiesPalette()

            => AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub.CbiPropertiesPalette.CmdShow();

[CommandMethod("CBI_MATCH_PIPERUN")]
        public static void CBI_MatchPipeRun()
            => PipeRunMatchService.RunInteractive();

        // ---- Project / Support scaffolding ----
        //[CommandMethod("CBI_NewProjectSupport")]
        //public static void CBI_NewProjectSupport()
        //    => AutoCAD2020.Utilities.SupportFileServiceCommands.NewProjectSupport();

        //[CommandMethod("CBI_OpenSupportFolder")]
        //public static void CBI_OpenSupportFolder()
        //    => AutoCAD2020.Utilities.SupportFileServiceCommands.OpenSupportFolder();

        // ---- Tagging ----
        [CommandMethod("CBI_AddTag")]
        public static void CBI_AddTag()
            => AutoCAD2020.Services.TagAddService.CBI_AddTag();

        // ---- Line Tagging ----
        [CommandMethod("CBI_PLACELINETAG", CommandFlags.Modal)]
        public static void CBI_PlaceLineTag()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null)
            {
                Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.ShowAlertDialog("Open a drawing to use PLACELINETAG.");
                return;
            }

            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // 1) Select a LINE or POLYLINE
            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect pipe (LINE or POLYLINE): ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nOnly Line/Polyline supported.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Line), true);
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);

            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            // 2) Pick label location
            var ppr = ed.GetPoint("\nPick label location: ");
            if (ppr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            // 3) Compute tangent rotation at pick
            double rot;
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                rot = LineTagService.GetTangentAngleAtPoint(ent, ppr.Value);
                tr.Commit();
            }

            // 4) Ensure tag + place label
            var svc = new LineTagService(new NODStoreAdapter(), new TagRenderOptions());
            string tagText;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            {
                using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    var ent = (Entity)tr.GetObject(per.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    tagText = svc.EnsureTag(ent);      // writes XData if missing, returns RAW
                    tr.Commit();
                }

                svc.PlaceLabel(tagText, ppr.Value, rot); // MTEXT at point/rotation
            }

            ed.WriteMessage($"\nPlaced line tag: {tagText}");
        }

[CommandMethod("DRAWTRUCKFULL")]
        public void DrawTruckFull()
        {
            Document doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            Database db = doc.Database;

            using (Transaction tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                BlockTable bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                BlockTableRecord btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[BlockTableRecord.ModelSpace], OpenMode.ForWrite);

                string csvPath = "C:\\CBICAD\\truck_full_outline.csv"; // Update this path if needed
                var lines = LineSegmentLoader.LoadLinesFromCsv(csvPath);
                LineSegmentLoader.InsertLinesToModelSpace(lines, btr, tr);

                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        [CommandMethod("ExportNODToExcel")]
        public static void ExportNODToExcel()
        {
            Document doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            Database db = doc.Database;
            Editor ed = doc.Editor;

            string desktopPath = Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.Desktop);
            string filePath = Path.Combine(desktopPath, "NOD_Export.xlsx");

            using (var workbook = new XLWorkbook())
            {
                var ws = workbook.Worksheets.Add("NOD Contents");
                ws.Cell(1, 1).Value = "Key";
                ws.Cell(1, 2).Value = "TypeCode";
                ws.Cell(1, 3).Value = "Value";

                int row = 2;

                using (Transaction tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    DBDictionary nod = tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead) as DBDictionary;

                    foreach (DBDictionaryEntry entry in nod)
                    {
                        string key = entry.Key;
                        DBObject obj = tr.GetObject(entry.Value, OpenMode.ForRead);

                        if (obj is Xrecord xrec)
                        {
                            ResultBuffer rb = xrec.Data;
                            if (rb != null)
                            {
                                foreach (TypedValue tv in rb)
                                {
                                    ws.Cell(row, 1).Value = key;
                                    ws.Cell(row, 2).Value = tv.TypeCode;
                                    ws.Cell(row, 3).Value = tv.Value?.ToString() ?? "(null)";
                                    row++;
                                }
                            }
                            else
                            {
                                ws.Cell(row, 1).Value = key;
                                ws.Cell(row, 2).Value = "(none)";
                                ws.Cell(row, 3).Value = "(Xrecord has no data)";
                                row++;
                            }
                        }
                        else
                        {
                            ws.Cell(row, 1).Value = key;
                            ws.Cell(row, 2).Value = "(not Xrecord)";
                            ws.Cell(row, 3).Value = obj.GetType().Name;
                            row++;
                        }
                    }

                    tr.Commit();
                }

                workbook.SaveAs(filePath);
            }

            ed.WriteMessage($"\nNOD exported to Excel:\n{filePath}");
        }

        // ---------- PIPE RUN ----------
        [CommandMethod("CBI_MAKE_PIPERUN")]
        public static void MakePipeRun()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect polyline to mark as Pipe Run: ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a Polyline.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(Polyline), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            EnsureRegApp("CBI_PIPE_RUN");

            var data = new XDataMap
            {
                ["SchemaVersion"] = "1.0",
                ["PackageItemTag"] = "PR-TEST-001",
                ["LineTag"] = "PR-TEST-001",
                ["Service"] = "Cooling Water",
                ["FluidCode"] = "CW",
                ["MainPipeSize"] = "4 in",
                ["Schedule"] = "40",
                ["Spec"] = "CW-STD",
                ["FlowDirection"] = "End 1 is upstream",
                ["DesignPressure"] = "150",
                ["DesignTemp"] = "90",
                ["ConstructionStatus"] = "New"
            };

            XDataHelpers.Write(per.ObjectId, "CBI_PIPE_RUN", data);
            ed.WriteMessage("\nAdded CBI_PIPE_RUN XData.");
            CbiPropertiesPalette.CmdShow();
        }

        // ---------- INSTRUMENT / TAG ----------

        public static void MakeInstrument()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect block to mark as Instrument: ");
            peo.SetRejectMessage("\nMust be a BlockReference.");
            peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), true);
            var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
            if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            EnsureRegApp("CBI_INSTRUMENT");

            var data = new XDataMap
            {
                ["SchemaVersion"] = "1.0",
                ["InstrTag"] = "TT-101",
                ["LoopTag"] = "Loop-101",
                ["DeviceType"] = "TT",
                ["Location"] = "Field",
                ["SignalType"] = "AI",
                ["RangeLow"] = "0",
                ["RangeHigh"] = "250",
                ["Setpoint"] = "120",
                ["Owner"] = "I&C",
                ["CalibrationDue"] = "2026-01"
            };

            XDataHelpers.Write(per.ObjectId, "CBI_INSTRUMENT", data);
            ed.WriteMessage("\nAdded CBI_INSTRUMENT XData.");
            CbiPropertiesPalette.CmdShow();
