## Response:
10/23/2025, 5:44:48 PM

> **Troubleshooting block insertion**
>
> It looks like there's an issue with the block not being found, possibly due to a mismatch between the block name in the code and the actual DWG block name. I should suggest aligning those names or implementing a loader. For missing blocks, I can recommend using a fallback mechanism. Also, the prompt issue could be fixed by adjusting how the prompt behaves during the jig. If needed, switching to a modal dialog might solve it.
>
> Thought for 19s

Great—all the compiler errors are gone 🎉. Now you’ve got two runtime things:

---

# 1) “Tag Manager” command does nothing

Make sure the command is wired to the palette host and that the `Show()` is actually opening a `PaletteSet`.

**Commands.cs**

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
public static void CBI_TagManager()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.FormLauncher.ShowTagManager();
```

**Services/FormLauncher.cs**

```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class FormLauncher
    {
        // Keep a single palette instance
        private static PaletteSet _tagMgrPs;

        public static void ShowTagManager()
        {
            if (_tagMgrPs == null)
            {
                _tagMgrPs = new PaletteSet("Tag Manager")
                {
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right
                };

                // IMPORTANT: this type must exist and be a WinForms control
                _tagMgrPs.Add("Home", new AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost());
            }

            _tagMgrPs.Visible = true;
            _tagMgrPs.Activate(0); // bring to front
        }
    }
}
```

If clicking the ribbon still does nothing, two quick checks in AutoCAD:

- In the command line, type `CBI_TagManager` manually. If it opens, your ribbon macro isn’t calling the right command.
- If it still doesn’t open, make sure `AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost` compiles and is public.

---

# 2) Block insert fails: “Block def not found: VALVE_BALL”

That exception means your **block name** coming from the resolver is `VALVE_BALL`, but the actual definition you ship/use is named **`INST_BALL-VALVE`** (your earlier code used that).

You can fix this in either place:

### Option A — Map the valve type to the correct block name

In your **`ValveDefinitionResolver`** (or wherever you resolve the symbol), normalize the names:

```csharp
public sealed class ValveDefinitionResolver
{
    public BlockDefinitionRef Resolve(ValveType type)
    {
        // Map to your real block names
        var name = type switch
        {
            ValveType.Ball      => "INST_BALL-VALVE",
            ValveType.Butterfly => "INST_BFLY-VALVE",
            ValveType.Check     => "INST_CHECK-VALVE",   // adjust if needed
            _                   => "INST_BALL-VALVE"
        };

        return new BlockDefinitionRef { Name = name, SourceDwgPath = FindBlockDwg(name) };
    }

    private string FindBlockDwg(string blockName)
    {
        // (1) try AutoCAD support paths
        try
        {
            return Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices
                   .Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Database is var db
                ? HostApplicationServices.Current.FindFile(blockName + ".dwg", db, FindFileHint.Default)
                : null;
        }
        catch { }

        // (2) your custom folders
        foreach (var dir in AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveInsertService.BlockLoadConfig.BlockSearchDirs)
        {
            var cand = System.IO.Path.Combine(dir, blockName + ".dwg");
            if (System.IO.File.Exists(cand)) return cand;
        }
        return null;
    }
}
```

### Option B — Teach your `BlockInsertService` to load the DWG if missing

In **`Valves/BlockInsertService.cs`**, make `EnsureAndInsert` import when the block isn’t in the current DB:

```csharp
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public sealed class BlockDefinitionRef
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }           // e.g., "INST_BALL-VALVE"
        public string SourceDwgPath { get; set; }  // optional .dwg path
    }

    public class BlockInsertService
    {
        public ObjectId EnsureAndInsert(
            BlockDefinitionRef def,
            Point3d position,
            double rotationRad,
            IDictionary<string, string> dynPropsOrAtts)
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var db = doc.Database;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);

                // ensure definition
                if (!bt.Has(def.Name))
                {
                    if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(def.SourceDwgPath) ||
                        !System.IO.File.Exists(def.SourceDwgPath))
                    {
                        // as a fallback, try to find/import by name
                        TryImportBlockDefinition(db, def.Name, ed);
                        bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                        if (!bt.Has(def.Name))
                            throw new System.Exception($"Block def not found: {def.Name}");
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        ImportBlockDefinition(db, def.Name, def.SourceDwgPath);
                        bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    }
                }

                var btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[def.Name], OpenMode.ForRead);

                // insert
                var msId = SymbolUtilityServices.GetBlockModelSpaceId(db);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(msId, OpenMode.ForWrite);

                var br = new BlockReference(position, btr.ObjectId)
                {
                    Rotation = rotationRad
                };
                var brId = ms.AppendEntity(br);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(br, true);

                // attributes (optional)
                if (btr.HasAttributeDefinitions && dynPropsOrAtts != null)
                {
                    foreach (ObjectId id in btr)
                    {
                        var obj = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as AttributeDefinition;
                        if (obj != null && !obj.Constant)
                        {
                            var ar = new AttributeReference();
                            ar.SetAttributeFromBlock(obj, br.BlockTransform);
                            if (dynPropsOrAtts.TryGetValue(obj.Tag, out var val))
                                ar.TextString = val ?? string.Empty;

                            br.AttributeCollection.AppendAttribute(ar);
                            tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(ar, true);
                        }
                    }
                }

                tr.Commit();
                return brId;
            }
        }

        private static void TryImportBlockDefinition(Database db, string blockName, Editor ed)
        {
            // Try AutoCAD search paths
            string path = null;
            try { path = HostApplicationServices.Current.FindFile(blockName + ".dwg", db, FindFileHint.Default); }
            catch { }
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(path))
            {
                ImportBlockDefinition(db, blockName, path);
                return;
            }

            // Try your custom directories
            foreach (var dir in AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveInsertService.BlockLoadConfig.BlockSearchDirs)
            {
                var cand = System.IO.Path.Combine(dir, blockName + ".dwg");
                if (System.IO.File.Exists(cand))
                {
                    ImportBlockDefinition(db, blockName, cand);
                    return;
                }
            }

            ed.WriteMessage($"\nCould not locate {blockName}.dwg in support paths or custom folders.");
        }

        private static void ImportBlockDefinition(Database destDb, string blockName, string dwgPath)
        {
            using (var srcDb = new Database(false, true))
            {
                srcDb.ReadDwgFile(dwgPath, FileOpenMode.OpenForReadAndAllShare, true, "");
                destDb.Insert(blockName, srcDb, false);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

With either (or both) changes:

- **Ball Valve** button should resolve to `INST_BALL-VALVE` (or whatever you actually have).
- If the block isn’t in the drawing, it will import it from a `.dwg` automatically.

---

## About the “dropdown follows the cursor”

That tiny floating box is AutoCAD’s **in-canvas keyword prompt** from `Editor.GetKeywords`. It appears near the cursor by design, and while you’re moving the mouse it “sticks” to it. That’s normal.

If you’d prefer the **command line** prompt (bottom area) instead of the in-canvas tooltip, set:

```csharp
var pko = new PromptKeywordOptions("\nValve type: ")
{
    AllowNone = false,
    // Force command-line area instead of in-canvas tooltip:
    UseFullScreenCursor = false // (or turn off dynamic input in AutoCAD)
};
```

(Users can also toggle Dynamic Input off — F12 — to keep prompts in the command line.)

---

## Quick checklist

- [ ] Update the resolver mapping so BALL → your real block name.
- [ ] Or/and allow `BlockInsertService` to import `.dwg` when missing.
- [ ] Confirm the DWG that contains the symbol is actually reachable (support paths or the custom folders you listed).
- [ ] Ensure `TagManagerPaletteHost` exists and is added to the palette set in `FormLauncher`.

If you paste back your current `ValveDefinitionResolver` and `BlockInsertService`, I’ll align them precisely to what you have so the button works first try.

## Response:
10/31/2025, 5:05:40 PM

Perfect photo — this tells me *exactly* what’s happening 👇  

---

### 💥 Problem
You’re getting:

> `CS1503: Argument 2 cannot convert from 'AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost' to 'System.Uri'`

That means the compiler is trying to use this **PaletteSet.Add** overload:
```csharp
PaletteSet.Add(string, System.Uri)
```
—which happens when `TagManagerPaletteHost` is **not** a WinForms `UserControl`.

---

### ✅ The fix

Your palette host **must inherit from `System.Windows.Forms.UserControl`**.

So open this file:
```
AutoCAD2020\Forms\TagManager\TagManagerPaletteHost.cs
```
and make sure the class definition starts like this 👇

```csharp
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
        }
    }
}
```

---

### ⚠️ Common mistake

Sometimes the file was accidentally declared like this:
```csharp
public class TagManagerPaletteHost
```
or worse:
```csharp
public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : Form
```

If it inherits from `Form`, the AutoCAD palette system can’t host it — it must be a **UserControl**, not a standalone Form.

---

### ✅ Once corrected

Rebuild the solution, and this line in `FormLauncher.cs` will compile cleanly:
```csharp
_tagMgrPs.Add("Home", new AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost());
```

---

### 💡 Quick test

After rebuild:
1. Launch AutoCAD.
2. Run your command manually:
   ```
   CBI_TagManager
   ```
3. A palette should dock on the right (or left) of AutoCAD titled **“Tag Manager.”**

If it doesn’t appear or throws an exception, send me the **first 10 lines of `TagManagerPaletteHost.cs`** so I can confirm it’s properly wired.

## Response:
10/31/2025, 5:30:50 PM

Perfect question — and great catch 👏  

Let’s clear this up carefully, because this distinction matters:

---

## 🧩 **Two different roles**

| Role | Inherits / Implements | Purpose |
|------|-----------------------|----------|
| **AutoCAD plug-in entry point** | `IExtensionApplication` | Runs **once** when AutoCAD loads your DLL (for startup logic, ribbon init, etc.). |
| **Palette host control** | `UserControl` | A **UI control** that lives **inside** a palette window (dockable). |

---

### 🔍 What happened

Your original `TagManagerPaletteHost` was acting as an **AutoCAD entry class** (implementing `IExtensionApplication`), *not* as a **UI control**.  
When we added it to the palette with:

```csharp
_tagMgrPs.Add("Home", new TagManagerPaletteHost());
```

AutoCAD expected a **WinForms control**, but instead got a plain class that only implemented `IExtensionApplication`, which caused the compile error.

---

## ✅ Correct structure

You need **both**, but as **two separate classes/files**:

---

### **1️⃣ TagManagerPaletteHost.cs** → the *UserControl* (UI)

```csharp
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
        }
    }
}
```

> ⚠️ You’ll get `InitializeComponent()` errors until you right-click the file → **View Designer** → drag a simple Panel or Label, then save.  
> That creates a `.Designer.cs` file automatically with the `InitializeComponent()` definition.

---

### **2️⃣ TagManagerApp.cs** → the *IExtensionApplication* entry point

If you still need the AutoCAD startup logic that was in the old class, move that to a new file:

```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public class TagManagerApp : IExtensionApplication
    {
        public void Initialize()
        {
            // Runs once when DLL loads
            Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Editor.WriteMessage(
                "\nCBI Tag Manager loaded.");
        }

        public void Terminate()
        {
            // Cleanup if needed
        }
    }
}
```

---

### ✅ Summary of what to do

1. **Keep `TagManagerPaletteHost`** as a `UserControl` (the one that displays inside the palette).  
   - Add a Designer file (View → Designer → Save).  
2. **Add a new file `TagManagerApp.cs`** to keep the `IExtensionApplication` interface (so AutoCAD still runs your startup code).  
3. Rebuild → the `PaletteSet.Add("Home", new TagManagerPaletteHost());` line will compile perfectly.  
4. Run your ribbon “Tag Manager” button — it should now dock and open properly.

---

Would you like me to generate a *minimal Designer layout* (so `InitializeComponent()` stops erroring immediately)?  
I can give you a ready-to-drop `TagManagerPaletteHost.Designer.cs` with a nice placeholder panel and label saying *“CBI Tag Manager Loaded”*.

## Response:
10/31/2025, 6:56:26 PM

You’re super close—this is just two small C# pitfalls:

1) **Your `Show()` method is hiding `Control.Show()`**  
   Rename your static method so it doesn’t collide with WinForms’ `Control.Show()`.

2) **`Application` is ambiguous**  
   Something else named `Application` is in scope (WinForms or a type in your project). Use an alias (or fully qualify) for the AutoCAD one—or better, don’t call it here at all since a `PaletteSet` doesn’t need it.

---

Here’s a clean drop-in for **`TagManagerPaletteHost.cs`** that fixes both issues:

```csharp
// File: Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows; // PaletteSet, DockSides, PaletteSetStyles

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent(); // (Designer file generates this)
        }

        /// <summary>Open (or focus) the Tag Manager palette.</summary>
        public static void Open()   // <-- renamed from Show()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ui = new TagManagerControl(); // must be a UserControl you already have

                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    Style =
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton    |
                        PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size        = new Size(520, 600)
                };

                _ps.Add("Home", _ui);
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            _ps.Activate(0);
        }
    }
}
```

And in **`FormLauncher.cs`** (or wherever you call it), switch to:

```csharp
public static void ShowTagManager()
{
    AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost.Open();
}
```

**Why this fixes your errors**

- Renaming `Show()` → `Open()` removes the “hides inherited member ‘Control.Show’” warning and avoids confusion.
- No direct reference to `Application` in this file, so the *ambiguous reference between … Application* error disappears. If you need AutoCAD’s `Application` elsewhere, use an alias at the top of that file:
  ```csharp
  using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
  ```

If the compiler still flags `InitializeComponent()`, open the file in the **Designer**, drop a `Panel` or `Label`, save—VS will generate `TagManagerPaletteHost.Designer.cs` with `InitializeComponent()`.

Want me to also generate a minimal `TagManagerControl` (a simple placeholder UserControl) so this compiles even if yours is empty?

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using System.Drawing;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    'public class TagManagerPaletteHost : IExtensionApplication
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {

        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private CheckBox checkBox1;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent();
        }

        public static void Show()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ui = new TagManagerControl { Dock = System.Windows.Forms.DockStyle.Fill };
                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right | DockSides.None,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size = new Size(520, 600),
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.Snappable
                };
                _ps.Add("Manager", _ui);

                // persist size/dock if you added PaletteStateManager earlier
                AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.LoadState(_ps, PaletteName);
                AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.AttachAutoSave(_ps, PaletteName);

                // Refresh when user switches drawings
                Application.DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
                {
                    if (_ps.Visible) _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();
                };
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();  // <- ensure load on show
        }

        [CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
        public void CmdShow() => Show();

        public void Initialize() { }
        public void Terminate()
        {
            if (_ps != null)
                AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.SaveState(_ps, PaletteName);

            this.Name = "TagManagerPaletteHost";

> **Reviewing code updates**
>

I’ll also move `[CommandMethod]`, fix the `DockEnabled` flags, and check for necessary methods like `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore`. I'll correct the exact changes and provide a revised code.

You’re almost there. Here’s **exactly what to change** to modernize this file and make it play nicely with your refactored “one-liner Commands.cs + FormLauncher” pattern.

---

# What to update (point-by-point)

1) **This class should be a pure WinForms host** (not an AutoCAD entry point).  
   - ✅ Keep it as `partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl`.  
   - ❌ Do **not** implement `IExtensionApplication` here.  
   - ❌ Do **not** put `[CommandMethod]` here (move the command to `Commands.cs`).

2) **Rename `Show()` -> `Open()`**  
   - Avoids hiding `Control.Show()` and the CS0108 warning.

3) **Create/own the `PaletteSet` here; launch it from `FormLauncher.ShowTagManager()`**  
   - Your launcher simply calls `TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()`.

4) **`Application` ambiguity**  
   - If you need AutoCAD’s app anywhere, alias it:  
     `using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;`  
     In this file, we actually don’t need it; the `PaletteSet` doesn’t require `Application`.

5) **`DockEnabled` flags**  
   - Don’t OR with `DockSides.None`. Just use `DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right`.

6) **State persistence calls** (optional but safe)  
   - Guard them if that utility is sometimes missing.

7) **Ensure the designer exists**  
   - You already have an inline `InitializeComponent()`. That’s fine. If you later switch to a designer file, keep the call and remove the inline method.

---

# Updated file (drop-in)

```csharp
// File: Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows; // PaletteSet, DockSides, PaletteSetStyles

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";

        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent(); // keep; works with inline or designer-based init
        }

        /// <summary>Open (or focus) the Tag Manager palette.</summary>
        public static void Open()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ui = new TagManagerControl
                {
                    Dock = DockStyle.Fill
                };

                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    Style =
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton    |
                        PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,

                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size        = new Size(520, 600)
                };

                _ps.Add("Manager", _ui);

                // Optional: persist location/size if your utility exists
#if true
                try
                {
                    AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.LoadState(_ps, PaletteName);
                    AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.AttachAutoSave(_ps, PaletteName);
                }
                catch { /* utility not present? safe to ignore */ }
#endif
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;

            // Refresh data on open (ignore if your control doesn’t have this yet)
            try { _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore(); } catch { /* no-op */ }
        }

        // ---- Inline minimal InitializeComponent so this compiles even without a designer ----
        private CheckBox checkBox1;
        private void InitializeComponent()
        {
            checkBox1 = new CheckBox
            {
                AutoSize = true,
                Location = new System.Drawing.Point(12, 12),
                Text = "Placeholder"
            };
            Controls.Add(checkBox1);
            Name = "TagManagerPaletteHost";
        }
    }
}
```

---

In **FormLauncher.cs**:

The steps involve modifying the initialization to create and host TagManagerControl, ensuring the FormLauncher calls Open, and managing docking/persistence. The code diff should modify the PaletteSet for TagManagerControl, and restore any necessary event watchers like DocumentActivated. Let’s make sure everything is correctly wired up!

I’ve got you—nothing’s “gone,” we just wired a placeholder. Let’s point the palette back at your real **TagManagerControl** so it looks like it used to.

## Do this:

1) **Ensure the control exists**
- You already have: `Forms/TagManager/TagManagerControl.cs` (designer + code-behind). Good.

2) **Replace the placeholder host with the real control**
Update **Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs** to create and add `TagManagerControl` (not a checkbox). This is a drop-in:

```csharp
// File: Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            // Designer is optional; keep the call harmless either way
            try { InitializeComponent(); } catch { }
        }

        public static void Open()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                // 1) Create your REAL UI
                _ui = new TagManagerControl
                {
                    Dock = DockStyle.Fill
                };

                // 2) Create palette and add the control
                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    Style =
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton    |
                        PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size        = new Size(520, 600)
                };

                _ps.Add("Manager", _ui);

                // 3) (Optional) restore/persist size & position
                try
                {
                    AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.LoadState(_ps, PaletteName);
                    AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.AttachAutoSave(_ps, PaletteName);
                }
                catch { /* utility may not be present in every build */ }

                // 4) Refresh when user switches drawings
                Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                    .DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
                    {
                        if (_ps.Visible)
                            SafeRefresh();
                    };
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            SafeRefresh();
        }

        private static void SafeRefresh()
        {
            try
            {
                // If your control exposes a different method name, change it here:
                _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();
            }
            catch { /* keep palette robust */ }
        }
    }
}
```

3) **Launcher and command (no changes if you already have these)**
```csharp
// Commands.cs
[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
public static void CBI_TagManager()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.FormLauncher.ShowTagManager();

// Services/FormLauncher.cs
namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class FormLauncher
    {
        public static void ShowTagManager()
            => AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost.Open();
    }
}
```

4) **Remove any leftover placeholder UI**
- If your `TagManagerPaletteHost` still had a checkbox in `InitializeComponent()`, it won’t hurt anything, but you can delete that code now.

---

### Why you saw the empty “Placeholder”
We temporarily hosted a blank `UserControl` so the palette infrastructure compiled. The fix above swaps in your **TagManagerControl** as the page content of the palette (`_ps.Add("Manager", _ui);`), and refreshes it on open + on document changes.

If the palette opens but the grid is empty, your control likely needs a project/drawing context—`TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()` (or whatever you named it) should populate the UI. I’ve wired that in two places: when the palette becomes visible and on `DocumentActivated`.

## Response:
11/3/2025, 10:27:50 AM

> **Providing guidance on service setup**
>
> For this setup, I'll suggest keeping Command.cs as one-liners while moving the relevant code to the `TagManagerUiService`. This service would handle opening, closing, and toggling the TagManager UI. Ribbon adjustments are needed to link to this service, and I’ll provide concise code snippets for implementation.
>
> Thought for 18s

Totally—keep `Commands.cs` as one-liners. Put the palette logic in a **feature-scoped service**. Here’s a clean drop-in service you can add at:

`AutoCAD2020/Services/TagManagerUiService.cs`

```csharp
// File: Services/TagManagerUiService.cs
// Purpose: Owns the Tag Manager palette lifecycle (open/close/toggle).
// Keeps Commands.cs as one-liners.

using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class TagManagerUiService
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";

        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerControl _ui;

        // Open (or bring to front)
        public static void Open()
        {
            EnsurePalette();
            _ps.Visible = true;
            RefreshIfPossible();
        }

        public static void Toggle()
        {
            EnsurePalette();
            _ps.Visible = !_ps.Visible;
            if (_ps.Visible) RefreshIfPossible();
        }

        public static void Close()
        {
            if (_ps == null) return;
            try { AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.SaveState(_ps, PaletteName); } catch { }
            _ps.Visible = false;
        }

        // ---------- internals ----------

        private static void EnsurePalette()
        {
            if (_ps != null) return;

            _ui = new AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerControl
            {
                Dock = DockStyle.Fill
            };

            _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
            {
                Style =
                    PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                    PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton    |
                    PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                Size        = new Size(520, 600)
            };

            _ps.Add("Manager", _ui);

            // optional: persist/restore position & size if your helper exists
            try
            {
                AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.LoadState(_ps, PaletteName);
                AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.AttachAutoSave(_ps, PaletteName);
            }
            catch { /* helper may not be present in all builds */ }

            // When user switches drawings, refresh the UI if the palette is showing
            Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application
                .DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
                {
                    if (_ps.Visible) RefreshIfPossible();
                };
        }

        private static void RefreshIfPossible()
        {
            try
            {
                // Call your control’s “reload” method (rename if yours differs)
                _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();
            }
            catch { /* never throw from UI service */ }
        }
    }
}
```

### Keep `Commands.cs` as one-liners

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
public static void CBI_TagManager() => AutoCAD2020.Services.TagManagerUiService.Open();

[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager_Toggle")]
public static void CBI_TagManager_Toggle() => AutoCAD2020.Services.TagManagerUiService.Toggle();

[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager_Close")]
public static void CBI_TagManager_Close() => AutoCAD2020.Services.TagManagerUiService.Close();
```

### Notes
- This replaces the need to call the UserControl directly from `Commands.cs`.
- If you still have `FormLauncher.ShowTagManager()`, make it just forward to the service:
  ```csharp
  public static void ShowTagManager() => TagManagerUiService.Open();
  ```
- Your existing `TagManagerPaletteHost` file isn’t required anymore. If you keep it, strip it down or have it delegate to the service to avoid two places managing the same `PaletteSet`.

This restores your old behavior (real Tag Manager control inside the palette) while sticking to the architecture: **commands → service → UI**.

Ah — perfect clarification 👍  

You’re absolutely right:  
since your **`Commands.cs` → FormLauncher.cs → UI host** chain already exists,  
you don’t need a new `TagManagerUiService.cs`.  

Let’s lock the correct architecture in clearly:

---

### ✅ **Current, correct flow**

```
Commands.cs
   ↓
FormLauncher.ShowTagManager()
   ↓
TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()
   ↓
TagManagerControl (real UI)
```

That’s the clean, layered pattern you and I standardized earlier:
- `Commands.cs` → one-liner, no logic.  
- `FormLauncher.cs` → *“router”* that knows where to find UI or service entry points.  
- `TagManagerPaletteHost` → the *owner* of the actual palette + control.  
- `TagManagerControl` → the functional UI.

---

### 🧩 **What you should keep**

**Commands.cs**
```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
public static void CBI_TagManager()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.FormLauncher.ShowTagManager();
```

**FormLauncher.cs**
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class FormLauncher
    {
        public static void ShowTagManager()
            => AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost.Open();
    }
}
```

**TagManagerPaletteHost.cs**
(contains the full palette creation logic and `TagManagerControl` embedding)

---

### ⚙️ Why this is better

| Layer | Purpose | Logic? |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Commands.cs | Entry points & command registration | ❌ None |
| FormLauncher | Routing layer to find the correct form/service | ✅ Minimal |
| *Feature host* (e.g., TagManagerPaletteHost) | Creates & manages palette lifecycle | ✅ Full |
| *Control* (TagManagerControl) | UI logic, binding, user interaction | ✅ Full |

---

### ✅ **Next step**

You’re now in the *perfect* structure — all that’s left is confirming `TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()` uses the real `TagManagerControl` instead of the placeholder checkbox (as fixed earlier).

Once that’s in place, your `CBI_TagManager` command should open the Tag Manager palette fully populated again.

## Response:
11/3/2025, 10:36:58 AM

> **Clarifying palette management**
>
> It looks like there's some duplication between FormLauncher and TagManagerPaletteHost when both try to create palettes. To avoid this, we should stick to one approach. Since they want a simple one-liner command, it might be best to have FormLauncher just call Open() on TagManagerPaletteHost and shift palette ownership to it. I'll give exact code snippets for this change.
>
> Thought for 26s

Great—thanks for pasting your current `FormLauncher`. Right now it’s creating the `PaletteSet` **and** adding `TagManagerPaletteHost` as a child control. That’s fine *if* `TagManagerPaletteHost` is a plain `UserControl` with no palette logic.  

But earlier we also gave a version where **`TagManagerPaletteHost` owns the PaletteSet** and exposes `Open()`. You should pick **one** owner to avoid conflicts and weird empty palettes.

## Pick one pattern (recommended = A)

### A) Host owns the palette (cleanest)
- `FormLauncher` becomes a one-liner.
- `TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()` creates/maintains the singleton `PaletteSet`.

**FormLauncher.cs**
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    public static class FormLauncher
    {
        public static void ShowTagManager()
            => AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerPaletteHost.Open();
    }
}
```

**TagManagerPaletteHost.cs** (partial; your designer file provides `InitializeComponent()` and the actual `TagManagerControl` is your real UI)
```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent(); // must exist in the *.Designer.cs
        }

        public static void Open()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ui = new TagManagerControl { Dock = DockStyle.Fill };

                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size = new Size(520, 600)
                };
                _ps.Add("Home", _ui);

                // optional persistence hooks if you have them
                // AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.LoadState(_ps, PaletteName);
                // AutoCAD2020.Utilities.PaletteStateManager.AttachAutoSave(_ps, PaletteName);
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            _ps.Activate(0);
            _ui.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();    // your existing refresh method
        }
    }
}
```

### B) FormLauncher owns the palette (then Host is *just* a UserControl)
- Keep your current `FormLauncher` (the one you pasted).
- Ensure `TagManagerPaletteHost` is **only** a `UserControl` (no static `Open()`, no `PaletteSet` references inside it).

If you stick with **B**, delete any static `Open()`/palette code from `TagManagerPaletteHost` so you don’t have two places trying to manage the same palette.

---

## What I’d do now
- Choose **A** (simpler, matches your “commands → launcher → host” layering).
- Replace `FormLauncher.ShowTagManager()` with the one-liner above.
- Keep all palette creation inside `TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()` exactly as shown.

If you do that, your `CBI_TagManager` command path stays a tidy one-liner and the Tag Manager should come back fully populated (no more “placeholder” panel).

## 2) Fix `InitializeComponent()` missing in `TagManagerPaletteHost`
Because we changed the host to a `UserControl` but you don’t have a `.Designer.cs`, define a tiny `InitializeComponent()` so the constructor compiles:

```csharp
// Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private const string PaletteName = "CBI Tag Manager";
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public TagManagerPaletteHost()
        {
            InitializeComponent(); // simple stub below
        }

        public static void Open()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ui = new TagManagerControl { Dock = DockStyle.Fill };

                _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
                {
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
                    Size = new Size(520, 600)
                };
                _ps.Add("Home", _ui);
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            _ps.Activate(0);
            _ui?.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();
        }

        // Minimal stub so constructor compiles without a Designer file
        private void InitializeComponent()
        {
            this.Name = nameof(TagManagerPaletteHost);
            this.Size = new Size(200, 100);
        }
    }
}
```

## Response:
11/3/2025, 10:54:47 AM

> **Planning selection handling**
>
> I
>
> Thought for 22s

You’re right—the palette is rendering, but the “brains” (project load + selection reactors) didn’t get re-hooked after the refactor. Two things usually power your Tag Manager:

1) it loads the active **Project Store** when the palette opens or the active document changes, and  
2) it listens for **selection changes** and tells the UI to show data for the currently selected block(s).

Here’s a compact, drop-in wiring that restores both, while keeping your one-liner command + FormLauncher clean.

---

# 1) Add a tiny wiring service
Create **`Services/TagManagerWiring.cs`**:

```csharp
// v0.2 — Tag Manager event wiring
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
{
    internal static class TagManagerWiring
    {
        private static bool _attached;
        private static AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerControl _ui;

        public static void Attach(AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager.TagManagerControl ui)
        {
            if (ui == null) return;
            _ui = ui;

            if (_attached) return;
            _attached = true;

            var dm = Application.DocumentManager;
            dm.DocumentActivated += (_, __) => SafeLoadProjectAndRefresh();
            dm.DocumentDestroyed += (_, __) => SafeLoadProjectAndRefresh();

            // Also attach to current editor selection notifications
            var doc = dm.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc != null)
            {
                var ed = doc.Editor;
                ed.SelectionAdded += OnSelectionChanged;
                ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSelectionChanged;
                ed.ImpliedSelectionChanged += OnImpliedSelectionChanged;
            }

            // initial load on first attach
            SafeLoadProjectAndRefresh();
            RefreshFromCurrentSelection();
        }

        private static void SafeLoadProjectAndRefresh()
        {
            try { _ui?.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore(); } catch { }
            RefreshFromCurrentSelection();
        }

        private static void OnImpliedSelectionChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
            => RefreshFromCurrentSelection();

        private static void OnSelectionChanged(object sender, SelectionAddedEventArgs e)
            => RefreshFromCurrentSelection();

        private static void RefreshFromCurrentSelection()
        {
            try
            {
                var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null || _ui == null) return;

                var ed = doc.Editor;
                ed.SelectImplied(); // updates implied
                var res = ed.SelectImplied();

                var ids = (res.Status == PromptStatus.OK)
                    ? res.Value.GetObjectIds()
                    : System.Array.Empty<Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices.ObjectId>();

                _ui.ShowForSelection(ids); // your control’s method
            }
            catch { /* never throw from reactors */ }
        }
    }
}
```

> This expects your `TagManagerControl` to expose `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()` and `ShowForSelection(ObjectId[] ids)` like before. If they’re named slightly differently, just adjust the two calls.

---

# 2) Call the wiring when the palette opens
Update **`Forms/TagManager/TagManagerPaletteHost.cs`** (only the `Open()` body changes):

```csharp
public static void Open()
{
    if (_ps == null)
    {
        _ui = new TagManagerControl { Dock = DockStyle.Fill };

        _ps = new PaletteSet(PaletteName)
        {
            Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                    PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                    PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
            DockEnabled = DockSides.Left | DockSides.Right,
            MinimumSize = new Size(300, 220),
            Size = new Size(520, 600)
        };
        _ps.Add("Home", _ui);

        // <-- re-attach the brains
        AutoCAD2020.Services.TagManagerWiring.Attach(_ui);
    }

    _ps.Visible = true;
    _ps.Activate(0);
    _ui?.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();  // ensure project shown immediately
}
```

(Keep the small `InitializeComponent()` stub we added so the constructor compiles.)

---

# 3) Verify your control surface still has these entry points

Inside **`TagManagerControl`** (the user control you already have), make sure these public methods exist:

```csharp
public void TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()
{
    // 1) read active project key (e.g., ProjectSettingsService)
    // 2) populate left panel (systems / tags list) from the store
    // 3) clear selection details until a selection arrives
}

public void ShowForSelection(Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices.ObjectId[] ids)
{
    // 1) open a tr; inspect first/each selected BlockReference
    // 2) read attributes/xdata into your view-model
    // 3) update the right-side property panel
}
```

If they already exist from the old implementation, you’re good—the wiring will call them again.

---

# 4) Common “project didn’t load” causes (quick checks)

- The path your `ProjectSettingsService` uses changed. Confirm it still points to your `Project_Store.json` and that the active project key is set.
- `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()` silently returns on exceptions. Add a one-line `ed.WriteMessage("\n[TagMgr] Loaded project: {0}", key);` while testing.
- The selection events were never reattached (fixed by the wiring above).

---

# 5) Quick test

1. Run `CBI_TagManager` → palette opens.  
2. Click a known project DWG where your `Project_Store.json` is valid. The left pane should populate after `Open()` (the second call to `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()`), or when you switch documents.  
3. Click a valve/tag block → the right pane should populate via `ShowForSelection`.

If `ShowForSelection` doesn’t fire, watch the command line—each click should trigger the reactor path. If not, we might need to also handle `Editor.SelectionChanged` (older ACADs) — easy to add.

---

If you paste your `TagManagerControl` public method signatures (just the method names), I’ll align the calls precisely.

```csharp
}
```

With those two in place, the `TagManagerWiring.Attach(_ui)` I gave earlier will:
- load the project when the palette opens / document changes,
- and update the right pane when you click a block.

---

### Quick sanity checklist
- `TagManagerPaletteHost.Open()` calls `TagManagerWiring.Attach(_ui)` (you added this).
- `TagManagerControl` now has the two public methods above (or your equivalents).
- Your `Project_Store.json` path is correct and has an **ActiveProjectKey** set.
- A block click triggers `ShowForSelection` (you can `ed.WriteMessage("\n[TagMgr] selection fired")` inside the wiring to confirm).

---

If you paste just the method headers (no private internals) from `TagManagerControl` and the class you use to load the project/tag data, I’ll tailor the wiring calls exactly to your API so functionality returns 1:1 with your old behavior.

using System.IO;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD2020.Services;
using DocumentFormat.OpenXml;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Data;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerControl : System.Windows.Forms.UserControl
    {
        private ProjectTagStore _store;
        private BindingList<TagRow> _rows = new BindingList<TagRow>();
        private string _storePath;

        public TagManagerControl()
        {
            InitializeComponent();

            // Make the grid columns once
            if (dgvTags.Columns.Count == 0)
            {
                dgvTags.AutoGenerateColumns = false;

                dgvTags.Columns.Add(new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
                {
                    Name = "colId",
                    HeaderText = "Id",
                    DataPropertyName = "Id",
                    AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.Fill,
                    FillWeight = 40
                });
                dgvTags.Columns.Add(new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
                {
                    Name = "colSystem",
                    HeaderText = "System",
                    DataPropertyName = "System",
                    Width = 120
                });
                dgvTags.Columns.Add(new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
                {
                    Name = "colStatus",
                    HeaderText = "Status",
                    DataPropertyName = "Status",
                    Width = 120
                });
                dgvTags.Columns.Add(new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
                {
                    Name = "colDrawing",
                    HeaderText = "Drawing",
                    DataPropertyName = "DrawingName",
                    AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.Fill,
                    FillWeight = 35
                });
                dgvTags.Columns.Add(new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
                {
                    Name = "colLastSeen",
                    HeaderText = "Last Seen (UTC)",
                    DataPropertyName = "LastSeenUtc",
                    Width = 150
                });
            }

            dgvTags.AutoGenerateColumns = false;
            // Define a few columns: Id, System, Status, DrawingName, LastSeenUtc
            dgvTags.DataSource = _rows;

            dgvTags.SelectionChanged += (s, e) => ShowSelectionDetails();
            btnLoadJson.Click += (s, e) => BrowseAndLoad();
            btnOpenFolder.Click += (s, e) => OpenFolder();
            btnReconcile.Click += (s, e) => ReconcileCurrentDwg();
            btnRelease.Click += (s, e) => ReleaseSelected();
            cboSystem.SelectedIndexChanged += (s, e) => ApplyFilters();
            cboStatus.SelectedIndexChanged += (s, e) => ApplyFilters();
            txtSearch.TextChanged += (s, e) => ApplyFilters();
        }

        public void TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            using (var tr = doc.Database.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                _storePath = ProjectTagPath.GetPath(doc.Database, tr);
                tr.Commit();
            }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_storePath) && File.Exists(_storePath))
            {
                LoadStore(_storePath);
            }
            else
            {
                SetHeader("No project loaded", "");
                _rows.Clear();
            }
        }

        private void BrowseAndLoad()
        {
            using (var ofd = new OpenFileDialog { Filter = "JSON files|*.json" })
            {
                if (ofd.ShowDialog() != DialogResult.OK) return;
                LoadStore(ofd.FileName);
            }
        }

        private void LoadStore(string path)
        {
            try
            {
                _storePath = path;
                _store = ProjectTagStore.LoadOrCreate(path);

                var projName = _store?.Project?.Name ?? System.IO.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
                SetHeader(projName, path);

                // Populate filters
                var systems = _store.Systems.Keys.OrderBy(x => x).ToList();
                systems.Insert(0, "(All)");
                cboSystem.ComboBox.DataSource = systems;    // <-- note ComboBox

                var statuses = new[] { "(All)", "Active", "MissingInDWG", "Released", "Reserved", "Superseded" };
                cboStatus.ComboBox.DataSource = statuses;   // <-- note ComboBox

                // (optional) lock editing
                cboSystem.ComboBox.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList;
                cboStatus.ComboBox.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList;

                // Project to grid rows
                _rows.Clear();
                foreach (var t in _store.Tags)
                    _rows.Add(TagRow.From(t));

                ApplyFilters();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                MessageBox.Show($"Failed to load tag store:\n{ex.Message}", "CBI Tag Manager");
            }
        }

        private void SetHeader(string projectName, string path)
        {
            lblProjectName.Text = projectName;
            lblPath.Text = path;
        }

        private void ApplyFilters()
        {
            if (_store == null) return;
            var sys = cboSystem.SelectedItem as string;
            var st = cboStatus.SelectedItem as string;
            var q = txtSearch.Text?.Trim();

            var filtered = _store.Tags.AsEnumerable();

            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(sys) && sys != "(All)")
                filtered = filtered.Where(t => string.Equals(t.System, sys, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));

            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(st) && st != "(All)")
                filtered = filtered.Where(t => string.Equals(t.Status, st, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));

            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(q))
                filtered = filtered.Where(t => (t.Id ?? "").IndexOf(q, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0
                                             || (t.DrawingName ?? "").IndexOf(q, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0);

            _rows.RaiseListChangedEvents = false;
            _rows.Clear();
            foreach (var t in filtered)
                _rows.Add(TagRow.From(t));
            _rows.RaiseListChangedEvents = true;
            _rows.ResetBindings();
        }

        private void ShowSelectionDetails()
        {
            if (dgvTags.CurrentRow?.DataBoundItem is TagRow r)
            {
                // You can show a PropertyGrid bound to the original TagRecord for rich view:
                var rec = _store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => t.Id == r.Id && t.DrawingPath == r.DrawingPath && t.Number == r.Number);
                propDetails.SelectedObject = rec; // or populate a details panel
                btnRelease.Enabled = (rec != null && rec.Status == "MissingInDWG");
            }
            else
            {
                propDetails.SelectedObject = null;
                btnRelease.Enabled = false;
            }
        }

        private void OpenFolder()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_storePath)) return;
            var folder = Path.GetDirectoryName(_storePath);
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(folder))
                System.Diagnostics.Process.Start(new System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo { FileName = folder, UseShellExecute = true });
        }

        private void ReconcileCurrentDwg()
        {
            // v1: keep it simple. Call your existing reconcile service here if you want,
            // or stub: mark MissingInDWG for any tag whose DrawingPath == current doc and handles not found.
            // After reconcile, reload store:
            // AutoCAD2020.Services.TagReclaimService.ReconcileCurrentDwg();  (you can make this)
            // For now:
            MessageBox.Show("Reconcile pass not wired yet in MVP.\nWe’ll hook this to TagReclaimService next.", "CBI");
        }

        private void ReleaseSelected()
        {
            if (_store == null) return;
            if (!(dgvTags.CurrentRow?.DataBoundItem is TagRow r)) return;

            var rec = _store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => t.Id == r.Id && t.DrawingPath == r.DrawingPath && t.Number == r.Number);
            if (rec == null) return;

            // Minimal release UX
            var confirm = MessageBox.Show($"Release {rec.Id} in system {rec.System} back to pool?",
                                          "CBI", MessageBoxButtons.YesNo);
            if (confirm != DialogResult.Yes) return;

            _store.ReleaseNumber(rec.System, rec.Number, "Manual release from Tag Manager");
            rec.Status = "Released";
            _store.Save(_storePath);
            LoadStore(_storePath); // refresh
        }

        // Row projection (keeps the grid light)
        private class TagRow
        {
            public string Id { get; set; }
            public string System { get; set; }
            public string Status { get; set; }
            public string DrawingName { get; set; }
            public string DrawingPath { get; set; }
            public string LastSeenUtc { get; set; }
            public int Number { get; set; }

            public static TagRow From(AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectTagStore.TagRecord t) => new TagRow
            {
                Id = t.Id,
                System = t.System,
                Status = t.Status,
                DrawingName = t.DrawingName ?? Path.GetFileName(t.DrawingPath),
                DrawingPath = t.DrawingPath,
                LastSeenUtc = t.LastSeenUtc?.ToString("u"),
                Number = t.Number
            };
        }
    }
}

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms
{
    public partial class BlockAttribPropPaletteControl : UserControl
    {
        //private IAcSmDatabase _currentDatabase; // Add this as a class-level variable

        // Animation Controls
        private Timer animationTimer;
        private int originalPanelWidth;
        private bool isCollapsed = false;
        private const int AnimationSpeed = 10;   // Smaller = faster
        private const int StepSize = 5;          // Width decrement per tick

        // Palette Reference
        private PaletteSet _paletteSet;
        private int originalPaletteWidth;
        private IAcSmDatabase _currentDatabase; // Add this as a class-level variable

        private WinFormsControl fadingOutGroup;
        private WinFormsControl fadingInGroup;
        private float currentAlpha = 1f;
        private bool fadingOutCurrent = true;

public BlockAttribPropPaletteControl(PaletteSet paletteSet)
        {
            InitializeComponent();         // Controls are defined in Designer
            splitContainer1.Panel1MinSize = 0; // set the width of the splitter to 0 to override the controls default min width
            trvDrawings.Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            _paletteSet = paletteSet;      // Store reference to palette
            SetupUI();                     // Hook up animation and event logic
        }

        private void SetupUI()
        {
            animationTimer = new Timer { Interval = AnimationSpeed }; animationTimer.Tick += AnimationTimer_Tick;

            btnShow.Click += BtnShow_Click;

            trvDrawings.CheckBoxes = true;
            grpCurrentDrawingOptions.Visible = true;
            grpBatchDrawingOptions.Visible = false;
            originalPanelWidth = splitContainer1.Panel1.Width;

        }
        //    animationTimer = new Timer 
        //{

//    //// Setup animation timer
        //    //animationTimer = new Timer { Interval = AnimationSpeed };
        //    //animationTimer.Tick += AnimationTimer_Tick;

        //    //btnApplyIndividual.Click += btnApplyIndividual_Click;
        //    //btnApplyGlobal.Click += btnApplyGlobal_Click;

        //    //// Hook up collapse button event
        //    //btnShow.Click += BtnShow_Click;

        //    //// Enable checkboxes on tree view
        //    //trvDrawings.CheckBoxes = true;
        //}

        private void AnimationTimer_Tick(object sender, EventArgs e)
        { // Animate Splitter if (!isCollapsed) { if (splitContainer1.SplitterDistance > StepSize) { splitContainer1.SplitterDistance -= StepSize; } else { splitContainer1.SplitterDistance = 0; isCollapsed = true; } } else { if (splitContainer1.SplitterDistance + StepSize < originalPanelWidth) { splitContainer1.SplitterDistance += StepSize; } else { splitContainer1.SplitterDistance = originalPanelWidth; isCollapsed = false; } }
            float percent = Math.Max(0f,Math.Min(1f,(float)splitContainer1.SplitterDistance / originalPanelWidth));
            SetGroupOpacity(grpCurrentDrawingOptions, percent);
            SetGroupOpacity(grpBatchDrawingOptions, 1f - percent);

            if ((isCollapsed && splitContainer1.SplitterDistance <= 0) ||
                (!isCollapsed && splitContainer1.SplitterDistance >= originalPanelWidth))
            {
                animationTimer.Stop();
                grpCurrentDrawingOptions.Visible = !isCollapsed;
                grpBatchDrawingOptions.Visible = isCollapsed;
            }

        }

        //{
        //if (!isCollapsed)
        //{
        //    // Collapse
        //    if (splitContainer1.SplitterDistance > StepSize)
        //    {
        //        splitContainer1.SplitterDistance -= StepSize;
        //    }
        //    else
        //    {
        //        splitContainer1.SplitterDistance = 0;

        //        isCollapsed = true;
        //        btnShow.Text = "}-->"; // Indicate expand
        //    }
        //}
        //else
        //{
        //    // Expand
        //    if (splitContainer1.SplitterDistance + StepSize < originalPanelWidth)
        //    {
        //        splitContainer1.SplitterDistance += StepSize;
