// Namespace: AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Models
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Models
{
    /// <summary>Lightweight link stamped on the label (in its ExtensionDictionary).</summary>
    public sealed class LabelLinkData
    {
        public string SourceHandle;  // hex handle of source polyline
        public double Param;         // curve parameter
        public double OffsetX;       // WCS offset from point-on-curve
        public double OffsetY;
        public bool RotateAlong;     // align text to tangent

        public const string DictKey = "CBI_LABEL_LINK";
        public const string XRecName = "Link";

        public static bool TryRead(Transaction tr, DBObject labelObj, out LabelLinkData data)
        {
            data = null;
            if (!(labelObj is Entity e) || e.ExtensionDictionary == ObjectId.Null) return false;

            var dict = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(e.ExtensionDictionary, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!dict.Contains(DictKey)) return false;

            var subId = dict.GetAt(DictKey);
            var subDict = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(subId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!subDict.Contains(XRecName)) return false;

            var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(subDict.GetAt(XRecName), OpenMode.ForRead);
            using (var rb = xr.Data)
            {
                var a = rb?.AsArray();
                if (a == null || a.Length < 5) return false;

                data = new LabelLinkData
                {
                    SourceHandle = a[0].Value as string,
                    Param = (double)a[1].Value,
                    OffsetX = (double)a[2].Value,
                    OffsetY = (double)a[3].Value,
                    RotateAlong = ((short)a[4].Value) != 0
                };
                return true;
            }
        }

        public void Write(Transaction tr, DBObject labelObj)
        {
            var e = (Entity)labelObj;
            if (e.ExtensionDictionary == ObjectId.Null)
            {
                e.UpgradeOpen();
                e.CreateExtensionDictionary();
            }

            var dict = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(e.ExtensionDictionary, OpenMode.ForWrite);

            ObjectId subId;
            if (!dict.Contains(DictKey))
            {
                var sub = new DBDictionary();
                subId = dict.SetAt(DictKey, sub);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(sub, true);
            }
            else subId = dict.GetAt(DictKey);

            var subDict = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(subId, OpenMode.ForWrite);

            Xrecord xr;
            if (subDict.Contains(XRecName))
                xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(subDict.GetAt(XRecName), OpenMode.ForWrite);
            else
            {
                xr = new Xrecord();
                var id = subDict.SetAt(XRecName, xr);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
            }

            xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(
                new TypedValue(1000, SourceHandle ?? ""),
                new TypedValue(1040, Param),
                new TypedValue(1040, OffsetX),
                new TypedValue(1040, OffsetY),
                new TypedValue(1070, (short)(RotateAlong ? 1 : 0))
            );
        }
    }
}

// v1.2.0 (C# 7.3)
using System;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Abstractions;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public class NodLineNumberStore : ITagNumberingStore
    {
        private const string RootKey = "CBI_TAG_COUNTERS";

        public NodLineNumberStore() { }

        // --- ITagNumberingStore ---
        public int Next(string scopeKey)
        {
            var current = Peek(scopeKey);
            var next = current + 1;
            SetValue(scopeKey, next);
            return next;
        }

        public int Peek(string scopeKey)
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return 0;
            var db = doc.Database;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!nod.Contains(RootKey)) { tr.Commit(); return 0; }

                var sub = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(RootKey), OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!sub.Contains(scopeKey)) { tr.Commit(); return 0; }

                var xrec = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(sub.GetAt(scopeKey), OpenMode.ForRead);
                var data = xrec.Data?.AsArray();
                tr.Commit();

                if (data != null && data.Length > 0 && data[0].TypeCode == 90)
                    return Convert.ToInt32(data[0].Value);
                return 0;
            }
        }

        public void SetValue(string scopeKey, int value)
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var db = doc.Database;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                DBDictionary sub;

                if (!nod.Contains(RootKey))
                {
                    nod.UpgradeOpen();
                    sub = new DBDictionary();
                    nod.SetAt(RootKey, sub);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(sub, true);
                }
                else
                {
                    sub = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(RootKey), OpenMode.ForRead);
                }

                if (!sub.Contains(scopeKey))
                {
                    sub.UpgradeOpen();
                    var xrec = new Xrecord { Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue(90, value)) };
                    sub.SetAt(scopeKey, xrec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xrec, true);
                }
                else
                {
                    var xrec = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(sub.GetAt(scopeKey), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                    xrec.Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue(90, value));
                }

                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        // ---- Back-compat shims (old callers) ----
        public void SetLineNextNumber(int next) => SetValue("LINE_DEFAULT", next);
        public void SetValveNextNumber(int next) => SetValue("VALVE_DEFAULT", next);
        public int Peek(string scopeKey, string _context, int defaultValue)
        {
            var v = Peek(scopeKey);
            return v == 0 ? defaultValue : v;
        }
    }
}

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectNodBinder
    {
        // Writes: NOD["CBI"]["Project"] = Xrecord( Id, Name, StorePath )
        public static void BindProjectToDrawing(Document doc, string projectId, string name, string storePath)
        {
            if (doc == null) return;
            var db = doc.Database;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);

                DBDictionary cbi;
                if (nod.Contains("CBI"))
                {
                    cbi = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt("CBI"), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                }
                else
                {
                    nod.UpgradeOpen();
                    cbi = new DBDictionary();
                    nod.SetAt("CBI", cbi);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(cbi, true);
                }

                var data = new ResultBuffer(
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, projectId ?? string.Empty),
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, name ?? string.Empty),
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, storePath ?? string.Empty)
                );

                if (cbi.Contains("Project"))
                {
                    var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(cbi.GetAt("Project"), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                    xr.Data = data;
                }
                else
                {
                    var xr = new Xrecord { Data = data };
                    cbi.SetAt("Project", xr);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
                }

                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Manages drawing-wide tag index and sequences in the NOD.
    /// NOD["CBI_TAG_ADMIN"] contains:
    ///  - Xrec "INDEX":  [ id, handle, id, handle, ... ]
    ///  - Xrec "SEQ":    [ scope, nextIntString, scope, nextIntString, ... ]
    /// </summary>
    public static class TagIndexUtil
    {
        private const string NodRoot = "CBI_TAG_ADMIN";
        private const string KeyIndex = "INDEX";
        private const string KeySeq = "SEQ";

        // ---- INDEX: id <-> handle ------------------------------------------------

        public static bool TryRegisterId(Transaction tr, string id, string handleHex, out string existingHandle)
        {
            id = (id ?? "").Trim();
            existingHandle = null;
            if (id.Length == 0 || handleHex == null) return false;

            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeyIndex);
            // Remove any stale mapping for this handle (in case we renamed the id)
            RemoveHandle(list, handleHex);

            // Check if id already exists
            int idx = FindKey(list, id);
            if (idx >= 0)
            {
                existingHandle = list[idx + 1].Value as string;
                // If it points to the same handle, it's fine (id unchanged on same object)
                if (string.Equals(existingHandle, handleHex, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
                    return true;
                }
                // Duplicate on another object
                xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
                return false;
            }

            // Insert new pair
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, id));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, handleHex));
            xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
            return true;
        }

        public static void UnregisterId(Transaction tr, string id)
        {
            id = (id ?? "").Trim();
            if (id.Length == 0) return;

            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeyIndex);
            int idx = FindKey(list, id);
            if (idx >= 0)
            {
                list.RemoveAt(idx + 1);
                list.RemoveAt(idx);
                xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
            }
        }

        public static string FindHandleById(Transaction tr, string id)
        {
            id = (id ?? "").Trim();
            if (id.Length == 0) return null;

            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeyIndex);
            int idx = FindKey(list, id);
            return idx >= 0 ? list[idx + 1].Value as string : null;
        }

        // ---- SEQ: scopes and next numbers ---------------------------------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Gets and increments the next number for a given scope. Returns the allocated int.
        /// </summary>
        public static int AllocateNext(Transaction tr, string scope)
        {
            scope = NormalizeScope(scope);
            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeySeq);

            int idx = FindKey(list, scope);
            int value = 1;
            if (idx >= 0)
            {
                int.TryParse(list[idx + 1].Value as string, out value);
                list[idx + 1] = new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, (value + 1).ToString());
            }
            else
            {
                // start at 2 after allocating 1
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, scope));
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, "2"));
                value = 1;
            }

            xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
            return value;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Peek without incrementing. If missing, returns 1.
        /// </summary>
        public static int PeekNext(Transaction tr, string scope)
        {
            scope = NormalizeScope(scope);
            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeySeq);
            int idx = FindKey(list, scope);
            if (idx >= 0 && int.TryParse(list[idx + 1].Value as string, out var next))
                return next;
            return 1;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Set next value explicitly (e.g., admin correction).
        /// </summary>
        public static void SetNext(Transaction tr, string scope, int next)
        {
            scope = NormalizeScope(scope);
            var (xr, list) = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeySeq);
            int idx = FindKey(list, scope);
            if (idx >= 0) list[idx + 1] = new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, next.ToString());
            else
            {
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, scope));
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, next.ToString()));
            }
            xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }

        // ---- Formatting helpers --------------------------------------------------

        public static string FormatId(string prefix, int number, int pad = 0, string separator = "-")
        {
            var num = pad > 0 ? number.ToString(new string('0', pad)) : number.ToString();
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(prefix)) return num;
            return string.IsNullOrEmpty(separator) ? (prefix + num) : (prefix + separator + num);
        }

        // ---- Internals -----------------------------------------------------------

        private static Tuple<Xrecord, List<TypedValue>> GetOrCreateXrec(Transaction tr, string xrecName)
        {
            var db = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Database;
            var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);

            DBDictionary root;
            if (!nod.Contains(NodRoot))
            {
                nod.UpgradeOpen();
                root = new DBDictionary();
                nod.SetAt(NodRoot, root);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(root, true);
            }
            else root = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(NodRoot), OpenMode.ForRead);

            Xrecord xr;
            if (!root.Contains(xrecName))
            {
                root.UpgradeOpen();
                xr = new Xrecord { Data = new ResultBuffer() };
                root.SetAt(xrecName, xr);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
            }
            else xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(root.GetAt(xrecName), OpenMode.ForWrite);

            var list = xr.Data != null ? xr.Data.AsArray().ToList() : new List<TypedValue>();
            return Tuple.Create(xr, list);
        }

        private static int FindKey(List<TypedValue> list, string key)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i + 1 < list.Count; i += 2)
            {
                if (list[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                    string.Equals(list[i].Value as string, key, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    return i;
            }
            return -1;
        }

        private static void RemoveHandle(List<TypedValue> list, string handleHex)
        {
            for (int i = 0; i + 1 < list.Count; i += 2)
            {
                var val = list[i + 1].Value as string;
                if (string.Equals(val, handleHex, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    list.RemoveAt(i + 1);
                    list.RemoveAt(i);
                    return;
                }
            }
        }

        public static IEnumerable<(string Id, string HandleHex)> EnumerateIndex(Transaction tr)
        {
            var tuple = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeyIndex);
            var list = tuple.Item2;
            for (int i = 0; i + 1 < list.Count; i += 2)
            {
                var id = list[i].Value as string;
                var hex = list[i + 1].Value as string;
                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(hex))
                    yield return (id, hex);
            }
        }

        public static IEnumerable<(string Scope, int Next)> EnumerateSequences(Transaction tr)
        {
            var tuple = GetOrCreateXrec(tr, KeySeq);
            var list = tuple.Item2;
            for (int i = 0; i + 1 < list.Count; i += 2)
            {
                var scope = list[i].Value as string ?? "GLOBAL";
                var nextStr = list[i + 1].Value as string ?? "1";
                int next = 1;
                int.TryParse(nextStr, out next);
                yield return (scope, next);
            }
        }

private static string NormalizeScope(string s)
            => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) ? "GLOBAL" : s.Trim();
    }
}

// XDataHelpers.cs  (C# 7.3)
// Shared helpers for reading/writing simple key/value XData under a RegApp.
// Layout written: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]…
// NOW: write is merge-safe (preserves other apps) + auto-normalizes our chunk.

using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;   // Document, Application, LockDocument()
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;      // ObjectId, Entity, ResultBuffer, RegAppTable, etc.

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    /// <summary>Lightweight map with a convenience getter.</summary>
    public sealed class XDataMap : Dictionary<string, string>
    {
        public string Get(string key) => TryGetValue(key, out var v) ? v : null;
    }

    public static partial class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>True if the entity has any XData for the given app name.</summary>
        public static bool HasAppName(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return false;
            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return false;
                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                return rb != null && rb.AsArray().Length > 0;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Reads app-specific XData into a string→string map. Missing/invalid returns empty map.</summary>
        public static XDataMap Read(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            var map = new XDataMap();
            if (id.IsNull) return map;

            using (var tr = id.Database.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) return map;

                var rb = ent.GetXDataForApplication(app);
                if (rb == null) return map;

                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                // Expect: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 value]...
                for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i += 2)
                {
                    if (arr[i].TypeCode == 1000 && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == 1000)
                    {
                        var k = arr[i].Value as string;
                        var v = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                        if (k != null) map[k] = v ?? string.Empty;
                    }
                }
            }
            return map;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Writes the map as XData (1001 app + 1000 key/value pairs).
        /// **Merge-safe**: preserves all other regapps; replaces only this app’s chunk.
        /// Auto-normalizes any legacy/bad pairs for this app by fully rewriting it.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Write(ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return;

            var db = id.Database;
            if (db == null) return;

            var doc = FindDocumentFor(db);

            // Lock when we can (palette/modeless safe); otherwise best-effort write.
            if (doc != null)
            {
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                {
                    WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
                }
            }
            else
            {
                WriteCore(db, id, app, map);
            }
        }

        // ---------- internals ----------

        private static void WriteCore(Database db, ObjectId id, string app, XDataMap map)
        {
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                // Ensure the RegApp exists in THIS transaction
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(app))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = app };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }

                // Open the entity for write even if its layer is locked
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                // Build our clean chunk: [1001 app][1000 key][1000 val]...
                var ourChunk = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
                if (map != null)
                {
                    foreach (var kv in map)
                    {
                        var k = kv.Key ?? string.Empty;
                        var v = kv.Value ?? string.Empty;
                        ourChunk.Add(new TypedValue(1000, k));
                        ourChunk.Add(new TypedValue(1000, v));
                    }
                }

                // Merge: keep all other apps; drop any existing chunk for this app; append ours.
                var merged = BuildMergedXData(ent.XData, app, ourChunk);

                ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(merged.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Creates a merged list of TypedValues that preserves all other apps,
        /// removes the target app’s existing chunk (if any), then appends ourChunk.
        /// </summary>
        private static List<TypedValue> BuildMergedXData(ResultBuffer existing, string app, List<TypedValue> ourChunk)
        {
            var result = new List<TypedValue>();

            if (existing != null)
            {
                var arr = existing.AsArray();
                string currentApp = null;
                bool skip = false;

                for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
                {
                    var tv = arr[i];
                    if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                    {
                        // new app section starts
                        currentApp = tv.Value as string;
                        skip = string.Equals(currentApp, app, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                        if (!skip) result.Add(tv); // keep other app headers
                        continue;
                    }

                    if (!skip)
                    {
                        // keep items belonging to other apps
                        result.Add(tv);
                    }
                    // else: we're skipping old values for our app
                }
            }

            // finally, append our clean, normalized chunk
            result.AddRange(ourChunk);
            return result;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Resolves the owning Document for a Database without using Database.OwnerDocument.
        /// Works across MDI; returns null if not found.
        /// </summary>
        private static Document FindDocumentFor(Database db)
        {
            if (db == null) return null;
            var dm = Application.DocumentManager;
            foreach (Document d in dm)
            {
                try { if (d != null && d.Database == db) return d; } catch { }
            }
            return null;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Ensures a RegApp exists (utility if you need it separately).
        /// </summary>
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, string appName)
        {
            if (db == null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(appName)) return;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var rat = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!rat.Has(appName))
                {
                    rat.UpgradeOpen();
                    var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                    rat.Add(rec);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}

// File: AutoCAD2020/PropertiesHub/XDataHelpers.Raw.cs
// v1.1 — low-level “raw” helpers used by QA/QC tools
// NOTE: pairs with XDataHelpers.cs (both are `partial`).

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.PropertiesHub
{
    public static partial class XDataHelpers
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Read *all* XData on an entity and group by regapp name.
        /// Returns: app -> list of TypedValue (NOT including the 1001 header).
        /// </summary>
        public static Dictionary<string, List<TypedValue>> ReadRawPerApp(Entity ent)
        {
            var perApp = new Dictionary<string, List<TypedValue>>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (ent == null) return perApp;

            var rb = ent.XData;
            if (rb == null) return perApp;

            var arr = rb.AsArray();
            string curApp = null;
            List<TypedValue> buf = null;

            for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
            {
                var tv = arr[i];
                if (tv.TypeCode == 1001) // regapp marker
                {
                    curApp = tv.Value as string ?? string.Empty;
                    if (!perApp.TryGetValue(curApp, out buf))
                    {
                        buf = new List<TypedValue>();
                        perApp[curApp] = buf;
                    }
                    continue;
                }

                if (curApp != null && buf != null)
                    buf.Add(tv);      // store items under current app
            }

            return perApp;
        }

        /// <summary>Overload that accepts an ObjectId and opens the entity for read.</summary>
        public static Dictionary<string, List<TypedValue>> ReadRawPerApp(ObjectId id)
        {
            var result = new Dictionary<string, List<TypedValue>>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            if (id.IsNull) return result;

            var db = id.Database;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity;
                if (ent != null) result = ReadRawPerApp(ent);
            }
            return result;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Write a raw XData section for the given app (exact items you pass),
        /// while preserving other apps. Items here are the tokens AFTER 1001.
        /// </summary>
        public static void WriteRaw(Entity ent, string app, IEnumerable<TypedValue> items)
        {
            if (ent == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(app)) return;

            // Ensure regapp exists (defined in XDataHelpers.cs)
            EnsureRegApp(ent.Database, app);

            // Build our chunk: [1001 app] + items...
            var ourChunk = new List<TypedValue> { new TypedValue(1001, app) };
            if (items != null) ourChunk.AddRange(items);

            // Merge against existing: keep others, drop old app chunk, add ours.
            var merged = BuildMergedXData(ent.XData, app, ourChunk);
            ent.UpgradeOpen();
            ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(merged.ToArray());
        }

        /// <summary>Overload that accepts an ObjectId and opens the entity for write.</summary>
        public static void WriteRaw(ObjectId id, string app, IEnumerable<TypedValue> items)
        {
            if (id.IsNull) return;
            var db = id.Database;

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent != null) WriteRaw(ent, app, items);
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        /// <summary>Remove an app’s XData section entirely (preserve other apps).</summary>
        public static void ClearApp(ObjectId id, string app)
        {
            if (id.IsNull || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(app)) return;

            var db = id.Database;
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForWrite, openErased: false, forceOpenOnLockedLayer: true) as Entity;
                if (ent == null) { tr.Abort(); return; }

                var existing = ent.XData;
                if (existing == null) { tr.Commit(); return; }

                var arr = existing.AsArray();
                var result = new List<TypedValue>();

                string cur = null;
                bool skipping = false;
                for (int i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++)
                {
                    var tv = arr[i];
                    if (tv.TypeCode == 1001)
                    {
                        cur = tv.Value as string;
                        skipping = string.Equals(cur, app, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
                        if (!skipping) result.Add(tv); // keep other app headers
                        continue;
                    }
                    if (!skipping) result.Add(tv);
                }

                ent.XData = new ResultBuffer(result.ToArray());
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }
    }
}

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Project: CBI Tools
// Module : XDataLinkUtil.cs
// Notes  : Helpers for reading/writing XData links on valves/bubbles.
// Target : C# 7.3
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.Linq;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations
{
    public static class XDataLinkUtil
    {
        // ---------------- RegApp ----------------
        public static void EnsureRegApp(Database db, Transaction tr, string appName)
        {
            var reg = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!reg.Has(appName))
            {
                reg.UpgradeOpen();
                var rec = new RegAppTableRecord { Name = appName };
                reg.Add(rec);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(rec, true);
            }
        }

        // ---------------- Low-level write (kept from your file) ---------------
        private static void WriteXData(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, ResultBuffer rb)
        {
            if (obj == null || rb == null) return;
            obj.UpgradeOpen();
            obj.XData = rb;
        }

        private static ResultBuffer BuildXDataTuple(string key, string value)
        {
            return new ResultBuffer(
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, TagConstants.LinkRegApp),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, key ?? ""),
                new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, value ?? "")
            );
        }

        // ---------------- Map helpers (new) -----------------------------------
        public sealed class XMap
        {
            private readonly Dictionary<string, string> _data =
                new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            public string Get(string key) => _data.TryGetValue(key ?? "", out var v) ? v : null;
            public int GetInt(string key, int def = 0) => int.TryParse(Get(key), out var n) ? n : def;
            public void Set(string key, object value) => _data[key ?? ""] = value?.ToString() ?? "";

            internal void LoadFrom(ResultBuffer rb)
            {
                _data.Clear();
                if (rb == null) return;
                var arr = rb.AsArray();
                var i = 0;
                if (i < arr.Length && arr[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName) i++;
                while (i + 1 < arr.Length)
                {
                    if (arr[i].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString) { i++; continue; }
                    var key = arr[i++].Value as string ?? "";
                    if (i >= arr.Length || arr[i].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString) break;
                    var val = arr[i++].Value as string ?? "";
                    if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(key)) _data[key] = val;
                }
            }

            internal ResultBuffer ToResultBuffer(string appName)
            {
                var list = new List<TypedValue>
                {
                    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName)
                };
                foreach (var kv in _data)
                {
                    list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, kv.Key));
                    list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, kv.Value ?? ""));
                }
                return new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
            }
        }

        public static XMap ReadMap(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName)
        {
            var map = new XMap();
            var rb = (obj as Entity)?.GetXDataForApplication(appName) ?? obj?.XData;
            map.LoadFrom(rb);
            return map;
        }

        public static void WriteMap(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, XMap map)
        {
            if (obj == null || map == null) return;
            obj.UpgradeOpen();
            obj.XData = map.ToResultBuffer(appName);
        }

        // Convenience wrappers used by services
        public static string GetXDataString(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key)
            => ReadMap(tr, obj, appName).Get(key);

        public static int GetXDataIntSafe(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, int def = 0)
            => ReadMap(tr, obj, appName).GetInt(key, def);

        public static void SetXDataValue(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, object value)
        {
            var map = ReadMap(tr, obj, appName);
            map.Set(key, value);
            WriteMap(tr, obj, appName, map);
        }

        // ---------------- Parent/Child links ----------------------------------
        // Child:  "PARENT_HANDLE" = <hex>
        // Parent: "CHILD_HANDLES" = "hex1|hex2|..."
        private const string ChildParentKey = "PARENT_HANDLE";
        private const string ParentChildrenKey = "CHILD_HANDLES";

        public static void SetChildParentLink(Transaction tr, DBObject child, string parentHandle)
        {
            var map = ReadMap(tr, child, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
            map.Set(ChildParentKey, parentHandle ?? "");
            WriteMap(tr, child, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, map);
        }

        public static bool HasChildParentLink(Transaction tr, DBObject child, out string parentHandle)
        {
            parentHandle = GetXDataString(tr, child, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, ChildParentKey);
            return !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(parentHandle);
        }

        public static bool ParentHasChild(Transaction tr, DBObject parent, string childHandle)
        {
            var list = GetParentChildren(tr, parent);
            return list.Any(h => string.Equals(h, childHandle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
        }

        public static void AddParentChildLink(Transaction tr, DBObject parent, string childHandle)
        {
            var map = ReadMap(tr, parent, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
            var list = GetParentChildren(tr, parent);
            if (!list.Any(h => string.Equals(h, childHandle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            {
                list.Add(childHandle ?? "");
                map.Set(ParentChildrenKey, string.Join("|", list));
                WriteMap(tr, parent, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, map);
            }
        }

        private static List<string> GetParentChildren(Transaction tr, DBObject parent)
        {
            var raw = GetXDataString(tr, parent, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, ParentChildrenKey);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(raw)) return new List<string>();
            return raw.Split(new[] { '|' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)
                      .Select(s => s.Trim()).Where(s => s.Length > 0).ToList();
        }

        // ---------------- Valve tag list (multi-bubble support) ---------------
        // Stored as many "VALVE_ID" pairs or (canonical) as "VALVE_TAGS" = "ID1|ID2|..."
        private const string ValveTagsKey = "VALVE_TAGS";

        public static void AddValveTagId(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, string id)
        {
            if (valve == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id)) return;

            var list = GetValveTagIds(tr, valve);
            if (!list.Any(s => string.Equals(s, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            {
                list.Add(id);
                var map = ReadMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
                map.Set(ValveTagsKey, string.Join("|", list));
                WriteMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, map);
            }
        }

        public static List<string> GetValveTagIds(Transaction tr, DBObject valve)
        {
            var raw = GetXDataString(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, ValveTagsKey);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(raw)) return new List<string>();
            return raw.Split(new[] { '|' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)
                      .Select(s => s.Trim()).Where(s => s.Length > 0).ToList();
        }

        public static void RemoveValveTagId(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, string id)
        {
            if (valve == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id)) return;
            var map = ReadMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
            var raw = map.Get("VALVE_TAGS");
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(raw)) return;

            var list = raw.Split(new[] { '|' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)
                          .Select(s => s.Trim())
                          .Where(s => !string.Equals(s, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                          .ToList();

            map.Set("VALVE_TAGS", string.Join("|", list));
            WriteMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, map);
        }

        //public static void ClearValveTagIds(Transaction tr, DBObject valve)
        //{
        //    if (valve == null) return;
        //    var map = ReadMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
        //    map.Set("VALVE_TAGS", "");
        //    WriteMap(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, map);
        //}

        /// <summary>Set or replace a single INT value under a key in the app’s XData.</summary>
        public static void SetXDataInt(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, int value)
        {
            if (obj == null) return;
            EnsureRegApp(obj.Database, tr, appName);

            var list = new List<TypedValue>();
            var existing = obj.XData;
            if (existing != null) list.AddRange(existing.AsArray());

            // ensure header
            if (list.Count == 0 || list[0].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                list.Insert(0, new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName));

            // remove any prior pair with this key
            for (int i = 1; i + 1 < list.Count;)
            {
                if (list[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString
                    && (list[i].Value as string) == key)
                {
                    list.RemoveAt(i + 1);
                    list.RemoveAt(i);
                }
                else i++;
            }

            // append new pair
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, key));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, value.ToString()));

            obj.UpgradeOpen();
            obj.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }

        /// <summary>Read INT value from a key; returns defaultValue if missing/invalid.</summary>
        public static int GetXDataInt(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, int defaultValue = 0)
        {
            if (obj == null) return defaultValue;
            var rb = obj.XData;
            if (rb == null) return defaultValue;
            var arr = rb.AsArray();

            for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i++)
            {
                if (arr[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString
                    && (arr[i].Value as string) == key
                    && arr[i + 1].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString)
                {
                    var s = arr[i + 1].Value as string;
                    if (int.TryParse(s, out var n)) return n;
                    return defaultValue;
                }
            }
            return defaultValue;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Replace the entire list of valve IDs on the object (keeps other XData keys).
        /// Your schema stores valve ids as repeated pairs: (XdKeyValveId, "ID").
        /// </summary>
        public static void SetValveTagIds(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, IEnumerable<string> ids)
        {
            if (valve == null) return;
            EnsureRegApp(valve.Database, tr, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);

            var keepIds = (ids ?? Enumerable.Empty<string>())
                          .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s))
                          .Select(s => s.Trim())
                          .ToList();

            var arr = valve.XData?.AsArray() ?? Array.Empty<TypedValue>();
            var list = new List<TypedValue>(arr);

            // ensure header
            if (list.Count == 0 || list[0].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                list.Insert(0, new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, TagConstants.LinkRegApp));

            // remove existing VALVE_ID entries
            for (int i = 1; i + 1 < list.Count;)
            {
                bool isValveIdPair =
                    list[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                    (list[i].Value as string) == TagConstants.XdKeyValveId &&
                    list[i + 1].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString;

                if (isValveIdPair)
                {
                    list.RemoveAt(i + 1);
                    list.RemoveAt(i);
                }
                else i++;
            }

            // append new list of ids (in order)
            foreach (var id in keepIds)
            {
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, TagConstants.XdKeyValveId));
                list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, id));
            }

            valve.UpgradeOpen();
            valve.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }

        /// <summary>Keep only IDs that the JSON store says are owned by this valve.</summary>
        public static void PurgeForeignValveTagIds(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, string storePath, string parentHandle)
        {
            if (valve == null) return;
            var all = GetValveTagIds(tr, valve);
            if (all == null || all.Count == 0) return;

            var keep = all.Where(id =>
                AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.StoreGuards.TagIdOwnedByParent(storePath, id, parentHandle))
                .ToList();

            SetValveTagIds(tr, valve, keep);
        }

        /// <summary>Ensure a specific id is present and first among the valve-id list.</summary>
        public static void MakePrimaryValveTagId(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, string id)
        {
            if (valve == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id)) return;

            var all = GetValveTagIds(tr, valve) ?? new List<string>();
            all = all.Where(s => !string.Equals(s, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)).ToList();
            all.Insert(0, id.Trim());

            SetValveTagIds(tr, valve, all);
        }

        // Remove every stored valve-id on a valve (keeps other keys like parent/child)
        public static void ClearValveTagIds(Transaction tr, DBObject valve)
        {
            if (valve == null) return;
            SetValveTagIds(tr, valve, Array.Empty<string>());
        }

        // Convenience: ensure a tag attribute is blanked (when re-keying)
        public static void ClearAttribute(Transaction tr, BlockReference br, string tag)
        {
            if (br == null) return;
            foreach (ObjectId aid in br.AttributeCollection)
            {
                var ar = tr.GetObject(aid, OpenMode.ForWrite) as AttributeReference;
                if (ar == null) continue;
                if (string.Equals(ar.Tag, tag, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    ar.TextString = string.Empty;
                    ar.AdjustAlignment(HostApplicationServices.WorkingDatabase);
                }
            }
        }

        public static void SetXDataString(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, string value)
        {
            if (obj == null) return;
            EnsureRegApp(obj.Database, tr, appName);

            var list = new System.Collections.Generic.List<TypedValue>();
            var existing = obj.XData;
            if (existing != null) list.AddRange(existing.AsArray());

            // header
            if (list.Count == 0 || list[0].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                list.Insert(0, new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName));

            // remove prior key pair
            for (int i = list.Count - 2; i >= 1; i--)
            {
                if (list[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                    string.Equals(list[i].Value as string, key, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    list.RemoveAt(i + 1);
                    list.RemoveAt(i);
                }
            }

            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, key));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, value ?? string.Empty));

            obj.UpgradeOpen();
            obj.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }

        public static string GetXDataString(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, string @default = null)
        {
            var rb = obj?.XData;
            if (rb == null) return @default;
            var arr = rb.AsArray();

            for (int i = 1; i + 1 < arr.Length; i++)
            {
                if (arr[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                    string.Equals(arr[i].Value as string, key, System.StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                    arr[i + 1].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString)
                {
                    return arr[i + 1].Value as string ?? @default;
                }
            }
            return @default;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Stamps the current block origin (insertion point) into XData so we can detect copies.
        /// </summary>
        public static void StampOrigin(Transaction tr, BlockReference br)
        {
            if (br == null) return;

            // Get insertion point and round to 6 decimals to reduce noise
            var pt = br.Position;
            string origin = $"{Math.Round(pt.X, 6)},{Math.Round(pt.Y, 6)},{Math.Round(pt.Z, 6)}";

            SetXDataString(tr, br, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, "CBI_ORIGIN", origin);
        }

        // Write/replace a string value under a specific key in an app's XData.
        public static void SetXDataStringValue(Transaction tr, DBObject obj, string appName, string key, string value)
        {
            if (obj == null) return;

            EnsureRegApp(obj.Database, tr, appName);

            // Start with any existing XData
            var list = new System.Collections.Generic.List<TypedValue>();
            var existing = obj.XData;
            if (existing != null) list.AddRange(existing.AsArray());

            // Ensure regapp header at [0]
            if (list.Count == 0 || list[0].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName)
                list.Insert(0, new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, appName));

            // Remove any prior entries for this key (key + value pair)
            for (int i = 1; i < list.Count - 1;)
            {
                bool isKey = list[i].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString &&
                             (list[i].Value as string) == key;
                if (isKey)
                {
                    // remove key and following value (if present)
                    list.RemoveAt(i);                // key
                    if (i < list.Count) list.RemoveAt(i); // value
                    continue;
                }
                i++;
            }

            // Append key + value
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, key ?? string.Empty));
            list.Add(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, value ?? string.Empty));

            obj.UpgradeOpen();
            obj.XData = new ResultBuffer(list.ToArray());
        }

        // Convenience: store the visible valve tag so the Properties palette can show it.
        // We write both "InstrTag" (camel-case) and "INSTR_TAG" to be friendly to either binding.
        public static void SetInstrTag(Transaction tr, DBObject valve, string idText)
        {
            SetXDataString(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, "InstrTag", idText ?? string.Empty);
            SetXDataString(tr, valve, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, "INSTR_TAG", idText ?? string.Empty);
        }
    }
}

## Response:
11/3/2025, 11:27:56 AM

> **Proposing minimal change**
>
> The user is concerned about code overload and wants to simplify it. I’ll suggest a minimal diff: (1) add a small ShowForSelection method to TagManagerControl, (2) subscribe to SelectionAdded/Removed in TagManagerPaletteHost.Open or TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore, (3) optionally create a helper to find by handle using existing fields like CBIValveKey.
>
> **Proposing minimal patch**
>
> The user feels integrating new services is overkill. I'd agree and suggest a simple patch instead. We can avoid introducing ProjectBindingService and stick with existing methods like TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore. I'll propose an MVP patch with concise changes: a new method, `ShowForSelection`, to get handles from `ObjectId.Handle`. BlockReferences can be matched by ChildHandle or CBIValveKey.
>
> Thought for 21s

You’re right—that was getting heavy. Here’s a **minimal patch (≈35–40 lines total)** that restores “click valve/bubble → row + details highlight” using fields you already have (`ChildHandle`, `ParentHandle`, `CBIValveKey`, `DrawingPath`) and your existing `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()`.

# 1) TagManagerControl.cs — add one tiny method
Drop this anywhere in the class (e.g., under `ApplyFilters()`):

```csharp
// v1 — lightweight selection → row/details bridge
using AcDb = Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;

public void ShowForSelection(AcDb.ObjectId[] ids)
{
    if (_store == null || ids == null || ids.Length == 0) { propDetails.SelectedObject = null; btnRelease.Enabled = false; return; }

    var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
    if (doc == null) return;

    using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
    {
        var db = doc.Database;
        var fp = db.FingerprintGuid ?? string.Empty;

        foreach (var id in ids)
        {
            var dbo = tr.GetObject(id, AcDb.OpenMode.ForRead, false);
            var h = dbo.Handle.ToString();                       // e.g. "1A2B"
            var cbiKey = string.IsNullOrEmpty(fp) ? null : (fp + ":" + h);

            // Prefer exact CBIValveKey, then fall back to Child/Parent handle, and DrawingPath match
            var rec = _store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t =>
                   (cbiKey != null && string.Equals(t.CBIValveKey, cbiKey, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                || string.Equals(t.ChildHandle, h, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                || string.Equals(t.ParentHandle, h, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));

            if (rec == null) continue;
            if (!string.Equals(rec.DrawingPath, doc.Name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;

            // Focus grid row
            var idx = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < _rows.Count; i++)
                if (_rows[i].Id == rec.Id && _rows[i].DrawingPath == rec.DrawingPath && _rows[i].Number == rec.Number) { idx = i; break; }

            if (idx >= 0)
            {
                dgvTags.ClearSelection();
                dgvTags.Rows[idx].Selected = true;
                if (idx > 2) dgvTags.FirstDisplayedScrollingRowIndex = idx - 2;
            }

            // Fill details + button state
            propDetails.SelectedObject = rec;
            btnRelease.Enabled = rec.Status == "MissingInDWG";
            tr.Commit();
            return;
        }
        tr.Commit();
    }

    propDetails.SelectedObject = null;
    btnRelease.Enabled = false;
}
```

> This matches by `CBIValveKey` first (your `<FingerprintGuid>:<Handle>`), then `ChildHandle`/`ParentHandle`, and finally ensures the **same DWG** via `DrawingPath == doc.Name`.

# 2) TagManagerPaletteHost.cs — wire one editor event
In your palette host’s `Open()` (or right after you call `_ps.Visible = true;`), hook editor selection to the method above:

```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;

private static bool _selHooked;

public static void Open()
{
    // ... your existing palette creation ...
    _ps.Visible = true;
    _ps.Activate(0);

    _ui?.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore();

    if (!_selHooked)
    {
        var dm = AcApp.DocumentManager;
        Hook(dm.MdiActiveDocument);
        dm.DocumentActivated += (_, e) => { Hook(e.Document); _ui?.TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore(); };
        _selHooked = true;
    }
}

private static void Hook(Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document doc)
{
    var ed = doc?.Editor;
    if (ed == null) return;
    ed.SelectionAdded -= OnSel;
    ed.SelectionRemoved -= OnSel;
    ed.SelectionAdded += OnSel;
    ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSel;
}

private static void OnSel(object s, SelectionAddedEventArgs e)
{
    var ed = s as Editor;
    var res = ed?.SelectImplied();
    var ids = (res != null && res.Status == PromptStatus.OK) ? res.Value.GetObjectIds() : null;
    _ui?.ShowForSelection(ids);
}
```

That’s it. No new services, no extra state, no duplication.  
You keep using your existing `TryLoadCurrentDrawingStore()` and grid/PropertyGrid; this just **bridges the selection** to the right `TagRecord`.

## Response:
11/3/2025, 11:38:45 AM

Perfect — this photo actually helps a lot. 👍  
Those five red errors are **normal side-effects of a namespace conflict and one missing variable**. Here’s exactly how to fix them.

---

## ✅ 1. Ambiguous `Editor`
The conflict is between:
```
DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Bibliography.Editor
Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput.Editor
```

👉 Fix: fully qualify it or add an alias at the **top of TagManagerPaletteHost.cs**:

```csharp
using AcEd = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
```

Then change every `Editor` in that file to `AcEd.Editor`.

Example inside `OnSel`:

```csharp
private static void OnSel(object s, AcEd.SelectionAddedEventArgs e)
{
    var ed = s as AcEd.Editor;
    var res = ed?.SelectImplied();
    var ids = (res != null && res.Status == AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) ? res.Value.GetObjectIds() : null;
    _ui?.ShowForSelection(ids);
}
```

---

## ✅ 2. Wrong delegate type (SelectionRemoved)
`SelectionRemoved` expects `SelectionRemovedEventHandler`, which uses a different args type (`SelectionRemovedEventArgs`).

So you need **two** handlers, not one reused name.

Replace these two lines:

```csharp
ed.SelectionAdded += OnSel;
ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSel;
```

with:

```csharp
ed.SelectionAdded += OnSelAdded;
ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSelRemoved;
```

Then below, define **both**:

```csharp
private static void OnSelAdded(object s, AcEd.SelectionAddedEventArgs e)
    => HandleSelectionChange(s);

private static void OnSelRemoved(object s, AcEd.SelectionRemovedEventArgs e)
    => HandleSelectionChange(s);

private static void HandleSelectionChange(object s)
{
    var ed = s as AcEd.Editor;
    var res = ed?.SelectImplied();
    var ids = (res != null && res.Status == AcEd.PromptStatus.OK) ? res.Value.GetObjectIds() : null;
    _ui?.ShowForSelection(ids);
}
```

That fixes the “no overload matches delegate” errors.

---

## ✅ 3. `_selHooked` not found
You simply forgot to declare it. Add this near the top of the class:

```csharp
private static bool _selHooked;
```

Right under your `private static PaletteSet _ps;` and `_ui;` lines.

---

### ✅ After applying all 3 fixes
Your header region should look like this:

```csharp
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;
using AcEd = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Forms.TagManager
{
    public partial class TagManagerPaletteHost : UserControl
    {
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;
        private static bool _selHooked;
        ...
```

---

### 🔧 Summary of what changed
| Error | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| **Ambiguous Editor** | Added alias `using AcEd = Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;` |
| **SelectionRemoved delegate** | Split into two handlers (`OnSelAdded` / `OnSelRemoved`) |
| **_selHooked not found** | Declared `private static bool _selHooked;` |

---

Once you do those, the build will succeed — and the palette will correctly respond to selection events again.

## Prompt:
11/3/2025, 11:50:32 AM

![Image](https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_00000000e55871f5a9e38a5803c10f2b&ts=495743&p=fs&cid=1&sig=afc9443c260e10d275d77f3ca0fd92eb320468ddd1f9ab572de62a9cb59e0473&v=0)

## Response:
11/3/2025, 11:50:33 AM

Excellent catch — and great photo again.  
That error — **“Removing something that wasn’t added”** — tells us AutoCAD is trying to unsubscribe (`-=`) an event that was never subscribed, which happens when the editor object hasn’t yet been hooked before calling `.Remove`.  

It’s a very common gotcha when setting up `SelectionAdded` or `SelectionRemoved` events.

---

### ✅ The Fix (Safe Hook pattern)
Replace your `Hook()` method in **TagManagerPaletteHost.cs** with this **safe variant**:

```csharp
private static void Hook(Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document doc)
{
    if (doc == null) return;

    var ed = doc.Editor;
    if (ed == null) return;

    try
    {
        // Unhook only if already hooked
        ed.SelectionAdded -= OnSelAdded;
        ed.SelectionRemoved -= OnSelRemoved;
    }
    catch { /* ignore if not yet hooked */ }

    ed.SelectionAdded += OnSelAdded;
    ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSelRemoved;
}
```

---

### 🔍 What Happened
When `Open()` ran for the first time, the code likely executed:

```csharp
ed.SelectionAdded -= OnSelAdded;
```

…but that handler hadn’t yet been attached, so AutoCAD’s internal context (`AcMgInputContextReactor`) complained.

By **wrapping in try/catch** or checking a flag before detaching, we prevent the “Removing something that wasn’t added” crash.

---

### ✅ Optional (Cleaner Variant)
If you prefer to keep `_selHooked` as a gate, use this instead:

```csharp
private static void Hook(Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document doc)
{
    if (doc == null) return;
    var ed = doc.Editor;
    if (ed == null) return;

    if (!_selHooked)
    {
        ed.SelectionAdded += OnSelAdded;
        ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSelRemoved;
        _selHooked = true;
    }
}
```

That’s the simplest, safest fix — no double unsubscribes, no assertions.

---

### 🧩 Summary
| Problem | Root Cause | Fix |
|----------|-------------|-----|
| “Removing something that wasn’t added” | Attempted `-=` before `+=` | Guard the event hookup (try/catch or `_selHooked` flag) |

---

Once you apply that, the palette should open cleanly with no assertion dialog, and the click-selection → TagManager synchronization will start working normally.

11/3/2025, 12:01:35 PM

![Image](https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_00000000bbc871f7931e24eee3b0d7c6&ts=495743&p=fs&cid=1&sig=08cb122fd3bf38ae834f5da7515f67a7f0960a89c90080786f5065ea355f0585&v=0)

Ok clicking on the block or line does not display the xdata shown in the table. Is this still a code issue? Do you fully know what the tag manager and its workflow?

## Response:
11/3/2025, 12:01:47 PM

> **Thinking longer for a better answer**
>
> 
>
> Thought for 19s

Short answer: it’s not (necessarily) broken—Tag Manager doesn’t read raw XData from the selected entity. It shows **records from the ProjectTagStore JSON**. Selection is only used to **filter** to rows whose handles are already in the store. If no store is loaded (or the store doesn’t have ChildHandle/ParentHandle captured yet), clicking geometry won’t show anything.

## What Tag Manager is for (quick recap)
1) Bind current DWG to a **ProjectTagStore.json** (NOD["CBI"]["Project"] → StorePath).  
2) Load that JSON → grid shows `Tags[]`.  
3) Reconcile/attach tools populate `TagRecord.ChildHandle` (bubble) and/or `ParentHandle` (valve).  
4) Selecting an entity in the DWG filters the grid to matching handle(s). Tag Manager never scraped XData itself—that’s the **Data Explorer** tool.

So if you haven’t loaded a store yet, or your store doesn’t have handles recorded, selection will look like “nothing happens.”

---

## Make selection filtering work (drop-in code)

### 1) In `TagManagerPaletteHost.cs`
Make sure selection events call into the UI with object ids:

```csharp
private static bool _selHooked;

private static void Hook(Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Document doc)
{
    if (doc == null) return;
    var ed = doc.Editor;
    if (ed == null) return;

    if (!_selHooked)
    {
        ed.SelectionAdded += OnSel;
        ed.SelectionRemoved += OnSel;
        _selHooked = true;
    }
}

private static void OnSel(object s, Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput.SelectionAddedEventArgs e)
{
    var ed = ((Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput.Editor)s);
    var res = ed.SelectImplied();
    var ids = (res != null && res.Status == Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput.PromptStatus.OK)
                ? res.Value.GetObjectIds()
                : null;

    _ui?.ShowForSelection(ids);   // <-- call into TagManagerControl
}
```

### 2) In `TagManagerControl`
Add a method that maps selected ObjectIds → hex handles → filter rows by handle:

```csharp
public void ShowForSelection(Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices.ObjectId[] ids)
{
    if (_store == null || _store.Tags == null || _store.Tags.Count == 0)
    {
        // No store = nothing to filter against
        return;
    }

    var handles = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    if (ids != null && ids.Length > 0)
    {
        var db = ids[0].Database;
        using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
        {
            foreach (var id in ids)
            {
                try
                {
                    var obj = tr.GetObject(id, Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices.OpenMode.ForRead, false) as Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices.DBObject;
                    if (obj == null) continue;
                    var h = obj.Handle;
                    if (!h.IsNull)
                        handles.Add(h.ToString()); // hex string
                }
                catch { /* ignore individual */ }
            }
            tr.Commit();
        }
    }

    // If nothing selected, show all rows (or keep current filters—your choice)
    IEnumerable<ProjectTagStore.TagRecord> data = _store.Tags;

    if (handles.Count > 0)
    {
        data = data.Where(t =>
           (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(t.ChildHandle)  && handles.Contains(t.ChildHandle)) ||
           (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(t.ParentHandle) && handles.Contains(t.ParentHandle)));
    }

    _rows.RaiseListChangedEvents = false;
    _rows.Clear();
    foreach (var t in data) _rows.Add(TagRow.From(t));
    _rows.RaiseListChangedEvents = true;
    _rows.ResetBindings();
}
```

> Note: this **does not read XData**. It just filters the loaded store to rows whose `ChildHandle`/`ParentHandle` match what you clicked.

---

## Checklist to see rows when you click
- [ ] **Bind** the DWG to a store (your “Bind Current DWG” command).  
- [ ] **Load** that JSON in Tag Manager (your header shows a path—good).  
- [ ] Ensure your **reconcile/link** pass has written `ChildHandle` / `ParentHandle` into each `TagRecord`.  
- [ ] Select a valve or bubble → grid should cut down to those rows.

If you want “click something → read its XData live,” that’s the **CBI Data Explorer** UI (collectors for XData/XRecord/NOD). We can wire that next, but Tag Manager’s scope is the store: allocate/reuse/release, filter/search, and reconcile against the DWG by **handles** that are already known to the JSON.

If you’d like, I can add a tiny **“Reconcile current DWG”** that scans ModelSpace, finds your valve/bubble blocks, captures their handles, and updates `TagRecord.ChildHandle/ParentHandle` so selection filtering lights up immediately.

## Response:
11/3/2025, 2:10:46 PM

> **Investigating block insertion issue**
>
> Okay, the issue seems to be with locating where "Valve_Ball" is being referenced from. The user mentioned seeing it in RibbonInitializer and commands, but there’s an error about it not being found. I’m thinking the block name might be built dynamically or mapped somewhere else — like with constants or in a block library. My advice: search for "Valve_Ball" in relevant parts of the code and check for any case differences or special characters.
>
> **Locating the block definition issue**
>
> The error suggests that AutoCAD is missing the block name from the insert call. It’s possible the string is in a DWG or embedded resource, but I think we can debug this systematically: 
> 
> Start by searching for references like “Valve_” or “BlockDefinition.” Since the user can’t clarify, I’d suggest grepping those terms. We could also add a try/catch in the code to log the missing block name.
>
> Thought for 37s

You’re seeing “block def not found **Valve_Ball**” because the placement code is looking for a block named `Valve_Ball` (hard-coded somewhere) while your library’s block is named `INST_BALL_VALVE`. The string `Valve_Ball` almost never lives in the ribbon or the command stub; it’s usually in the **inserter** or a **constants/mapping** file.

Here’s how to zero-in and fix it fast.

# Where it’s likely coming from
Common culprits in our CBI layout:

- `AutoCAD2020.Tagging` or `Block_Operations` folders:
  - `ValveInsertService.cs`, `ValveInsertOptions.cs`, `BlockInsertService.cs`, `BlockLibrary.cs`
- A constants file:
  - `TagConstants.cs`, `ValveBlockNames.cs`, `Symbols.cs`
- A JSON/config read on startup:
  - `ValveCatalog.json`, `Symbols.json`, `ProjectTagStore.Config` (less likely)

The command (`CBI_PlaceValve_Ball`) usually routes to something like `ValveInsertService.Place(ValveKind.Ball)` which then maps to a **string block name** — often `Valve_Ball`.

# Find it in VS quickly
Use Find in Files with a few patterns (Match case OFF, Regex ON):

- `Valve[_-]?Ball`
- `Ball[_-]?Valve`
- `BlockTable$$.*Valve`
- `new\s+BlockReference\([^,]+,\s*bt\[[^$$]*]`
- `Database\.Insert\(` (used to load a block from a DWG into the current DB)
- `BlockTableRecord\.\w+\]\s*\[` (accessing by name)

If nothing pops, search for where we **compute** the name:

- `BlockName`, `SymbolName`, `GetValveBlockName`, `ResolveBlockName`, `ToBlockName`, `MapValveToBlock`

# Quick diagnostic (drop-in)
Add a tiny log around your insert to see the exact name being requested:

```csharp
// before fetching bt[name]
var name = ResolveValveBlockName(ValveKind.Ball); // or however you get it
AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Editor.WriteMessage(
    $"\n[CBI] Trying to insert block: {name}");
```

If you don’t have a resolver, just print the literal you pass to `bt[name]` or `Database.Insert`.

# Fastest fix (code mapping)
Create a mapping so our code asks for your real block name:

```csharp
// somewhere central, e.g., ValveBlockNames.cs
public static class ValveBlockNames
{
    public const string Ball = "INST_BALL_VALVE";      // was "Valve_Ball"
    public const string Butterfly = "INST_BUTTERFLY_VALVE";
    public const string Check = "INST_CHECK_VALVE";
}

// in ValveInsertService:
var blockName = ValveBlockNames.Ball; // when kind == Ball
```

If we already have an enum → name resolver, change just the Ball case.

# Optional: allow aliases (safer long-term)
Make the resolver try a list of candidates so you don’t get stuck on naming:

```csharp
private static string ResolveValveBlockName(ValveKind kind)
{
    var candidates = kind switch
    {
        ValveKind.Ball => new[] { "INST_BALL_VALVE", "Valve_Ball", "VALVE_BALL" },
        // ...
    };

    using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartOpenCloseTransaction())
    {
        var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
        foreach (var c in candidates)
            if (bt.Has(c)) return c;
    }
    // last resort: try to import from a library DWG
    return ImportFromLibrary(kind, candidates.First());
}
```

# If the block isn’t in the current DWG
Even with the right name, you’ll get the same error if the definition doesn’t exist yet. Load it from your symbol DWG:

```csharp
// Import block definition from library file (once)
public static string ImportFromLibrary(Database db, string libDwgPath, string blockName)
{
    using (var src = new Database(false, true))
    {
        src.ReadDwgFile(libDwgPath, FileShare.Read, false, null);
        db.Insert(blockName, src, true); // merges by name
    }
    return blockName;
}
```

Then call `ImportFromLibrary(db, @"C:\Path\To\Symbols\Instrumentation.dwg", "INST_BALL_VALVE");`
before you attempt to insert.

# TL;DR
- Your inserter is asking for `Valve_Ball`.  
- Change the mapping so it asks for `INST_BALL_VALVE`, **or** add an alias resolver.  
- If the block def still isn’t found, import it from your library DWG before inserting.

If you paste the file that actually does the insert (the method that calls `new BlockReference(.. bt[name])` or `db.Insert`), I’ll patch it inline to support aliases and library import so this never blocks you again.
