It’s helpful if you plan to document the architecture inside your Obsidian vault or GitHub README.

Here’s a tight, current-state architecture snapshot for the CBI Properties / Pipe-Run + Labeling toolset (as it stands in this thread).

High-level Workflow

1. User selects a pipe polyline

2. CBI Properties Palette resolves a provider → shows editable fields (PipeRunProperties)

3. Apply writes fields back to entity XData (CBI_PIPE_RUN) and (policy) updates layer if needed

4. Label tools create or refresh linked MTEXT/DBTEXT from XData (ItemTag), keeping rotation parallel to the run and preserving user offsets

5. Match Properties clones XData from a “source” pipe to one or more “targets” (skipping TagSeqNo & recomputing ItemTag)

6. Data Explorer can read entity XData, XRecords, and drawing-level NOD to inspect/zoom/select

Main Modules & Responsibilities

CbiPropertiesPalette (host)

Owns the PaletteSet lifecycle, hooks selection & command events

Clears UI on ESC/no selection; binds on valid selection

Commands: CBI_PROP_PALETTE, plus float/dock helpers

PropertiesControl (UI)

Toolstrip + PropertyGrid (auto-apply)

Save/Load presets (JSON) for the visible model

Resize grip (works when palette is floating)

PropertyRouter

Chooses a provider for the current selection (first match)

Calls Apply on provider when user applies changes

PipeRunPropertyProvider

Claims polylines (existing or not yet stamped)

Maps XData ⇄ PipeRunProperties

Ensures RegApp, writes schema, computes ItemTag

Layer policy (Primary/Secondary → layer rule) with user override

PipeRunProperties (model)

Identification & Physical fields (e.g., FluidCode, NominalDiameter, PipingMaterialClass, etc.)

Computed ItemTag: NominalDiameter-FluidCode-TagSeqNo-PipingMaterialClass-InsulationPurpose

(Dropdowns) LayerName (live list), PipingRunType (Primary/Secondary)

XDataHelpers / XDataMap

Minimal helpers to read/write string-pair XData under a RegApp (e.g., CBI_PIPE_RUN)

LayerRules / Layer utilities

Maps PipingRunType → standard layer (e.g., G-Piping-Primary/Secondary)

Ensures layer exists; applies it to entity when policy says so

Match Properties (PipeRunMatchCommands)

CBI_MATCH_PIPERUN: pick SOURCE → pick TARGETS

Copies XData keys (skips TagSeqNo,ItemTag)

Allocates TagSeqNo if missing on target; recomputes ItemTag

Applies layer: source layer > target layer > policy rule

LineTag / Labeling

LineTagService: create/ensure tag text, place MTEXT, compute tangent

Policy Helpers: “readable” rotation parallel to line (never upside-down), default “above line” offset based on tangent

LabelLinkData: stores link to source (handle), param on curve, offset (X/Y), RotateAlong flag

LabelLinkService:

Create: user picks pipe & point → stores param/offset, places label with policy rotation

Refresh: re-gets ItemTag from XData, recomputes tangent-parallel rotation, keeps stored offset, updates text/pos/rot

CBI Data Explorer (UI)

Pluggable collectors: XDataCollector, XRecordCollector, NODCollector

Tree (Source → Key → Scope) + grid; right-click: Zoom / Select / Copy Handle

Reads live selection & drawing to present property inventories

Command Surface (current)

Palette / Editing

CBI_PROP_PALETTE — open Properties palette (auto-bind on selection, Apply → write XData)

Match / Clone

CBI_MATCH_PIPERUN — clone pipe-run XData from one to many, re-seq, recompute ItemTag, apply layer

Labeling

CBI_MAKE_LABEL (or unified label command) — place linked label w/ correct rotation & offset

CBI_LABEL_REFRESH — refresh all/selected linked labels (keeps user offsets; fixes rotation parallel to run)

Data Contracts (key pieces)

XData (CBI_PIPE_RUN)

Strings: SchemaVersion, LayerName, FluidCode, FluidSystem, PipingRunType, PlantGroupName,
PlantGroupType, PlantItemType, TagSeqNo, TagSuffix, UnitNumber,
InsulationPurpose, NominalDiameter, PipingMaterialClass, ItemTag

LabelLinkData (per label, XRecord/ExtensionDict)

SourceHandle, Param (curve parameter), OffsetX, OffsetY, RotateAlong (bool)

Eventing & UX Policies

Palette listens to: SelectionAdded/Removed, CommandEnded/Cancelled/Failed, DocumentActivated

ESC / empty selection → palette clears (placeholder)

Property changes with dropdowns can auto-apply (layer policy kicks in)

Label placement/refresh always parallel to the pipe and readable; refresh preserves user-moved offsets

Known Nice-to-Haves (backlog)

Palette clear edge case (rare editor states) – finalize fallback timer/idle poll if needed

Data Explorer: guard against bad/huge NOD graphs & add filters for regapps/keys

Label “leader mode” (when dragged far) + anchor grip workflows

Multi-select property editing in the palette (batch apply)

Central TagSeqNo allocator (per system) integrated with ProjectStore v1.0

If you want this rendered as a printable diagram or living Architecture.md for Obsidian, say the word and I’ll generate a clean doc with a simple UML component sketch and the command matrix.

Here’s the compact workflow/architecture snapshot for the Label ↔ Host Link tool we’ve been shaping (LabelLinkService + ReverseSync). It’s scoped to AutoCAD 2020 / C# 7.3 and matches your Command Registration Standard.

High-level goal

Keep labels (DBText/MText/AttrRef) and their host entities (e.g., polylines) in two-way sync:

Forward sync: when the host geometry changes, re-compute/relocate/update the label.

Reverse sync: when a user edits the label text, push that value back into the host’s XData/NOD.

Core components

1. LabelLinkService (forward watcher)

Attaches per-document DB event hooks:

Database.ObjectModified, ObjectErased, ObjectAppended

Filters to “watched” host entities (e.g., your linked polylines).

Queues dirty ObjectIds and flushes once post-command using Application.DocumentManager.ExecuteInCommandContextAsync(...).

In Flush() (inside one doc.LockDocument() + one Transaction), runs UpdateLinkedLabels:

Re-computes anchor (e.g., Bottom-Middle, preserves user offset if present).

Updates label geometry/text only when needed (to minimize churn).

2. LabelReverseSyncService (reverse watcher)

Hooks Database.ObjectModified and focuses on label types:

DBText, MText, (optional) AttributeReference

On change, compares current text vs a “last-synced” token on the label.

If changed by user, finds the host via a backlink and updates the host’s XData/NOD, then refreshes the label’s “last-synced” token to avoid loops.

3. Link storage (lightweight, local on label)

RegApp: CBI_LINK

XData keys on the label:

HOST_HANDLE → hex handle string of the host polyline for fast lookup.

LAST_TEXT  → last value written by sync (prevents ping-pong).

XData (or NOD) on the host:

e.g., LABEL_TEXT → canonical text the system considers authoritative.

(You can add others like ManualOffset vector, AnchorMode, etc.)

4. Guards & safety

_handling boolean to prevent re-entrancy while you write inside event responses.

Defer writes: events capture; writes occur in Flush (forward) or in a guarded short write (reverse) with doc.LockDocument().

Idempotency: forward/ reverse both check current state vs desired state before editing.

Data flow (happy paths)

A. Host edited → label updated (forward sync)

1. User grips/edits host polyline.

2. ObjectModified fires → LabelLinkService marks host as dirty.

3. After command ends, Flush() opens one transaction:

Finds labels linked to host (via your index or host-side XData list).

Computes new anchor/rotation/justification.

Applies minimal changes to the label; updates label’s LAST_TEXT if text changed by your code.

B. Label text edited → host updated (reverse sync)

1. User double-clicks label, edits text.

2. ObjectModified fires on the label → LabelReverseSync:

Reads current text; compares to label’s LAST_TEXT.

If different, resolves HOST_HANDLE → host ObjectId.

Writes the new value to host’s XData/NOD key (e.g., LABEL_TEXT).

Updates label’s LAST_TEXT to the new value (prevents recursion).

C. Label erased → host cleanup (optional)

On ObjectErased for a label, you can remove the backlink on the host (or mark orphaned) during a scheduled maintenance pass.

Initialization & lifecycle

Attach on load/startup (per document):

LabelLinkService.AttachEntityModifiedWatcher(doc)

LabelReverseSyncService.Attach(doc)

Ensure RegApp CBI_LINK exists.

Detach on document close/unload:

Matching Detach(...) calls remove handlers and clear per-doc queues.

Commands (per your Commands.cs “switchboard”)

// CBI_ prefix, one-liners that route to real logic:

[CommandMethod("CBI_Label_Watch_On")]
public static void CBI_Label_Watch_On()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.LabelLinkService.AttachEntityModifiedWatcher(
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument);

[CommandMethod("CBI_Label_Watch_Off")]
public static void CBI_Label_Watch_Off()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.LabelLinkService.DetachEntityModifiedWatcher(
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument);

[CommandMethod("CBI_Label_ReverseSync_On")]
public static void CBI_Label_ReverseSync_On()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.LabelReverseSyncService.Attach(
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument);

[CommandMethod("CBI_Label_ReverseSync_Off")]
public static void CBI_Label_ReverseSync_Off()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.LabelReverseSyncService.Detach(
        Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument);

// Optional manual maintenance:
[CommandMethod("CBI_Label_RepairLinks")]
public static void CBI_Label_RepairLinks()
    => AutoCAD2020.Services.LabelRepairService.ScanAndRebind(); // e.g., rebuild HOST_HANDLE, fix orphans

Key decisions & conventions

Authoritative text: Store on the host (LABEL_TEXT), mirror to the label; reverse sync updates the host first, then stamps LAST_TEXT on the label.

Backlink on label (HOST_HANDLE) for O(1) host resolution; more robust cross-drawing identity can live in NOD (with doc GUID) for deeper scenarios.

Event strategy: Observe now, mutate later (forward). For reverse, small atomic mutation guarded by _handling is fine.

User intent preservation: If a user drags a label, store a ManualOffset vector on the label; forward recompute = canonical anchor + ManualOffset.

Extensibility hooks (near-term)

Placement policy provider: interface to swap anchor modes (Bottom-Middle vs Middle-Center), rotation alignment (parallel to segment vs fixed), and offsets.

Batch refresh: a command that triggers UpdateLinkedLabels over a selection set or entire drawing.

Undo breadcrumbs: record change notes into your ChangeHistory manager (you already have the pattern).

Xref awareness: ignore xref’d entities unless explicitly allowed.

Minimal contract (so everything “just works”)

RegApp: CBI_LINK

Label XData: HOST_HANDLE, LAST_TEXT

Host XData/NOD: LABEL_TEXT (and any extras you choose)

Public APIs used by the services (expected in your codebase):

XDataUtils.HasApp/ReadString/WriteString

LinkIndex.TryGetLinkedLabels(hostId, out IEnumerable<ObjectId> labels) (or equivalent)

LabelPlacement.Compute(..) + Apply(..) (anchor, rotation, offset)

ChangeHistory.Log(..) (optional)

---

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page Architecture.md (with a small UML/sequence ASCII diagram) that drops into your Almighty_Vault/CBI_Tools/Labels/ folder.

Here’s the current workflow schema / architecture summary for the Pipeline Label – “Label Anchor + Refresh” tool (reactor-light, plain AutoCAD).

Overview (v0.3 – C# 7.3+)

Goal: Civil 3D-style label behavior using polylines + text, with anchors stored in XData and an index in NOD. Users get two simple actions: Place and Refresh.

---

Components

Commands (per your registry standard)

CBI_PlacePipeLabels → routes to AutoCAD2020.PipeLabels.PlaceService.Run()

CBI_RefreshPipeLabels → routes to AutoCAD2020.PipeLabels.RefreshService.Run()

(Optional) CBI_RebuildPipeLabelRole → routes to …RebuildService.Run(role)

Data Contracts

XData AppName: CBI_PIPELAB

Host (Polyline) XData:

SchemaVersion:int (e.g., 1)

HostId:string (handle)

LabelSetId:guid

Anchors: list<Anchor> (see Anchor schema below)

Label (MText/MLeader) XData:

HostId:string

LabelId:guid

LabelRole:string (e.g., LineNumber, Service)

NOD Key: CBI/PIPELAB/Index

HostId → LabelSetId

LabelId → HostId

(Optional) HostId → GeometryHash for dirty checks

Anchor Schema (per label)

Param (0–1 normalized along polyline length)

Side (Left|Right)

Offset (double, drawing units)

Orientation (Tangent|World)

Role (LineNumber|Spec|Service|…)

StyleHint (text height or named key)

Jitter (Δx, Δy user nudge; optional)

Services

AnchorCompute: Param↔station mapping, tangent/normal frame, offset/orient math

XDataIo / NodIndexIo: read/write contracts, versioning, validation

LabelFactory: create/update text leaders by role & style

SelectionUtil: host selection, layer/space checks

TransactionScope: grouped updates, undo safety

ReportLog: user-facing summary (skips, fixes, orphans)

UI / UX

Ribbon: Place Labels, Refresh Labels (and optional Rebuild [role])

Palette (optional): status, role toggles, “Dirty? Refresh now” prompt

Zero training: standard selection prompts; no new custom objects

---

Workflow (Swimlane)

User          PlaceService                 XDataIo/NodIndexIo         LabelFactory/AnchorCompute
 ──select host→ validate host ────────┐
                      │               │
                      │     create anchors (Param/Side/Offset/Role)
                      │               │
                      │──write host XData + NOD index──────────────→
                      │               │
                      └────render labels via LabelFactory──────────→ place MText/MLeader

User        RefreshService            XDataIo/NodIndexIo           AnchorCompute/LabelFactory
 ──click Refresh→ gather hosts (NOD or selection)
                   │
                   │──for each host: read anchors──────────────→
                   │                     │
                   │               compute P,tangent,offset,angle→
                   │                     │
                   │──update/move labels; recreate missing──────→
                   │
                   └──report summary (moved N, rebuilt M, skipped K)

---

Deterministic Placement Math (per anchor)

1. Param → (segment, t) along polyline cumulative length

2. point = Evaluate(segment, t)

3. tangent = UnitTangent(segment, t); normal = Rotate90(tangent)

4. pos = point + (±normal * Offset) (flip for Right)

5. angle = (Orientation == Tangent ? atan2(tangent) : 0)

6. pos += Jitter (if present)

---

Stability & Safety

No per-entity reactors. Optional: one doc-level “dirty flag” helper (set after your own edit commands) → palette/ribbon prompt only.

Single transaction per host batch for proper Undo/Redo.

Resilience: if host/label missing → mark orphan → offer Rebind or Purge.

Versioning: SchemaVersion gate; upgrade anchors in place if you evolve the schema.

State etiquette: preserve space (Model/Paper), UCS, layer, dimscale; never hijack user context.

---

Edge Cases Covered

Host reversed direction → tangent flips; labels still align correctly (Param is normalized).

Host length changes (join/split) → Param remains valid; stationing variant re-normalizes.

Tight vertices → use local tangent smoothing to avoid 90° hops.

Crossings → optional side flip or offset staggering by role priority.

---

Extension Points (near-term)

Role-specific rebuilds (faster partial refresh)

Collision avoidance (simple nudge rules, then persist as Jitter)

Batch mode (process selected hosts or entire drawing)

Style profiles (per-project text height/leader style via your ProjectStore v1.0)

---

Deliverables & Files (naming)

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/PlaceService.cs

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/RefreshService.cs

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/LabelFactory.cs

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/AnchorCompute.cs

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/XDataIo.cs

AutoCAD2020/PipeLabels/NodIndexIo.cs

AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs (switchboard routes per your standard)

---

SOP Recap (for the drafter)

1. Pick polyline → Place Labels

2. Grip edit the line as needed

3. Click Refresh Labels → labels snap back into place

4. (Optional) Nudge a label → your tweak persists next refresh

---

If you want, I can turn this into a single-page architecture diagram (PNG) or a repo-ready README.md with this exact structure and your command names.

Line Tagging Tool — Workflow & Architecture (v0)

Purpose

Place a pipeline line number label aligned to a picked LINE/POLYLINE, and persist its data (size, fluid, area, seq, spec, insul) on the entity. Autonumbering is per-scope.

---

Command Surface

CBI PLACELINETAG

Pick target entity (LINE/POLYLINE)

Pick label location

Tool computes tangent rotation, ensures tag on the entity (creates if missing), then renders MTEXT.

---

Namespaces & Files (C# 7.3; lang ver ≥ 9 OK)

AutoCAD_Shared_Project (no Autodesk refs)

AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Tagging

TagIdParts.cs — generic DTO

TagIdParser.cs — split/join dash tokens

AutoCAD2020 (AutoCAD-specific)

AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Parsers

LineTagParts.cs — { Size, Fluid, Area, LineNo, Spec, Insul, Raw }

LineTagParser.cs — Parse(string), Format(LineTagParts)

(Valve side: ValveTagParser.cs, etc., used by AddTag)

AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services

LineTagService.cs — core flow (XData read/write, tangent calc, label call)

NodLineNumberStore.cs — scope counters in NOD/XRecord (sanitized keys)

AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Rendering

TagRenderOptions.cs — style/height/mask

MTextRenderer.cs — places MTEXT in Modelspace; creates TextStyle if missing

AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands

LineTagCommands.cs — wires CBI PLACELINETAG

Root: Commands.cs — alternatively hosts the command (your setup)

---

Data Model & Storage

XData on the line entity

RegApp: CBI_LINE

Order (string 1000 values):
[ Size, Fluid, Area, LineNo, Spec, Insul, Raw ]

Example Raw: 1"-MR-2554-01CS1S09-N

Numbering store (per-scope counters)

NOD root dictionary: CBI_LINE_STORE

Key: sanitized "{Fluid}|{Area}|{Spec}" → e.g., MR_2554_CS1S09

Value: Xrecord with TypedValue(90, next); allocation returns current, then increments.

> Sanitization prevents eInvalidKey (replaces disallowed chars like | with _).

---

End-to-End Sequence

1. Command starts → validate active doc.

2. Pick target LINE/POLYLINE; pick label location.

3. Compute rotation
LineTagService.GetTangentAngleAtPoint(entity, pickPoint)

4. Ensure tag

Read XData (CBI_LINE).

If missing:

Build scope = "{Fluid}|{Area}|{Spec}" (defaults for v0; later prompts/config).

NodLineNumberStore.Next(scope) → LineNo (2-digit string).

Compose Raw via LineTagParser.Format.

Write XData array: [Size, Fluid, Area, LineNo, Spec, Insul, Raw].

5. Render label

MTextRenderer.Place(db, tr, Raw, pick, rotation, TagRenderOptions)

MTEXT uses Attachment=MiddleCenter, BackgroundFill=true, style CBI-LineTag (auto-created, default Consolas), height 0.10.

6. Redraw: entities call RecordGraphicsModified(true) as needed.

7. Done: editor echoes placed tag.

---

Error Handling & Guards

No active document → alert & return.

Invalid NOD key characters → sanitized in NodLineNumberStore.

RegApp (CBI_LINE) ensured before XData writes.

All DB ops within transactions; MTEXT/attribute alignment corrected.

---

Extensibility Hooks (near-term)

Replace v0 defaults with:

Prompts (fluid/spec/size/insul) or

Palette/Ribbon options or

ProjectStore v1.0 lookups (when line schema is added there).

Swap label style/height via TagRenderOptions.

Add LineTagValidator (regex/rules) in Shared; enforce in service.

Add RefreshLineTag command (re-reads XData and re-renders label).

---

Interop with Valve Tagging

Valve tool (CBI_AddTag) remains intact; it now parses IDs via ValveTagParser.

Both tools share the Shared TagIdParser for consistent dash-token logic.

---

If you want, I can generate a one-page printable PDF version of this for your CBI_Support\Readme folder, and a short QA checklist (things to verify after each change).

Here’s the concise workflow schema / architecture summary for the CBI Ribbon tool (runtime ribbon, no XML/CUIX).

Purpose

Create and manage a dynamic “CBI Tools” ribbon tab in AutoCAD 2020 that routes UI clicks to your existing commands (per your Command Registration Standard).

User-Facing Workflow

1. AutoCAD starts → add-in loads → ribbon appears (CBI Tools tab).

2. User clicks buttons/split/toggle/textbox on panels (Home, Utilities, etc.).

3. Each click maps to a short action string → executes your real command (e.g., opencbisearch, openfindform).

Runtime Flow

1. IExtensionApplication.Initialize()

Ensure ribbon UI is visible (RIBBON).

Create (or reuse) tab CBI_TOOLS_TAB.

Build panels with columns (RibbonRowPanel) and rows (RibbonRowBreak).

Optionally attach slide-out content (RibbonPanelBreak or SlideOut).

2. Interaction

Each control has CommandParameter (e.g., CBISEARCH).

Central RibbonCommandHandler switches on the parameter and calls SendStringToExecute(...) with the actual AutoCAD command.

3. Terminate()

(Optional) Remove the tab when the DLL unloads.

Code Structure (C# 7.3, LangVersion ≥ 9.0)

AutoCAD2020\RibbonInitializer.cs

RibbonInitializer : IExtensionApplication

Initialize(), Terminate()

BuildHomePanel(), BuildUtilitiesPanel(), etc.

Helpers: EnsureRibbonVisible(), MakeBtn(...)

RibbonCommandHandler : ICommand

Routes CommandParameter → SendStringToExecute(...)

No XML/CUIX; pure runtime API via Autodesk.Windows (Ribbon).

Command Registry Integration (your standard)

Keep all [CommandMethod] entries in AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs (switchboard only).

Ribbon buttons call the real command names you already expose (e.g., opencbisearch), maintaining the one-line routing pattern.

Ribbon Layout Schema (key concepts)

Tab: RibbonTab (“CBI Tools”)

Panels: RibbonPanel with RibbonPanelSource (e.g., “Home”, “Utilities”)

Columns: each RibbonRowPanel added to Source.Items becomes a column

Rows in a column: insert RibbonRowBreak

Slide-out/fly-out: everything after RibbonPanelBreak (or use Source.SlideOut)

Items: RibbonButton, RibbonSplitButton, RibbonToggleButton, RibbonTextBox, RibbonCombo, RibbonSeparator

Sizing: RibbonItemSize.Large|Standard; no absolute width control (influence via columns/rows)

State & Behavior

Toggles: handled in RibbonCommandHandler (set a static flag or invoke a toggle command).

TextBox: EnterPressed → send command with the textbox text.

Icons: assign Image (16×16) / LargeImage (32×32 PNG).

Error Handling

Catch and log exceptions in Initialize() (write to editor).

Guard against ComponentManager.Ribbon == null (auto-show ribbon).

Versioning & Docs (your preference)

File headers with Version + Changelog.

XML docs on public helpers.

Keep semantic version for the ribbon module (e.g., 1.1.0 added Column 3 grid).

Deployment & Autoload

Build for AutoCAD 2020 (.NET 4.7.2 typical).

Place DLL + resources under C:\CBICAD\AutoCAD (your convention).

Load via NETLOAD or autoload (e.g., Registry/Bundle). Ensure references: acmgd.dll, acdbmgd.dll, AdWindows.dll.

Extensibility Points

Add more panels (e.g., Tags, QA/QC, Sheet Sets).

Swap SendStringToExecute with direct .NET calls if you want tighter coupling.

Add contextual tabs later (show/hide by selection).

Dependencies

Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices

Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime

Autodesk.Windows (AdWindows.dll)

Known Constraints

No fixed panel width API; layout is driven by columns/rows/item sizes.

Ribbon is recreated each load; persistent user re-arrangements are not stored unless you implement them separately.

If you want, I can generate a mini class map of your current ribbon (tab → panels → columns → rows → items) from the code we just wrote so you can paste it into your Obsidian architecture index.

Here’s the up-to-date workflow/architecture snapshot for the CBI Tags toolset (what we just wired into your Ribbon).

CBI Tags — Workflow & Architecture Summary

Purpose

Consistent valve tagging across drawings:

place and link bubble blocks to valve blocks,

keep tag text in sync,

report/fix broken links,

allocate IDs from the project’s JSON store (no duplicates).

UI & Commands

Ribbon (Tab: “CBI Tools” → Panel: “Tags”)

Tag Manager (large button) → CBI_TagManager

Add Tag → CBI_AddTag

Link Tag → CBI_LinkTag

Sync Bubbles → CBI_SyncBubbles

Report Links → CBI_ReportLinks

Wiring

RibbonInitializer builds the tab/panels/buttons.

RibbonCommandHandler routes ribbon button clicks to your existing commands via SendStringToExecute.

Key Classes (where the work happens)

TagAddService
Inserts/places bubble block, sets attributes, allocates ID if the valve doesn’t have one yet, writes/updates XData link record.

XDataLinkUtil
Low-level XData helpers: ensure RegApp, read/write link tuples, add/read valve ID list, etc.

TagConstants
Central names/keys used across the toolset:

LinkRegApp = "CBI_TAG_LINKS"

XData keys: CHILD, PARENT, VALVE_TAG_ID

Bubble attribute tags: INST_LOOP (number/top), INSTR_FUNC (letters/bottom)

ProjectTagStore / ProjectTagPath
Reads the ProjectStore v1.0 JSON (systems/prefix/pad/range), allocates next number per system. NOD key for the path: CBI/ProjectStore/Path.

ProjectTagStoreCfg
Small DTO/config used by allocation (system prefix, pad width, etc.).

Data Model

On Valve (BlockReference XData under RegApp CBI_TAG_LINKS):

One or more VALVE_TAG_ID strings (e.g., "V0123"). Supports many bubbles pointing to same valve.

On Bubble (BlockReference XData under same RegApp):

PARENT = valve handle (string)

CHILD  = bubble handle (redundant but useful during audits)

(Bubble attributes mirror the ID text: number/letters split across INST_LOOP + INSTR_FUNC.)

Core Workflows

1) Add Tag (CBI_AddTag)

1. User picks valve (or confirm target).

2. XDataLinkUtil.GetValveTagIds(tr, valve)

If an ID already exists: reuse it (no new allocation).

Insert bubble, set attributes from existing ID, write/refresh link XData.

If no ID: allocate via ProjectTagStore.AllocateNextInSystem(system) using JSON system (Prefix, Pad, Next), set bubble attributes, write VALVE_TAG_ID to valve XData, write link tuple.

3. Commit.

2) Link Tag (CBI_LinkTag)

1. Pick valve, then pick existing bubble with correct geometry.

2. Validate: bubble block name matches TagConstants.BubbleBlockName (or current chosen type).

3. Read or allocate valve ID (only if missing), then set bubble attributes and write link XData (parent/bubble + keep VALVE_TAG_ID list on valve).

4. Commit.

3) Sync Bubbles (CBI_SyncBubbles)

1. For each bubble linked to a valve, recompute text from the valve’s current VALVE_TAG_ID (first entry or chosen one), update attributes (INST_LOOP / INSTR_FUNC).

2. Heal missing/old XData where possible.

4) Report Links (CBI_ReportLinks)

Scan drawing:

orphan bubbles (no parent or parent missing),

valves with no VALVE_TAG_ID,

duplicate or conflicting IDs,

mismatched attribute text ↔ stored ID.

Output to command line and/or a grid in Tag Manager UI.

Persistence & Contracts

Project JSON (ProjectStore v1.0)
Systems→{Prefix, Pad, Start, End, Next} controls ID allocation.
All tools locate JSON via DWG NOD key CBI/ProjectStore/Path and validate with ProjectStoreValidator, IO via ProjectStoreIo (atomic save).

DWG XData under RegApp CBI_TAG_LINKS is the source of truth inside a drawing. JSON remains the allocator/history; XData is runtime truth.

Error Handling & Guards

Always EnsureRegApp(db, tr, TagConstants.LinkRegApp) before XData writes.

Safe reads via tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead/Write) and UpgradeOpen() only when needed.

Commands respect document locks; long operations run within one transaction loop per drawing.

Images & Ribbon Notes

Large button uses 32×32 PNG; small buttons 16×16 PNG (or 24×24 if supported) via Resources.resx.

Panel layout: one large button column, one stacked small-button column (AutoCAD may compress to a flyout at narrow widths; widen panel to keep expanded).

What’s Working Now

Ribbon tab/panel created (RibbonInitializer); split across two panels or one with two columns depending on width.

Commands are hooked through your RibbonCommandHandler.

TagConstants, XDataLinkUtil, and TagAddService aligned to use CBI_TAG_LINKS schema and valve-ID reuse.

Next Steps

1. Finish Tag Manager form (host quick actions + report grid).

2. Add CBI_SyncBubbles & CBI_ReportLinks implementations (audit helpers exist in XDataLinkUtil; wire them).

3. Polish Ribbon images/widths; consider a “Panel Title = Tags” for clarity.

4. Add basic unit/integration tests for ID allocation (JSON Next roll-forward and bounds).

If you want, I can generate a compact README.md for your repo and an Obsidian note version that matches this outline.

Here’s the current workflow + architecture summary for the CBI_AddTag command and the CBI_TagManager palette — tight, presentation-ready.

CBI Tagging Tool — Workflow & Architecture (v1.0)

1) Purpose

Consistent creation, allocation, and management of valve tags in AutoCAD P&ID drawings, backed by a project-level store and surfaced in a dockable Tag Manager palette.

---

2) Commands & Entry Points

CBI_AddTag — interactive placement/assignment of a valve tag.

CBI_TagManager — opens the Tag Manager palette for browse/search/fix.

Registration Standard: AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs contains one-line [CommandMethod] shims that route to logic classes (no business logic in the registry). (Ref: Command Registration Standard)

---

3) High-Level Components

AutoCAD2020

Services

TagAddService — user flow for picking a target, allocating/assigning tag, inserting/refreshing graphics, and linking metadata.

TagAllocationService — hands out the next number from ProjectStore Systems (with Prefix/Pad/Next).

TagQueryService — enumerates existing/duplicate/orphan tags in the current drawing.

ProjectStore

ProjectStoreResolver — locates the JSON store path from NOD.

ProjectStoreValidator — ensures schema compliance (v1.0).

ProjectStoreIo — atomic read/write of the JSON store.

Tagging

ValveTagParser — parse/format strings (e.g., V-0123).

XDataLinkUtil — ties DWG entities to tag IDs via XData/handles.

Palette

TagManagerPaletteControl — dockable UI (grid, filters, actions).

TagActions — zoom/highlight, reassign, release/reuse, renumber.

Shared Project

Utilities/DTOs

TagRecord, TagStatus (Active/Orphan/Duplicate), TagChangeLogEntry.

SystemCounter (Prefix/Pad/Start/End/Next).

Guard/Result helpers, logging primitives.

---

4) Data Model (ProjectStore v1.0 — Canonical)

{
  "Version": "1.0",
  "Config": { "GraceDays": 7, "PreferReleasedLowestFirst": true },
  "Project": { "Name": "...", "CreatedUtc": "..." },
  "Systems": {
    "GENERAL": { "Prefix": "V", "Pad": 4, "Start": 2001, "End": 9999, "Next": 2001 }
  },
  "Blocks": {
    "Valve": {
      "Types": {
        "Ball": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BALL-VALVE.dwg",
        "Butterfly": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BFLY-VALVE.dwg"
        // Optional: "Check": ...
      }
    }
  },
  "Tags": [
    // { "Id":"V-0201", "System":"GENERAL", "EntityHandle":"...", "Status":"Active", "History":[...] }
  ],
  "Released": [],
  "History": []
}

NOD Key (DWG): CBI/ProjectStore/Path → absolute JSON path.

Valve block defs: see Blocks.Valve.Types above.

---

5) Storage & Linking

Project-level JSON (single source of truth) — read/write via ProjectStoreIo (atomic save).

Drawing-level XData — entity ↔ tag link: stores Tag Id + lightweight metadata; primary key is Tag Id; resilient handle re-bind where possible.

Derived Index in Memory — TagManager grid uses a live projection merged from DWG + ProjectStore for status (Active/Orphan/Duplicate/Missing).

---

6) End-to-End Workflows

A) Add Tag (CBI_AddTag)

1. Resolve Store: read NOD CBI/ProjectStore/Path → load JSON → validate schema v1.0.

2. Pick Target: user selects valve block (or insertion point for placement).

3. Determine System: default GENERAL (or prompt/derive via layer/selection).

4. Allocate Number: TagAllocationService.Next(system) → build Prefix + padded number (e.g., V-0201) and pre-validate against duplicates.

5. Insert/Refresh Graphics:

If placing new: insert proper valve block (from Blocks.Valve.Types), set attributes.

If tagging existing: update attributes / mtext, ensure orientation/rotation rules (parallel or standard orientation per your palette settings).

6. Link Entity: attach XData with Tag Id + store the entity handle in JSON.

7. Persist: update ProjectStore (Tags[], Systems[sys].Next++) with an atomic save.

8. Notify UI: palette (if open) refreshes and highlights the new/updated record.

B) Manage & Fix (CBI_TagManager palette)

1. Load & Merge: read ProjectStore; scan current DWG for known tag links.

2. Surface Status:

Duplicate: same Tag Id bound to >1 entity.

Orphan: Tag Id in store, but entity missing/not found.

Unlinked: entity shows tag graphics but no store record.

3. User Actions:

Zoom/Highlight an entry.

Reassign orphan Tag Id to a picked entity.

Release/Recycle a Tag Id back to available pool (rules-guarded).

Batch Renumber within system range using Prefix/Pad; updates graphics + store.

4. Persist: changes recorded with History entries; atomic write per operation.

---

7) Validation, Guardrails & Errors

Schema Gate: ProjectStoreValidator (must be Version 1.0, required keys).

Range Gate: on allocate/renumber, ensure Start ≤ Next ≤ End.

Collision Gate: forbid allocating an Id already Active; duplicates flagged and require manual resolution.

DWG Safety: use transactions; ensure write access; handle eLockViolation gracefully (retry/upgrade open).

Atomicity: ProjectStore writes are all-or-nothing; temp file + swap.

---

8) UI/UX (Palette)

Grid Columns: Tag Id, System, Status, EntityHandle, LastChanged, Notes.

Filters: Status filter (Active/Orphan/Duplicate), System, Text search.

Context Menu: Zoom to entity, Reassign, Release, Copy tag, Open block path.

Non-destructive: changes previewed; commit shows a concise diff log.

---

9) Deployment & Startup

Build target: .NET Framework compatible with AutoCAD 2020; C# 7.3 (language version 9.0+ OK per compiler setting).

Output path: C:\CBICAD\AutoCAD\ (DLLs + Blocks).

Auto-load: LISP/registry or bundle to load the DLL and register commands at AutoCAD startup.

Ribbon: single button (or dropdown) to CBI_AddTag, another to open Tag Manager; does not replace stock panels.

---

10) Extensibility Hooks

Systems: add more (e.g., UTILITY/PROCESS) with their own Prefix/Pad ranges.

Valve Types: extend Blocks.Valve.Types (e.g., Check) with explicit DWG paths.

Project Store: Released and History enable audit trails and future “release locking”.

Batch Ops: future: cross-drawing reconciliation (multi-file session with single ProjectStore).

---

11) Logging & Change History

Palette Log Pane: human-readable actions (“Allocated V-0201 in GENERAL”).

History[] in ProjectStore: timestamp, actor, action, before/after snapshot for compliance.

Crash-ready: when reporting issues, use CBI Crash Report Template (tool, steps, exception, stack snippet, extra details).

---

12) Known Constraints / Current TODOs

Duplicate resolution UX: confirm final tie-break rules when two entities claim the same Id.

Orphan reclaim: optional grace-period before recycling.

Rotation policy: ensure final consistent rule (parallel to run vs fixed angles) is applied in both AddTag and Refresh flows.

Cross-DWG coherence: next phase for project-wide scans.

---

If you’d like, I can turn this into:

a one-page PDF handout (with a block diagram),

or a slide outline (title + bullets per section) you can drop into PowerPoint.

Certainly! Based on our recent conversations and implementations, here is the current workflow schema / architecture summary for the JSON + XData / Drawing Dictionary Integration Tool:

---

🧠 Tool Name (Working):

CBI_MetadataStorageTool

---

🏗️ Purpose:

Enable structured storage of custom metadata in AutoCAD drawings using JSON—either embedded as XData on entities or stored globally in the Named Objects Dictionary (NOD).

---

⚙️ Architecture Overview

🔹 1. Metadata Source

User or Tool-defined metadata (e.g., pipe attributes, project tags, notes)

Represented as a strongly typed C# object:

public class EntityMetadata
{
    public string Material { get; set; }
    public double Diameter { get; set; }
    public DateTime InstallDate { get; set; }
}

---

🔹 2. Serialization to JSON

Metadata is converted to a JSON string using:

var json = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(metadata);

---

🔹 3. Storage Location Options

a. 🔸 XData on Entity

Target entity gets a new XData entry:

RegApp Name: CBI_Metadata

TypedValue Format:

new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataRegAppName, "CBI_Metadata"),
new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString, json)

b. 🔸 Named Objects Dictionary (NOD)

Global metadata stored under:

Key: "CBI/GlobalMetadata"

Structure:

A DBDictionary sub-entry for grouping.

One or more Xrecords for entries:

xrec.Data = new ResultBuffer(
    new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, json)
);

---

🔹 4. Deserialization / Retrieval

Retrieve the JSON string from XData or NOD.

Deserialize back into typed object:

var metadata = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<EntityMetadata>(json);

---

🔹 5. User Interface Options

Hidden by default.

Optional UI extensions:

Context menu: “View Metadata”

Ribbon button: “Manage Entity Metadata”

Palette for editing JSON as form fields

---

🧱 Component Structure

Layer	Class / Module	Responsibility

Data Contract	EntityMetadata.cs	Defines structured fields to be serialized
Serialization	JsonMetadataHelper.cs	Handles JSON (de)serialization
XData Layer	XDataWriter, XDataReader	Attach/read JSON to/from entity
NOD Layer	NodMetadataStore.cs	Write/read JSON to/from drawing dictionary
Integration	CBI_MetadataService.cs	Coordinates tagging, retrieval, and display
UI Layer (optional)	MetadataPalette.cs	Provides editing UI (if enabled)

---

🧪 Typical Workflow

🔁 Entity Metadata (XData Path):

1. User selects an entity.

2. Tool prompts for metadata or loads from config.

3. JSON is serialized and stored as XData.

4. On retrieval, JSON is parsed and displayed.

🌍 Global Metadata (Dictionary Path):

1. Project-wide config or tag is generated.

2. JSON written to the NOD as an Xrecord.

3. External tools (or other drawings) can access this data.

---

✅ Benefits

Human-readable but structured metadata

No need for custom object classes inside AutoCAD

Compatible across versions (as it's just strings)

Supports internal and external data interchange

---

Let me know if you'd like a diagram or want this formatted into a Markdown spec for Obsidian or documentation.

CBI Data Explorer — Workflow & Architecture Summary

Purpose

A dockable, developer-focused palette that inspects metadata attached to drawings and entities: NOD (Named Objects Dictionary), entity XRecords (Extension Dictionary), and XData. Read-only first; extensible to search, edit, export, and navigate.

---

User Workflow (Happy Path)

1. Load & Open

NETLOAD the DLL → run CBI-DATA-EXPLORER → palette appears.

2. Inspect Selection (entity-scoped)

Select one or more entities in the drawing.

Click Refresh Selection → collectors enumerate XData and XRecords for each selected entity.

Tree shows: Source → Key → Scope (e.g., XData → CBI_TEST → 2A7B (BlockReference)).

Grid shows typed rows: Source | Scope | Key | Type | DXF | Value.

3. Inspect Drawing (drawing-scoped)

Click Refresh NOD (Drawing) → NOD + nested dictionaries + XRecords load.

4. Navigate/Act on Entities

Left-click a Scope node → highlight entity.

Right-click a Scope node → Zoom to / Select in drawing / Copy handle.

5. Filter

Tree selection + text filter compose into the grid without recursion (crash-safe).

---

Commands (Registration Standard)

CBI-DATA-EXPLORER → opens the palette (PaletteSetManager.Show()).

(Dev helpers, optional) CBI_ADD_XDATA, CBI_ADD_XREC → attach sample data for testing.

> Matches your Command Registration Standard: thin [CommandMethod] stubs in AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs route to UI/logic.

---

Project Structure (recommended)

AutoCAD2020/
  CBI_Data_Explorer/
    Models/
      PropertyItem.cs
    Collectors/
      IPropertyCollector.cs
      XDataCollector.cs
      XRecordCollector.cs
      NODCollector.cs
    UI/
      CBIDataExplorerControl.cs           // logic/events
      CBIDataExplorerControl.Designer.cs  // UI layout
      PaletteSetManager.cs
    Commands/
      DataExplorerCommands.cs

---

Core Components

Palette UI (CBIDataExplorerControl)

TreeView (left) shows hierarchical sources.

DataGridView (right) shows typed rows.

Buttons: Refresh Selection, Refresh NOD; optional Find by RegApp / XRecord key.

Text filter: dynamic, composes with tree selection.

Context menu on scope nodes: Zoom to, Select, Copy handle.

Highlight manager: tracks/unhighlights previous selections.

Binding Model (PropertyItem)

Source (XData/XRecord/NOD)

Scope (e.g., 2A7B (BlockReference) or Drawing (NOD))

Key (RegApp / Dictionary key / path)

Type, DxfCode, Value (stringified, typed)

Collectors (plug-in model)

IPropertyCollector → CollectForObject(...), CollectForDrawing(...)

XDataCollector: reads Entity.XData → groups by RegApp.

XRecordCollector: reads per-entity Extension Dictionary XRecords.

NODCollector: walks Named Objects Dictionary (and nested dictionaries) for XRecords.

---

Data Flow (Selection Refresh)

User selects entities → Click "Refresh Selection"
  → Doc.LockDocument()
  → StartTransaction()
  → For each selected ObjectId:
        tr.GetObject(..., ForRead)
        foreach collector:
            items += collector.CollectForObject(tr, dbObj)
  → Commit
  → UpdateItemsAndTree(items)
       - rebuild Tree: Source → Key → Scope
       - RefreshGridFromFilters()   // applies tree + text filter safely

Data Flow (NOD Refresh)

Click "Refresh NOD (Drawing)"
  → Doc.LockDocument()
  → StartTransaction()
  → foreach collector:
        items += collector.CollectForDrawing(tr, db)
  → Commit
  → UpdateItemsAndTree(items) → RefreshGridFromFilters()

---

UI Interaction Logic (crash-safe)

No recursion. A single RefreshGridFromFilters() composes:

1. tree selection filter → 2) text filter → bind grid.

Reentrancy Guard (_isFiltering) prevents rapid event loops.

Tree Node Mouse:

Left-click Scope → HighlightOnly(ObjectId)

Right-click Scope → context menu → ZoomToEntity(ObjectId) / SelectInDrawing(ObjectId) / Clipboard handle.

---

AutoCAD Integration Notes

Always wrap DB access inside:

using (doc.LockDocument())

using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())

Open entities ForRead for inspect; upgrade to ForWrite only when editing is added.

Convert handle hex → ObjectId via db.TryGetObjectId(new Handle(hex), out id).

---

Dependencies & Targets

AutoCAD 2020, .NET Framework for that version

C# 7.3 (Language Version 9.0+ is OK per your preference)

WinForms (explicit aliases avoid WPF collisions):

using WinForms = System.Windows.Forms;

using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;

---

Error Handling & Stability

Guarded filters: single flow avoids AfterSelect ↔ TextChanged recursion.

Try/catch around handle parsing / ObjectId resolution (fail soft).

Null/XData missing: collectors return empty lists (no exceptions).

Tiny extents: zoom pad + fallback width/height to avoid invalid views.

---

Extensibility Roadmap

1. Quality-of-life

Copy cell/row to clipboard; export JSON/CSV of current grid.

Status bar: N items from M entities.

2. Search

Find by RegApp / XRecord key across full DB (already sketched).

“Has XData” / “Has Extension Dict” filters.

3. Editing (opt-in, guarded)

Inline edit grid → propose change → confirm dialog → apply:

XData: rebuild ResultBuffer, assign to Entity.XData.

XRecord/NOD: open Xrecord ForWrite, set Data.

Validation profiles (e.g., Valve parent/child link sanity).

4. Link with other CBI tools

Double-click row → open the related Tag Manager / Properties Palette context.

Respect ProjectStore v1.0 if record keys reference project paths.

---

Versioning & Delivery (per your standards)

Assembly: CBI.DataExplorer v0.1.0

CHANGELOG.md:

0.1.0 – Initial read-only palette, Tree + Grid, Selection/NOD refresh, highlight/zoom, crash-safe filters.

XML docs on public types (PropertyItem, collectors, UI entry points).

Unit tests (where possible) for collectors using in-memory DB stubs.

Deployment: copy DLLs to C:\CBICAD\AutoCAD\ (or your E:\Development\Company\... layout).

Scope Guard: This tool is an inspector only; editing is a separate scope flag.

---

If you want, I can generate a printable architecture PDF (class diagram + sequence) or wire the export JSON/CSV next as a safe incremental feature.

Here’s the current workflow schema / architecture summary for the Layer Utility Tool — fully aligned to our split-deserializer, dual-project architecture (AutoCAD2020 + Shared Project).

---

🧱 Layer Utility Tool — Architecture Summary (v3.5)

🗂️ Primary Purpose

A form-based AutoCAD utility to:

Import layer definitions from Excel.

Validate them (type, range, and structure).

Preview & correct in a DataGridView.

Apply them to the current drawing.

Write back clean definitions to the NOD (Named Objects Dictionary).

---

🔩 Architecture Layers

1️⃣ AutoCAD_Shared_Project

Purpose: Excel handling, shared data classes, string-based operations only.

Folder	Key Files	Responsibility

Layer_Operations	LayerDefinition.cs	Plain data model — all string properties (e.g. ColorIndex, Lineweight, Plot).
	LayerDefinitionDeserializer.cs	Excel reader version — parses from Excel or JSON, no AutoCAD references.
	LayerExcelImporter.cs	Reads .xlsx → builds Dictionary<string, List<LayerDefinition>> grouped by discipline.
	ColorNameHelper.cs (Removed)	Replaced by AutoCAD2020 version (requires Autodesk.AutoCAD.Colors).

🧠 Role: Everything here is pure data, no AutoCAD types, and safe to use outside of AutoCAD.

---

2️⃣ AutoCAD2020

Purpose: AutoCAD-specific operations: deserialization, validation, and NOD writing.

Folder	Key Files	Responsibility

CadInterop	LayerDefinitionDeserializer.cs	NOD reader version — converts ResultBuffer → typed LayerDefinition list.
Layer_Operations	ColorNameHelper.cs	Manages ACI ↔ ColorName lookups. Provides GetColorName(int) and GetIndexFromNameOrDefault(string, int).
	LineweightHelper.cs (added)	Manages conversion between string names (e.g. "0.25 mm") and numeric/enum values.
NOD_X_Data	XRecordHelper.cs, NODWriter.cs	Handles reading/writing NOD entries and layer buffers.
Forms	LayerUtility.cs, LayerUtilityValidation.cs	UI layer: import Excel, validate, show preview grid, apply layers, write to NOD.

---

⚙️ Process Flow Overview

Step 1 — Import Excel

📍 File: LayerUtility.cs → setupTemplateToolStripMenuItem_Click

1. User chooses Excel file.

2. LayerExcelImporter.LoadFromExcelGroupedBySheet() reads data.

3. Data stored as Dictionary<string, List<LayerDefinition>> (discipline → layers).

---

Step 2 — Validation Preview

📍 Files:

LayerUtility.cs — builds List<LayerValidationResult>

LayerUtilityValidation.cs — DataGridView preview

1. For each LayerDefinition, convert to:

Readable color name via ColorNameHelper.GetColorName(int)

Readable lineweight via LayerUtilityValidation.GetLineweightName(LineWeight)

2. Populate DataGridView in LayerUtilityValidation.

3. User reviews / skips rows.

---

Step 3 — Apply Changes

📍 File: LayerUtility.cs → btnApply_Click

1. Validate data types again using NODTypeValidator.

2. Open AutoCAD document lock via DocumentLockHelper.WithDocumentLock.

3. Use CadLayerService.CreateLayersFromDefinitions(validLayers) to create layers.

4. Write results to NOD using XRecordHelper.WriteLayerDefinitionsToNOD.

---

Step 4 — Save to NOD

📍 File: NODWriter.cs

Serializes each LayerDefinition as a sequence of 6 TypedValue entries:

LayerName
DisciplineKey
ColorIndex
Linetype
Lineweight
Plot
-----

---

🧩 Type-Split Design

The tool relies on two deserializers to handle the same schema safely across both layers:

Context	Source	Deserializer Used	Project	Notes

Import from Excel	ClosedXML workbook	AutoCAD_Shared_Project.Layer_Operations.LayerDefinitionDeserializer	Shared	All strings.
Read from AutoCAD NOD	ResultBuffer	AutoCAD2020.CadInterop.LayerDefinitionDeserializer	AutoCAD2020	Converts to typed values.

---

🔍 Known Conversion Points

Property	Excel → NOD Conversion	Helper Class

ColorIndex	ColorNameHelper.GetIndexFromNameOrDefault(string, default)	✅ ColorNameHelper
Lineweight	LineweightHelper.GetValueFromNameOrDefault(string)	✅ LineweightHelper
Plot	Convert.ToBoolean(string)	Inline conversion
Name, DisciplineKey, Linetype	ExtractRightSide(string)	Inline method

---

💾 Final Data Flow Summary

Excel (.xlsx)
   ↓
[Shared] LayerExcelImporter
   ↓
[Shared] LayerDefinition (all string)
   ↓
UI Preview (LayerUtilityValidation)
   ↓
User Approval (Skip/Valid)
   ↓
[AutoCAD2020] Conversion helpers
   ↓
Typed LayerDefinition
   ↓
CadLayerService → CreateLayers
   ↓
XRecordHelper → NOD Write

---

🧭 Current Development Status

✅ Excel import logic working
✅ NOD deserializer functional
✅ Validation grid functional
⚠️ Type conversions (ColorIndex, Lineweight) under refinement
⚠️ Missing LoadValidationResults method (simple fix)

**Cbi Unified Workflow Schema V1**

# CBI Unified Workflow Schema v1.0 (Master)

**Date:** 2025-11-03  
**Maintainers:** Brian Ranstead · ChatGPT (GPT‑5 Thinking)  
**Scope:** Consolidated architecture + workflows across: Ribbon, Properties Palette, Pipe/Valve Tagging, Label Anchor/Refresh + Reverse Sync, Valve Insert Split‑Button, Line Tagging, Data Explorer, JSON/XData/NOD Metadata Tool, Layer Utility.  
**Canonical Contracts:** ProjectStore v1.0 · NOD/XData naming · Command Registration Standard.

---

## 0) North‑Star Goals
- One **canonical ProjectStore v1.0** controls systems, numbering, and block definitions.
- All tools resolve **NOD key** `CBI/ProjectStore/Path` → validate with **ProjectStoreValidator** → read/write via **ProjectStoreIo** (atomic).
- **Command Registry Standard** enforced: all `[CommandMethod]` live in `AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs` as one‑line forwarders.
- **Providers + Routers** pattern for UI inspectors (Properties Palette) and **Services** pattern for edit/insert flows.
- **XData for per‑entity**, **NOD/XRecords for drawing/global**, **JSON for project**; never duplicate truths.

---

## 1) Shared Contracts & Naming

### 1.1 ProjectStore v1.0 (canonical)
```
Version:"1.0"
Config, Project,
Systems:{ <key>:{ Prefix, Pad, Start, End, Next } },
Blocks:{ Valve:{ Types:{ Ball="INST_BALL-VALVE", Butterfly="INST_BFLY-VALVE", Check? } } },
Tags, Released, History
```
**DWG locator:** NOD key `CBI/ProjectStore/Path` (absolute path).  
**Validation:** `ProjectStoreValidator` (must match v1.0).  
**IO:** `ProjectStoreIo` (atomic save w/ temp+swap).

### 1.2 XData / RegApps
- **CBI_PIPE_RUN** – pipe‑run schema (properties + computed ItemTag)
- **CBI_INSTRUMENT** – instrument schema
- **CBI_COMPONENT** – valve/inline component schema
- **CBI_TAG_LINKS** – tag linking & IDs (VALVE_TAG_ID, PARENT/CHILD handles)
- **CBI_LINK** – label↔host linking (HOST_HANDLE, LAST_TEXT)
- **CBI_PIPELAB** – label anchors set (HostId, LabelSetId, Anchors[..])
- **CBI_LINE** – line tag tokens ([Size, Fluid, Area, LineNo, Spec, Insul, Raw])
- **CBI_Metadata** – JSON blob per entity (metadata tool)

### 1.3 NOD / Dictionaries
- `CBI/ProjectStore/Path` – string path (ResultBuffer Text)
- `CBI_LINE_STORE` – per‑scope counters (DXF 90 Int for `Next`)
- `CBI/PIPELAB/Index` – host/label index
- Layer Utility storage: one XRecord per layer (6‑tuple + separator `"-----"`)

### 1.4 Command Registration Standard
`AutoCAD2020\Commands.cs` only routes to logic; no business logic.

---

## 2) Ribbon Runtime (no XML/CUIX)
**Module:** `RibbonInitializer` (+ `RibbonCommandHandler`)  
**Tab:** CBI Tools  
**Panels:** Home · Tags · Valve Tools · Labels · Utilities  
**Behavior:** Build at `Initialize()`; items dispatch via `SendStringToExecute` to real commands.

---

## 3) Properties Palette (Inspector/Editor)
**Host:** `CbiPropertiesPalette` (owns `PropertiesControl`)  
**Router:** `PropertyRouter` → ordered list of `IPropertySourceProvider`  
**Providers:** `PipeRunPropertyProvider` (CBI_PIPE_RUN), `ComponentPropertyProvider` (CBI_COMPONENT), `InstrumentPropertyProvider` (CBI_INSTRUMENT), `LineTagSettingsProvider` (tool home).  
**Persistence:** XData for entity fields; **no doc‑level lock** needed for XData writes; **ForWrite** inside tx.  
**UI:** PropertyGrid + Toolbar (Apply/Revert/Save/Load, Auto‑Apply).  
**Home Preview:** `NodLineNumberStore.Peek("LINE"/"TAG")`.

**Event Flow (ASCII)**
```
Selection -> CbiPropertiesPalette -> PropertyRouter.Resolve(ctx) -> Provider.GetSelectedObject()
Apply -> PropertiesControl.ApplyRequested -> PropertyRouter.ApplyIfPossible(model, ctx) -> Provider.Apply()
```

---

## 4) Valve Insert Split‑Button (Ball/Butterfly/Check)
**UI:** RibbonSplitButton → `CBI_InsertValve_Ball|Butterfly|Check`  
**Service:** `ValveInsertService.InsertValveBlock(blockKey)`  
**Block map:** `ProjectStore.Blocks.Valve.Types` (Ball, Butterfly, Check).  
**Prefill:** read defaults from ProjectStore; create `AttributeReferences` on insert.  
**Path convention:** `C:\CBICAD\AutoCAD\Blocks\Instrumentation\…`

---

## 5) Tagging Suite (Valve/Control)
**Commands:** `CBI_AddTag`, `CBI_TagManager`, `CBI_LinkTag`, `CBI_SyncBubbles`, `CBI_ReportLinks`  
**Allocator:** `TagAllocationService.Next(system)` ← `ProjectStore.Systems`  
**On Valve (XData CBI_TAG_LINKS):** one or more `VALVE_TAG_ID` entries  
**On Bubble (XData CBI_TAG_LINKS):** `PARENT` (valve handle), `CHILD` (self)  
**Reuse rule:** Same valve → reuse existing ID; copied foreign IDs → purge & allocate.

**Happy Path (AddTag)**
```
Resolve ProjectStore → Pick valve → ensure/reuse Id or allocate → insert/refresh bubble attrs
→ write link XData both sides → persist ProjectStore (Tags, Systems.Next++)
```

---

## 6) Labeling (Anchors, Refresh, Reverse Sync)
**Place/Refresh:** `CBI_PlacePipeLabels`, `CBI_RefreshPipeLabels`  
**Anchor schema:** Param (0–1), Side (L/R), Offset, Orientation (Tangent/World), Role, StyleHint, Jitter  
**Index:** `CBI/PIPELAB/Index` maps HostId ↔ LabelSetId/LabelIds

**Forward watcher:** `LabelLinkService` queues host edits; flush post‑command; re‑compute and move labels (respect `ManualOffset`).

**Reverse watcher:** `LabelReverseSyncService` watches label text edits → updates host key in XData/NOD; stamps `LAST_TEXT` on label to avoid ping‑pong.

---

## 7) Line Tagging (PIPE LINE number)
**Command:** `CBI_PLACELINETAG`  
**Store:** XData (CBI_LINE) `[Size, Fluid, Area, LineNo, Spec, Insul, Raw]`  
**Counters:** `NodLineNumberStore` in `CBI_LINE_STORE` keyed by sanitized `{Fluid}|{Area}|{Spec}`  
**Render:** `MTextRenderer` (Attachment MiddleCenter, bg fill, style `CBI-LineTag`).

---

## 8) Data Explorer (Inspector)
**Command:** `CBI-DATA-EXPLORER`  
**Collectors:** `XDataCollector`, `XRecordCollector`, `NODCollector`  
**UI:** Tree (Source→Key→Scope) + Grid (typed rows) + context menu (Zoom/Select/Copy handle).  
**Safety:** doc locks + tx; crash‑safe filters.

---

## 9) JSON + XData/NOD Metadata Tool
**Goal:** Persist typed metadata via JSON either per‑entity (XData `CBI_Metadata`) or drawing‑wide (NOD `CBI/GlobalMetadata`).  
**Helpers:** `JsonMetadataHelper`, `XDataWriter/Reader`, `NodMetadataStore`.  
**Optional UI:** Palette/editor w/ schema‑driven forms.

---

## 10) Layer Utility (Excel→Validate→Apply→NOD)
**Shared (no Autodesk refs):** Excel import → `LayerDefinition` (all strings).  
**AutoCAD2020:** conversion helpers (`ColorNameHelper`, `LineweightHelper`), `CadLayerService`, `XRecordHelper/NODWriter`.  
**Write schema:** per layer 6‑tuple (Name, DisciplineKey, ColorIndex, Linetype, Lineweight, Plot, `"-----"`).

---

## 11) Cross‑Tool Dependency Graph (text)
```
ProjectStore v1.0 (JSON)
  ├─ ValveInsertService (Blocks map)
  ├─ TagAllocationService (Systems.Next)
  ├─ TagManager (Tags[], Released, History)
  └─ (future) Label style profiles

NOD
  ├─ CBI/ProjectStore/Path → ProjectStore resolver
  ├─ CBI_LINE_STORE → NodLineNumberStore (LINE/TAG counters)
  └─ CBI/PIPELAB/Index → Label index

XData
  ├─ CBI_PIPE_RUN / CBI_COMPONENT / CBI_INSTRUMENT → Properties Palette
  ├─ CBI_TAG_LINKS → Tagging suite
  ├─ CBI_LINK → Label reverse/forward sync
  ├─ CBI_PIPELAB → Anchors
  └─ CBI_LINE → LineTag parts
```

---

## 12) Uniform Policies & Guardrails
- **Document Etiquette:** Save/restore active space/view; avoid hijacking UCS; minimal redraws.
- **Transactions:** Grouped ops; open ForRead and UpgradeOpen only when needed.
- **Locks:** Use `doc.LockDocument()` for batch edits; XData writes can be light, still within tx.
- **RegApp:** Always ensure before XData writes.
- **Change History:** Optional log via `ChangeHistoryManager` for tools that mutate.
- **Error Classes:** Graceful `eLockViolation` handling; retry UpgradeOpen; report via palette log.
- **Versioning:** Semantic versions on assemblies; XML docs; CHANGELOGs; unit test seeds where feasible.
- **Scope Guard:** Keep each UI/tool within its declared scope; start new threads for new tools.

---

## 13) Open Issues / TODOs (Prioritized)
1. **ProjectStore enforcement everywhere** (ensure all tools call `ProjectStoreValidator` up front).  
2. **Valve Insert: autoload missing blocks** from canonical path before insert.  
3. **Tag Manager palette:** implement `SyncBubbles` + `ReportLinks` + reassign/release UX.  
4. **Labeling:** finalize placement policy provider + `ManualOffset` persistence; add batch `Refresh` by selection.  
5. **Properties Palette:** add multi‑entity batch edit; provider checklist doc.  
6. **Layer Utility:** finalize type conversions; end‑to‑end Excel→NOD→Reimport test.  
7. **Data Explorer:** add JSON/CSV export; search by RegApp/Key.  
8. **Reverse Sync:** guard loop with `LAST_TEXT` token (done conceptually; verify).

---

## 14) Command Matrix (excerpt)
- **Ribbon / Setup**: `CBI_TOOLS_INIT` (optional), `CBI_RIBBON_REBUILD`
- **Palette**: `CBI_PROP_PALETTE`
- **Valve Insert**: `CBI_InsertValve_Ball|Butterfly|Check`
- **Tags**: `CBI_AddTag`, `CBI_TagManager`, `CBI_LinkTag`, `CBI_SyncBubbles`, `CBI_ReportLinks`
- **Labels**: `CBI_PlacePipeLabels`, `CBI_RefreshPipeLabels`, `CBI_Label_Watch_On|Off`, `CBI_Label_ReverseSync_On|Off`
- **Line**: `CBI_PLACELINETAG`
- **Explorer**: `CBI-DATA-EXPLORER`
- **Layer Utility**: `CBI_Layer_Import`, `CBI_Layer_Validate`, `CBI_Layer_Apply`

---

## 15) Appendices

### A) Provider Checklist (Properties Palette)
1) Model class → attributes; 2) Provider impl; 3) Register in `PropertyRouter`; 4) Read/Write XData map; 5) Optional CAD edits; 6) UI verify.

### B) Deterministic Label Math
Param→segment,t → point → tangent/normal → offset side → angle (Tangent/World) → add Jitter → apply.

### C) Sanitization Rules
NOD keys: replace `|` `:` `\` `/` with `_`. Handles as hex strings; always verify `TryGetObjectId`.

---

**End of v1.0 Master**

Awesome — let’s turn this thread into your **CBI Fix Hub** and run a clean, repeatable health check you can keep in the repo.

Below is a tiny, production-ready diagnostic you can drop into your solution. It verifies the **ProjectStore v1.0** contract, drawing **NOD** key, **RegApps**, and **Valve block availability**, then prints a pass/fail report with fixes.

---

# 1) Drop-in “Environment Health Check” command

**File:** `AutoCAD2020/Diagnostics/EnvironmentHealth.cs`  
**Adds command:** `CBI_ENV_HEALTH` (follows your Command Registration Standard)

```csharp
// v0.1.0 (C# 7.3) — CBI Environment Health Check
// Purpose: One-shot diagnostics for ProjectStore v1.0 + DWG state (NOD, RegApps, blocks)

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Newtonsoft.Json;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Diagnostics
{
    internal static class EnvironmentHealth
    {
        // --- Canonical constants per CBI contract ---
        private const string NodKey_ProjectStorePath = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";
        private static readonly string[] RequiredRegApps = {
            "CBI_TAG_LINKS", "CBI_PIPE_RUN", "CBI_INSTRUMENT", "CBI_COMPONENT", "CBI_LINK"
        };

        // Optional but recommended block names (will often be loaded from paths in ProjectStore)
        private static readonly string[] SuggestedValveBlocks = {
            "INST_BALL-VALVE", "INST_BFLY-VALVE" // add "INST_CHECK-VALVE" when ready
        };

        // --- POCOs to minimally validate ProjectStore v1.0 ---
        private class ProjectStore
        {
            public string Version { get; set; }
            public Dictionary<string, SystemCounter> Systems { get; set; }
            public BlocksSection Blocks { get; set; }
        }

        private class SystemCounter
        {
            public string Prefix { get; set; }
            public int Pad { get; set; }
            public int Start { get; set; }
            public int End { get; set; }
            public int Next { get; set; }
        }

        private class BlocksSection
        {
            public ValveSection Valve { get; set; }
        }

        private class ValveSection
        {
            public TypesSection Types { get; set; }
        }

        private class TypesSection
        {
            public string Ball { get; set; }
            public string Butterfly { get; set; }
            public string Check { get; set; } // optional in your spec
        }

        [CommandMethod("CBI_ENV_HEALTH")]
        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed  = doc.Editor;
            var db  = doc.Database;

            ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Environment Health Check starting…\n");

            var passes = new List<string>();
            var fails  = new List<string>();
            var notes  = new List<string>();

            try
            {
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    // 1) ProjectStore path in NOD
                    string storePath = TryReadProjectStorePath(tr, db, out string nodMsg);
                    if (storePath == null)
                    {
                        fails.Add($"NOD: Missing key '{NodKey_ProjectStorePath}'. {nodMsg}");
                        notes.Add("Fix: Write the absolute ProjectStore JSON path into NOD at that key.");
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        passes.Add($"NOD: Found ProjectStore path → {storePath}");
                        // 2) Load & validate ProjectStore v1.0
                        ValidateProjectStore(storePath, passes, fails, notes);
                    }

                    // 3) RegApps present?
                    ValidateRegApps(tr, db, passes, fails, notes);

                    // 4) Valve blocks loaded (optional hint)
                    ValidateValveBlocks(tr, db, passes, notes);

                    tr.Commit();
                }
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                fails.Add($"Exception during health check: {ex.GetType().Name}: {ex.Message}");
            }

            // --- Report ---
            ed.WriteMessage("\n==== CBI ENV HEALTH REPORT =============================\n");
            foreach (var s in passes) ed.WriteMessage("✔ " + s + "\n");
            foreach (var s in fails)  ed.WriteMessage("✘ " + s + "\n");
            if (notes.Count > 0)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNotes / Fixes:\n");
                foreach (var s in notes) ed.WriteMessage("• " + s + "\n");
            }
            ed.WriteMessage("=======================================================\n");

            if (fails.Count == 0)
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Environment looks good. ✅\n");
            else
                ed.WriteMessage("\n[CBI] Issues found. See items marked ✘ and Notes.\n");
        }

        private static string TryReadProjectStorePath(Transaction tr, Database db, out string msg)
        {
            msg = "";
            var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!nod.Contains(NodKey_ProjectStorePath))
            {
                msg = "Key not present in Named Objects Dictionary.";
                return null;
            }
            var xrecId = nod.GetAt(NodKey_ProjectStorePath);
            var xrec   = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(xrecId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            var data   = xrec.Data;
            if (data == null || data.AsArray().Length == 0)
            {
                msg = "XRecord has no data.";
                return null;
            }
            var tvs = data.AsArray();
            // Accept Text or AsciiString; first string is path
            foreach (var tv in tvs)
            {
                if (tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.Text || tv.TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.ExtendedDataAsciiString)
                {
                    var path = tv.Value as string;
                    return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path) ? null : path;
                }
            }
            msg = "No string payload found in XRecord.";
            return null;
        }

        private static void ValidateProjectStore(string path, List<string> passes, List<string> fails, List<string> notes)
        {
            if (!File.Exists(path))
            {
                fails.Add($"ProjectStore: File not found → {path}");
                notes.Add("Fix: Update NOD path to a valid JSON file accessible from this machine.");
                return;
            }

            try
            {
                var json = File.ReadAllText(path);
                var store = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<ProjectStore>(json);

                if (store == null)
                {
                    fails.Add("ProjectStore: JSON deserialization returned null.");
                    notes.Add("Fix: Validate JSON syntax; ensure root object matches v1.0 contract.");
                    return;
                }

                if (!string.Equals(store.Version, "1.0", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    fails.Add($"ProjectStore: Version is '{store.Version ?? "null"}' (expected '1.0').");
                    notes.Add("Fix: Migrate or stamp Version:\"1.0\" per CBI canonical contract.");
                }
                else
                {
                    passes.Add("ProjectStore: Version 1.0 ✅");
                }

                // Systems existence and minimal shape
                if (store.Systems == null || store.Systems.Count == 0)
                {
                    fails.Add("ProjectStore: Systems[] missing or empty.");
                    notes.Add("Fix: Add at least one system with Prefix/Pad/Start/End/Next.");
                }
                else
                {
                    // Spot-check one system for required fields
                    foreach (var kv in store.Systems)
                    {
                        var s = kv.Value;
                        if (s == null || s.Prefix == null || s.Pad <= 0)
                        {
                            fails.Add($"ProjectStore: System '{kv.Key}' missing fields (Prefix/Pad).");
                        }
                    }
                    passes.Add($"ProjectStore: Systems OK ({store.Systems.Count} system(s)).");
                }

                // Valve block mappings (Ball & Butterfly required by your spec)
                if (store.Blocks?.Valve?.Types == null)
                {
                    fails.Add("ProjectStore: Blocks.Valve.Types missing.");
                    notes.Add("Fix: Add block mappings under Blocks.Valve.Types (Ball, Butterfly).");
                }
                else
                {
                    var okBall = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(store.Blocks.Valve.Types.Ball);
                    var okBfly = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(store.Blocks.Valve.Types.Butterfly);
                    if (!okBall) fails.Add("ProjectStore: Blocks.Valve.Types.Ball missing.");
                    if (!okBfly) fails.Add("ProjectStore: Blocks.Valve.Types.Butterfly missing.");
                    if (okBall && okBfly) passes.Add("ProjectStore: Valve block map OK (Ball/Butterfly present).");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                fails.Add($"ProjectStore: Error reading JSON → {ex.GetType().Name}: {ex.Message}");
                notes.Add("Fix: Ensure JSON is valid UTF-8 and accessible; check file locks.");
            }
        }

        private static void ValidateRegApps(Transaction tr, Database db, List<string> passes, List<string> fails, List<string> notes)
        {
            var regTable = (RegAppTable)tr.GetObject(db.RegAppTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            foreach (var name in RequiredRegApps)
            {
                if (regTable.Has(name)) passes.Add($"RegApp: {name} present.");
                else
                {
                    fails.Add($"RegApp: {name} is missing.");
                    notes.Add($"Fix: Ensure writers create/ensure RegApp '{name}' before XData writes.");
                }
            }
        }

        private static void ValidateValveBlocks(Transaction tr, Database db, List<string> passes, List<string> notes)
        {
            var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            var found = new List<string>();
            foreach (var b in SuggestedValveBlocks)
            {
                if (bt.Has(b)) found.Add(b);
            }
            if (found.Count > 0)
                passes.Add($"Blocks: Present in drawing → {string.Join(", ", found)}");
            else
                notes.Add("Hint: No valve blocks currently in drawing. That’s OK if you autoload from ProjectStore paths at insert time.");
        }
    }
}
```

**Commands.cs** (add one-liner):

```csharp
// v0.1.0 — Diagnostics command registration (switchboard only)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static class Commands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_ENV_HEALTH")]
        public static void CBI_ENV_HEALTH() 
            => AutoCAD2020.Diagnostics.EnvironmentHealth.Run();
    }
}
```

> Build, NETLOAD, then run **`CBI_ENV_HEALTH`** in a problem drawing.  
> You’ll get a crisp ✔/✘ report and specific “Fix:” notes right in the command line.

---

# 2) 10-minute “Fix Order” (do this after you run the command)

1) **ProjectStore path in NOD**  
   - If ✘: write the absolute JSON path under NOD key **`CBI/ProjectStore/Path`**.  
   - Re-run `CBI_ENV_HEALTH` to confirm.

2) **ProjectStore v1.0 contract**  
   - Ensure `Version:"1.0"`  
   - **Systems** has at least one entry with `Prefix/Pad/Start/End/Next`.

4) **Valve Insert tool**

6) **Properties Palette**

## Response:
11/3/2025, 4:16:49 PM

Exactly 👍 — this diagnostic runs **inside a drawing** (the active DWG in AutoCAD).  

Here’s what happens when you type `CBI_ENV_HEALTH` after loading the DLL:

---

### 🧩 **Purpose**
It checks whether your **current drawing** (the one open right now) is properly linked into your unified CBI environment.  
In other words, it verifies that all the “connection points” between the DWG and your ProjectStore JSON are healthy.

---

### 🧠 **What it tests inside that drawing**

| Category | What it checks | Why it matters |
|-----------|----------------|----------------|
| **1️⃣ NOD Link** | Looks for a record in the drawing’s Named Objects Dictionary under the key `CBI/ProjectStore/Path`. | Every tool (Valve Insert, Tag Manager, etc.) finds the JSON store path here. If missing, tools don’t know where to read or write project data. |
| **2️⃣ ProjectStore JSON** | Opens that JSON file and confirms it matches your canonical v1.0 structure: <br>• `Version:"1.0"` <br>• `Systems` with Prefix/Pad/Start/End/Next <br>• `Blocks.Valve.Types` (Ball, Butterfly). | Ensures all tools speak the same schema and your JSON isn’t missing expected keys. |
| **3️⃣ RegApps** | Scans the RegAppTable to confirm required application names (`CBI_TAG_LINKS`, `CBI_PIPE_RUN`, `CBI_INSTRUMENT`, `CBI_COMPONENT`, `CBI_LINK`). | These are what your Properties Palette and Tag Manager rely on to read/write XData. |
| **4️⃣ Valve Blocks (optional)** | Checks if `INST_BALL-VALVE` and `INST_BFLY-VALVE` are already loaded in the block table. | Helps you know if your valve symbols are ready or if they’ll need to auto-load from ProjectStore paths. |

---

### 📊 **What you’ll see**
After running it, AutoCAD’s command line will print a report like:

```
[CBI] Environment Health Check starting…

✔ NOD: Found ProjectStore path → C:\CBICAD\Project_Store.json
✔ ProjectStore: Version 1.0 ✅
✔ ProjectStore: Systems OK (1 system)
✔ ProjectStore: Valve block map OK (Ball/Butterfly present)
✘ RegApp: CBI_TAG_LINKS missing.
✘ RegApp: CBI_COMPONENT missing.
✔ Blocks: Present in drawing → INST_BALL-VALVE

Notes / Fixes:
• Ensure RegApp 'CBI_TAG_LINKS' is created before XData writes.
• Ensure RegApp 'CBI_COMPONENT' is created before XData writes.

[CBI] Issues found. See items marked ✘ and Notes.
```

---

### 🧰 **Next Steps**
If you confirm, I’ll:
1. Give you the **`CBI_SET_STORE_PATH`** command next — it writes the correct JSON path into the drawing’s NOD so the health check passes.
2. Then we’ll use `CBI_ENV_HEALTH` again to confirm the drawing is officially “linked” and ready for Tag Manager, Valve Insert, and others to run off the same ProjectStore.

Would you like me to generate that `CBI_SET_STORE_PATH` command next?

Totally agree—let’s **lock the ProjectStore JSON v1.0 first**, then wire every tool to that single source of truth. Here’s a clean, drop-in “canonical” store plus a short rollout plan.

# ProjectStore v1.0 — Canonical (ready to save)
Save this as something like:  
`C:\CBICAD\ProjectStore\MyProject\ProjectStore.json`

```json
{
  "Version": "1.0",
  "Config": {
    "GraceDays": 7,
    "PreferReleasedLowestFirst": true
  },
  "Project": {
    "Name": "MyProject",
    "CreatedUtc": "2025-11-03T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "Systems": {
    "GENERAL": { "Prefix": "V", "Pad": 4, "Start": 2001, "End": 9999, "Next": 2001 }
  },
  "Blocks": {
    "Valve": {
      "Types": {
        "Ball": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BALL-VALVE.dwg",
        "Butterfly": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BFLY-VALVE.dwg"
        // Optional future:
        // "Check": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_CHECK-VALVE.dwg"
      }
    }
  },
  "Tags": [],
  "Released": [],
  "History": []
}
```

### Notes
- `Systems` drives **number allocation** (Prefix/Pad/Start/End/Next). You can add more later (e.g., `UTILITY`, `PROCESS`), each with its own range.
- `Blocks.Valve.Types` must include at least **Ball** and **Butterfly** (per our spec). Add **Check** when you’re ready.
- `Tags`, `Released`, `History` start empty; tools append here.
- All tools resolve this file via the DWG NOD key: **`CBI/ProjectStore/Path`**.

---

## Minimal rollout plan (no drawing diagnostics yet)

1) **Pick the path & save the JSON**
- Create folder: `C:\CBICAD\ProjectStore\MyProject\`
- Save the JSON above as `ProjectStore.json`.

2) **Wire a drawing to the store (once per DWG)**
- Run: **`CBI_SET_STORE_PATH`**  
  Prompts for a file path → writes NOD key **`CBI/ProjectStore/Path`** with your JSON path.

3) **Validate the store file (schema gate)**
- Run: **`CBI_STORE_VALIDATE`**  
  Confirms `Version:"1.0"` and required keys (`Systems`, `Blocks.Valve.Types`).  
  If it prints ✅, you’re green to proceed.

4) **Use tools against the store**
- **Valve Insert Split-Button** → reads `Blocks.Valve.Types` and inserts the mapped block.
- **CBI_AddTag / Tag Manager** → allocates IDs from `Systems.GENERAL.Next` and updates `Tags[]` and `Next`.
- **Properties Palette / Label tools** → no schema changes needed; they just benefit from the unified store.

---

## Guardrails the tools will enforce
- `Version` must be `"1.0"`.
- Every `System` must have `Prefix`, `Pad`, `Start`, `End`, `Next` with `Start ≤ Next ≤ End`.
- Block maps must point at real DWG files (your autoloader can fetch if not resident).

---

## Common variants you might want later (keep v1.0 intact)

**Multiple systems**
```json
"Systems": {
  "GENERAL":  { "Prefix": "V", "Pad": 4, "Start": 2001, "End": 4999, "Next": 2001 },
  "UTILITY":  { "Prefix": "U", "Pad": 4, "Start": 1001, "End": 1999, "Next": 1001 }
}
```

**Add Check valve mapping**
```json
"Blocks": {
  "Valve": {
    "Types": {
      "Ball": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BALL-VALVE.dwg",
      "Butterfly": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_BFLY-VALVE.dwg",
      "Check": "C:\\CBICAD\\AutoCAD\\Blocks\\Instrumentation\\INST_CHECK-VALVE.dwg"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Tiny helper commands (what you’ll type)

- **`CBI_SET_STORE_PATH`**  
  Writes DWG NOD key **`CBI/ProjectStore/Path`** to the path you choose.

- **`CBI_STORE_VALIDATE`**  
  Opens that JSON, asserts the v1.0 contract, and echoes any missing keys/ranges.

*(Your existing tools—Valve Insert, Tagging, Properties Palette—already expect this path & schema.)*

---

## Quick sanity checklist (once)
- File exists at the chosen path.
- The DWG has the NOD link set (`CBI_SET_STORE_PATH`).
- `CBI_STORE_VALIDATE` says ✅.
- Try **Valve Insert → Ball** once (block loads), then **CBI_AddTag** once (ID increments `Systems.GENERAL.Next`).

If you want, I can also output an **Obsidian-ready** `ProjectStore_v1.0.md` explainer (schema + examples) and a **CHANGELOG** starter for your vault.
