### 📄 **CBI_Class.snippet**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<CodeSnippets xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/2005/CodeSnippet">
  <CodeSnippet Format="1.0.0">
    <Header>
      <Title>CBI Class File Template</Title>
      <Shortcut>cbiclass</Shortcut>
      <Description>Creates a new CBI AutoCAD class file with header and skeleton.</Description>
      <Author>Brian Ranstead</Author>
    </Header>
    <Snippet>
      <Declarations>
        <Literal>
          <ID>FileName</ID>
          <ToolTip>File name (without extension)</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyClass</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Namespace</ID>
          <ToolTip>Namespace of this file</ToolTip>
          <Default>AutoCAD2020.Services</Default>
```

### 📄 **CBI_Service.snippet**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<CodeSnippets xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/2005/CodeSnippet">
  <CodeSnippet Format="1.0.0">
    <Header>
      <Title>CBI Service Template</Title>
      <Shortcut>cbiservice</Shortcut>
      <Description>Creates a standardized CBI AutoCAD service class with header and skeleton methods.</Description>
      <Author>Brian Ranstead</Author>
    </Header>

    <Snippet>
      <Declarations>
        <Literal>
          <ID>FileName</ID>
          <ToolTip>File name (without extension)</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyService</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Namespace</ID>
          <ToolTip>Namespace for this file</ToolTip>
          <Default>AutoCAD2020.Services</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Purpose</ID>
          <ToolTip>Short summary of purpose</ToolTip>
          <Default>Provides specialized AutoCAD service logic.</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Version</ID>
          <ToolTip>Version number</ToolTip>
          <Default>v1.0</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Updated</ID>
          <ToolTip>Last updated date</ToolTip>
          <Default>2025-11-04</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Change</ID>
          <ToolTip>Reason for change</ToolTip>
          <Default>Initial creation</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>ClassName</ID>
          <ToolTip>Service class name</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyService</Default>
        </Literal>
      </Declarations>

      <Code Language="csharp"><![CDATA[
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: $FileName$.cs
// Module: $Namespace$
// Author: Brian Ranstead
// Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
// Purpose: $Purpose$
// Updated: $Updated$ — $Change$
// Version: $Version$
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using System;

namespace $Namespace$
{
    /// <summary>
    /// $Purpose$
    /// </summary>
    public static class $ClassName$
    {
        private static bool _initialized = false;

        /// <summary>
        /// Initializes any shared state or event hooks.
        /// Should be called once at application load.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Init()
        {
            if (_initialized) return;
            _initialized = true;

            // Example: hook document events, load resources, etc.
            Application.DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
            {
                var ed = e.Document?.Editor;
                ed?.WriteMessage("\\n[$ClassName$ initialized]");
            };
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Example method for external command dispatchers.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Execute()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            try
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\\nExecuting $ClassName$ service...");
                // TODO: add implementation logic here
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\\n[$ClassName$] Error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
    }
}
]]></Code>
    </Snippet>
  </CodeSnippet>
</CodeSnippets>
```

### 🧭 Installation

   ```
   C:\Users\<you>\Documents\Visual Studio <version>\Code Snippets\Visual C#\My Code Snippets\CBI_Service.snippet
   ```

2. Restart Visual Studio or **import via Tools → Code Snippets Manager → Visual C# → Import**.

   ```csharp
   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   // File: TagManagerService.cs
   // Module: AutoCAD2020.Services
   // Author: Brian Ranstead
   // Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
   // Purpose: Provides centralized palette logic for Tag Manager.
   // Updated: 2025-11-04 — Converted to static service pattern

   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

   using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
   using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
   using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
   using System;

   namespace AutoCAD2020.Services
   {
       public static class TagManagerService
       {
           private static bool _initialized = false;

           public static void Init()
           {
               if (_initialized) return;
               _initialized = true;
               Application.DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
               {
                   e.Document?.Editor.WriteMessage("\n[TagManagerService initialized]");
               };
           }

           public static void Execute()
           {
               var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
               var ed = doc.Editor;
               ed.WriteMessage("\nExecuting TagManagerService...");
           }
       }
   }
   ```

Awesome — here’s your **Palette Tool Template** snippet. It scaffolds a production-ready palette **host** with header, docking, size persistence, and easy `Show/Hide/Toggle`. You just plug in your own `UserControl`.

---

### 📄 `CBI_Palette.snippet`  (shortcut: `cbipalette`)
*Drops in a static host class you can call from your Commands switchboard.*

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<CodeSnippets xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/2005/CodeSnippet">
  <CodeSnippet Format="1.0.0">
    <Header>
      <Title>CBI Palette Host Template</Title>
      <Shortcut>cbipalette</Shortcut>
      <Description>Creates a standardized AutoCAD PaletteSet host with size/dock persistence and Show/Hide/Toggle methods.</Description>
      <Author>Brian Ranstead</Author>
    </Header>

    <Snippet>
      <Declarations>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Namespace</ID>
          <ToolTip>Namespace for this file</ToolTip>
          <Default>AutoCAD2020.Palettes</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>HostClass</ID>
          <ToolTip>Palette host class name</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyPaletteHost</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>ControlType</ID>
          <ToolTip>UserControl type to host (must exist)</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyPaletteControl</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>PaletteTitle</ID>
          <ToolTip>Palette title shown in AutoCAD</ToolTip>
          <Default>CBI – My Palette</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>PaletteGuid</ID>
          <ToolTip>Unique GUID for this palette</ToolTip>
          <Default>{6E3B0C3C-3F0F-4E1A-8B3E-5A7F2E2A9B77}</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Purpose</ID>
          <ToolTip>Short purpose text</ToolTip>
          <Default>Dockable UI host for $ControlType$.</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Updated</ID>
          <ToolTip>Last updated</ToolTip>
          <Default>2025-11-04</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Version</ID>
          <ToolTip>Version</ToolTip>
          <Default>v1.0</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>RegRoot</ID>
          <ToolTip>Registry root for palette layout persistence</ToolTip>
          <Default>Software\\CBI\\Palettes\\$HostClass$</Default>
        </Literal>
      </Declarations>

      <Code Language="csharp"><![CDATA[
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: $HostClass$.cs
// Module: $Namespace$
// Author: Brian Ranstead
// Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
// Purpose: $Purpose$
// Updated: $Updated$
// Version: $Version$
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Microsoft.Win32;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace $Namespace$
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Static host for a dockable PaletteSet that contains a single UserControl ($ControlType$).
    /// Provides Show/Hide/Toggle helpers and persists size/position between sessions.
    /// </summary>
    public static class $HostClass$
    {
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static $ControlType$ _ui;
        private static bool _wiredDocEvents;

        // Registry keys for persistence
        private const string RegKey = "$RegRoot$";
        private const string K_Left = "Left";
        private const string K_Top = "Top";
        private const string K_Width = "Width";
        private const string K_Height = "Height";
        private const string K_Visible = "Visible";
        private const string K_Docked = "Docked";
        private const string K_DockSide = "DockSide";

        /// <summary>Show (or create) the palette and bring it to front.</summary>
        public static void Show()
        {
            Ensure();
            if (_ps == null) return;

            if (!_ps.Visible) _ps.Visible = true;
            _ps.KeepFocus = true;
            _ps.Activate(0);
        }

        /// <summary>Hide the palette (keeps instance alive).</summary>
        public static void Hide()
        {
            if (_ps == null) return;
            _ps.Visible = false;
            SaveLayout();
        }

        /// <summary>Toggle palette visibility.</summary>
        public static void Toggle()
        {
            Ensure();
            if (_ps == null) return;
            _ps.Visible = !_ps.Visible;
            if (_ps.Visible) _ps.Activate(0);
            SaveLayout();
        }

        /// <summary>Returns true if the palette is currently visible.</summary>
        public static bool IsVisible => _ps != null && _ps.Visible;

        /// <summary>Ensures the PaletteSet and control exist and are wired.</summary>
        private static void Ensure()
        {
            if (_ps != null && _ui != null) return;

            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ps = new PaletteSet("$PaletteTitle$", new Guid("$PaletteGuid$"))
                {
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton
                          | PaletteSetStyles.ShowPropertiesMenu
                          | PaletteSetStyles.Snappable,
                    MinimumSize = new Size(260, 180),
                    Size = new Size(360, 520)
                };

                _ps.VisibleChanged += (s, e) => SaveLayout();
                _ps.SizeChanged += (s, e) => SaveLayout();
                _ps.LocationChanged += (s, e) => SaveLayout();
            }

            if (_ui == null)
            {
                _ui = new $ControlType$() { Dock = DockStyle.Fill };
                _ps.AddVisual("Main", _ui);
            }

            RestoreLayout();
            WireDocEventsOnce();
        }

        /// <summary>Wire minimal doc events once (refresh or light UX hooks).</summary>
        private static void WireDocEventsOnce()
        {
            if (_wiredDocEvents) return;
            _wiredDocEvents = true;

            Application.DocumentManager.DocumentActivated += (s, e) =>
            {
                // Optional: light ping so you know the palette is alive
                // e.Document?.Editor?.WriteMessage("\\n[$HostClass$ ready]");
            };
        }

        // ----------------------------- Persistence -----------------------------

        private static void SaveLayout()
        {
            try
            {
                using (var key = Registry.CurrentUser.CreateSubKey(RegKey))
                {
                    if (key == null || _ps == null) return;

                    key.SetValue(K_Visible, _ps.Visible ? 1 : 0, RegistryValueKind.DWord);

                    // Dock state
                    key.SetValue(K_Docked, _ps.DockEnabled ? 1 : 0, RegistryValueKind.DWord);
                    key.SetValue(K_DockSide, (int)_ps.Dock, RegistryValueKind.DWord);

                    // Floating geometry
                    var r = _ps.Rectangle;
                    key.SetValue(K_Left, r.Left, RegistryValueKind.DWord);
                    key.SetValue(K_Top, r.Top, RegistryValueKind.DWord);
                    key.SetValue(K_Width, r.Width, RegistryValueKind.DWord);
                    key.SetValue(K_Height, r.Height, RegistryValueKind.DWord);
                }
            }
            catch
            {
                // swallow – persistence should never crash prod
            }
        }

        private static void RestoreLayout()
        {
            try
            {
                using (var key = Registry.CurrentUser.OpenSubKey(RegKey))
                {
                    if (key == null || _ps == null) return;

                    int vis = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Visible, 1));
                    int docked = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Docked, 0));
                    int dockSide = (int)(key.GetValue(K_DockSide, (int)DockSides.Left));

                    int left = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Left, 100));
                    int top = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Top, 100));
                    int width = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Width, 360));
                    int height = (int)(key.GetValue(K_Height, 520));

                    // Apply geometry first (floating), then dock if requested
                    _ps.SetLocation(new Rectangle(left, top, width, height));

                    if (docked == 1)
                    {
                        // Try to dock to last side
                        _ps.DockEnabled = true;
                        _ps.Dock = (DockSides)dockSide;
                    }

                    _ps.Visible = (vis == 1);
                }
            }
            catch
            {
                // fallback defaults already set in Ensure()
            }
        }
    }
}
]]></Code>
    </Snippet>
  </CodeSnippet>
</CodeSnippets>
```

---

### How to use it (quick)

1. Save the snippet to:
   ```
   C:\Users\<you>\Documents\Visual Studio <ver>\Code Snippets\Visual C#\My Code Snippets\CBI_Palette.snippet
   ```
2. In your project, **create your `UserControl`** (e.g., `MyPaletteControl` in `AutoCAD2020.Palettes.UI`).
3. Add a new class file, type `cbipalette` → **Tab, Tab**.
4. Fill the fields:
   - `Namespace` (e.g., `AutoCAD2020.Palettes`)
   - `HostClass` (e.g., `TagManagerPaletteHost`)
   - `ControlType` (your control type, e.g., `TagManagerControl`)
   - `PaletteTitle` (e.g., `CBI – Tag Manager`)
   - `PaletteGuid` (leave or replace with a new GUID)
5. Wire your **Commands.cs** one-liner to show it:
   ```csharp
   [CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager")]
   public static void CBI_TagManager() => AutoCAD2020.Palettes.TagManagerPaletteHost.Show();
   ```

It automatically includes a `ToolStrip`, a `DataGridView`, a clean layout, and an event hook for future logic.

---

### 📄 `CBI_PaletteControl.snippet` (shortcut: `cbipalette_control`)
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<CodeSnippets xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/2005/CodeSnippet">
  <CodeSnippet Format="1.0.0">
    <Header>
      <Title>CBI Palette UserControl Template</Title>
      <Shortcut>cbipalette_control</Shortcut>
      <Description>Creates a standardized AutoCAD palette UserControl with ToolStrip and DataGridView scaffold.</Description>
      <Author>Brian Ranstead</Author>
    </Header>

    <Snippet>
      <Declarations>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Namespace</ID>
          <ToolTip>Namespace for this control</ToolTip>
          <Default>AutoCAD2020.Palettes.UI</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>ControlName</ID>
          <ToolTip>Name of the control class</ToolTip>
          <Default>MyPaletteControl</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Purpose</ID>
          <ToolTip>Purpose or short summary</ToolTip>
          <Default>Provides the primary UI for an AutoCAD palette.</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Version</ID>
          <ToolTip>Version number</ToolTip>
          <Default>v1.0</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Updated</ID>
          <ToolTip>Last updated date</ToolTip>
          <Default>2025-11-04</Default>
        </Literal>
      </Declarations>

      <Code Language="csharp"><![CDATA[
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: $ControlName$.cs
// Module: $Namespace$
// Author: Brian Ranstead
// Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
// Purpose: $Purpose$
// Updated: $Updated$
// Version: $Version$
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace $Namespace$
{
    /// <summary>
    /// $Purpose$
    /// </summary>
    public partial class $ControlName$ : UserControl
    {
        private readonly ToolStrip _toolStrip;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRefresh;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnAction;
        private readonly DataGridView _grid;

        public $ControlName$()
        {
            // --- Basic sizing ---
            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            BackColor = SystemColors.ControlLightLight;

            // --- ToolStrip setup ---
            _toolStrip = new ToolStrip
            {
                GripStyle = ToolStripGripStyle.Hidden,
                Dock = DockStyle.Top,
                Stretch = true
            };

            _btnRefresh = new ToolStripButton("Refresh", null, (s, e) => OnRefreshClicked())
            {
                ToolTipText = "Reload data or resync from drawing"
            };

            _btnAction = new ToolStripButton("Action", null, (s, e) => OnActionClicked())
            {
                ToolTipText = "Perform selected action"
            };

            _toolStrip.Items.AddRange(new ToolStripItem[]
            {
                _btnRefresh,
                new ToolStripSeparator(),
                _btnAction
            });

            // --- Grid setup ---
            _grid = new DataGridView
            {
                Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill,
                AllowUserToAddRows = false,
                AllowUserToDeleteRows = false,
                ReadOnly = true,
                MultiSelect = false,
                SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
                BackgroundColor = SystemColors.Window,
                BorderStyle = BorderStyle.Fixed3D
            };

            _grid.DataSourceChanged += (s, e) => _grid.AutoResizeColumns();
            _grid.CellDoubleClick += (s, e) => OnRowDoubleClicked(e.RowIndex);

            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(_toolStrip);
        }

        // -------------------- Public API --------------------

        /// <summary>
        /// Load or refresh content in the grid (override or replace).
        /// </summary>
        public virtual void RefreshData()
        {
            // TODO: Replace with real data-binding logic
            var sample = new[]
            {
                new { Id = 1, Name = "Sample Row 1" },
                new { Id = 2, Name = "Sample Row 2" }
            };
            _grid.DataSource = sample;
        }

        // -------------------- Internal helpers --------------------

        private void OnRefreshClicked() => RefreshData();

        private void OnActionClicked()
        {
            MessageBox.Show("Action clicked", "$ControlName$", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information);
        }

        private void OnRowDoubleClicked(int rowIndex)
        {
            if (rowIndex < 0) return;
            var row = _grid.Rows[rowIndex].DataBoundItem;
            MessageBox.Show($"Row double-clicked:\n{row}", "$ControlName$");
        }
    }
}
]]></Code>
    </Snippet>
  </CodeSnippet>
</CodeSnippets>
```

---

1. Save as  
   ```
   C:\Users\<you>\Documents\Visual Studio <ver>\Code Snippets\Visual C#\My Code Snippets\CBI_PaletteControl.snippet
   ```

2. Restart Visual Studio or import through **Tools → Code Snippets Manager → Visual C# → Import**.
3. In your Palettes UI folder, create a new class file → type **`cbipalette_control`** → **Tab Tab**.

### 📄 `cbiribbon_splitbutton.snippet`
**Shortcut:** `cbiribbon_splitbutton`  
**Purpose:** Generates a ready-to-wire split button (main + dropdowns) for the CBI ribbon panel.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<CodeSnippets xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/VisualStudio/2005/CodeSnippet">
  <CodeSnippet Format="1.0.0">
    <Header>
      <Title>CBI Ribbon Split Button</Title>
      <Shortcut>cbiribbon_splitbutton</Shortcut>
      <Description>Creates a Ribbon SplitButton with dropdown items linked to CBI commands.</Description>
      <Author>Brian Ranstead</Author>
    </Header>

    <Snippet>
      <Declarations>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Namespace</ID>
          <Default>AutoCAD2020.Ribbon</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>PanelName</ID>
          <Default>Tagging</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>ButtonId</ID>
          <Default>VALVE_INSERT</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>MainLabel</ID>
          <Default>Insert Valve</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>MainCommand</ID>
          <Default>CBI_InsertValve_Ball</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Updated</ID>
          <Default>2025-11-04</Default>
        </Literal>
        <Literal>
          <ID>Version</ID>
          <Default>v1.0</Default>
        </Literal>
      </Declarations>

      <Code Language="csharp"><![CDATA[
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: RibbonSplitButton_$ButtonId$.cs
// Module: $Namespace$
// Author: Brian Ranstead
// Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
// Purpose: Ribbon SplitButton for grouped tool actions (main + dropdown)
// Updated: $Updated$
// Version: $Version$
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using Autodesk.Windows;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;

namespace $Namespace$
{
    public static class RibbonSplitButton_$ButtonId$
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Builds and returns the split button for inclusion in a ribbon panel.
        /// </summary>
        public static RibbonSplitButton Create(IRibbonPanel panel = null)
        {
            // Main button
            var mainBtn = new RibbonButton
            {
                Id = "$ButtonId$_Main",
                Name = "$MainLabel$",
                Text = "$MainLabel$",
                ShowText = true,
                CommandHandler = new RibbonCommandHandler(),
                CommandParameter = "$MainCommand$",
                Orientation = System.Windows.Controls.Orientation.Vertical,
                LargeImage = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_main_32.png"),
                Image = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_main_16.png"),
                ToolTip = "Inserts a valve block"
            };

            // SplitButton
            var split = new RibbonSplitButton
            {
                Id = "$ButtonId$_Split",
                Text = "$MainLabel$",
                ShowText = true,
                Orientation = System.Windows.Controls.Orientation.Vertical,
                Size = RibbonItemSize.Large,
                IsSplit = true
            };

            split.Items.Add(mainBtn);

            // Dropdown items
            split.Items.Add(new RibbonButton
            {
                Id = "$ButtonId$_Ball",
                Text = "Ball Valve",
                CommandHandler = new RibbonCommandHandler(),
                CommandParameter = "CBI_InsertValve_Ball",
                Image = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_ball_16.png")
            });

            split.Items.Add(new RibbonButton
            {
                Id = "$ButtonId$_Butterfly",
                Text = "Butterfly Valve",
                CommandHandler = new RibbonCommandHandler(),
                CommandParameter = "CBI_InsertValve_Butterfly",
                Image = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_bfly_16.png")
            });

            split.Items.Add(new RibbonButton
            {
                Id = "$ButtonId$_Check",
                Text = "Check Valve",
                CommandHandler = new RibbonCommandHandler(),
                CommandParameter = "CBI_InsertValve_Check",
                Image = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_check_16.png")
            });

            panel?.Source.Items.Add(split);
            return split;
        }
    }
}
]]></Code>
    </Snippet>
  </CodeSnippet>
</CodeSnippets>
```


Each drop-down action triggers one of your CBI commands (Ball, Butterfly, Check).  
It uses your existing `RibbonCommandHandler` so everything stays unified.

### 📄 `RibbonImageHelper.cs`

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// File: RibbonImageHelper.cs
// Module: AutoCAD2020.Ribbon
// Author: Brian Ranstead
// Project: CBI Tools for AutoCAD 2020
// Purpose: Lightweight image loader for ribbon buttons
// Updated: 2025-11-04
// Version: v1.0
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

using System;
using System.Drawing;
using System.IO;
using Autodesk.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Ribbon
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Provides safe loading of small and large ribbon icons from disk or resources.
    /// </summary>
    public static class RibbonImageHelper
    {
        private static readonly string _defaultImagePath =
            Path.Combine(AppDomain.CurrentDomain.BaseDirectory, "Resources");

        /// <summary>
        /// Loads a PNG or ICO file from the Resources folder (or full path).
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="fileName">Filename or full path to the image.</param>
        /// <returns>BitmapImage (WPF) for RibbonButton.Image or LargeImage</returns>
        public static System.Windows.Media.ImageSource Load(string fileName)
        {
            try
            {
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileName))
                    return null;

                string fullPath = fileName;
                if (!File.Exists(fullPath))
                    fullPath = Path.Combine(_defaultImagePath, fileName);

                if (!File.Exists(fullPath))
                    return null;

                using (var bmp = new Bitmap(fullPath))
                {
                    var mem = new System.IO.MemoryStream();
                    bmp.Save(mem, System.Drawing.Imaging.ImageFormat.Png);
                    mem.Position = 0;
                    var img = new System.Windows.Media.Imaging.BitmapImage();
                    img.BeginInit();
                    img.StreamSource = mem;
                    img.CacheOption = System.Windows.Media.Imaging.BitmapCacheOption.OnLoad;
                    img.EndInit();
                    img.Freeze();
                    return img;
                }
            }
            catch
            {
                return null;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Convenience: loads from a subfolder (e.g. "Icons\\valve_ball_16.png").
        /// </summary>
        public static System.Windows.Media.ImageSource LoadFromSub(string folder, string file)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(folder) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(file))
                return null;
            return Load(Path.Combine(_defaultImagePath, folder, file));
        }
    }
}
```

---

✅ **Purpose:**  
Lets your ribbon code use:
```csharp
LargeImage = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_main_32.png");
Image      = RibbonImageHelper.Load("valve_main_16.png");
```

If `Resources/valve_main_32.png` exists beside your DLL, it will load instantly.  
If not, no crash — it just skips the icon.

## Response:
11/4/2025, 11:33:17 AM

Excellent 👏 — you’re doing great with this pack setup.  
Those **two squigglies** are totally normal and easily fixable — here’s the breakdown:

---

### ⚠️ **Error 1 – CS1061**
> `'RibbonButton' does not contain a definition for 'Parent'`

✅ **Why:**  
In Autodesk’s Ribbon API (`Autodesk.Windows.RibbonButton`), the button does not expose a `.Parent` property.  
That property exists in WPF controls, not in the Autodesk Ribbon UI layer.

✅ **Fix:**  
Remove or comment the “dropdown close” line temporarily:
```csharp
// --- 1️⃣ Close dropdown immediately (prevents dragging ghost) ---
// (btn.Parent as RibbonSplitButton)?.TryCloseDropDown();  // ❌ Remove or comment
```

We’ll later replace that with a proper “close split dropdown” call through the RibbonControl’s hierarchy.

---

### ⚠️ **Error 2 – CS8370**
> “Feature 'not pattern' is not available in C# 7.3…”

✅ **Why:**  
Your project is targeting **C# 7.3**, and pattern matching `if (parameter is not RibbonButton btn)` is a **C# 9.0 feature**.

✅ **Fix (C# 7.3 compatible):**
Replace this:
```csharp
if (parameter is not RibbonButton btn) return;
```
with this older-style equivalent:
```csharp
var btn = parameter as RibbonButton;
if (btn == null) return;
```

---

### ✅ Final Fix (C# 7.3 Safe)
Here’s the **corrected section**:
```csharp
public void Execute(object parameter)
{
    // Autodesk ribbon passes the RibbonButton as the parameter
    var btn = parameter as RibbonButton;
    if (btn == null) return;

    // --- 1️⃣ Close dropdown immediately (prevents dragging ghost) ---
    // (btn.Parent as RibbonSplitButton)?.TryCloseDropDown();

    // --- 2️⃣ Extract action name ---
    var action = (btn.CommandParameter ?? string.Empty).ToString().Trim();
    if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(action)) return;

    var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
    if (doc == null) return;
```

---

Awesome—jumping back into Tag Manager now. Here’s a clean, C# 7.3-safe glue layer that ties your **ProjectStoreV1 / TagStoreV1** to AutoCAD selection, XData links, and simple commands you can route via your central `Commands.cs`.

---

# TagManager glue (allocate / release / scan)

```csharp
// v0.9.0 (C# 7.3) — Tag Manager glue for ProjectStoreV1
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/TagManagerService.cs

using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

using AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations; // XDataLinkUtil
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;       // TagStoreV1
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;    // ProjectStoreV1, TagStatusV1

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging
{
    public static class TagManagerService
    {
        // ---- Public entry points (call these from Commands.cs) ----------------

        // Allocate a new tag ID to a selected valve block (and stamp XData)
        public static void AddTagToSelectedValve()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            // Pick a block
            var br = PromptForBlockReference(ed, "Select valve block to tag");
            if (br == null) return;

            // Choose a system (keywords from JSON Systems)
            var systemKey = PromptForSystemKey(ed, store);
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(systemKey)) return;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var brObj = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(br.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                var handleHex = brObj.Handle.ToString();

                // Allocate — prefer released pool if configured
                var rec = TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(
                    store,
                    systemKey,
                    drawing: doc.Name ?? "",
                    entityHandle: handleHex,
                    by: Environment.UserName ?? "unknown",
                    notes: "TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve");

                // XDATA: add ID, make it primary, also mirror visible attribute
                XDataLinkUtil.AddValveTagId(tr, brObj, rec.Id);
                XDataLinkUtil.MakePrimaryValveTagId(tr, brObj, rec.Id);
                XDataLinkUtil.SetInstrTag(tr, brObj, rec.Id);

                tr.Commit();
            }

            // Persist JSON
            ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            ed.WriteMessage($"\nTag allocated and applied.");
        }

        // Mark current ID on a selected valve as orphan, then try to release
        public static void ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            var br = PromptForBlockReference(ed, "Select valve block to release its primary tag");
            if (br == null) return;

            string idToRelease = null;
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var brObj = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(br.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                var ids = XDataLinkUtil.GetValveTagIds(tr, brObj);
                if (ids != null && ids.Count > 0)
                    idToRelease = ids[0];

                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(idToRelease))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\nNo valve tag IDs found on selection.");
                    return;
                }

                // Mark orphan (so grace logic is respected)
                TagStoreV1.MarkOrphan(store, idToRelease, Environment.UserName, "User requested release");

                // Attempt immediate release (will respect grace days > 0)
                var ok = TagStoreV1.Release(store, idToRelease, "User release", Environment.UserName);
                if (!ok)
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nRelease deferred (within grace period of {store.Config?.GraceDays ?? 0} days).");

                // Remove from XDATA list and visible attr
                XDataLinkUtil.RemoveValveTagId(tr, brObj, idToRelease);
                // If it was primary, the MakePrimary step is skipped—list is already rewritten without it
                XDataLinkUtil.SetInstrTag(tr, brObj, ids.Count > 1 ? ids[1] : string.Empty);

                tr.Commit();
            }

            ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            ed.WriteMessage("\nTag unlinked from block and store updated.");
        }

        // Sweep-orphan → release any eligible IDs across the whole store
        public static void SweepAutoReleaseOrphans()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            var count = StoreGuards.AutoReleaseAllEligible(store, "AutoSweep", Environment.UserName ?? "unknown");
            if (count > 0)
            {
                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nAuto-released {count} orphaned tag(s).");
            }
            else
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo eligible orphaned tags to release.");
            }
        }

        // ---- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------

        private static BlockReferencePromptResult PromptForBlockReference(Editor ed, string msg)
        {
            var po = new PromptEntityOptions($"\n{msg}:");
            po.SetRejectMessage("\n…must be a block reference.");
            po.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), exactMatch: false);
            var pr = ed.GetEntity(po);
            if (pr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            return new BlockReferencePromptResult(pr.ObjectId);
        }

        private static string PromptForSystemKey(Editor ed, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            var keys = (store.Systems ?? new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>()).Keys.ToList();
            if (keys.Count == 0)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo Systems defined in Project Store.");
                return null;
            }

            // If there’s only one, use it silently
            if (keys.Count == 1) return keys[0];

            var pko = new PromptKeywordOptions("\nSelect System");
            pko.AllowNone = false;
            foreach (var k in keys) pko.Keywords.Add(k);
            var r = ed.GetKeywords(pko);
            return (r.Status == PromptStatus.OK) ? r.StringResult : null;
        }

        // Load store by reading the NOD pointer "CBI/ProjectStore/Path"; prompt if missing.
        private static bool TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(
            Document doc,
            Editor ed,
            out string storePath,
            out ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            storePath = null;
            store = null;

            // 1) Try NOD
            storePath = TryReadStorePathFromNod(doc.Database);
            // 2) If missing, prompt user once
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
            {
                var ps = new PromptOpenFileOptions("\nSelect ProjectStore.json");
                ps.Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json|All Files (*.*)|*.*";
                var pr = ed.GetFileNameForOpen(ps);
                if (pr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return false;
                storePath = pr.StringResult;

                // (Optional) persist to NOD so next time it just works
                TryWriteStorePathToNod(doc.Database, storePath);
            }

            // Load or initialize file
            if (File.Exists(storePath))
            {
                store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
            }
            else
            {
                store = ProjectStoreService.CreateDefault();
                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            }

            return store != null;
        }

        private static string TryReadStorePathFromNod(Database db)
        {
            const string nodKey = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!nod.Contains(nodKey)) return null;
                var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(nodKey), OpenMode.ForRead);
                var data = xr?.Data?.AsArray();
                if (data != null && data.Length > 0 && data[0].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.Text)
                    return data[0].Value as string;
                tr.Commit();
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static void TryWriteStorePathToNod(Database db, string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) return;
            const string nodKey = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                Xrecord xr;
                if (nod.Contains(nodKey))
                {
                    xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(nodKey), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                }
                else
                {
                    xr = new Xrecord();
                    nod.SetAt(nodKey, xr);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
                }

                xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, path ?? ""));
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        // Small holder so PromptEntity result is easy to pass around
        private sealed class BlockReferencePromptResult
        {
            public ObjectId ObjectId { get; }
            public BlockReferencePromptResult(ObjectId id) { ObjectId = id; }
        }
    }
}
```

---

# Wire up the commands (one-liners per your registry standard)

In `AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs`, add these:

```csharp
// v0.9.0 — Tag Manager command routes (C# 7.3)
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging;

namespace AutoCAD2020
{
    public static partial class Commands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_AddToValve")]
        public static void CBI_Tag_AddToValve() => TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve")]
        public static void CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve() => TagManagerService.ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans")]
        public static void CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans() => TagManagerService.SweepAutoReleaseOrphans();
    }
}
```

---

## Notes & how this fits your store

- Uses your v1 contract (from our earlier updates): `Systems`, `Tags`, `Released`, `History`, grace-days, and released-pool reuse.
- Reads/writes the **NOD pointer** at `CBI/ProjectStore/Path` (as you asked in your canonical contract), and will prompt the first time if it’s missing.
- **XData integration**: adds/removes the tag id list and mirrors the visible `InstrTag` field so the properties palette can show the current ID.
- **Grace handling**: `ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve` first marks orphan, then attempts `Release(...)`; if grace isn’t met, it quietly defers. The periodic sweep command will finish the job later.

If you paste these two snippets in place, you should be able to:

1) `CBI_Tag_AddToValve` → pick a valve → pick a system → ID applied + JSON saved.  
2) `CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve` → pick a valve → primary ID removed, marked/released per grace, JSON saved.  
3) `CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans` → cleans up any IDs past grace.

## Response:

Excellent question — you’re remembering our **standardized CBI/PSBA service header convention** 👏  

Here’s your reference template (you can copy/paste and edit the fields for any new service class like `TagManagerService.cs`):

---

### ✅ **Standard CBI Header Block (for service or module classes)**

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// Project   : CBI Tools – AutoCAD 2020
// Module    : TagManagerService.cs
// Purpose   : Core service for valve tag allocation, release, and orphan cleanup.
// Author    : Brian Ranstead / ChatGPT CoDev
// Created   : 2025-11-04
// Version   : v0.9.0 (C# 7.3 compatible)
//
// Summary   :
//  - Integrates TagStoreV1 and ProjectStoreV1 logic into AutoCAD.
//  - Handles allocation/release of tag IDs directly from valve blocks.
//  - Reads/writes ProjectStore.json path from NOD (CBI/ProjectStore/Path).
//  - Adds/Removes XData and InstrTag attributes on valve entities.
//  - Provides 3 routed commands: Add, Release, and Sweep.
//
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations.XDataLinkUtil
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.TagStoreV1
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1.ProjectStoreV1
//
// Notes:
//  - This class follows the CBI standard “service-layer” design (no UI).
//  - All entry points are static and callable via Commands.cs.
//
// Change Log:
//  v0.9.0  Initial integration for Tag Manager – added AddTag, ReleaseTag, Sweep commands.
// ============================================================================
```

---

### 🔧 How to use it
When you create a new class:
1. Place this header at the **very top of the file** (before `using` statements).  
2. Update:
   - `Module` = the filename
   - `Purpose` = what the class does
   - `Created` = current date
   - `Version` = next semantic increment (v0.9.1, v1.0.0, etc.)
3. Add a one-line **summary** in the `Change Log` whenever you modify the class.

---

Would you like me to format and show you the **final complete file** (`TagManagerService.cs`) with this header already inserted and ready to drop into your `AutoCAD2020\Tagging` folder?

Awesome — let’s jump straight into building the **Tag Manager Palette UI** wired to your **ProjectStore v1 + TagStoreV1**. Below are drop-in, C# 7.3–compatible files (no nullable refs, no designer needed) that create a dockable palette with a grid + action bar and hook into your existing store.

# 1) `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerCommands.cs`

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Project: CBI Tools
// Module : TagManagerCommands.cs
// Target : AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
// Desc   : Command shims for Tag Manager palette (switchboard pattern)
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Runtime;

namespace AutoCAD2020.TagManager
{
    public static class TagManagerCommands
    {
        [CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager", CommandFlags.Modal)]
        public static void OpenTagManager() => TagManagerPalette.Show();

        [CommandMethod("CBI_TagManager_Refresh", CommandFlags.Modal)]
        public static void RefreshTagManager() => TagManagerPalette.RefreshIfOpen();
    }
}
```

---

# 2) `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerPalette.cs`

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Project: CBI Tools
// Module : TagManagerPalette.cs
// Target : AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
// Desc   : Hosts the PaletteSet and our TagManagerControl
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.TagManager
{
    public static class TagManagerPalette
    {
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _ui;

        public static void Show()
        {
            if (_ps == null)
            {
                _ps = new PaletteSet("CBI Tag Manager")
                {
                    Style = PaletteSetStyles.NameEditable | PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                            PaletteSetStyles.Snappable | PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton
                };
                _ps.MinimumSize = new System.Drawing.Size(500, 300);

                _ui = new TagManagerControl();
                _ps.Add("Manager", _ui);
            }

            _ps.Visible = true;
            _ui?.RefreshData();
        }

        public static void RefreshIfOpen()
        {
            if (_ps != null && _ps.Visible)
                _ui?.RefreshData();
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 3) `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerControl.cs` (WinForms, programmatic UI)

```csharp
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Project: CBI Tools
// Module : TagManagerControl.cs
// Target : AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
// Desc   : Dockable UI (grid + toolbar) for viewing/acting on tag records
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Linq;
using System.Windows.Forms;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore; // ProjectStoreService, TagStoreV1

namespace AutoCAD2020.TagManager
{
    public class TagManagerControl : UserControl
    {
        private readonly ToolStrip _bar = new ToolStrip();
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRefresh = new ToolStripButton("Refresh");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnZoom = new ToolStripButton("Zoom");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnReassign = new ToolStripButton("Reassign…");
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRelease = new ToolStripButton("Release");
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep = new ToolStripSeparator();
        private readonly TextBox _filter = new TextBox(){ Dock = DockStyle.Top, PlaceholderText = "filter…" };
        private readonly DataGridView _grid = new DataGridView(){ Dock = DockStyle.Fill, ReadOnly = true, AutoGenerateColumns = false };
        private readonly BindingList<TagRow> _rows = new BindingList<TagRow>();

        public TagManagerControl()
        {
            // toolbar
            _btnRefresh.Click += (s, e) => RefreshData();
            _btnZoom.Click += (s, e) => ZoomSelected();
            _btnReassign.Click += (s, e) => ReassignSelected();
            _btnRelease.Click += (s, e) => ReleaseSelected();

            _bar.GripStyle = ToolStripGripStyle.Hidden;
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRefresh);
            _bar.Items.Add(_sep);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnZoom);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnReassign);
            _bar.Items.Add(_btnRelease);

            // grid columns
            _grid.AllowUserToAddRows = false;
            _grid.AllowUserToDeleteRows = false;
            _grid.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect;
            _grid.MultiSelect = true;
            _grid.RowHeadersVisible = false;
            _grid.DataSource = _rows;

            AddCol("Id", "Id", 110);
            AddCol("System", "System", 90);
            AddCol("Status", "Status", 85);
            AddCol("Drawing", "Drawing", 200);
            AddCol("EntityHandle", "EntityHandle", 110);
            AddCol("OrphanedAt", "OrphanedAt", 120);
            AddCol("Notes", "Notes", 200);

            _filter.TextChanged += (s, e) => ApplyFilter();

            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(_filter);
            Controls.Add(_bar);
            _bar.Dock = DockStyle.Top;

            // initial
            this.Load += (s, e) => RefreshData();
        }

        private void AddCol(string header, string prop, int width)
        {
            var c = new DataGridViewTextBoxColumn
            {
                DataPropertyName = prop,
                HeaderText = header,
                Width = width,
                AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.None
            };
            _grid.Columns.Add(c);
        }

        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        // Public entry
        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        public void RefreshData()
        {
            try
            {
                var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null) return;

                var db = doc.Database;
                string storePath;
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    storePath = TryGetProjectStorePathFromNod(db, tr);
                    tr.Commit();
                }

                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
                {
                    Echo("ProjectStore path missing (NOD key CBI/ProjectStore/Path).");
                    _rows.Clear();
                    return;
                }

                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
                BindRows(store);
                Echo($"Loaded {store?.Tags?.Count ?? 0} tag(s) from store.");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Echo("Refresh failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        private void BindRows(ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            _rows.Clear();
            if (store == null || store.Tags == null) return;

            foreach (var t in store.Tags.OrderBy(x => x.Id, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            {
                _rows.Add(new TagRow
                {
                    Id = t.Id,
                    System = t.System,
                    Status = t.Status.ToString(),
                    Drawing = t.Drawing ?? "",
                    EntityHandle = t.EntityHandle ?? "",
                    OrphanedAt = t.OrphanedAtUtc.HasValue ? t.OrphanedAtUtc.Value.ToString("u") : "",
                    Notes = t.Notes ?? ""
                });
            }
            ApplyFilter();
        }

        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        // Actions
        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        private TagRow GetSelectedRow()
        {
            if (_grid.SelectedRows.Count == 0) return null;
            return _grid.SelectedRows[0].DataBoundItem as TagRow;
        }

        private void ZoomSelected()
        {
            var row = GetSelectedRow();
            if (row == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(row.EntityHandle)) return;

            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            using (doc.LockDocument())
            using (var tr = doc.Database.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                if (TryGetIdFromHandle(doc.Database, row.EntityHandle, out var id))
                {
                    var ed = doc.Editor;
                    try
                    {
                        ed.SetImpliedSelection(new ObjectId[] { id });
                        ed.UpdateScreen();
                        ZoomExtentsAround(tr, id);
                    }
                    catch { /* best effort */ }
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        private void ReassignSelected()
        {
            var row = GetSelectedRow();
            if (row == null) return;

            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            // ask user to pick a new entity, keep drawing = current
            var ed = doc.Editor;
            var p = ed.GetEntity("\nPick new entity for tag " + row.Id + ": ");
            if (p.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            var newHandle = p.ObjectId.Handle.ToString();
            var drawing = System.IO.Path.GetFileName(doc.Name);

            try
            {
                string storePath;
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = doc.Database.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    storePath = TryGetProjectStorePathFromNod(doc.Database, tr);
                    tr.Commit();
                }
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) { Echo("Store path missing."); return; }

                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
                var rec = TagStoreV1.Reassign(store, row.Id, newHandle, drawing, Environment.UserName ?? "user");
                if (rec != null)
                {
                    ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                    RefreshData();
                    Echo($"Reassigned {row.Id} → {drawing}:{newHandle}");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Echo("Reassign failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        private void ReleaseSelected()
        {
            var row = GetSelectedRow();
            if (row == null) return;

            try
            {
                var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
                if (doc == null) return;

                string storePath;
                using (doc.LockDocument())
                using (var tr = doc.Database.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    storePath = TryGetProjectStorePathFromNod(doc.Database, tr);
                    tr.Commit();
                }
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath)) { Echo("Store path missing."); return; }

                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
                var ok = TagStoreV1.Release(store, row.Id, "UI release", Environment.UserName ?? "user");
                if (!ok)
                {
                    Echo("Release skipped (not orphan or grace not met).");
                    return;
                }

                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                RefreshData();
                Echo($"Released {row.Id} back to pool.");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Echo("Release failed: " + ex.Message);
            }
        }

        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        // Helpers
        // ------------------------------------------------------------
        private void ApplyFilter()
        {
            var text = (_filter.Text ?? "").Trim();
            _grid.DataSource = string.IsNullOrEmpty(text)
                ? _rows
                : new BindingList<TagRow>(_rows.Where(r =>
                        r.Id.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
                        r.System.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
                        r.Status.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
                        r.Drawing.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
                        r.EntityHandle.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 ||
                        r.Notes.IndexOf(text, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0
                    ).ToList());
        }

        private static string TryGetProjectStorePathFromNod(Database db, Transaction tr)
        {
            // NOD key: "CBI/ProjectStore/Path" → XRecord with a single string (path)
            var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!nod.Contains("CBI/ProjectStore/Path")) return null;
            var xrId = (ObjectId)nod.GetAt("CBI/ProjectStore/Path");
            var xr = tr.GetObject(xrId, OpenMode.ForRead) as Xrecord;
            if (xr == null || xr.Data == null) return null;

            var arr = xr.Data.AsArray();
            if (arr.Length == 0) return null;
            var s = arr[0].Value as string;
            return s;
        }

        private static bool TryGetIdFromHandle(Database db, string handleHex, out ObjectId id)
        {
            id = ObjectId.Null;
            try
            {
                var h = new Handle(Convert.ToInt64(handleHex, 16));
                return db.TryGetObjectId(h, out id);
            }
            catch { return false; }
        }

        private static void ZoomExtentsAround(Transaction tr, ObjectId id)
        {
            var ed = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument.Editor;
            try
            {
                using (var ent = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead) as Entity)
                {
                    if (ent == null) return;
                    var ext = ent.GeometricExtents;
                    var vps = Autodesk.AutoCAD.Internal.Utils.GetAcadWindow();
                    // Simple focus via implied selection; more precise zoom can be added later
                    ed.UpdateScreen();
                }
            }
            catch { /* best effort */ }
        }

        private static void Echo(string msg)
        {
            var ed = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument?.Editor;
            ed?.WriteMessage("\n[TagManager] " + msg);
        }

        // Simple DTO bound to grid
        private sealed class TagRow
        {
            public string Id { get; set; }
            public string System { get; set; }
            public string Status { get; set; }
            public string Drawing { get; set; }
            public string EntityHandle { get; set; }
            public string OrphanedAt { get; set; }
            public string Notes { get; set; }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## How to wire it up quickly

1) **Add files** in your solution exactly at:
- `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerCommands.cs`
- `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerPalette.cs`
- `AutoCAD2020/TagManager/TagManagerControl.cs`

2) **Build**, then in AutoCAD:
- `NETLOAD` your DLL
- Run `CBI_TagManager`

3) The palette:
- **Refresh** loads `ProjectStoreV1` using NOD key `CBI/ProjectStore/Path`
- **Zoom** highlights & focuses on the selected tag’s entity (best-effort)
- **Reassign** prompts you to pick a new entity and updates the record via `TagStoreV1.Reassign(...)`
- **Release** attempts to release an **Orphan** back to `Released` (grace-days aware)

> This is intentionally minimal and safe. Once it’s working, we can add multi-select actions, a status filter dropdown, and a right-click context menu.

---

## Nice follow-ups (when you’re ready)
- “Mark Orphan” button (for an entity you’re un-linking)
- “Allocate Next” test button (quickly hands out the next ID and echoes)
- Status filter (Active / Orphan / Released)
- Export current view to CSV/JSON

Want me to add a **Mark Orphan** + **Allocate Next** button next so you can fully round-trip without leaving the palette?

Let’s clarify your **folder and namespace strategy** before we drop this new Tag Manager in, because it affects how future modules (like Orphan Sweep, Tag Audit, Tag Diagnostics, etc.) will interoperate cleanly.

---

## 🗂️ Folder Decision Guide

### ✅ Option A — **Keep in existing `Tagging` folder** *(Recommended for now)*
Since your earlier Tag Manager logic, TagStore, and insertion tools all live under `AutoCAD2020\Tagging`,  
you should place these three new files there.

**Path structure:**
```
AutoCAD2020/
 └─ Tagging/
     ├─ TagManagerCommands.cs
     ├─ TagManagerPalette.cs
     ├─ TagManagerControl.cs
     ├─ TagManagerService.cs        (existing or planned)
     ├─ ValveTagInsertService.cs    (existing)
     └─ TagValidator.cs             (future)
```

**Namespace convention:**
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging
```

This keeps all *tag-centric logic* together — palette, commands, and services — while letting the backend stay in `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore`.

---

### ⬜ Option B — New folder `TagManager`
Use this **only** if you later spin out Tag Manager as its own full subsystem (like the Layer Utility or Properties Hub).  
Then you could eventually version it (`TagManager.V1`, `TagManager.V2`), but right now it’s overkill.

---

## ✅ Recommendation
➡️ **Stay with Option A (use your existing `Tagging` folder).**

It aligns with your established conventions:
- `AutoCAD2020.Tagging` = UI + service layer for tag operations  
- `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore` = persistence backend  
- `AutoCAD2020.Commands` = entry points only (switchboard pattern)

### `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Commands/TagManagerCommands.cs`
```csharp
// ============================================================================
//  CBI Tools — Tag Manager (Commands)
//  File:    Tagging/Commands/TagManagerCommands.cs
//  Author:  ChatGPT (GPT-5 Thinking) — helper
//  Target:  AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
//  Notes:   Called from the central Commands.cs “switchboard”
//           e.g. [CommandMethod] in Commands.cs => TagManagerCommands.ShowPalette();
// ============================================================================
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.UI;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands
{
    public static class TagManagerCommands
    {
        /// <summary>Show (or focus) the Tag Manager palette.</summary>
        public static void ShowPalette()
        {
            TagManagerPalette.Show();
        }

        /// <summary>Optional helper if you want to programmatically refresh.</summary>
        public static void Refresh()
        {
            TagManagerPalette.RefreshIfOpen();
        }
    }
}
```

### `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/UI/TagManagerPalette.cs`
```csharp
// ============================================================================
//  CBI Tools — Tag Manager (Palette host)
//  File:    Tagging/UI/TagManagerPalette.cs
//  Author:  ChatGPT (GPT-5 Thinking) — helper
//  Target:  AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
//  Notes:   Owns a single PaletteSet instance and a TagManagerControl
// ============================================================================
using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Windows;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.UI
{
    public static class TagManagerPalette
    {
        private static PaletteSet _ps;
        private static TagManagerControl _control;

        /// <summary>Create the palette if needed.</summary>
        private static void EnsureCreated()
        {
            if (_ps != null && _control != null) return;

            _control = new TagManagerControl();

            _ps = new PaletteSet("CBI — Tag Manager")
            {
                Style = PaletteSetStyles.ShowAutoHideButton |
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowCloseButton |
                        PaletteSetStyles.ShowPropertiesMenu,
                MinimumSize = new System.Drawing.Size(420, 320)
            };

            _ps.Add("Manager", _control);
            _ps.Size = new System.Drawing.Size(520, 420);

            // Optional: dock right on first open
            try { _ps.DockEnabled = (DockSides)((int)DockSides.Left | (int)DockSides.Right); }
            catch { /* some environments restrict docking flags; ignore */ }
        }

        public static void Show()
        {
            EnsureCreated();
            if (!_ps.Visible) _ps.Visible = true;
            _ps.Activate(0);
        }

        public static void Hide()
        {
            if (_ps != null) _ps.Visible = false;
        }

        public static void RefreshIfOpen()
        {
            if (_ps != null && _ps.Visible && _control != null)
                _control.SafeRefresh();
        }

        /// <summary>Access to the underlying control if you need to hook events.</summary>
        public static TagManagerControl Control => _control;

        /// <summary>Good practice when closing drawing/app.</summary>
        public static void DisposeIfAny()
        {
            try
            {
                if (_ps != null) _ps.Visible = false;
                if (_control != null) _control.Dispose();
                _ps = null;
                _control = null;
            }
            catch { /* no-op */ }
        }
    }
}
```

---

### `AutoCAD2020/Tagging/UI/TagManagerControl.cs`
```csharp
// ============================================================================
//  CBI Tools — Tag Manager (WinForms control)
//  File:    Tagging/UI/TagManagerControl.cs
//  Author:  ChatGPT (GPT-5 Thinking) — helper
//  Target:  AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
//  Notes:   Lightweight UI shell. Buttons are wired to TODO hooks so this
//           compiles now; we’ll connect to your services next.
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.ComponentModel;
using System.Drawing;
using System.Linq;
using System.Windows.Forms;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.UI
{
    public sealed class TagManagerControl : UserControl
    {
        private readonly ToolStrip _toolbar;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRefresh;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnAllocate;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnRelease;
        private readonly ToolStripButton _btnScanOrphans;
        private readonly ToolStripSeparator _sep1;
        private readonly ToolStripLabel _lblStore;
        private readonly DataGridView _grid;
        private readonly StatusStrip _status;
        private readonly ToolStripStatusLabel _statusText;

        public TagManagerControl()
        {
            // --- layout
            Dock = DockStyle.Fill;
            BackColor = SystemColors.Control;

            _toolbar = new ToolStrip { GripStyle = ToolStripGripStyle.Hidden, Dock = DockStyle.Top };
            _btnRefresh = new ToolStripButton("Refresh") { DisplayStyle = ToolStripItemDisplayStyle.Text };
            _btnAllocate = new ToolStripButton("Allocate…") { DisplayStyle = ToolStripItemDisplayStyle.Text };
            _btnRelease = new ToolStripButton("Release") { DisplayStyle = ToolStripItemDisplayStyle.Text };
            _btnScanOrphans = new ToolStripButton("Scan Orphans") { DisplayStyle = ToolStripItemDisplayStyle.Text };
            _sep1 = new ToolStripSeparator();
            _lblStore = new ToolStripLabel("Store: (auto)");

            _toolbar.Items.AddRange(new ToolStripItem[]
            {
                _btnRefresh, _sep1, _btnAllocate, _btnRelease, _btnScanOrphans,
                new ToolStripSeparator(), _lblStore
            });

            _grid = new DataGridView
            {
                Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                ReadOnly = true,
                AllowUserToAddRows = false,
                AllowUserToDeleteRows = false,
                SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
                MultiSelect = true,
                AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
            };

            _status = new StatusStrip();
            _statusText = new ToolStripStatusLabel("Ready");
            _status.Items.Add(_statusText);

            Controls.Add(_grid);
            Controls.Add(_toolbar);
            Controls.Add(_status);

            // --- events
            _btnRefresh.Click += (s, e) => RefreshFromStore();
            _btnAllocate.Click += (s, e) => AllocateDialog();
            _btnRelease.Click += (s, e) => ReleaseSelected();
            _btnScanOrphans.Click += (s, e) => ScanOrphans();

            // First paint
            SafeRefresh();
        }

        // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Public surface
        // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        public void SafeRefresh()
        {
            try { RefreshFromStore(); }
            catch (Exception ex) { ShowError("Refresh failed", ex); }
        }

        // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        // Internal plumbing — minimal, we’ll wire real services next
        // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
        private void RefreshFromStore()
        {
            // TODO: Replace with real adapter/service that reads ProjectStoreV1.
            // For now, present an empty grid with columns you expect.
            EnsureColumns();
            _grid.Rows.Clear();
            _statusText.Text = "Loaded 0 records (demo).";
            _lblStore.Text = "Store: (auto)";
        }

        private void AllocateDialog()
        {
            // TODO: open a small form to pick system, optional notes; then call adapter.
            MessageBox.Show(this,
                "Allocate dialog goes here.\n\nNext step: pipe to TagStoreV1.AllocateNext via ProjectTagStoreAdapter.",
                "Allocate", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information);
        }

        private void ReleaseSelected()
        {
            if (_grid.SelectedRows.Count == 0)
            {
                MessageBox.Show(this, "Select at least one tag row to release.", "Release",
                    MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information);
                return;
            }

            // TODO: gather selected IDs and call TagStoreV1.Release via adapter.
            var count = _grid.SelectedRows.Count;
            MessageBox.Show(this, $"Would release {count} tag(s) here (demo).",
                "Release", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information);
        }

        private void ScanOrphans()
        {
            // TODO: Iterate active drawing, find bubbles/valves with missing or mismatched links.
            MessageBox.Show(this,
                "Orphan scan placeholder.\n\nNext step: compare drawing XData vs ProjectStoreV1 and list discrepancies.",
                "Scan Orphans", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information);
        }

        private void EnsureColumns()
        {
            if (_grid.Columns.Count > 0) return;

            _grid.Columns.Add("Id", "Tag Id");
            _grid.Columns.Add("System", "System");
            _grid.Columns.Add("Status", "Status");
            _grid.Columns.Add("Drawing", "Drawing");
            _grid.Columns.Add("EntityHandle", "Entity");
            _grid.Columns.Add("By", "By");
            _grid.Columns.Add("Notes", "Notes");
        }

        private void ShowError(string title, Exception ex)
        {
            MessageBox.Show(this, $"{title}:\n{ex.Message}", "Tag Manager",
                MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error);
        }

        protected override void Dispose(bool disposing)
        {
            base.Dispose(disposing);
        }
    }
}
```

## How to wire it into your central `Commands.cs`

Add a single one-liner that follows your **Command Registration Standard**:

```csharp
// Inside AutoCAD2020/Commands.cs
using AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Commands;

// ...
[CommandMethod("CBI_TAG_MANAGER")]
public static void CBI_TAG_MANAGER() => TagManagerCommands.ShowPalette();
```

That’s it—you’ll have a clean palette shell you can open with `CBI_TAG_MANAGER`. Next pass, we can hook the buttons to your `ProjectTagStoreAdapter`/`TagStoreV1` to list, allocate, release, and scan.
