```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/Tagging/Services/TagReconcileService.cs
// Version: v0.9.0  (C# 7.3)
// Author: CBI Tools
// Purpose: "Missing in DWG" reconcile pass for ProjectStore v1.0
// Notes:
//  - Scans the current drawing for any tags that reference missing entities.
//  - Converts those tags to Orphan (adds history) and (optionally) auto-release
//    if grace window already elapsed.
//  - Uses StoreGuards to resolve store path and ProjectStoreValidator to sanity-check.
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1 (models)
//  - AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards
//  - AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreValidator
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public static class TagReconcileService
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Reconcile current drawing: mark missing-entity tags as Orphan; auto-release if grace satisfied.
        /// Returns number of tags affected.
        /// </summary>
        public static int ReconcileCurrentDrawing()
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return 0;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                string storePath;
                if (!ProjectStore.StoreGuards.TryResolvePath(doc.Database, tr, out storePath))
                {
                    // Not bound -> nothing to do
                    tr.Commit();
                    return 0;
                }
                tr.Commit();

                // Load + validate
                var store = AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards.OpenReadOnly(storePath);
                ProjectStore.ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);

                var db = doc.Database;
                var fp = db.FingerprintGuid ?? string.Empty;
                var changed = 0;

                using (var tr2 = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
                {
                    // Only tags that belong to this DWG (by path) or carry this fingerprint
                    var query = store.Tags.Where(t =>
                        string.Equals(t.DrawingPath ?? "", doc.Name ?? "", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                        || (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(t.CBIValveKey) && t.CBIValveKey.StartsWith(fp + ":", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)));

                    foreach (var tag in query)
                    {
                        // Determine any handle we can test (prefer Child/Parent, then CBIValveKey)
                        var handleHex = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tag.ChildHandle) ? tag.ChildHandle
                                      : !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tag.ParentHandle) ? tag.ParentHandle
                                      : null;

                        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(handleHex) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tag.CBIValveKey))
                        {
                            var idx = tag.CBIValveKey.IndexOf(':');
                            if (idx > 0 && idx + 1 < tag.CBIValveKey.Length)
                                handleHex = tag.CBIValveKey.Substring(idx + 1);
                        }

                        var exists = false;
                        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(handleHex))
                        {
                            try
                            {
                                var h = new Handle(Convert.ToInt64(handleHex, 16));
                                var id = db.GetObjectId(false, h, 0);
                                if (!id.IsNull)
                                {
                                    var dbo = tr2.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead, false);
                                    exists = dbo != null;
                                }
                            }
                            catch { exists = false; }
                        }

                        if (!exists && tag.Status != TagStatusV1.Orphan)
                        {
                            tag.Status = TagStatusV1.Orphan;
                            tag.OrphanedAtUtc = DateTime.UtcNow;
                            tag.History.Add(new TagHistoryV1 { Action = "MarkedOrphan", By = Environment.UserName ?? "unknown", Details = "Entity not found in DWG" });
                            store.History.Add(new HistoryEntryV1 { Action = "MarkedOrphan", By = Environment.UserName ?? "unknown", Details = tag.Id });
                            changed++;

                            // Optional auto-release if grace already elapsed
                            var grace = store.Config != null ? store.Config.GraceDays : 0;
                            if (ProjectStore.StoreGuards.CanRelease(tag, DateTime.UtcNow, grace))
                            {
                                store.Released.Add(new TagReleaseRecordV1
                                {
                                    Id = tag.Id,
                                    Reason = "Auto-release on reconcile",
                                    By = Environment.UserName ?? "unknown"
                                });
                                tag.Status = TagStatusV1.Released;
                                tag.History.Add(new TagHistoryV1 { Action = "Released", By = Environment.UserName ?? "unknown", Details = "Auto-release (grace met)" });
                                store.History.Add(new HistoryEntryV1 { Action = "Released", By = Environment.UserName ?? "unknown", Details = tag.Id });
                            }
                        }
                    }

                    tr2.Commit();
                }

                // Save atomically
                AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards.SafeSave(storePath, store);
                return changed;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/Valves/InsertValveCommand.cs
// Version: v0.9.0  (C# 7.3)
// Author: CBI Tools
// Purpose: Unified valve insertion entrypoint (Ball/Butterfly/Check)
// Notes:
//  - Prompts for valve type (unless pre-specified by CommandParameter).
//  - Resolves block name via ProjectStore v1.0 (Blocks.Valve.Types map).
//  - Inserts block and stamps XData links we rely on later.
//  - Delegates visible tag text to ValveTagCommand after insert.
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1
//  - AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards + ProjectStoreValidator
//  - XData helpers in AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations.XDataLinkUtil
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.Geometry;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
using AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public static class InsertValveCommand
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Interactive insert. If typeHint is null, user is prompted (Ball/Butterfly/Check).
        /// </summary>
        public static void Run(string typeHint = null)
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            // Type selection
            var type = typeHint;
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(type))
            {
                var op = new PromptKeywordOptions("\nValve type")
                {
                    AllowArbitraryInput = false,
                    Message = "\nSelect valve type"
                };
                op.Keywords.Add("Ball");
                op.Keywords.Add("Butterfly");
                op.Keywords.Add("Check");
                op.Keywords.Default = "Ball";
                var kres = ed.GetKeywords(op);
                if (kres.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;
                type = kres.StringResult;
            }

            // Pick insert point
            var ppr = ed.GetPoint("\nPick insertion point: ");
            if (ppr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;

            // Load store + resolve block name
            ProjectStoreV1 store;
            string storePath;
            using (var tr0 = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                if (!ProjectStore.StoreGuards.TryResolvePath(doc.Database, tr0, out storePath))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: This drawing is not bound to a Project Store (CBI/ProjectStore/Path).");
                    tr0.Commit();
                    return;
                }
                tr0.Commit();
            }
            store = ProjectStore.StoreGuards.OpenReadOnly(storePath);
            ProjectStore.ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);

            string blockName;
            if (store.Blocks == null || store.Blocks.Valve == null || store.Blocks.Valve.Types == null
                || !store.Blocks.Valve.Types.TryGetValue(type, out blockName) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(blockName))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Block mapping for valve type '{type}' not found in Project Store.");
                return;
            }

            // Insert the block
            ObjectId brId = ObjectId.Null;
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var db = doc.Database;
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);

                // Ensure the block is present (user handles missing definition/import outside MVP)
                if (!bt.Has(blockName))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Block '{blockName}' not found in drawing. Import it and try again.");
                    tr.Commit();
                    return;
                }

                var btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(bt[blockName], OpenMode.ForRead);
                var ms = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(SymbolUtilityServices.GetBlockModelSpaceId(db), OpenMode.ForWrite);

                var br = new BlockReference(ppr.Value, btr.ObjectId);
                ms.AppendEntity(br);
                tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(br, true);

                // Stamp origin + linkage app
                XDataLinkUtil.EnsureRegApp(db, tr, TagConstants.LinkRegApp);
                XDataLinkUtil.StampOrigin(tr, br);

                // Record a parent-handle (self for parent) so child bubbles can link later
                XDataLinkUtil.SetXDataString(tr, br, TagConstants.LinkRegApp, "PARENT_HANDLE", br.Handle.ToString());

                brId = br.ObjectId;
                tr.Commit();
            }

            if (brId.IsNull) return;

            // Delegate to tag command to place visible tag (or attach later)
            ValveTagCommand.Run(brId);
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/Valves/ValveTagCommand.cs
// Version: v0.9.0  (C# 7.3)
// Author: CBI Tools
// Purpose: Show/attach the visible valve tag for a valve BlockReference.
// Notes:
//  - Accepts optional preselected valve (ObjectId). If null, asks user to pick.
//  - Allocates next ID (via TagStoreV1) and stamps XData + writes visible attribute.
//  - Primary ID becomes first in XData list, additional bubbles can mirror via XData.
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1 + TagStoreV1 helpers
//  - AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards + ProjectStoreValidator
//  - AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations.XDataLinkUtil
// ============================================================================
using System;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
using AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations;
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Valves
{
    public static class ValveTagCommand
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// Attach/ensure a primary valve tag to a valve entity.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Run(ObjectId preselectedValve = default(ObjectId))
        {
            var doc = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            ObjectId valveId = preselectedValve;
            if (valveId.IsNull)
            {
                var peo = new PromptEntityOptions("\nSelect valve block:")
                {
                    AllowObjectOnLockedLayer = true
                };
                peo.SetRejectMessage("\nOnly block references, please.");
                peo.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), exactMatch: false);

                var per = ed.GetEntity(peo);
                if (per.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return;
                valveId = per.ObjectId;
            }

            // Resolve store
            string storePath;
            ProjectStoreV1 store;
            using (var tr0 = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                if (!StoreGuards.TryResolvePath(doc.Database, tr0, out storePath))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\nCBI: This drawing is not bound to a Project Store.");
                    tr0.Commit();
                    return;
                }
                tr0.Commit();
            }
            store = StoreGuards.OpenReadOnly(storePath);
            ProjectStoreValidator.ValidateOrThrow(store);

            // Choose system
            var systemKey = StoreGuards.PromptForSystemKey(ed, store);
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(systemKey)) return;

            string allocatedId = null;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var br = tr.GetObject(valveId, OpenMode.ForRead) as BlockReference;
                if (br == null) { tr.Commit(); return; }

                // Allocate next ID via TagStoreV1
                var rec = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1.TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(
                    store,
                    systemKey,
                    drawing: doc.Name,
                    entityHandle: br.Handle.ToString(),
                    by: Environment.UserName ?? "unknown",
                    notes: "ValveTagCommand");

                allocatedId = rec != null ? rec.Id : null;

                // Stamp XData ownership
                XDataLinkUtil.AddValveTagId(tr, br, allocatedId);
                XDataLinkUtil.MakePrimaryValveTagId(tr, br, allocatedId);
                XDataLinkUtil.SetInstrTag(tr, br, allocatedId);

                tr.Commit();
            }

            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(allocatedId))
            {
                // Save back to disk (atomic)
                StoreGuards.SafeSave(storePath, store);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Assigned {allocatedId}.");
            }
        }
    }
}
```

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/StoreGuards.cs
// Version: v0.9.0  (C# 7.3)
// Author: CBI Tools
// Purpose: Lightweight, tool-friendly guards for ProjectStore v1.0
// Notes:
//  - No UI here (except PromptForSystemKey convenience using Editor).
//  - Path binding via NOD key: "CBI/ProjectStore/Path" (Xrecord, single string).
//  - SafeSave performs atomic write (temp file + replace).
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1 (models)
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreService (Load/Save)
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore; // ProjectStoreService

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class StoreGuards
    {
        private const string NodKey = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";

        public static bool TryResolvePath(Database db, Transaction tr, out string path)
        {
            path = null;
            if (db == null || tr == null) return false;

            var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
            if (!nod.Contains(NodKey)) return false;

            var xrec = tr.GetObject((ObjectId)nod.GetAt(NodKey), OpenMode.ForRead) as Xrecord;
            if (xrec == null) return false;

            var rb = xrec.Data;
            if (rb == null) return false;

            var arr = rb.AsArray();
            if (arr.Length == 0 || arr[0].TypeCode != (int)DxfCode.Text) return false;

            path = arr[0].Value as string;
            return !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path);
        }

        public static ProjectStoreV1 OpenReadOnly(string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) throw new ArgumentException("Store path is null/empty.");
            if (!File.Exists(path)) throw new FileNotFoundException("Project Store not found.", path);
            return ProjectStoreService.LoadV1(path); // Provided by your Shared ProjectStoreService
        }

        public static void SafeSave(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) throw new ArgumentException("Path is null/empty.");
            if (store == null) throw new ArgumentNullException("store");

            var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(path);
            var tmp = Path.Combine(dir ?? "", Path.GetFileName(path) + ".tmp");

            ProjectStoreService.SaveV1(tmp, store);
            // Replace atomically when possible
            if (File.Exists(path))
            {
                File.Replace(tmp, path, null);
            }
            else
            {
                File.Move(tmp, path);
            }

            // Read-back check (light)
            var check = ProjectStoreService.LoadV1(path);
            if (check == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(check.Version))
                throw new IOException("Atomic save verification failed (empty or unreadable).");
        }

        public static bool CanRelease(TagRecordV1 tag, DateTime nowUtc, int graceDays)
        {
            if (tag == null || tag.OrphanedAtUtc == null) return false;
            if (graceDays <= 0) return true;
            return (nowUtc - tag.OrphanedAtUtc.Value).TotalDays >= graceDays;
        }

        public static string PromptForSystemKey(Editor ed, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            if (ed == null || store == null || store.Systems == null || store.Systems.Count == 0) return null;

            var keys = new List<string>(store.Systems.Keys);
            keys.Sort(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            var msg = "\nSystem key (" + string.Join("/", keys.ToArray()) + "): ";
            var pr = ed.GetString(msg);
            if (pr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return null;

            var key = (pr.StringResult ?? "").Trim();
            if (!store.Systems.ContainsKey(key))
            {
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nCBI: Unknown system '{key}'.");
                return null;
            }
            return key;
        }
    }
}
```

---

```csharp
// ============================================================================
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreValidator.cs
// Version: v0.9.0  (C# 7.3)
// Author: CBI Tools
// Purpose: Consistency validation for ProjectStore v1.0
// Notes:
//  - Throws InvalidOperationException with concise messages on failure.
//  - Keep fast: linear scans + simple lookups.
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1 (models)
// ============================================================================
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectStoreValidator
    {
        public static void ValidateOrThrow(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            if (s == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Store is null.");
            if (!string.Equals(s.Version ?? "", "1.0", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                throw new InvalidOperationException("Unsupported ProjectStore version (expected '1.0').");

            if (s.Config == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Config section missing.");
            if (s.Project == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Project section missing.");
            if (s.Systems == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Systems section missing.");
            if (s.Blocks == null || s.Blocks.Valve == null || s.Blocks.Valve.Types == null)
                throw new InvalidOperationException("Blocks.Valve.Types mapping missing.");
            if (s.Tags == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Tags collection missing.");
            if (s.Released == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Released collection missing.");
            if (s.History == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("History collection missing.");

            // Systems
            foreach (var kv in s.Systems)
            {
                var key = kv.Key ?? "";
                var sys = kv.Value;
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key)) throw new InvalidOperationException("Blank system key.");
                if (sys == null) throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{key}' is null.");
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sys.Prefix)) throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{key}' has blank Prefix.");
                if (sys.Pad < 1) throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{key}' has invalid Pad '{sys.Pad}'.");
                if (sys.Start < 0 || sys.End < sys.Start) throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{key}' has invalid range [{sys.Start},{sys.End}].");
                if (sys.Next < sys.Start || sys.Next > sys.End + 1) // allow End+1 as "exhausted"
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{key}' has out-of-range Next '{sys.Next}'.");
            }

            // Tag uniqueness + format rules
            var seen = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            foreach (var t in s.Tags)
            {
                if (t == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Null tag record.");
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(t.Id)) throw new InvalidOperationException("Tag with blank Id.");
                if (!seen.Add(t.Id)) throw new InvalidOperationException($"Duplicate Tag Id '{t.Id}'.");

                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(t.System) || !s.Systems.ContainsKey(t.System))
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"Tag '{t.Id}' references unknown System '{t.System}'.");

                var sys = s.Systems[t.System];
                var rx = new Regex("^" + Regex.Escape(sys.Prefix) + "-" + "\\d{" + sys.Pad + "}$", RegexOptions.CultureInvariant);
                if (!rx.IsMatch(t.Id))
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"Tag '{t.Id}' does not match system '{t.System}' format ({sys.Prefix}-{new string('0', sys.Pad)}).");

                // Coherent status
                if (t.Status != TagStatusV1.Active &&
                    t.Status != TagStatusV1.Orphan &&
                    t.Status != TagStatusV1.Released &&
                    t.Status != TagStatusV1.Reserved &&
                    t.Status != TagStatusV1.Superseded)
                {
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"Tag '{t.Id}' has invalid Status '{t.Status}'.");
                }
            }

            // Released must not collide with active tags
            var tagIds = new HashSet<string>(s.Tags.Select(x => x.Id), StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            foreach (var r in s.Released)
            {
                if (r == null) throw new InvalidOperationException("Null Released record.");
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(r.Id)) throw new InvalidOperationException("Released item with blank Id.");
                if (tagIds.Contains(r.Id))
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"Released Id '{r.Id}' collides with active tag list.");
            }

            // Basic block map sanity
            if (s.Blocks.Valve.Types.Count == 0)
                throw new InvalidOperationException("Blocks.Valve.Types is empty.");
        }
    }
}
```

### 🧩 CBI Build Report — Tag/Valve/Store Integration Pass

#### **Tool / Module**
Milestones 10–12 integration (TagReconcileService, InsertValveCommand, ValveTagCommand, StoreGuards, Validator)

| CS0117 | `ProjectStoreService` does not contain `LoadV1` / `SaveV1` | Your Shared ProjectStoreService uses different method names. We’ll match them. |
| CS0104 | Ambiguous reference between `StoreGuards` | Namespace collision (`AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.StoreGuards` vs `AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore.StoreGuards`). We need an alias or remove old one. |
| CS0117 | `TagManagerService` missing methods (`AddTagToSelection`, `ReleaseSelected`, `SweepAutoRelease`, `ShowPalette`) | Old TagManagerService was in Shared; the new one lives under `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services`. We’ll update `Commands.cs` references. |
| CS0246 / CS0117 | `TagStoreV1`, `TagRecordV1`, `TagStatusV1` unresolved | Your Shared namespace uses `AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1_0` (or similar). We’ll check the actual folder name and align `using`. |
| CS0117 | `TagStatusV1` missing enum members `Reserved` / `Superseded` | Your enum only defines Active, Orphan, Released. We’ll add safe fallback cases. |
| CS0117 | `TagRecordV1` missing `DrawingPath`, `CBIValveKey` | Your TagRecord uses different property names (e.g. `Drawing` / `ValveHandle`). We’ll map accordingly. |

| **ProjectStoreService** | Different naming convention | Identify your actual `Load` and `Save` methods (e.g. `LoadFromFile`, `SaveToFile`, `Read`, `Write`) then update the 2 calls in `StoreGuards`. |
| **Namespace conflict** | You imported both Shared and Local StoreGuards | Remove `using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;` from AutoCAD2020 files that already declare `namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore`. |
| **TagManagerService** | Ref to obsolete service in Shared project | Change `Commands.cs` references to `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.TagManagerService`. |
| **TagStore classes** | Wrong namespace name | Verify your folder under Shared (`ProjectStore\V1` or `V1_0`) and update `using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;` accordingly. |
| **Enums / Models** | Older schema | Add missing enum members or remove checks for them. |
| **TagRecord property names** | Different JSON schema | Rename the usages to match your actual class (`DrawingPath`→`Drawing`, `CBIValveKey`→`ValveKey`). |

// v1.0 Tag logic helpers (allocation, orphan/release, reassign)
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/TagStoreV1.cs
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static class TagStoreV1
    {
        // ---------------- helpers ----------------
        private static string FormatId(V1.SystemCounterV1 sys, int number)
            => $"{sys.Prefix}-{number.ToString().PadLeft(sys.Pad, '0')}";

        private static bool TryParseNumericSuffix(string id, out int n)
        {
            n = 0;
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(id)) return false;
            var dash = id.LastIndexOf('-');
            if (dash < 0 || dash == id.Length - 1) return false;
            return int.TryParse(id.Substring(dash + 1), out n);
        }

        // Find a tag record (case-insensitive)
        public static V1.TagRecordV1 Find(V1.ProjectStoreV1 s, string id)
            => s?.Tags?.Find(t => string.Equals(t.Id, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));

        // ---------------- Allocate (full) ----------------
        // Full overload (covers all call-sites going forward)
        public static V1.TagRecordV1 AllocateNext(
            V1.ProjectStoreV1 store,
            string systemKey,
            string drawing,
            string entityHandle,
            string reason,
            string by,
            string source,
            string note)
        {
            if (store is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(store));
            if (!store.Systems.TryGetValue(systemKey, out var sys))
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"System '{systemKey}' not found.");

            // Prefer released pool first (lowest numeric in this system/prefix)
            if (store.Config?.PreferReleasedLowestFirst == true && store.Released.Count > 0)
            {
                var candidate = store.Released
                    .Where(r => r.Id.StartsWith(sys.Prefix + "-", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    .OrderBy(r => TryParseNumericSuffix(r.Id, out var num) ? num : int.MaxValue)
                    .ThenBy(r => r.Id, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                    .FirstOrDefault();

                if (candidate != null)
                {
                    store.Released.Remove(candidate);

                    var rec = new V1.TagRecordV1
                    {
                        Id = candidate.Id,
                        System = systemKey,
                        Drawing = drawing ?? string.Empty,
                        EntityHandle = entityHandle ?? string.Empty,
                        Status = V1.TagStatusV1.Active,
                        By = by ?? string.Empty,
                        Notes = note ?? string.Empty
                    };

                    rec.History.Add(new V1.TagHistoryV1
                    {
                        Action = "AllocatedFromReleased",
                        By = by ?? string.Empty,
                        Details = $"{reason ?? "Reused"} (source: {source ?? "n/a"})"
                    });

                    store.Tags.Add(rec);
                    store.History.Add(new V1.HistoryEntryV1
                    {
                        Action = "AllocateFromReleased",
                        By = by ?? string.Empty,
                        Details = rec.Id
                    });

                    return rec;
                }
            }

            // Normal allocation from counter
            if (sys.Next < sys.Start) sys.Next = sys.Start;
            if (sys.Next > sys.End)
                throw new InvalidOperationException(
                    $"System '{systemKey}' exhausted (Next={sys.Next}, End={sys.End}).");

            var id = FormatId(sys, sys.Next);
            sys.Next++; // advance counter

            var record = new V1.TagRecordV1
            {
                Id = id,
                System = systemKey,
                Drawing = drawing ?? string.Empty,
                EntityHandle = entityHandle ?? string.Empty,
                Status = V1.TagStatusV1.Active,
                By = by ?? string.Empty,
                Notes = note ?? string.Empty
            };

            record.History.Add(new V1.TagHistoryV1
            {
                Action = "Allocated",
                By = by ?? string.Empty,
                Details = $"{reason ?? "Allocated"} (source: {source ?? "n/a"})"
            });

            store.Tags.Add(record);
            store.History.Add(new V1.HistoryEntryV1
            {
                Action = "Allocate",
                By = by ?? string.Empty,
                Details = id
            });

            return record;
        }

        // ---------------- Allocate (compat overloads) ----------------
        // 6-arg: reason + by (defaults source/note)
        public static V1.TagRecordV1 AllocateNext(
            V1.ProjectStoreV1 store,
            string systemKey,
            string drawing,
            string entityHandle,
            string reason,
            string by)
            => AllocateNext(store, systemKey, drawing, entityHandle, reason, by, "CBI_AddTag", null);

// 4-arg: no reason/by (most relaxed)
        public static V1.TagRecordV1 AllocateNext(
            V1.ProjectStoreV1 store,
            string systemKey,
            string drawing,
            string entityHandle)
            => AllocateNext(store, systemKey, drawing, entityHandle,
                            "Manual allocation", Environment.UserName ?? "unknown",
                            "CBI_AddTag", null);

        // ---------------- Status changes ----------------
        public static bool MarkOrphan(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, string id, string by, string details = null)
        {
            var rec = store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => string.Equals(t.Id, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
            if (rec == null) return false;

            rec.Status = V1.TagStatusV1.Orphan;
            rec.OrphanedAtUtc = DateTime.UtcNow;
            rec.History.Add(new V1.TagHistoryV1 { Action = "MarkedOrphan", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = details ?? string.Empty });
            store.History.Add(new V1.HistoryEntryV1 { Action = "MarkedOrphan", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = id });
            return true;
        }

        public static bool Release(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, string id, string reason, string by)
        {
            var rec = store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => string.Equals(t.Id, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
            if (rec == null) return false;

            if (rec.Status != V1.TagStatusV1.Orphan)
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"Tag '{id}' is not an Orphan.");

            var grace = Math.Max(0, store.Config?.GraceDays ?? 0);
            var ok = !rec.OrphanedAtUtc.HasValue || (DateTime.UtcNow - rec.OrphanedAtUtc.Value).TotalDays >= grace;
            if (!ok) return false;

            store.Released.Add(new V1.TagReleaseRecordV1
            {
                Id = rec.Id,
                Reason = reason ?? string.Empty,
                By = by ?? string.Empty
            });

            rec.Status = V1.TagStatusV1.Released;
            rec.History.Add(new V1.TagHistoryV1 { Action = "Released", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = reason ?? string.Empty });
            store.History.Add(new V1.HistoryEntryV1 { Action = "Released", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = id });
            return true;
        }

        public static V1.TagRecordV1 Reassign(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, string id, string newEntityHandle, string newDrawing, string by)
        {
            var rec = store.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => string.Equals(t.Id, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
            if (rec == null) return null;

            var from = $"{rec.Drawing}:{rec.EntityHandle}";
            rec.EntityHandle = newEntityHandle ?? string.Empty;
            rec.Drawing = newDrawing ?? rec.Drawing;
            rec.History.Add(new V1.TagHistoryV1 { Action = "Reassigned", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = $"{from} → {rec.Drawing}:{rec.EntityHandle}" });
            store.History.Add(new V1.HistoryEntryV1 { Action = "Reassigned", By = by ?? string.Empty, Details = id });
            return rec;
        }

        // ---------------- queries ----------------
        public static IEnumerable<V1.TagRecordV1> FindOrphans(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => store.Tags.Where(t => t.Status == V1.TagStatusV1.Orphan);

        public static bool Exists(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, string id)
            => store.Tags.Any(t => string.Equals(t.Id, id, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
    }
}

# 1) Fix `StoreGuards.cs`
**Why:** Your Shared service exposes `Load(string)` and `SaveAtomic(string, ProjectStoreV1)`, not `LoadV1/SaveV1`. Also there’s a name clash between Shared and Local `StoreGuards` namespaces.

**Patch (replace the whole file):**
```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/StoreGuards.cs
// v0.9.1 — Local store guards (uses Shared ProjectStoreService.Load/SaveAtomic)

using System;
using System.Linq;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;      // ProjectStoreService
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;   // ProjectStoreV1, TagRecordV1, TagStatusV1

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class StoreGuards
    {
        // ---- IO helpers -----------------------------------------------------
        public static ProjectStoreV1 OpenReadOnly(string path)
            => ProjectStoreService.Load(path);

        public static void SafeSave(string path, ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => ProjectStoreService.SaveAtomic(path, store);

        // ---- Queries / guards -----------------------------------------------
        public static bool TagIdOwnedByParent(ProjectStoreV1 s, string tagId, string parentHandle)
        {
            if (s == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tagId) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(parentHandle))
                return false;

            var rec = s.Tags.FirstOrDefault(t => string.Equals(t.Id, tagId, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
            if (rec == null) return false;

            // Your v1 schema uses ParentHandle/EntityHandle/Drawing (no CBIValveKey).
            return string.Equals(rec.ParentHandle ?? "", parentHandle ?? "", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
        }

        public static class DuplicateGroup
        {
            public string Id { get; set; }
            public System.Collections.Generic.List<TagRecordV1> Records { get; set; }
        }

        public static System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<DuplicateGroup> FindDuplicateIds(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            return s.Tags
                .GroupBy(t => (t.Id ?? "").Trim(), StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                .Where(g => g.Count() > 1)
                .Select(g => new DuplicateGroup { Id = g.Key, Records = g.ToList() });
        }

        public static System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<TagRecordV1> FindOrphans(ProjectStoreV1 s)
            => s.Tags.Where(t => t.Status == TagStatusV1.Orphan);

        // Auto-release candidates (grace check is done in TagStoreV1.Release)
        public static System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<TagRecordV1> FindAutoReleaseEligible(ProjectStoreV1 s)
        {
            return s.Tags.Where(t => t.Status == TagStatusV1.Orphan);
        }

        public static int AutoReleaseAllEligible(ProjectStoreV1 s, string reason, string by)
        {
            var list = FindAutoReleaseEligible(s).ToList();
            foreach (var r in list) TagStoreV1.Release(s, r.Id, reason, by);
            return list.Count;
        }
    }
}
```

---

# 4) Fix `ReconcileCurrentDrawing.cs` (milestone 10)
**Why:** Your v1 record uses `Drawing` + `EntityHandle` (not `DrawingPath` / `CBIValveKey`).  
**Drop-in reconcile pass (replace the file):**
```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ReconcileCurrentDrawing.cs
// v0.9.1 — MissingInDWG scan using Drawing + EntityHandle

using System;
using System.Linq;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using AcApp = Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices.Application;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ReconcileCurrentDrawing
    {
        public static void Run()
        {
            var doc = AcApp.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var path = ProjectStorePaths.GetPath(doc.Database, tr);
                tr.Commit();

                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) { AcApp.ShowAlertDialog("No Project Store bound."); return; }

                var store = ProjectStoreService.Load(path);

                var db = doc.Database;
                var present = GetAllHandlesInDrawing(db); // HashSet<string> of handles

                int orphaned = 0;
                foreach (var rec in store.Tags.Where(t => string.Equals(t.Drawing ?? "", doc.Name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
                {
                    var h = (rec.EntityHandle ?? "").Trim();
                    if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(h) || !present.Contains(h))
                    {
                        if (rec.Status != V1.TagStatusV1.Orphan)
                        {
                            TagStoreV1.MarkOrphan(store, rec.Id, Environment.UserName, "Missing in DWG");
                            orphaned++;
                        }
                    }
                }

                ProjectStoreService.SaveAtomic(path, store);
                AcApp.ShowAlertDialog($"Reconcile complete.\nMarked Orphan: {orphaned}");
            }
        }

        private static System.Collections.Generic.HashSet<string> GetAllHandlesInDrawing(Database db)
        {
            var set = new System.Collections.Generic.HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var bt = (BlockTable)tr.GetObject(db.BlockTableId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                foreach (ObjectId btrId in bt)
                {
                    var btr = (BlockTableRecord)tr.GetObject(btrId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                    foreach (ObjectId id in btr)
                    {
                        var dbo = tr.GetObject(id, OpenMode.ForRead, false);
                        set.Add(dbo.Handle.ToString());
                    }
                }
                tr.Commit();
            }
            return set;
        }
    }
}
```

---


Not too late at all—this is actually perfect timing. Your **v1 enum** is:

```csharp
public enum TagStatusV1 { Active = 0, Orphan = 1, Released = 2 }
```

So we’ll keep the whole pipeline aligned to **only these three** states. Here’s what to tweak so the new code compiles cleanly and the UI still says “MissingInDWG” where you like:

## What to change (fast checklist)

1) **Remove legacy states** from any code
- Delete checks/usages of `Reserved` / `Superseded`.
- Typical files: `ValveTagCommand.cs`, `ProjectStoreValidator.cs`, anywhere a filter list was built.

2) **Validator: allow only the 3 statuses**
Replace any status validation with:

```csharp
// Allowed statuses in v1
if (rec.Status != V1.TagStatusV1.Active &&
    rec.Status != V1.TagStatusV1.Orphan &&
    rec.Status != V1.TagStatusV1.Released)
{
    rec.Status = V1.TagStatusV1.Active; // normalize unknowns
}
```

3) **UI filter list in Tag Manager**  
If you want the UI wording “MissingInDWG” but the store to use `Orphan`, do this:

**Status dropdown source:**
```csharp
var statuses = new[] { "(All)", "Active", "MissingInDWG", "Released" };
cboStatus.ComboBox.DataSource = statuses;
```

**Filter mapping:**
```csharp
var st = cboStatus.SelectedItem as string;

if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(st) && st != "(All)")
{
    if (string.Equals(st, "MissingInDWG", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        filtered = filtered.Where(t => t.Status == "Orphan");
    else
        filtered = filtered.Where(t => string.Equals(t.Status, st, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
}
```

**Enable/disable Release button:**
```csharp
btnRelease.Enabled = (rec != null && rec.Status == "Orphan");
```

## Response:
11/5/2025, 10:13:51 AM

> **Providing fix plan**
>
> To resolve errors: 
> 
> - Remove duplicate TagStoreV1 files/classes from the project, keeping just one and ensuring all files use distinct namespaces.
>   
> - Add stub/adapter methods in ProjectStoreService for missing older API calls (e.g., LoadV1/SaveV1), pointing to newer methods.
> 
> - Provide a static helper method for ProjectStorePaths.GetPath.
> 
> - Ensure static method signatures are correct in TagManagerService, especially within the proper namespace.
>
> **Addressing errors**
>
> - CS0542 error: likely due to a method named TagManagerService within the class. Renaming it to ShowPalette should fix the conflict.
> 
> - Resolve ambiguous overloads in TagStoreV1 by keeping only one class definition.
> 
> - Fix missing 'notes' parameter on AllocateNext by updating to the appropriate method.
> 
> - Fully qualify or enforce correct namespace for StoreGuards and ProjectStoreValidator.
> 
> - Add necessary adapters and fix LineTagSettings.
>
> **Creating code snippets**
>
> I'll add compatibility shims to `ProjectStoreService.cs` like `LoadV1`, `SaveV1`, `CreateDefault`, and `GetSystemNextList`. These will map to existing methods, offering backward compatibility.
> 
> For ProjectStorePaths, I'll implement `GetPath` to forward calls from `ProjectStorePaths` to `ProjectTagPath` in the correct namespace.
> 
> The goal is to ensure minimal changes to compile cleanly without breaking existing functionality.
>
> Thought for 42s

You didn’t break it—this is just the “API drift” we expected after trimming statuses. Let’s knock out the squiggles in three quick passes:

---

# 1) Kill the duplicates (most of the CS0121/CS0111)
You currently have **two copies** of `TagStoreV1` in the *shared* project (the error list shows the same members “already defined” at multiple lines).

**Action:** keep only one file:

- Keep: `AutoCAD_Shared_Project\ProjectStore\TagStoreV1.cs` (the newer one with `AllocateNext(...reason, by, source, note)` and `Orphan/Released` only).
- Delete/Exclude the other duplicate `TagStoreV1.cs` from the project (or rename it to `TagStoreV1_OLD.cs` and set **Build Action = None**).

Once there’s only one `TagStoreV1`, all the “ambiguous”/“already defines” errors for `AllocateNext/Find/FormatId/...` will disappear.

---

# 2) Add a tiny **compat shim** to `ProjectStoreService`
Older code is calling legacy method names. Drop these **shims** at the bottom of your existing `ProjectStoreService` (shared project), below your real `Load/SaveAtomic` methods.

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD_Shared_Project/ProjectStore/ProjectStoreService.cs
// v1.0 — Compatibility shims for older call-sites

using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore
{
    public static partial class ProjectStoreService
    {
        // Old names -> new implementations
        public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 LoadV1(string path) => Load(path);
        public static void SaveV1(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store) => SaveAtomic(path, store);

        // Provide a basic default store (used by some UIs)
        public static V1.ProjectStoreV1 CreateDefault(string projectName = "Untitled")
            => new V1.ProjectStoreV1
            {
                Project = new V1.ProjectInfoV1 { Name = projectName },
                Config  = new V1.StoreConfigV1()
            };

        // Simple “peek nexts” for UI dropdowns
        public static Dictionary<string, int> GetSystemNextList(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => store?.Systems?.ToDictionary(kv => kv.Key, kv => kv.Value?.Next ?? 0)
               ?? new Dictionary<string, int>();

        // Global config reads/writes (v1 schema)
        public static string ReadGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => store?.Config?.GlobalValvePrefix ?? "V";

        public static int ReadGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => store?.Config?.GraceDays ?? 7;

        public static bool ReadPreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
            => store?.Config?.PreferReleasedLowestFirst ?? true;

        public static void WriteGlobalValvePrefix(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, string prefix)
        {
            if (store?.Config == null) return;
            store.Config.GlobalValvePrefix = prefix ?? "V";
        }

        public static void WriteGraceDays(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, int days)
        {
            if (store?.Config == null) return;
            store.Config.GraceDays = days < 0 ? 0 : days;
        }

        public static void WritePreferReleasedLowestFirst(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store, bool value)
        {
            if (store?.Config == null) return;
            store.Config.PreferReleasedLowestFirst = value;
        }

        // Legacy “Save” alias some files call
        public static void Save(string path, V1.ProjectStoreV1 store) => SaveAtomic(path, store);

        // Optional preview strings used by some property UIs
        public static string PeekLineTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            if (store?.Systems == null || store.Systems.Count == 0) return null;
            var sys = store.Systems.Values.FirstOrDefault();
            if (sys == null) return null;
            var n = (sys.Next < sys.Start) ? sys.Start : sys.Next;
            return $"{sys.Prefix}-{n.ToString().PadLeft(sys.Pad, '0')}";
        }

        public static string PeekValveTagString(V1.ProjectStoreV1 store) => PeekLineTagString(store);
    }
}
```

This removes all the “`ProjectStoreService` does not contain a definition for …” errors.

---

# 3) Provide a **ProjectStorePaths.GetPath** adapter (used by Reconcile)
Your reconcile code expects a `ProjectStorePaths.GetPath(...)`. Add this **lightweight forwarder** in the AutoCAD project:

```csharp
// File: AutoCAD2020/ProjectStore/ProjectStorePaths.cs
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;

namespace AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore
{
    public static class ProjectStorePaths
    {
        // Forward to your existing helper (you already use ProjectTagPath elsewhere)
        public static string GetPath(Database db, Transaction tr)
            => ProjectTagPath.GetPath(db, tr);
    }
}
```

---

# 4) Fix TagManagerService signatures and namespace
A few errors say methods don’t exist or there’s a member named the same as the class:

- Ensure the class is `namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services { public static class TagManagerService { ... } }`
- Make sure **no method** is literally named `TagManagerService()` (that causes CS0542).
- Ensure these **public static** methods exist with exact names (they can just call the palette/service you already wired):
  - `AddTagToSelectedValve()`
  - `ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve()`
  - `SweepAutoReleaseOrphans()`
  - `ShowPalette()`

If you only have the palette host: have `ShowPalette()` call your `TagManagerCommands.ShowPalette()` (or the palette host directly).

---

# 5) Calls using `notes` on `AllocateNext`
You trimmed to the **6-arg** overload (reason, by) and the **4-arg** overload. Any call like:

```csharp
TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(store, sys, dwg, handle, by: "me", notes: "x")
```

should switch to one of:

- 6-arg form (include a reason & by):
```csharp
TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(store, sys, dwg, handle, "Manual allocation", by);
```

- or the relaxed 4-arg:
```csharp
TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(store, sys, dwg, handle);
```

That clears all the “no parameter named ‘notes’” errors.

---

# 6) Ambiguous `ProjectStoreValidator`
You have two types named `ProjectStoreValidator` (one under `AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore` and the one we added in the shared project). Pick **one** and fully-qualify where needed. I recommend using the **shared** one everywhere:

```csharp
using V1 = AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;
// ...
AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.ProjectStoreValidator.Validate(store);
```

(or `using` alias for the validator class).

---

# 7) `StoreGuards` missing in TagManagerService
Add the correct `using` and/or qualify:

```csharp
using AutoCAD2020.ProjectStore;
// ...
var owned = StoreGuards.TagIdOwnedByParent(path, tagId, parentHandle);
```

(If your `StoreGuards` lives in the shared project, qualify that namespace instead.)

---

## Sanity order to apply
1) Remove the extra/old `TagStoreV1.cs` from the project.
2) Paste the **compat shim** into `ProjectStoreService` (shared).
3) Add `ProjectStorePaths.cs` (AutoCAD project).
4) Ensure `TagManagerService` has those four **public static** methods and no constructor-like member.
5) Change any `AllocateNext(... notes: ...)` calls to 6-arg or 4-arg.
6) Fully-qualify `ProjectStoreValidator`.
7) Add the right `using` for `StoreGuards`.

Do those and the sea of red should collapse fast. If any specific line still complains after this pass, paste that snippet and I’ll give the exact replacement.

// ============================================================================
// Project   : CBI Tools – AutoCAD 2020
// Module    : TagManagerService.cs
// Purpose   : Core service for valve tag allocation, release, and orphan cleanup.
// Author    : Brian Ranstead / ChatGPT CoDev
// Created   : 2025-11-04
// Version   : v0.9.0 (C# 7.3 compatible)
//
// Summary   :
//  - Integrates TagStoreV1 and ProjectStoreV1 logic into AutoCAD.
//  - Handles allocation/release of tag IDs directly from valve blocks.
//  - Reads/writes ProjectStore.json path from NOD (CBI/ProjectStore/Path).
//  - Adds/Removes XData and InstrTag attributes on valve entities.
//  - Provides 3 routed commands: Add, Release, and Sweep.
//
// Dependencies:
//  - AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations.XDataLinkUtil
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.TagStoreV1
//  - AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1.ProjectStoreV1
//
// Notes:
//  - This class follows the CBI standard “service-layer” design (no UI).
//  - All entry points are static and callable via Commands.cs.
//
// Change Log:
//  v0.9.0  Initial integration for Tag Manager – added AddTag, ReleaseTag, Sweep commands.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.ApplicationServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.DatabaseServices;
using Autodesk.AutoCAD.EditorInput;

using AutoCAD2020.Block_Operations; // XDataLinkUtil
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;       // TagStoreV1
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;    // ProjectStoreV1, TagStatusV1

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging
{
    public static class TagManagerService
    {
        // ---- Public entry points (call these from Commands.cs) ----------------

        // Allocate a new tag ID to a selected valve block (and stamp XData)
        public static void AddTagToSelectedValve()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            // Pick a block
            var br = PromptForBlockReference(ed, "Select valve block to tag");
            if (br == null) return;

            // Choose a system (keywords from JSON Systems)
            var systemKey = PromptForSystemKey(ed, store);
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(systemKey)) return;

            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var brObj = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(br.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                var handleHex = brObj.Handle.ToString();

                // Allocate — prefer released pool if configured
                var rec = TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(
                    store,
                    systemKey,
                    drawing: doc.Name ?? "",
                    entityHandle: handleHex,
                    by: Environment.UserName ?? "unknown",
                    notes: "TagManagerService.AddTagToSelectedValve");

                // XDATA: add ID, make it primary, also mirror visible attribute
                XDataLinkUtil.AddValveTagId(tr, brObj, rec.Id);
                XDataLinkUtil.MakePrimaryValveTagId(tr, brObj, rec.Id);
                XDataLinkUtil.SetInstrTag(tr, brObj, rec.Id);

                tr.Commit();
            }

            // Persist JSON
            ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            ed.WriteMessage($"\nTag allocated and applied.");
        }

        // Mark current ID on a selected valve as orphan, then try to release
        public static void ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            var br = PromptForBlockReference(ed, "Select valve block to release its primary tag");
            if (br == null) return;

            string idToRelease = null;
            using (var tr = doc.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var brObj = (BlockReference)tr.GetObject(br.ObjectId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                var ids = XDataLinkUtil.GetValveTagIds(tr, brObj);
                if (ids != null && ids.Count > 0)
                    idToRelease = ids[0];

                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(idToRelease))
                {
                    ed.WriteMessage("\nNo valve tag IDs found on selection.");
                    return;
                }

                // Mark orphan (so grace logic is respected)
                TagStoreV1.MarkOrphan(store, idToRelease, Environment.UserName, "User requested release");

                // Attempt immediate release (will respect grace days > 0)
                var ok = TagStoreV1.Release(store, idToRelease, "User release", Environment.UserName);
                if (!ok)
                    ed.WriteMessage($"\nRelease deferred (within grace period of {store.Config?.GraceDays ?? 0} days).");

                // Remove from XDATA list and visible attr
                XDataLinkUtil.RemoveValveTagId(tr, brObj, idToRelease);
                // If it was primary, the MakePrimary step is skipped—list is already rewritten without it
                XDataLinkUtil.SetInstrTag(tr, brObj, ids.Count > 1 ? ids[1] : string.Empty);

                tr.Commit();
            }

            ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            ed.WriteMessage("\nTag unlinked from block and store updated.");
        }

        // Sweep-orphan → release any eligible IDs across the whole store
        public static void SweepAutoReleaseOrphans()
        {
            var doc = Application.DocumentManager.MdiActiveDocument;
            if (doc == null) return;
            var ed = doc.Editor;

            if (!TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(doc, ed, out var storePath, out var store))
                return;

            var count = StoreGuards.AutoReleaseAllEligible(store, "AutoSweep", Environment.UserName ?? "unknown");
            if (count > 0)
            {
                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
                ed.WriteMessage($"\nAuto-released {count} orphaned tag(s).");
            }
            else
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo eligible orphaned tags to release.");
            }
        }

        // ---- Helpers ----------------------------------------------------------

        private static BlockReferencePromptResult PromptForBlockReference(Editor ed, string msg)
        {
            var po = new PromptEntityOptions($"\n{msg}:");
            po.SetRejectMessage("\n…must be a block reference.");
            po.AddAllowedClass(typeof(BlockReference), exactMatch: false);
            var pr = ed.GetEntity(po);
            if (pr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return null;
            return new BlockReferencePromptResult(pr.ObjectId);
        }

        private static string PromptForSystemKey(Editor ed, ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            var keys = (store.Systems ?? new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>()).Keys.ToList();
            if (keys.Count == 0)
            {
                ed.WriteMessage("\nNo Systems defined in Project Store.");
                return null;
            }

            // If there’s only one, use it silently
            if (keys.Count == 1) return keys[0];

            var pko = new PromptKeywordOptions("\nSelect System");
            pko.AllowNone = false;
            foreach (var k in keys) pko.Keywords.Add(k);
            var r = ed.GetKeywords(pko);
            return (r.Status == PromptStatus.OK) ? r.StringResult : null;
        }

        // Load store by reading the NOD pointer "CBI/ProjectStore/Path"; prompt if missing.
        private static bool TryLoadStoreForActiveDoc(
            Document doc,
            Editor ed,
            out string storePath,
            out ProjectStoreV1 store)
        {
            storePath = null;
            store = null;

            // 1) Try NOD
            storePath = TryReadStorePathFromNod(doc.Database);
            // 2) If missing, prompt user once
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(storePath))
            {
                var ps = new PromptOpenFileOptions("\nSelect ProjectStore.json");
                ps.Filter = "JSON (*.json)|*.json|All Files (*.*)|*.*";
                var pr = ed.GetFileNameForOpen(ps);
                if (pr.Status != PromptStatus.OK) return false;
                storePath = pr.StringResult;

                // (Optional) persist to NOD so next time it just works
                TryWriteStorePathToNod(doc.Database, storePath);
            }

            // Load or initialize file
            if (File.Exists(storePath))
            {
                store = ProjectStoreService.Load(storePath);
            }
            else
            {
                store = ProjectStoreService.CreateDefault();
                ProjectStoreService.Save(storePath, store);
            }

            return store != null;
        }

        private static string TryReadStorePathFromNod(Database db)
        {
            const string nodKey = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";
            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForRead);
                if (!nod.Contains(nodKey)) return null;
                var xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(nodKey), OpenMode.ForRead);
                var data = xr?.Data?.AsArray();
                if (data != null && data.Length > 0 && data[0].TypeCode == (int)DxfCode.Text)
                    return data[0].Value as string;
                tr.Commit();
            }
            return null;
        }

        private static void TryWriteStorePathToNod(Database db, string path)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(path)) return;
            const string nodKey = "CBI/ProjectStore/Path";

            using (var tr = db.TransactionManager.StartTransaction())
            {
                var nod = (DBDictionary)tr.GetObject(db.NamedObjectsDictionaryId, OpenMode.ForWrite);
                Xrecord xr;
                if (nod.Contains(nodKey))
                {
                    xr = (Xrecord)tr.GetObject(nod.GetAt(nodKey), OpenMode.ForWrite);
                }
                else
                {
                    xr = new Xrecord();
                    nod.SetAt(nodKey, xr);
                    tr.AddNewlyCreatedDBObject(xr, true);
                }

                xr.Data = new ResultBuffer(new TypedValue((int)DxfCode.Text, path ?? ""));
                tr.Commit();
            }
        }

        // Small holder so PromptEntity result is easy to pass around
        private sealed class BlockReferencePromptResult
        {
            public ObjectId ObjectId { get; }
            public BlockReferencePromptResult(ObjectId id) { ObjectId = id; }
        }
    }
}

// ============================================================================
//  CBI Tools — Tag Manager (Service layer)
//  File:    Tagging/Services/TagManagerService.cs
//  Author:  ChatGPT (GPT-5 Thinking) — helper
//  Target:  AutoCAD 2020 (C# 7.3)
//  Summary: Facade used by TagManagerControl. Ships with a safe in-memory
//           gateway so it compiles immediately. Replace the gateway with your
//           ProjectTagStoreAdapter/ProjectStoreService to go live.
// ============================================================================

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

// Shared store types
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore;
using AutoCAD_Shared_Project.ProjectStore.V1;

namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public static class TagManagerService
    {
        /// <summary>Simple DTO for the grid.</summary>
        public sealed class TagRow
        {
            public string Id { get; set; }
            public string System { get; set; }
            public string Status { get; set; }
            public string Drawing { get; set; }
            public string EntityHandle { get; set; }
            public string By { get; set; }
            public string Notes { get; set; }
        }

        /// <summary>Abstracts how we load/save the ProjectStore.</summary>
        public interface IProjectStoreGateway
        {
            /// <summary>Load current store (return null if not available).</summary>
            ProjectStoreV1 Load();

            /// <summary>Persist the store back to disk.</summary>
            void Save(ProjectStoreV1 store);

            /// <summary>Optional: where the store came from (for a status label).</summary>
            string SourceLabel { get; }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Default, safe gateway that keeps everything in memory.
        /// Swap this with your real ProjectTagStoreAdapter when wiring live.
        /// </summary>
        internal sealed class InMemoryGateway : IProjectStoreGateway
        {
            private ProjectStoreV1 _store;

            public InMemoryGateway()
            {
                // Minimal, valid v1 store seeded with an example system
                _store = new ProjectStoreV1
                {
                    Version = "1.0",
                    Config = new StoreConfigV1 { GraceDays = 7, PreferReleasedLowestFirst = true },
                    Systems = new Dictionary<string, SystemCounterV1>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                {
                    { "INST", new SystemCounterV1 { Prefix = "INST", Pad = 4, Start = 1, End = 9999, Next = 1 } }
                },
                    Tags = new List<TagRecordV1>(),
                    Released = new List<TagReleaseRecordV1>(),
                    History = new List<HistoryEntryV1>()
                };
            }

            public ProjectStoreV1 Load() => _store;

            public void Save(ProjectStoreV1 store)
            {
                if (store != null) _store = store;
            }

            public string SourceLabel => "(in-memory demo)";
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Public service surface the UI calls. Keeps POCOs and store logic apart.
        /// </summary>
        public sealed class TagManagerService
        {
            // Singleton is fine for palette usage.
            public static readonly TagManagerService Instance =
                new TagManagerService(new InMemoryGateway());

            private readonly IProjectStoreGateway _gw;

            /// <summary>
            /// Inject the real gateway here when you wire the live adapter.
            /// Example:
            ///   new TagManagerService(new ProjectTagStoreAdapterGateway());
            /// </summary>
            public TagManagerService(IProjectStoreGateway gateway)
            {
                _gw = gateway ?? new InMemoryGateway();
            }

            /// <summary>Human label for the UI status line.</summary>
            public string SourceLabel => _gw.SourceLabel ?? "(auto)";

            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            // Reads
            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            public IList<TagRow> GetAllTags()
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null || s.Tags == null) return new List<TagRow>();

                return s.Tags.Select(ToRow).ToList();
            }

            public IList<TagRow> GetOrphans()
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null || s.Tags == null) return new List<TagRow>();

                return s.Tags
                         .Where(t => t.Status == TagStatusV1.Orphan)
                         .Select(ToRow)
                         .ToList();
            }

            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            // Mutations
            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            /// <summary>
            /// Allocate next tag for a system. Returns the created row, or null if system invalid.
            /// </summary>
            public TagRow AllocateNext(string systemKey, string drawing, string entityHandle, string by, string notes)
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null) return null;

                // NOTE: using TagStoreV1 from shared project (already added earlier)
                var rec = TagStoreV1.AllocateNext(
                    s, systemKey ?? "", drawing ?? "", entityHandle ?? "", by ?? "", notes);

                _gw.Save(s);
                return ToRow(rec);
            }

            /// <summary>Release one or more orphaned IDs back to the pool.</summary>
            public int ReleaseMany(IEnumerable<string> ids, string reason, string by)
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null) return 0;

                var list = (ids ?? Enumerable.Empty<string>())
                           .Where(x => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(x))
                           .Select(x => x.Trim())
                           .Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                           .ToList();

                var count = 0;
                foreach (var id in list)
                {
                    try
                    {
                        if (TagStoreV1.Release(s, id, reason ?? "", by ?? "")) count++;
                    }
                    catch
                    {
                        // ignore invalid states; UI can surface a message if needed
                    }
                }

                if (count > 0) _gw.Save(s);
                return count;
            }

            /// <summary>Mark a tag as orphan (e.g., entity deleted/unlinked).</summary>
            public bool MarkOrphan(string id, string by, string details)
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null) return false;
                var ok = TagStoreV1.MarkOrphan(s, id ?? "", by ?? "", details ?? "");
                if (ok) _gw.Save(s);
                return ok;
            }

            /// <summary>Reassign a tag to a new entity/drawing.</summary>
            public bool Reassign(string id, string newEntityHandle, string newDrawing, string by)
            {
                var s = _gw.Load();
                if (s == null) return false;
                var rec = TagStoreV1.Reassign(s, id ?? "", newEntityHandle ?? "", newDrawing ?? "", by ?? "");
                if (rec != null) _gw.Save(s);
                return rec != null;
            }

            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            // Helpers
            // ---------------------------------------------------------------------
            private static TagRow ToRow(TagRecordV1 r)
            {
                if (r == null) return null;
                return new TagRow
                {
                    Id = r.Id,
                    System = r.System,
                    Status = r.Status.ToString(),
                    Drawing = r.Drawing,
                    EntityHandle = r.EntityHandle,
                    By = r.By,
                    Notes = r.Notes
                };
            }
        }
    }
}

## Response:
11/5/2025, 10:38:33 AM

> **Resolving TagManagerService duplication**
>
> We have two conflicting `TagManagerService` classes. I'll suggest keeping one as `TagManagerService` for palette functions and renaming the other to `ValveTagActions` for valve-related actions. Or, rename the service function to `TagManagerFacade` or `TagManagerApi`. This will resolve the duplication and eliminate errors from the previous conflicts in the code.
>
> **Addressing duplication and file organization**
>
> There’s an issue with a duplicate class, `TagManagerService`, causing errors. One class is static and the other is sealed, which is resulting in CS0542. To fix it, I suggest renaming the nested class to `Api` or `Manager`, and moving it into the appropriate folder. Additionally, I’ll recommend a file name change to `ValveTagActions.cs` for clarity.
>
> **Refining TagManager naming**
>
> I've noticed that in the second file, two classes, `TagManagerService`, are nested oddly, one static and one sealed. To fix this, I'll rename the outer static class to `TagManagerApi` and the sealed class to `Core`. This will simplify usage and make the code more maintainable by removing nesting. I’ll adjust the references accordingly.
>
> **Resolving CS0542 conflict**
>
> To resolve the CS0542 error, I will rename the inner class `TagManagerService` to `TagManagerCore`, while keeping the outer static class as `TagManagerService`. I’ll add a static `Instance` field in the outer class pointing to `TagManagerCore`. This fix won’t affect existing references, as they were already referring to the outer class.
>
> Thought for 41s

Yep—this is the root cause: you’ve got **two different classes with the same name** `TagManagerService` in two different namespaces (`AutoCAD2020.Tagging` and `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services`). That creates collisions (including the `CS0542` “member names cannot be the same as their enclosing type”).

Here’s the clean fix (no churn, minimal edits):

# 1) Rename the “valve actions” class
**File:** the one currently in `namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging` that contains `AddTagToSelectedValve`, `ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve`, and `SweepAutoReleaseOrphans`.

- Rename the class to **`ValveTagActions`**.
- (Optional but recommended) Move the file into `Tagging/Services` so all servicey things are together.  
  Namespace can remain `AutoCAD2020.Tagging` (that’s fine), or change to `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services`. Either way works—just be consistent with the using site.

**New header:**
```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging
{
    public static class ValveTagActions
    {
        // AddTagToSelectedValve()
        // ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve()
        // SweepAutoReleaseOrphans()
    }
}
```

# 2) Unclash the “palette service facade”
**File:** the one in `namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services` that currently defines:
- an outer **`public static class TagManagerService`** (with DTOs/gateway),
- and an inner **`public sealed class TagManagerService`** (the core).

Rename the **inner** one to **`TagManagerCore`**. Keep the outer name as-is.

Minimal change (only the class name):

```csharp
namespace AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services
{
    public static class TagManagerService
    {
        // TagRow, IProjectStoreGateway, InMemoryGateway ...

        public sealed class TagManagerCore
        {
            public static readonly TagManagerCore Instance =
                new TagManagerCore(new InMemoryGateway());

            // methods: GetAllTags(), AllocateNext(...), ReleaseMany(...), etc.
        }
    }
}
```

> This removes the `CS0542` (type name equals enclosing type) without touching any public API your UI might call.

# 3) Update call sites (exactly three)
In **Commands.cs** (and anywhere else you’re calling those three “actions”) switch to the new name:

```csharp
[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_AddToValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_AddToValve()
    => AutoCAD2020.Tagging.ValveTagActions.AddTagToSelectedValve();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ReleaseFromValve()
    => AutoCAD2020.Tagging.ValveTagActions.ReleaseTagFromSelectedValve();

[CommandMethod("CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans")]
public static void CBI_Tag_ScanOrphans()
    => AutoCAD2020.Tagging.ValveTagActions.SweepAutoReleaseOrphans();
```

If you moved the actions file into the `Services` namespace, the calls become:

```csharp
=> AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.ValveTagActions.AddTagToSelectedValve();
```

# 4) Quick sanity checks
- Search solution for `TagManagerService.` usages:
  - If it’s meant to open the **palette**, it should reference your **palette host/commands** (e.g., `TagManagerCommands.ShowPalette()`), not the service classes.
  - If it’s the **grid backing service** (data for the palette), it should use `AutoCAD2020.Tagging.Services.TagManagerService.TagManagerCore.Instance...`
- Search solution for `AddTagToSelectedValve` and confirm they now point to `ValveTagActions`.

---

## Why this works
- We avoid deleting either file; we **give each a unique purpose & name**:
  - `ValveTagActions` = user-facing command ops (touch DWG + XData + JSON).
  - `TagManagerService.TagManagerCore` = palette-friendly data/service façade.
- We remove the **type/name collision** that triggered the squiggle storm.
- We keep your current folder structure intention (Services under Tagging).

If you want, I can also spit out the exact patched headers for both files and the three `Commands.cs` edits in one go—just say the word.
